30 — The Traffic Type subfield is set to 1 for a periodic traffic pattern (e.g., isochronous TS of MSDUs or
31 A-MSDUs, with constant or variable sizes, that are originated at fixed rate) or set to 0 for an
32
aperiodic, or unspecified, traffic pattern (e.g., asynchronous TS of low-duty cycles).

information. It is reserved if the reported AP is operating in the 2.4GHz or 5 GHz bands.

43 detected by a STA receiving this frame. This means that all APs operating in the 6 GHz band that are part of that ESS

9.4.2.45 Multiple BSSID element
32
33
34 Change paragraphs 3 and 4 as follows:
35
36 The MaxBSSID Indicator field contains a value assigned to n, where 2n is the maximum number of BSSIDs
37
38 in the multiple BSSID set, including the reference BSSID (see 11.10.14 (Multiple BSSID set)).
39
NOTE—1 ≤ n ≤ 8 since the BSSID Index field in 9.4.2.73 (Multiple BSSID-Index element) indicates the number of
40
BSSIDs in a multiple BSSID set.
41
42
43 The actual number of BSSIDs in the multiple BSSID set is not explicitly signaled. The BSSID(i) value
44 corresponding to the ith BSSID in the multiple BSSID set is derived from a reference BSSID (REF_BSSID)
45 as follows:
46
47
BSSID(i) = BSSID_A | BSSID_B
48
49
50 where
51
52 BSSID_A is ( REF_BSSID & ZERO[(47-n+1):47] )
53
54 BSSID_B is ( ZERO[0:(47-n)] & bin[( ( dec(REF_BSSID[(47-n+1):47]) + i) mod 2n), n] )
55
56 and
57
ZERO[b:c] denotes bits b to c inclusive of a 48-bit address set to 0
58
REF_BSSID[b:c] denotes bits b to c inclusive of the REF_BSSID address
59
60
NOTE—For example, assuming the maximum number of BSSIDs in the multiple BSSID set is 8 (i.e., the MaxBSSID
61
Indicator field (n) is set to 3) and the transmitted (reference) BSSID is 8c:fd:0f:7f:1e:f5, the results would be as follows:
62
63
64 ZERO[(47-3+1):47] = ZERO[45:47]
65 Therefore, BSSID_A = ( 8c:fd:0f:7f:1e:f5 & ZERO[45:47] ) = 8c:fd:0f:7f:1e:f0
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 171
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 172 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 For BSSID(5), bin[( ( dec(REF_BSSID[45:47]) + 5) mod 8), 3] = bin[((dec(101)+5) mod 8) , 3] = bin[2, 3]
2 Therefore, BSSID(5) = 8c:fd:0f:7f:1e:f2
3
Similarly, BSSID(2) = 8c:fd:0f:7f:1e:f7

single BSSID across two Multiple BSSID elements unless the length contents of the nontransmitted BSSID

45 width of the pre-HE modulated fields of the HE TB PPDU transmitted by a STA. Each Local Maximum

47

53 If the BSS bandwidth is 20, 40, 80 or 160 MHz, then the Maximum Transmit PSD 1-N subfields correspond

56

57

61 Transmit PSD 1-M subfields correspond to the 20 MHz channels from lowest to highest frequency, respec-
62
tively, within the BSS bandwidth where M is 1, 2 or 4 for 20, 40 or 80 MHz BSS bandwidth, respectively.
63

64

25 The OCT Recommended subfield is set to 1 to indicate that OCT is recommended to exchange MMPDUs
26 with the AP identified in the TBTT Information field (see 11.32.5 (On-channel Tunneling (OCT) opera-
27
tion)), through over-the-air transmissions with the AP sending the Reduced Neighbor Report element. It is
28
set to 0 otherwise.
29
30
31 The Same SSID subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the reported AP has the same SSID as the reporting AP. It
32 is set to 0 otherwise.
33
34
The Multiple BSSID subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the reported AP is part of a multiple BSSID set. It is
35
36 set to 0 otherwise.
37
38 The Transmitted BSSID subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the reported AP is a transmitted BSSID. It is set
39
to 0 if the reported AP is a nontransmitted BSSID. It is reserved if the Multiple BSSID subfield is set to 0.
40
41
42 The Member Of ESS With 2.4/5 GHz Co-Located AP subfield is set to 1 if the reported AP is part of an ESS
43 where each AP in the ESS and operating in the same band as the reported AP (irrespective of the operating
44 channel in that band) that might be detected by a STA receiving this frame (see the detected access point
45
(AP) definition in 3.2 (Definitions specific to IEEE 802.11)) has dot11MemberOfColocated6GHzESSOp-
46
tionActivated equal to true and also has a corresponding AP operating in the 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz bands that is
47
48 in the same co-located AP set as that AP. It is set to 0 otherwise or if the reporting AP does not have that
49 information. It is reserved if the reported AP is operating in the 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz bands.
50
51 NOTE—This subfield indicates that the reported AP is part of an ESS that has no 6 GHz-only APs that might be
52 detected by a STA receiving this frame. This means that all APs operating in the 6 GHz band that are part of that ESS
53 that might be detected by a STA receiving this frame can be discovered in the 2.4 GHz and/or 5 GHz bands.
54
55 The Unsolicited Probe Responses Active subfield is set to 1 if the reported AP is part of an ESS where all the
56
APs that operate in the same channel as the reported AP and that might be detected by a STA receiving this
57
frame (see the detected access point (AP) definition in 3.2 (Definitions specific to IEEE 802.11)) have
58
59 dot11UnsolicitedProbeResponseOptionActivated equal to true and are transmitting unsolicited Probe
60 Response frames every 20 TUs or less (see 26.17.2.3 (Scanning in the 6 GHz band)). It is set to 0 otherwise
61 or if the reporting AP does not have that information.
62
63
The Co-Located AP subfield is set to 1 if every AP in this Neighbor AP Information field is in the same
64
65 co-located AP set as the transmitting AP. It is set to 0 otherwise.

1 The Negotiation Type subfield indicates whether the information included in the TWT element is for the
2 negotiation of parameters of broadcast or individual TWT(s) or a Wake TBTT interval. The MSB of the
3
Negotiation Type subfield is the Broadcast field.
4
5
6 The TWT Information Frame Disabled subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the reception of TWT Information
7 frames is disabled by the STA; otherwise, it is set to 0.
8
9
The Wake Duration Unit subfield indicates the unit of the Nominal Minimum TWT Wake Duration field.
10
The Wake Duration Unit subfield is set to 0 if the unit is 256 us and is set to 1 if the unit is a TU. A non-HE
11
12 STA sets the Wake Duration Unit subfield to 0.
13
14 If the Broadcast field of the Negotiation Type subfield is 1, then one or more broadcast TWT parameter sets
15
are contained in the TWT element (see Figure9-687b (Broadcast TWT Parameter Set field format)). If the
16
Broadcast field of the Negotiation Type subfield is 0, then only one Individual TWT parameter set is con-
17
18 tained in the TWT element (see Figure9-687a (Individual TWT Parameter Set field format)). An S1G STA
19 sets the Negotiation Type subfield to 0.
20
21

22
ment.

27

## 第 189 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 The Flow Type subfield indicates the type of interaction between the TWT requesting STA or TWT sched-
2
uled STA and the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP at a TWT. Setting the Flow Type subfield
3
to 0 indicates an announced TWT in which the TWT requesting STA or TWT scheduled STA will send a
4
5 PS-Poll or an APSD trigger frame (see 11.2.3.5 (Power management with APSD)) to signal its awake state
6 to the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP before a frame that is not a Trigger frame is sent from
7
the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP to the TWT requesting STA or TWT scheduled STA. A
8
9 TWT SP that is set up under an announced TWT agreement is an announced TWT SP. Setting the Flow
10 Type subfield to 1 indicates an unannounced TWT in which the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling
11
AP will send a frame to the TWT requesting STA or TWT scheduled STA at TWT without waiting to
12
13 receive a PS-Poll or an APSD trigger frame from the TWT requesting STA or TWT scheduled STA. A TWT
14 SP that is set up under an unannounced TWT agreement is an unannounced TWT SP.
15
16
NOTE—The TWT requesting STA is expected to send the PS-Poll or APSD trigger frame if the TWT is a trig-
17
ger-enabled TWT.
18
19

22 this TWT request uniquely from other requests made between the same TWT requesting STA and TWT
23 responding STA pair. The Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield contains a value that indicates recom-
24
mendations on the types of frames that are transmitted by TWT scheduled STAs and scheduling AP during
25
26 the broadcast TWT SP, encoded according to the Broadcast TWT Recommendation field for a broadcast
27 TWT element as defined in Table9-297a (Broadcast TWT Recommendation field for a broadcast TWT ele-
28
ment). The Broadcast TWT Recommendation is reserved if transmitted by a TWT scheduled STA.

36 The Nominal Minimum TWT Wake Duration field indicates the minimum amount of time, in the units of

43 Change the 22nd and subsequent two paragraphs as follows:
44
45
When transmitted by a TWT requesting STA, the TWT Channel field contains a bitmap indicating which
46
47 channel the STA requests to use as a temporary primary channel during a TWT SP. When transmitted by a
48 TWT responding STA, the TWT Channel field contains a bitmap indicating which channel the TWT
49 requesting STA is allowed to use as a temporary channel during the TWT SP. The TWT Channel field
50
includes a bitmap that provides the channel that is being negotiated by a STA as a temporary channel during
51
a TWT SP. Each bit in the bitmap corresponds to one minimum width channel for the band in which the
52
53 TWT responding STA's associated BSS is currently operating, with the least significant bit corresponding to
54 the lowest numbered channel of the operating channels of the BSS. In an S1G BSS, the The minimum width
55 channel is equal to the SST Channel Unit field of the SST Operation element if such an element has been
56
previously received or is equal to 1 MHz for a BSS with a BSS primary channel width of 1 MHz and 2 MHz
57
for a BSS with a BSS primary channel width of 2 MHz if no such element has been previously received from
58
59 the AP to which the SST STA is associated. In an HE BSS, the minimum width channel is equal to 20 MHz.
60 A value of 1 in a bit position in the bitmap transmitted by a TWT requesting STA means that operation with
61 that channel as the primary temporary channel is requested during a TWT SP. A value of 1 in a bit position
62
in the bitmap transmitted by a TWT responding STA means that operation with that channel as the primary
63
temporary channel is allowed during the TWT SP. The TWT Channel field is used by an S1G STA as
64
65 defined in 10.53 (Subchannel Selective Transmission (SST)) and is used by an HE STA as defined in 26.8.7

1 (HE subchannel selective transmission).If the TWT channel field is 0 then the STA operates as define in

5
6 vide protection of the set of TWT SPs corresponding to the requested TWT flow identifier by allocating

34 9.4.2.248.5 PPE Thresholds field
35
36
The PPE Thresholds field determines the nominal packet padding value (see 26.12 (HE PPDU post-FEC
37
padding and packet extension)) for an HE PPDU of a particular RU allocation size and NSTS value. The for-
38
39 mat of the PPE Thresholds field is defined in Figure9-788f (PPE Thresholds field format).
40
41
42 B0 B2 B3 B6
43
44 PPE
RU Index
45 NSTS Bitmask Thresholds PPE Pad
Info
46
47
48 Bits: 3 4 variable 0 to 7
49
50 Figure 9-788f—PPE Thresholds field format
51
52
The NSTS subfield contains an unsigned integer that is the number of NSTS values minus 1 for which PPE
53
54 threshold values are included in the PPE Thresholds List subfield.
55
56
The RU Index Bitmask subfield contains a bitmask that indicates whether the PPE Thresholds Info field
57
contains PPET16 and PPET8 values for the four possible RU sizes indicated in Table9-322e (RU allocation
58
59 index). The PPET16 and PPET8 values for RU allocation index k is present in the PPE Thresholds Info field
60 only if bit k of the RU Index Bitmask subfield (bit 3+k of the PPE Thresholds field) is 1. For example, if B0
61 of the RU Index Bitmask subfield (B3 of the PPE Thresholds field) is 1, PPET16 and PPET8 values are
62
present in the PPE Thresholds Info field for the RU allocation size corresponding to RU allocation index 0
63
(242-tone RU). If B0 of the RU Index Bitmask subfield is 0, PPET16 and PPET8 values are not present in
64
65 the PPE Thresholds Info field for the RU allocation size corresponding to RU allocation index 0.

defined in Figure9-788g (PPE Thresholds Info field format).

20
fields corresponding to a single value of NSTS, lower numbered PPE Thresholds Info field bits contain
21
PPET16 and PPET8 subfields corresponding to lower numbered RU index values. The PPET16 NSTSn
22
23 RUb and PPET8 NSTSn RUb subfields are present for all values of n and b where 1≤n≤(NSTS+1) and
24 where b = [x, …, m] is the set of integers equal to the ordered list of bit positions of all bits that are set to 1 in
25 the RU Index Bitmask subfield, with x being the lowest value.

1 The Default PE Duration subfield indicates the PE field duration in units of 4 µs for an HE TB PPDU that is
2 solicited with a TRS Control subfield and its use is defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU
3
operation). Values 5-7 of the Default PE Duration subfield are reserved.
4
5
6 The TWT Required subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the AP requires its associated non-AP HE STAs that
7 have declared support for TWT by setting any one of TWT Requester Support or TWT Responder Support
8
or Broadcast TWT Support subfield in HE Capabilities element that it transmits to 1 to operate in the role of
9
either TWT requesting STA by following the rules in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements), or TWT sched-
10
11 uled STA by following the rules in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation) and set to 0 otherwise.
12
13
The TXOP Duration RTS Threshold subfield enables an HE AP to manage RTS/CTS usage by non-AP HE
14
STAs that are associated with it (see 26.2.1 (TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS)). The TXOP Duration RTS
15
16 Threshold subfield contains the TXOP duration RTS threshold in units of 32 µs, which enables the use of
17 RTS/CTS except for the value 1023. The value 1023 indicates that TXOP duration-based RTS is disabled.
18 The value of 0 is allowed in Beacon and Probe Response frames and indicates that the previously announced
19
TXOP duration RTS threshold remains in effect. In all other frames, the value of 0 is reserved.
20
21
22 The VHT Operation Information Present subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the VHT Operation Information
23 field is present in the HE Operation element and set to 0 otherwise. The VHT Operation Information Present
24
subfield is set as defined in 26.17 (HE BSS operation).
25
26
27 The Co-Hosted BSS subfield is set to 1 to indicate that the AP transmitting this element shares the same
28 operating class, channel, receive antenna connector, and transmit antenna connector with at least one other
29
AP that is providing its BSS information by transmitting Beacon and Probe Response frames. Otherwise the
30
subfield is set to 0. An AP operating in the 6 GHz band, a TDLS STA, an IBSS STA, a mesh STA, or an AP
31
32 with dot11MultiBSSImplemented equal to true sets the subfield to 0.
33
34
The ER SU Disable subfield indicates whether 242-tone HE ER SU PPDU reception by the AP is disabled
35
36 or enabled. The ER SU Disable subfield is set to 1 to indicate that it is disabled and set to 0 to indicate that it
37 is enabled.
38
39
The 6 GHz Operation Information Present field is set to 1 to indicate that the 6 GHz Operation Information
40
41 field is present and set to 0, otherwise. The 6 GHz Operation Information Present field is set to 1 by an AP
42 operating in the 6 GHz band.
43
44
The BSS Color Information field is defined in Figure9-788j (BSS Color Information field format).

33
34
Bits: 1 1 1 1 1 3
35
36
Figure 9-788r—SR Control field format
37
38
39
The PSR Disallowed subfield in the SR Control field indicates whether or not PSR-based SR transmissions
40
41 are allowed at non-AP STAs that are associated with the AP that transmitted this element. PSR-based SR
42 transmissions are disallowed when the PSR Disallowed subfield is 1. PSR-based SR transmissions are
43 allowed when the PSR Disallowed subfield is 0. The PSR Disallowed subfield also affects the value of the
44
SPATIAL_REUSE parameter of the TXVECTOR as described in 26.11.6 (SPATIAL_REUSE).
45
46
47 The Non-SRG OBSS PD SR Disallowed subfield in the SR Control field indicates whether non-SRG OBSS
48
PD SR transmissions are allowed or not at non-AP STAs that are associated with the AP that transmitted this
49
element. Non-SRG OBSS PD SR transmissions are disallowed when the Non-SRG OBSS PD SR Disal-
50
51 lowed subfield is 1. Non-SRG OBSS PD SR transmissions are allowed when the Non-SRG OBSS PD SR
52 Disallowed subfield is 0.
53
54
55 The Non-SRG Offset Present subfield in the SR Control field indicates whether or not the Non-SRG OBSS
56 PD Max Offset field is present in the element. If the Non-SRG Offset Present subfield is 1, then the
57 Non-SRG OBSS PD Max Offset field is present; otherwise the Non-SRG OBSS PD Max Offset field is not
58
present.
59
60
61 The SRG Information Present subfield in the SR Control field indicates whether or not the SRG OBSS PD
62
Min Offset, SRG OBSS PD Max Offset, SRG BSS Color Bitmap and SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap fields are
63
present in the element. If the SRG Information Present subfield is 1, then the subfields are present; otherwise
64
65 the fields are not present.
220 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 221 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 The HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed subfield in the SR Control field indicates whether non-AP
2 STAs that are associated with the AP that transmitted this element can set the TXVECTOR parameter SPA-
3
TIAL_REUSE to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED. The subfield is set as described in
4
26.11.6 (SPATIAL_REUSE).
5
6
7 The Non-SRG OBSS PD Max Offset field contains an unsigned integer that is added to –82 dBm to generate
8
the value of the Non-SRG OBSS PD Max parameter.
9
10
11 The SRG OBSS PD Min Offset field contains an unsigned integer that is added to –82 dBm to generate the
12 value of the SRG OBSS PD Min parameter.
13
14
15 The SRG OBSS PD Max Offset field contains an unsigned integer that is added to –82 dBm to generate the
16 value of the SRG OBSS PD Max parameter.
17
18
19 The SRG BSS Color Bitmap field is a bitmap that indicates the BSS color values used by members of the
20 SRG of which the transmitting STA is a member. Each bit of the bitmap corresponds to one of the 64 BSS
21 colors, where the lowest numbered bit corresponds to BSS color value 0 and the highest numbered bit corre-
22
sponds to BSS color value 63. A BSS color value is used by at least one BSS that is a member of the same
23
SRG of the transmitting STA if the corresponding bit of the bitmap is 1. If a bit in the bitmap is 0, then no
24
25 BSS in the same SRG of the transmitting STA uses the corresponding BSS color value. The bit in the bitmap
26 that corresponds to the BSS color value 0 is reserved.
27
28
The SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap field is a bitmap that indicates the Partial BSSID values used by members
29
30 of the SRG of which the transmitting STA is a member. Each bit of the bitmap corresponds to one of the 64
31 possible values of BSSID[39:44], where the lowest numbered bit corresponds to Partial BSSID value 0 and
32 the highest numbered bit corresponds to Partial BSSID value 63. A Partial BSSID value is used by at least
33
one BSS that is a member of the same SRG of the transmitting STA if the corresponding bit of the bitmap is
34
1. If a bit in the bitmap is 0, then no BSS in the same SRG of the transmitting STA uses the corresponding
35
36 Partial BSSID value.
37
38

31 9.4.2.255.2 Quiet Time Period Setup
32
33
34 The Quiet Time Period Setup subtype defines a period of time for QTP (see 26.17.5 (Quiet HE STAs in an
35
HE BSS)). The QTP can be used by an AP to mitigate interference by reducing the contention from HE
36
37 STAs in a period that gives preference to HE STAs participating in the exchange of specific frames using
38 peer-to-peer link.
39
40
41 The content of the Quiet Time Content subfield in the Quiet Time Period Setup subtype is shown
42

43
44
45
46
47

48
Period Specific
49 Duration Identifier
50
51
Octets: 2 2
52
53
Figure 9-788w—Quiet Time Content subfield format in Quiet Time Period Setup subtype
54
55
56
The Quiet Period Duration field is set to the duration of the QTP, in units of 32 µs, that is no larger than the
57
58 value indicated in the Quiet Period Interval subtype field of the Quiet Time Period Request subtype sent by
59 the QTP requesting STA.
60
61
62 The Service Specific Identifier field contains an identifier assigned by a peer-to-peer application to identify
63
specific frame exchanges using peer-to-peer links during which HE STAs that have requested participation
64
65 of the specified frame exchanges might transmit frames during the quiet time period.

1 9.4.2.255.3 Quiet Time Period Request
2
3

4
5 requests the QTP AP to schedule.

8
Figure9-788x (Quiet Time Content subfield format in Quiet Time Period Request subtype).

23

25
26 The Quiet Period Offset field is set to the offset of the first QTP from the TBTT expressed in TUs.
27
28

30 expressed in TUs.
31

34
35 The Repetition Count field is set to the number of requested QTPs. A repetition count equal to 0 indicates
36 the setup time of the QTP is for an one time operation. Repetition count equals to 0xFF indicates the setup of
37
the QTP is canceled.
38
39
40 The Service Specific Identifier field contains an identifier assigned by a peer-to-peer application to identify
41 specific frame exchanges using peer-to-peer links during which HE STAs that have requested the participa-
42
tion of the specified frame exchanges might transmit frames during the quiet time period.

13 The Bitmap subfield has a length of 2n bits where n is the value carried in the MaxBSSID Indicator field of

47 Short SSID).

38 Figure 9-970a—MU EDCA Control field format
39
40
The Affected ACs subfield contains a bitmap that indicates the ACs for which the MUEDCATimer[AC] as
41
42 defined in 26.2.7 (EDCA operation using MU EDCA parameters) are reset to 0 upon receipt of the MU
43 EDCA Reset frame for affected STAs. Each bit in the bitmap corresponds to one AC with B0 mapped to
44 AC_BK, B1 mapped to AC_BE, B2 mapped to AC_VI and B3 mapped to AC_VO. A value of 1 in the bit
45
position corresponding to a given AC indicates that the MUEDCATimer[AC] for that AC is reset to 0, oth-
46
erwise, the value of the bit is 0.

1 — 0–3 octets in the Padding subfield of the final A-MPDU subframe (see Figure 9-740 (A-MPDU sub-
2 frame format)) before any EOF padding subframes. The content of these octets is unspecified.
3
4 — Zero or more EOF padding subframes in the EOF Padding Subframes subfield.
5
— 0–3 octets in the EOF Padding Octets subfield. The content of these octets is unspecified.
6
7
8
An A-MPDU pre-EOF padding refers to the contents of the A-MPDU up to, but not including, the EOF Pad-
9
ding field.
10
11
NOTE—A-MPDU pre-EOF padding includes any A-MPDU subframes with 0 in the MPDU Length field and 0 in the
12
EOF/Tag field inserted in order to meet the minimum MPDU start spacing requirement.
13
14
15 The maximum length of an A-MPDU in an HT PPDU is 65535 octets. The maximum length of an
16 A-MPDU in a DMG PPDU is 262143 octets. The maximum length of an A-MPDU pre-EOF padding in a
17
VHT PPDU is 1048575 octets. The maximum length of an A-MPDU pre-EOF padding in an HE PPDU is
18
6500631 octets. The length of an A-MPDU addressed to a particular STA can be further constrained as
19
20 described in 10.12.2 (A-MPDU length limit rules).

32 9.7.3 A-MPDU contents
33
34
Change the 1st paragraph of this subclause as follows:
35
36
37 In a non-DMG PPDU, an A-MPDU is a sequence of A-MPDU subframes carried in a single PPDU with one
38
of the following combinations of RXVECTOR or TXVECTOR parameter values:
39
40 — The FORMAT parameter set to VHT
41
— The FORMAT parameter set to HT_MF or HT_GF and the AGGREGATION parameter set to 1
42
43 — The FORMAT parameter set to S1G, S1G_DUP_1M, or S1G_DUP_2M and the AGGREGATION
44
parameter set to 1
45
46 — The FORMAT parameter set to HE_SU, HE_MU, HE_TB or HE_ER_SU
47
48
Insert the following after the 1st paragraph:
49
50
51 An A-MPDU carried in an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, HE TB PPDU or HE MU PPDU can include
52
MPDUs with different values of the TID field as described in 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled
53
single-TID A-MPDU).
54
55
56 Change the 3rd paragraph as follows:
57
58
All of the MPDUs within an A-MPDU are addressed to the same RA. All of the MPDUs within an
59
60 A-MPDU have the same TA. All QoS Data frames within an A-MPDU that have a TID for which an
61 HT-immediate block ack agreement exists have the same value for the Ack Policy Indicator subfield of the
62 QoS Control field.

1 10. MAC sublayer functional description
2
3
4
10.1 Introduction
5
6
7 Change as follows:
8
9
10 The MAC functional description is presented in this clause. The architecture of the MAC sublayer, including
11 the distributed coordination function (DCF), the hybrid coordination function (HCF), the mesh coordination
12 function (MCF), the triggered UL access (TUA), and their coexistence in an IEEE 802.11 LAN are intro-
13
duced in 10.2 (MAC architecture). These functions are expanded on in 10.3 (DCF), 10.23 (HCF), and 10.24
14
(Mesh coordination function (MCF)), and 26.2 (HE channel access). Fragmentation and defragmentation are
15
16 defined in 10.4 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and MMPDU fragmentation) and 10.5 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and
17 MMPDU defragmentation). Multirate support is addressed in 10.6 (Multirate support). A number of addi-
18 tional restrictions to limit the cases in which MSDUs are reordered or discarded are described in 10.7
19
(MSDU transmission restrictions). Operation across regulatory domains is defined in 10.22 (Operation
20
across regulatory domains). The block ack mechanism is described in 10.25 (Block acknowledgment (block
21
22 ack)). The No Ack mechanism is described in 10.26 (No Acknowledgment (No Ack)). The protection mech-
23 anism is described in 10.27 (Protection mechanisms). Rules for processing MAC frames are described in
24 10.28 (MAC frame processing).
25
26
27

12 10.2.3.2 HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)
13
14
Insert the following after the 4th paragraph:
15
16
17 An HE AP can additionally provide MU EDCA parameters for non-AP HE STAs as defined in 26.2.7
18 (EDCA operation using MU EDCA parameters).
19
20
Change the 6th paragraph as follows:
21
22
23 A QoS STA, on receiving an updated EDCA Parameter Set element, should update the dot11EDCATable as
24
soon as practical in the implementation and shall update the dot11EDCATable within an interval of time
25
equal to one beacon interval. QoS STAs update the dot11EDCATable and store the EDCA Parameter Set
26
27 update count value in the QoS Info field. An S1G STA shall update its dot11EDCATable if its STA type is
28 indicated by the STA Type subfield contained in the received EDCA Parameter Set element (see 10.61 (S1G
29 flow control)). An AP may change the EDCA access parameters by changing the EDCA Parameter Set ele-
30
ment in the Beacon frame, Probe Response frame, and (Re)Association Response frame. However, the AP
31
should change them only rarely. A QoS STA shall use the EDCA Parameter Set Update Count Value sub-
32
33 field in the QoS Capability element of all Beacon frames to determine whether the STA is using the current
34 EDCA Parameter Values. If the EDCA Parameter Set update count value in the QoS Capability element is
35 different from the value that has been stored, the QoS STA shall query the updated EDCA parameter values
36
by sending a Probe Request frame to the AP.
37
38
39 Change the 13th paragraph as follows:
40
41
BlockAckReq and BlockAck frames shall be sent using the same access category for medium access as the
42
corresponding QoS Data frames. When a BlockAckReq, Multi-TID BlockAckReq or Multi-STA BlockAck
43
44 frame is transmitted by an HE STA, its operation is defined in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure).
45
46 Insert a new subclause as follows after 10.2.4:
47
48
49 10.2.4a Triggered uplink access (TUA)
50
51 A non-AP HE STA supports trigger-based UL access methods. Triggered UL access (TUA) is used when an
52
HE AP triggers one or more non-AP HE STAs to transmit HE TB PPDUs simultaneously. The optional UL
53
OFDMA-based random access (UORA) additionally allows a non-AP HE STA to access one of a number of
54
55 resource units designated for random access by the HE AP. See 26.5.2 (UL MU operation) and 26.5.4 (UL
56 OFDMA-based random access (UORA)).
57
58
Change the title and first paragraph of 10.2.5 as follows:
59
60
61 10.2.5 Combined use of DCF, and HCF, and TUA
62
63
The DCF, and the hybrid coordination function and TUA are defined so they may operate within the same
64
65 BSS. The HCF access methods (controlled and contention based) operate sequentially. Sequential operation
258 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 259 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 allows the polled and contention based access methods to alternate, within intervals as short as the time to
2 transmit a frame exchange sequence, under rules defined in 10.23 (HCF).
3
4
5 10.3 DCF
6
7
8 10.3.1 General
9
10 Change the 6th paragraph as follows:
11
12
13 The virtual CS mechanism is achieved by distributing reservation information announcing the impending
14 use of the medium. The exchange of RTS and CTS frames prior to the actual Data frame is one means of dis-
15 tribution of this medium reservation information. The RTS and CTS frames contain a Duration field that
16
defines the period of time that the medium is to be reserved to transmit the actual Data frame and the return-
17
ing Ack frame. A STA receiving either the RTS frame (sent by the originating STA) or the CTS frame (sent
18
19 by the destination STA) shall process the medium reservation. Thus, a STA might be unable to receive from
20 the originating STA and yet still know about the impending use of the medium to transmit a Data frame. The
21 exchange of an MU-RTS Trigger frame and simultaneous CTS frame responses by HE STAs prior to the
22
actual Data frames is another means of distribution of this medium reservation information.
23
24
25 Change the 11th paragraph as follows:
26
27 The use of the RTS/CTS mechanism under control of dot11RTSThreshold if dot11TXOPDurationRTS-
28
Threshold is 1023 or is not present is described in 10.3.5 (Individually addressed MPDU transfer proce-
29
dure).
30
31
32 The use of the RTS/CTS mechanism is under the control of dot11TXOPDurationRTSThreshold if dot11TX-
33 OPDurationRTSThreshold is present and is not 1023. If this mechanism is enabled, a non-AP HE STA shall
34
initiate a TXOP that is used for individually addressed frames with an RTS/CTS exchange as defined in
35
26.2.1 (TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS).
36
37
38 10.3.2 Procedures common to the DCF and EDCAF
39
40
10.3.2.1 CS mechanism
41
42
43 Change the 4th paragraph of the subclause as follows:
44
45
The NAV maintains a prediction of future traffic on the medium based on duration information that is
46
announced in RTS/CTS frames by non-DMG STAs, MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frames by HE STAs as defined
47
48 in 26.2.6 (MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frame exchange procedure), and RTS/DMG CTS frames by DMG STAs
49 prior to the actual exchange of data. The duration information is also available in the MAC headers of all
50 frames other than PV1 MAC frames and PS-Poll frames, and during the BTI, the A-BFT, the ATI, the
51
CBAP, and the SP. The duration information in a frame transmitted by an S1G STA is also available in PS-
52
Poll+BDT frames, in NDP CTS frames, in NDP Ack frames whose Idle Indication field value is 0, and in
53
54 NDP_2M PS-Poll-Ack frames whose Idle Indication field is 0. The duration information might also be
55 available in the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION when an HE PPDU is received (see 26.11.5
56 (TXOP_DURATION)).
57
58
59 Change the 7th paragraph as follows:
60
61 The CS mechanism combines the NAV state, and in S1G STAs also the RID state, and the STA’s transmitter
62
status with physical CS to determine the busy/idle state of the medium. The NAV and RID may be thought of
63
as a counter, which counts down to 0 at a uniform rate. In non-S1G STAs, when the NAV counter is 0, the vir-
64
65 tual CS indication is that the medium is idle; when the counter is nonzero, the indication is busy. In S1G STAs,
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 259
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 260 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 when both NAV and RID counters are 0, the virtual CS indication is that the medium is idle; when either the
2 NAV counter or the RID counter is nonzero the indication is that the medium is busy. The virtual CS indication
3
of medium for HE STAs with two NAVs is described in 26.2.4 (Updating two NAVs). If a DMG STA supports
4
multiple NAVs as defined in 10.40.10 (Updating multiple NAV) and all counters are 0, the virtual CS indica-
5
6 tion is that the medium is idle; when at least one of the counters is nonzero, the indication is busy. The medium
7 shall be determined to be busy when the STA is transmitting.
8
9
10.3.2.3 IFS
10
11
12 10.3.2.3.3 SIFS
13
14 Insert the following before the 1st paragraph:
15
16
An HE STA that transmits an HE TB PPDU a SIFS after the end of a received PPDU follows the procedure
17
18 in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
19
20 10.3.2.3.7 EIFS
21
22
Change as follows:
23
24
25 A DCF shall use EIFS before transmission, when it determines that the medium is idle immediately follow-
26
ing reception of a frame for which the PHY-RXEND.indication primitive contained an error or a frame for
27
which the FCS value was not correct. Similarly, a STA's EDCA mechanism under HCF shall use the EIFS-
28
29 DIFS+AIFS[AC] interval. The duration of an EIFS is defined in 10.3.7 (DCF timing relations). The EIFS or
30 EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC] interval shall begin following indication by the PHY that the medium is idle after
31 detection of the erroneous frame, without regard to the virtual CS mechanism. The STA shall not begin a
32
transmission until the expiration of the later of the NAV and EIFS or EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC]. The EIFS and
33
EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC] are defined to provide enough time for another STA to acknowledge what was, to
34
35 this STA, an incorrectly received frame before this STA commences transmission. Reception of an error-
36 free frame or reception of an HE PPDU with the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION that is not set
37 to UNSPECIFIED during the EIFS or EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC] resynchronizes the STA to the actual busy/
38
idle state of the medium, so the EIFS or EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC] is terminated and medium access (using
39
DIFS or AIFS as appropriate and, if necessary, backoff) continues following reception of that the error-free
40
41 frame or following an expected end of the received HE PPDU. At the expiration or termination of the EIFS
42 or EIFS-DIFS+AIFS[AC], the STA reverts to the NAV and physical CS to control access to the medium.
43
44
EIFS shall not be invoked for an A-MPDU if one or more of its frames are received correctly. EIFS shall not
45
be invoked if the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION of a received HE PPDU is not set to
46
47 UNSPECIFIED.
48
49 10.3.2.4 Setting and resetting the NAV
50
51
Change paragraphs 1-2 as follows:
52
53
54 This subclause describes the setting and resetting of the NAV for non-DMG STAs and DMG STAs that sup-
55 port a single NAV. DMG STAs that support multiple NAVs shall update their NAVs according to the proce-
56
dures described in 10.39.10 (Updating multiple NAVs). HE STAs with two NAV timers shall update their
57
NAV timers according to the procedures described in 26.2.4 (Updating two NAVs).
58
59
60 A STA that receives at least one valid frame in a PSDU can update its NAV with the information from any
61 valid Duration field in the PSDU. When the received frame’s RA is equal to the STA’s own MAC address,
62
the STA shall not update its NAV. Further, when the received frame is a DMG CTS frame and its TA is
63
equal to the STA’s own MAC address, the STA shall not update its NAV. For all other received frames the
64
65 STA shall update its NAV when the received Duration is greater than the STA’s current NAV value. Upon
260 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 261 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 receipt of a PS-Poll frame, a STA, except for an S1G STA for which the RXVECTOR parameter RESPON-
2 SE_INDICATION of the received PS-Poll frame is NDP Response, shall update its NAV settings as appro-
3
priate under the data rate selection rules using a duration value equal to the time, in microseconds, required
4
to transmit one Ack frame plus one SIFS, but only when the new NAV value is greater than the current NAV
5
6 value. If the calculated duration includes a fractional microsecond, that value is rounded up to the next
7 higher integer. When the NAV is reset, a PHY-CCARESET.request primitive shall be issued. This NAV
8 update operation is performed when the PHY-RXEND.indication primitive is received, except when the
9
PHY-RXEND.indication primitive is received before the end of the PPDU, in which case the NAV update is
10
performed at the expected end of the PPDU.
11
12

14

48 Change the 6th and 7th paragraph as follows:
49
50 A STA that used information from an RTS frame or MU-RTS Trigger frame as the most recent basis to update
51
its NAV setting is permitted to reset its NAV if no PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive is received from the
52
PHY during a NAVTimeout period starting when the MAC receives a PHY-RXEND.indication primitive cor-
53
54 responding to the detection of the RTS frame or MU-RTS Trigger frame.
55
56
In non-DMG BSS, NAVTimeout period is equal to (2 × aSIFSTime) + (CTS_Time) + aRxPHYStartDelay + (2
57
× aSlotTime). In a non-S1G STA, if an RTS frame is used for the most recent NAV update, the CTS_Time
58
59 shall be calculated using the length of the CTS frame and the data rate at which the RTS frame used for the
60 most recent NAV update was received. If an MU-RTS Trigger frame was used for the most recent NAV
61 update, CTS_Time shall be calculated using the length of the CTS frame and the 6 Mb/s data rate (see 26.2.6
62
(MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frame exchange procedure)). In an S1G STA, the CTS_Time shall be calculated using
63
the time required to transmit an NDP CTS frame that is equal to NDPTxTime, which is specified in 10.3.2.5.2
64
65 (RID update).

1 10.3.2.6 RTS/CTS with fragmentation
2
3
Change the 2nd paragraph as follows:
4
5
6 Each frame contains information that defines the duration of the next transmission. The duration information
7 from RTS frames shall be used to update the NAV to indicate busy until the end of Ack frame 0. The dura-
8 tion information from the CTS frame shall also be used to update the NAV to indicate busy until the end of
9
Ack frame 0. Both Fragment 0 and Ack frame 0 shall contain duration information to update the NAV to
10
indicate busy until the end of Ack frame 1. This shall be done by using the Duration/ID field in the Data and
11
12 Ack frames. This shall continue until the last fragment, which shall have a duration of one Ack time plus one
13 SIFS, and its Ack frame, which shall have its Duration/ID field set to 0. Each fragment and Ack frame acts
14 as a virtual RTS frame and CTS frame; therefore no further RTS/CTS frames need to be generated after the
15
RTS/CTS that began the frame exchange sequence even though the PSDUs carrying subsequent fragments
16
may be larger than dot11RTSThreshold.

24 10.3.2.13.1 Acknowledgment procedure for DL MU PPDU in SU PPDU
25
26
Change the subclause as follows:
27
28
29 The acknowledgment procedure performed by a STA that receives MPDUs that were transmitted within a
30 VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU is the same as the acknowledgment procedure for MPDUs that were
31 not transmitted within a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU sent by an AP, except if the STA is an HE
32
STA that follows the rules defined in 26.3 (Fragmentation and defragmentation) and in 26.4 (HE acknowl-
33
edgment procedure).
34
35
36 The acknowledgment procedure performed by a STA that receives MPDUs that were transmitted within a
37
VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU is the same as the acknowledgment procedure for MPDUs that were
38
not transmitted within a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU sent by an AP, except if the STA is an HE
39
40 STA that follows the rules defined in 26.3 (Fragmentation and defragmentation) and in 26.4 (HE acknowl-
41 edgment procedure).
42
43 NOTE—All MPDUs transmitted within a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU are contained within A-MPDUs, and
44 the rules specified in 9.7.3 (A-MPDU contents) prevent an immediate response carried in an SU PPDU to more than one
45 of the A-MPDUs.
46
47 Responses to A-MPDUs within a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU for DL transmission that are not
48 immediate responses to the VHT MU PPDU or the HE MU PPDU are transmitted in response to explicit
49
BlockAckReq frames by the AP. Examples of VHT MU PPDU frame exchange sequences are shown in Fig-
50
ure 10-11 (An example of a TXOP containing a VHT MU PPDU transmission with an immediate acknowl-
51
52 edgment to the VHT MU PPDU) and Figure 10-15 (An example of a TXOP containing a VHT MU PPDU
53 transmission with no immediate acknowledgment to the VHT MU PPDU).
54
55
Recovery within the TXOP that contains a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU can be performed accord-
56
ing to the rules of 10.23.2.8 (Multiple frame transmission in an EDCA TXOP). BlockAckRequest frames
57
58 related to A-MPDUs within a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU can be transmitted in a TXOP separate
59 from the one that contained the VHT MU PPDU or the HE MU PPDU.
60
61 NOTE 1—A BlockAck frame or an Ack frame is sent in immediate response to the BlockAckReq frame for HT-imme-
62 diate or HT-delayed block ack, respectively. An Ack frame might be sent in immediate response carried in an SU PPDU
63 to an S-MPDU in the VHT MU PPDU or the HE MU PPDU. Responses to S-MPDUs for more than one STA contained
64 in an HE MU PPDU are transmitted as specified in 10.3.2.13.2 (Acknowledgment procedure for DL MU PPDU in MU
65 format). A Multi-STA BlockAck frame is sent in immediate response to a Multi-TID BlockAckReq frame.

55 A If dot11TXOPDurationRTSThreshold is 1023 or not present, a STA shall use an RTS/CTS preceding a

61 A fragment is an MPDU, the Frame Body field of which carries only a portion of an MSDU, A-MSDU or

63
security mechanism) shall be limited by dot11FragmentationThreshold and the number of octets in the
64
65 MPDU that have yet to be assigned to a fragment at the instant the fragment is constructed for the first time.

56 f) A control response frame may be carried in a VHT PPDU or HT PPDU if the eliciting frame was a

1 10.6.6.6 Channel Width selection for Control frames
2
3
Change the 2nd paragraph as follows:
4
5
6 If a VHT or HE STA transmits to another VHT or HE STA a Control frame that is not an RTS frame or a
7 CF-End frame, if that Control frame is an HE NDP Announcement frame or elicits a control response frame,
8 or a VHT Compressed Beamforming frame, or an HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame, and
9
— If the Control frame is transmitted in a non-HT duplicate PPDU (channel width 40 MHz or wider),
10
11 the transmitting VHT or HE STA shall set the TA field to a bandwidth signaling TA.
12
— If the Control frame is transmitted in a non-HT PPDU (channel width 20 MHz), the transmitting
13
VHT or HE STA may set the TA field to a bandwidth signaling TA.

## 第 273 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 10.6.11 Non-HT basic rate calculation
2
3
4 Change as follows:
5
6
This subclause defines how to convert an HT-MCS or, a VHT-MCS or an HE-MCS to a non-HT basic rate for
7
8 the purpose of determining the rate of the response frame. It consists of two steps as follows:
9
10 a) Use the modulation and coding rate determined from the HT-MCS (defined in 19.5 (Parameters for
11 HT-MCSs)) or VHT-MCS (defined in 21.5 (Parameters for VHT-MCSs)) or HE-MCS (defined in
12 27.5 (Parameters for HE-MCSs)) to locate a non-HT reference rate by lookup into Table10-10
13 (Non-HT reference rate).1 In the case of an MCS with UEQM, the modulation of stream 1 is used.
14
15 b) The non-HT basic rate is the highest rate in the BSSBasicRateSet that is less than or equal to this
16
non-HT reference rate.
17
18
NOTE 1—The selection of a non-HT basic rate for the frame sent in response to an HE PPDU is not influenced by DCM
19
encoding in the HE PPDU.
20
21
NOTE 2—In a TVWS band, the non-HT reference rate is scaled as described in 22.2.4.
22
23
24 Change Table10-10 (Non-HT reference rate) as follows:
25
26
27

1 10.6.12 Channel Width in non-HT and non-HT duplicate PPDUs
2
3
Change the 1st paragraph as follows:
4
5
6 A non-VHT STA shall include neither the CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter nor the
7 DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter in either of the TXVECTOR or RXVECTOR for NON_HT
8
PPDUs. A non-VHT STA shall not set the TA field to a bandwidth signaling TA. A VHT STA shall include
9
neither the CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter nor the DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT
10
11 parameter in the TXVECTOR of a non-HT PPDU containing frames addressed to a non-VHT STA. A VHT
12 STA shall not set the TA field to a bandwidth signaling TA in a frame addressed to a non-VHT STA. The
13 TA field shall not be set to a bandwidth signaling TA in a frame carried in a DSSS/CCK PPDU. A VHT
14
STA that includes the DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter in the TXVECTOR shall also include
15
the CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter in the TXVECTOR. A VHT STA shall not include the
16
17 DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter in the TXVECTOR for transmitted frames other than RTS
18 frames with bandwidth signaling TA and that are sent in a non-HT PPDU. A STA that transmits an RTS
19 frame with a bandwidth signaling TA shall include the DYN_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameter in
20
the TXVECTOR. A VHT STA shall include both the CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT and DYN_BAND-
21
WIDTH_IN_NON_HT parameters in the RXVECTOR if the PPDU format is NON_HT.

10.6.13.3 Additional rate selection constraints for VHT PPDUs
27

29 Replace the 1st paragraph with the following:
30
31
A STA should not transmit a 20MHz or 40MHz VHT PPDU with a <VHT-MCS, NSS> tuple that has
32
VHT-MCS 0, 1, 2 or 3 and NSS less than or equal to 4 to a receiver STA that has marked as unsupported the
33
34 HT-MCS with value VHT-MCS+8×(NSS–1) in the Rx MCS Bitmask subfield in the Supported MCS
35 Set field in the HT Capabilities element it transmits. The transmission of a 20 MHz or 40 MHz VHT PPDU
36 with VHT-MCS greater than 3 is not subject to this constraint.
37
38
A STA should not transmit an 80 MHz, 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz VHT PPDU with a <VHT-MCS, NSS>
39
40 tuple that has VHT-MCS 0 or 1 and NSS less than or equal to 4 to a receiver STA that has marked as unsup-
41 ported the HT-MCS values of both 2×VHT-MCS+8×(NSS–1) and 2×VHT-MCS+1+8×(NSS–1)
42 in the Rx MCS Bitmask subfield in the Supported MCS Set field in the HT Capabilities element it transmits.
43
The transmission of an 80 MHz, 160MHz or 80+80MHz VHT PPDU with VHT-MCS greater than 1 is not
44
subject to this constraint.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 If the HT Control field is present in an MPDU aggregated in an A-MPDU, then all MPDUs of the same
2 frame type (i.e., having the same value for the Type subfield of the Frame Control field) aggregated in the
3
same A-MPDU shall contain the same HT Control field. If MSDU(s) received in an MPDU are delivered via
4
an MA-UNITDATA.indication, the drop eligible parameter of the MA-UNITDATA.indication is set to true
5
6 if the MPDU contains an HT variant HT Control field and the DEI subfield is equal to 1 and set to false oth-
7 erwise.
8
9
An HE STA that transmits a frame containing an A-Control subfield shall include at least one Control sub-
10
field in the A-Control subfield and the included Control subfields shall be those that are supported by the
11
12 receiving STAs unless the Control ID subfield is 15.
13
14
An HE STA that receives a Control subfield in an A-Control subfield with a Control ID subfield value that is
15
not recognized or not supported by the HE STA shall ignore the Control subfield and the remainder of the A-
16
17 Control subfield. If more than one Control subfield is present in an A-Control subfield, the Control subfields
18 shall not have the same Control ID value.
19
20
An HE STA that receives a ONES Control subfield shall ignore the remainder of the A-Control subfield.
21
22
23 If the HT variant HT Control field is present in an MPDU, the DEI subfield provides information on the
24 drop eligibility of the contents of the received MPDU. When there are insufficient resources in a STA, the
25
STA arbitrarily discards frames in order to recover from the lack of resources. With the information from the
26
DEI subfield, a STA may selectively drop frames with the DEI subfield set to 1 in preference to frames with
27
28 the DEI subfield set to 0, if resources are insufficient. Note that this might not help in the recovery in all con-
29 ditions, and the STA might still have to fall back to the arbitrary discard strategy. The mechanisms for deter-
30 mining whether resources are insufficient or when to discard MSDUs or A-MSDUs are beyond the scope of
31
this standard.
32
33
34 If the value of dot11S1GControlFieldOptionImplemented is true, an S1G STA shall set the +HTC-VHT
35 Capable subfield of the S1G Capabilities Information field of the S1G Capabilities element that it transmits
36 to 1.
37
38
39 An S1G shall not use an HT Control field other than a VHT variant HT Control field. An S1G STA shall not
40 use a VHT variant HT Control field for any purpose other than link adaptation (see 10.32.3 (Link adaptation
41 using the VHT variant HT Control field)).

48
49

1 10.12.3 Minimum MPDU Sstart Sspacing field rules

3
If the intended receiver is a non-HE STA, a A STA shall not start the transmission of more than one MPDU
4
within the time limit described in the Minimum MPDU Start Spacing field declared by the intended receiver. If
5
6 the intended receiver is an HE STA, an HE STA shall not start the transmission of more than one QoS Data
7 frame, QoS Null frame or Management frame within the time limit described in the Minimum MPDU Start
8 Spacing field declared by the intended receiver. To satisfy this requirement, the number of octets between the
9
start of two consecutive MPDUs in an A-MPDU, N, measured at the PHY SAP, shall be equal to or greater
10
than meet the condition defined by Equation(10-9).

10.12.6 A-MPDU padding for VHT, HE or S1G PPDU

Change the 2nd to last paragraph as follows:
7
8
9 An A-MPDU subframe with EOF set to 0 shall not be added after any A-MPDU subframe with EOF set to 1,
10 except if the A-MPDU subframe is in an ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU or ack-enabled multi-TID A-
11

14 Change the title of 10.12.7 as follows:
15
16
17 10.12.7 Setting the EOF/Tag field of the MPDU delimiter
18
19
Change as follows:
20
21
22 The EOF/Tag field may be set to 1 in an A-MPDU subframe carried in a VHT PPDU, HE PPDU or S1G
23
PPDU if the subframe’s MPDU Length field is nonzero and the subframe is the only subframe that has a
24
nonzero MPDU Length field. The EOF/Tag field of each A-MPDU subframe with an MPDU Length field
25
26 with a nonzero value that is not the only A-MPDU subframe with MPDU Length field with a nonzero value
27 in the A-MPDU carried in a VHT PPDU or S1G PPDU shall be set to 0. The EOF/Tag field shall be set to 0
28 in all A-MPDU subframes that are carried in an HT PPDU.
29
30
31 An MPDU that is the only MPDU in an A-MPDU and that is carried in an A-MPDU subframe with 1 in the
32 EOF/Tag field is called an S-MPDU.
33
34
35 An MPDU that is carried in an A-MPDU subframe with the EOF/Tag field in the MPDU delimiter set to 1 is
36 called a tagged MPDU. An S-MPDU is a tagged MPDU, but a tagged MPDU is not necessarily an S-
37 MPDU.
38
39
40 An MPDU that is carried in an A-MPDU subframe with the EOF/Tag field in the MPDU delimiter set to 0 is
41 called an untagged MPDU.
42
43

Table27-54 (HE PHY characteristics).

1 10.22 Operation across regulatory domains

3
10.22.3 Operation with operating classes
4
5
6 Change as follows:
7
8
The following, and only the following, are extended spectrum management capable: a VHT STA, an HE STA
9
10 and a STA that has dot11ExtendedSpectrumManagementImplemented true. A non-VHT STA that has
11 dot11ExtendedSpectrumManagementImplemented true shall indicate that it is extended spectrum management
12 capable using the Extended Spectrum Management Capable field of the Extended Capabilities element.
13
14
When communicating with a STA that supports global operating classes, all requests and Action and Action
15
16 No Ack frames that convey elements containing operating classes shall use global operating class values.
17
18
When dot11OperatingClassesImplemented is true, the following statements apply:
19
20 — When dot11OperatingClassesRequired is false, or where operating classes domain information is
21 not present in a STA, that STA is not required to change its operation in response to an element that
22
contains an operating class.
23
24 — When dot11OperatingClassesRequired is true, or where operating classes domain information is
25 present in a STA, the STA shall indicate current operating class information in the Country element
26
and Supported Operating Classes element, except for the following cases:
27
28 • that a A VHT STA may omit, from the Country element, any Operating Triplet field for an Oper-
29 ating Class for which the Channel spacing (MHz) column indicates 80 MHz or wider and for
30
which the Behavior limits set column in the applicable table in AnnexE contains only a blank
31
entry or either or both of “80+” and “UseEirpForVHTTxPowEnv.”
32
33 • An HE STA may omit from the Country element any Operating Triplet field for an operating
34 class for which the Channel starting frequency (GHz) column in Table E-4 is greater than or
35 equal to 5.925 and less than or equal to 7.125.
36
37 — When dot11OperatingClassesRequired and dot11ExtendedChannelSwitchActivated are true and a
38 STA is capable of operating as specified in more than one operating class, the STA shall include the
39 Supported Operating Classes element in Association frames and Reassociation frames.
40
41 — When dot11OperatingClassesRequired is true, or where operating classes domain information is
42 present and the STA parsing a Country element finds an invalid First Channel Number field or
43 Operating Class field with a value that is reserved, the STA shall ignore the remainder of the
44
Country element and shall parse any remaining management frame body for additional elements.
45
46 — When dot11OperatingClassesRequired is true and the STA supports one or more global operating
47 classes, or where global operating classes domain information is present in a STA, the STA shall
48
indicate current operating class information in the Country element and Supported Operating
49
Classes element using the country string for the global operating classes, except for the following
50
51 cases:
52
• that a A VHT STA may omit from the Country element any Operating Triplet field for an Oper-
53
ating Class for which the Channel spacing (MHz) column indicates 80 MHz or wider and for
54
55 which the Behavior limits set column in the applicable table in Annex E contains only a blank
56 entry or either or both of “80+” and “UseEirpForVHTTxPowEnv.”
57
• An HE STA may omit from the Country element any Operating Triplet field for an Operating
58
Class for which the Channel starting frequency (GHz) column in Table E-4 is greater than or
59
60 equal to 5.925 and less than or equal to 7.125.

64
65 Change as follows:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 283
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 284 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 A STA that is not operating in the 6 GHz band and is extended spectrum management capable and that has
2 dot11SpectrumManagementRequired or dot11RadioMeasurementActivated equal to true shall determine a
3
local maximum transmit power from a Transmit Power Envelope element for which the Local Maximum
4
Transmit Power Unit Interpretation subfield indicates EIRP.
5
6
7 A STA that is operating in the 6 GHz band shall determine local and regulatory client maximum transmit
8
powers from Transmit Power Envelope element(s) according to local regulations known at the STA (see
9
AnnexE.2.7). A STA shall ignore Transmit Power Envelope element(s) indicating transmit power category
10
11 values that the STA is unable to interpret for the current country.
12
NOTE—The Default category value (0) is applicable to, and so can be interpreted for, all countries (see 11.7.5 (Specifi-
13
cation of regulatory and local maximum transmit power levels)). An AP in the 6 GHz band has dot11SpectrumManage-
14
mentRequired equal to true, and so transmits a Country element in Beacon and Probe Response frames.
15
16
17 A STA that sends two or more Transmit Power Envelope elements in a frame shall order the elements by
18 increasing values of their Local Maximum Transmit Power Unit Interpretation subfields.
19
20
21 A STA that is operating in the 6 GHz band that sends two or more Transmit Power Envelope elements in a
22 frame with the same value in the Maximum Transmit Power Interpretation subfield shall order the elements by
23 increasing values of their Maximum Transmit Power Category subfields.
24
25 NOTE—The Maximum Transmit Power Category subfield is reserved except in the 6 GHz band.
26
27
If a STA that is extended spectrum management capable finds an unknown value in the Local Maximum
28
Transmit Power Unit Interpretation subfield in a Transmit Power Envelope element, then the STA shall ignore
29
30 that and subsequent Transmit Power Envelope elements.
31
32 A STA that receives two or more Transmit Power Envelope elements in the same frame with known values in
33
their Local Maximum Transmit Power Unit Interpretation subfields shall process all of the elements according
34
to the local regulations known at the STA.
35
36
37 NOTE—If a STA receives two Transmit Power Envelope elements, each with a known value in the Local Maximum
38 Transmit Power Unit Interpretation subfield, then the expected possibilities are as follows:
39 — The STA complies with either element (shared spectrum),
40
— The STA complies with both elements (tightened regulations), or
41
— The STA complies with the second element (changed regulations).
42
43
44 If a STA receives a Transmit Power Envelope element with the Local Maximum Transmit Power Interpreta-
45 tion subfield equal to 1 or 3 (EIRP PSD) and the Maximum Transmit Power Count subfield indicating a
46
value of N greater than 1, 2, 4, or 8 from an AP that the STA identifies to have BSS bandwidth of 20, 40, 80,
47
or 160/80+80 MHz, respectively, then the STA shall use the Maximum Transmit PSD 1-M subfields (M
48
49 equal to 1, 2, 4 or 8 for BSS bandwidth of 20, 40, 80 or 160/80+80 MHz, respectively) to determine the max-
50 imum transmit PSD for each 20 MHz channel within the BSS bandwidth. The STA shall ignore the Maxi-
51 mum Transmit PSD X subfields with X > M.
52
53 NOTE—This might occur when the AP supports PHY mode(s) unknown to the STA, and the actual BSS bandwidth is
54 wider than the BSS bandwidth recognized by the STA.
55

1 10.23.2 HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)
2
3
10.23.2.2 EDCA backoff procedure
4
5
6 Change the 2nd paragraph as follows:
7
8 For the purposes of this subclause, transmission failure of an MPDU is defined as follows:
9
10 — After transmitting an MPDU (even if it is carried in an A-MPDU or as part of a VHT or S1G MU
11 PPDU or as part of an HE MU PPDU that is sent using TXVECTOR parameter NUM_USERS > 1)
12 that requires an immediate response:
13
14 • The STA shall wait for a timeout interval of duration aSIFSTime + aSlotTime + aRxPHYStart-
15 Delay, starting when the MAC receives a PHY-TXEND.confirm primitive. If a PHY-
16 RXSTART.indication primitive does not occur during the timeout interval, the transmission of
17
the MPDU has failed.
18
• If a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive does occur during the timeout interval, the STA shall
19
20 wait for the corresponding PHY-RXEND.indication primitive to recognize a valid response
21 MPDU (see Annex G) that either does not have a TA field or is sent by the recipient of the
22 MPDU requiring a response. If anything else, including any other valid frame, is recognized, the
23
transmission of the MPDU has failed.
24
25 — The nonfinal (re)transmission of an MPDU that is delivered using the GCR unsolicited retry retrans-
26 mission policy (10.23.2.12.2 (Unsolicited retry procedure)) is defined to be a failure.
27
— In all other cases, the transmission of the MPDU has not failed.
28
29
30 Change the 4th and 5th paragraph as follows:
31
32
The backoff procedure shall be invoked by an EDCAF when if any of the following events occurs:
33
34 a) An MA-UNITDATA.request primitive is received that causes a frame with that AC to be queued for
35 transmission such that one of the transmit queues associated with that AC has now become non-
36
empty and any other transmit queues associated with that AC are empty; the medium is busy on the
37
primary channel as indicated by any of the following:
38
39 — physical CS;
40
— virtual CS;
41
42 — a nonzero TXNAV timer value;
43
— a mesh STA that has dot11MCCAActivated true and a nonzero RAV timer value, and the
44
45 backoff counter has a value of 0 for that AC.
46
b) The transmission of the MPDU in the final PPDU transmitted by the TXOP holder during the TXOP
47
for that AC has completed and the TXNAV timer has expired, the PPDU does not solicit an HE TB
48
49 PPDU, and the AC was a primary AC. (See 10.23.2.7).
50 c) The transmission of an MPDU in the initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as defined in this subclause, the
51
PPDU does not solicit an HE TB PPDU, and the AC was a primary AC.
52
53 d) The transmission attempt collides internally with another EDCAF of an AC that has higher priority,
54 that is, two or more EDCAFs in the same STA are granted a TXOP at the same time.
55
56 e) The transmission of at least one MPDU in the final PPDU transmitted by the TXOP holder during
57 the TXOP for that AC has completed, the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU
58 and the TXNAV timer has expired.
59
f) The transmission of all MPDUs in the initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as defined in this subclause,
60
61 and the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU.
62
g) If explicitly indicated, such as in 26.17.2.3.3 (Non-AP STA scanning behavior)
63
64
65 In addition, the backoff procedure may be invoked by an EDCAF when if:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 285
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 286 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 h) The transmission by the TXOP holder of an MPDU in a non-initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as
2 defined in this subclause, and an MPDU in the non-initial PPDU does not solicit an HE TB PPDU.
3
4
i) The transmission by the TXOP holder of all MPDUs in a non-initial PPDU of a TXOP fails, as
5
6 defined in this subclause, and the PPDU contains an MPDU that solicits an HE TB PPDU.
7
8
Change the last two paragraphs as follows:
9
10
11 If the backoff procedure is invoked for reason a) above, the value of CW[AC] shall be left unchanged. If the
12 backoff procedure is invoked for reason b) and f) above, the value of CW[AC] shall be reset to CWmin[AC].
13
14
If the backoff procedure is invoked for reason c), d), e), g), h) or f) i) above, the value of CW[AC] shall be
15
updated as follows before invoking the backoff procedure:
16
17 — If the QSRC[AC] is less than dot11ShortRetryLimit,
18
19 • QSRC[AC] shall be incremented by 1
20 • CW[AC] shall be set to the lesser of CWmax[AC] and 2QSRC[AC] × (CWmin[AC] + 1) – 1
21
— Else
22
23 • QSRC[AC] shall be set to 0
24
• CW[AC] shall be set to CWmin[AC]
25
26 — When dot11RobustAVStreamingImplemented is true and either the QSDRC[AC] or the
27 QLDRC[AC] has reached dot11ShortDEIRetryLimit or dot11LongDEIRetryLimit, respectively,
28 CW[AC] shall be reset to CWmin[AC].
29
30 NOTE—An HE STA updates its local MIB variables related to CWmin and CWmax as defined in 26.2.7 (EDCA opera-
31 tion using MU EDCA parameters).
32
33
Insert the following at the end of the subclause:
34
35
36 After transmission of an MPDU in an HE TB PPDU, an HE STA resumes the EDCA backoff procedure
37 without modifying CW or the backoff counter for the associated EDCAF regardless of whether the STA has
38 received the corresponding acknowledgment frame in response to the MPDU sent in the HE TB PPDU.

10.23.2.4 Obtaining an EDCA TXOP
41
42
43 Change the last paragraph as follows:
44
45
A STA shall save the TXOP holder address for the BSS in which it is associated, which. The TXOP holder
46
47 address is the MAC address from the Address 2 field of the frame that initiated a frame exchange sequence
48 except when if this is a CTS frame, in which case the TXOP holder address is the Address 1 field. If the
49 TXOP holder address is obtained from a Control frame, a VHT STA or HE STA shall save the nonband-
50
width signaling TA value obtained from the Address 2 field. If a non-VHT non-HE STA receives an RTS
51
frame with the RA matching the MAC address of the STA and the MAC address in the TA field in the RTS
52
53 frame matches the saved TXOP holder address, then the STA shall send the CTS frame after SIFS, without
54 regard for, and without resetting, its NAV. If a VHT STA or HE STA receives an RTS frame with the RA
55 matching the MAC address of the STA and the nonbandwidth signaling TA value obtained from the Address
56
2 field in the RTS frame matches the saved TXOP holder address, then the STA shall send the CTS frame
57
after SIFS, without regard for, and without resetting, its NAV. If a CMMG STA receives an RTS frame with
58
59 the RA matching the MAC address of the STA and the TA value obtained from the Address 2 field in the
60 RTS frame matches the saved TXOP holder address, then the STA shall send the CTS frame after SIFS,
61 without regarding for, and without resetting its NAV. When a STA receives a frame addressed to it that
62
requires an immediate response, except for RTS and Trigger frames (see 26.5.2.5 (UL MU CS mechanism)),
63
it shall transmit the response independent of its NAV. The saved TXOP holder address shall be cleared
64
65 when the NAV is reset or when the NAV counts down to 0.

10.23.2.7 Sharing an EDCA TXOP
27
28
29 Change as follows:
30
31
The AC associated with the EDCAF that gains an EDCA TXOP is referred to as the primary AC. Frames
32
from ACs other than the primary AC shall not be included in the TXOP, with the following exceptions
33
34 (TXOP sharing):
35 — For a multi-TID A-MPDU in an HE MU PPDU transmitted by an AP, frames from any AC may be
36
included as defined in 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU). Other-
37
wise, frames Frames from a higher priority AC may be included when at least one frame from the
38
39 primary AC has been transmitted and all frames from the primary AC have been transmitted.
40 NOTE 1—The frames from a higher priority AC might be included in successive PPDUs in the TXOP and/or in
41
one or more MU PPDUs.
42
43 — When an VHT AP supports DL-MU-MIMO, frames from a lower priority AC may be included in an
44 VHT MU PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter NUM_USERS > 1 when these frames do not
45 increase the duration of the VHT MU PPDU beyond that required for the transmissions of the frames
46
of the primary AC and any frames from a high priority AC. For a given user, any frames from the
47
primary AC shall be transmitted first and then any frames from a higher priority AC immediately
48
49 next.
50 — When an HE AP transmits an HE MU PPDU, frames from higher or lower priority AC may be
51
included in the HE MU PPDU when these frames do not increase the duration of the HE MU PPDU
52
beyond that required for the transmissions of the frames of the primary AC and any frames from a
53
54 high priority AC. For a given user, any frames from the primary AC shall be transmitted first and
55 then any frames from a higher priority AC immediately next.
56
57
The EDCAF remains bound by the TXOP limit for its AC (i.e., the primary AC), irrespective of the AC(s) of
58
59 the frames transmitted during the TXOP.
60
61 When sharing, the TXOP limit that applies is the TXOP limit of the primary AC.
62
63
With respect to admission control (see 10.23.4.2 (Contention based admission control procedures)), all
64
65 frames transmitted under TXOP sharing shall be treated as if they were from the primary AC.

31 10.23.2.9 TXOP limits
32
33
Change as follows:
34
35
36 The duration of a TXOP is the time a STA obtaining a TXOP (the TXOP holder) maintains uninterrupted con-
37 trol of the medium, and it includes the time required to transmit frames sent as an immediate response to TXOP
38
holder transmissions. The TXOP holder shall, subject to the exceptions below, ensure that the duration of a
39
TXOP does not exceed the TXOP limit, when nonzero.
40
41
42 The TXOP limits are advertised by the AP in the EDCA Parameter Set element in Beacon and Probe Response
43 frames transmitted by the AP.
44
45
A TXOP limit of 0 indicates that the TXOP holder may transmit or cause to be transmitted (as responses) the
46
47 following within the current TXOP:
48
a) One of the following at any rate, subject to the rules in 10.6 (Multirate support)
49
50 1) One or more SU PPDUs carrying fragments of a single MSDU or MMPDU
51
2) An SU PPDU or a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU or an HE TB PPDU carrying a single
52
53 MSDU, a single MMPDU, a single A-MSDU, or a single A-MPDU
54 3) A VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU carrying A-MPDUs to different users (a single A-
55
MPDU to each user)
56
57 4) A QoS Null frame or PS-Poll frame that is not an PS-Poll+BDT frame
58
5) A Basic Trigger frame, BSRP Trigger frame or BQRP Trigger frame
59
60 6) An HE TB PPDU carrying A-MPDUs from different users (a single A-MPDU from each user)
61
b) Any required acknowledgments
62
63 c) Any frames required for protection, including one of the following:
64
65 1) An RTS/CTS or MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frame exchange
290 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 291 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 2) CTS to it
2
3) Dual CTS as specified in 10.3.2.10 (Dual CTS protection)
3
4 d) Any frames required for beamforming as specified in 10.32 (Sounding PPDUs), 10.37.5 (VHT
5
sounding protocol), 26.7 (HE sounding protocol) and 10.43 (DMG beamforming).
6
7 e) Any frames required for link adaptation as specified in 10.33 (Link adaptation) and 27.13 (Link
8 adaptation using the HE variant HT Control field).
9
10 f) Any number of BlockAckReq, MU-BAR Trigger or Multi-TID BlockAckReq or GCR MU-BAR
11 Trigger frames
12
13 NOTE 1—This is a rule for the TXOP holder. A TXOP responder need not be aware of the TXOP limit nor of when the
14 TXOP was started.
15
NOTE 2—This rule prevents the use of RD, BDT, and TXOP sharing when the TXOP limit is 0.
16
17
18 When dot11OCBActivated is true, TXOP limits shall be 0 for each AC.
19
20
The TXOP holder may exceed the TXOP limit only if it does not transmit more than one Data or Manage-
21
ment frame in the TXOP, only if it does not transmit a DL-MU-MIMO PPDU in the TXOP, and only for the
22
23 following situations:
24
— Retransmission of an MPDU, not in an A-MPDU consisting of more than one MPDU, where the size
25
of the retransmitted MPDU is the same as the initially transmitted MPDU
26
27 — Transmission of an MSDU or MMPDU less than 600 octets by an S1G non-sensor STA
28
29 — Transmission of a fragment of an MSDU or MMPDU, the fragment being less than 256 octets, by an
30 S1G non-sensor STA
31
— Initial transmission of an MSDU under a block ack agreement, where the MSDU is not in an A-
32
MPDU consisting of more than one MPDU and the MSDU is not in an A-MSDU
33
34 — Transmission of a Control frame or a QoS Null frame, not in an A-MPDU consisting of more than
35
one MPDU
36
37 — Initial transmission of a non-dynamic fragment of an MSDU or MMPDU (see 10.4 (MSDU, A-
38 MSDU, and MMPDU fragmentation)), if a previous fragment of that MSDU or MMPDU was
39
retransmitted
40
41 — Transmission of a non-dynamic fragment of an MSDU or MMPDU fragmented into 16 fragments
42
— Transmission of the 16th dynamic fragment of an MSDU or MMPDU
43
44 — Initial transmission of the first dynamic fragment of an MSDU or MMPDU, where the size of the
45
first fragment is equal to the minimum fragment size specified by the receiver STA and the MSDU
46
or MMPDU is not in an A-MPDU consisting of more than one MPDU
47
48 — Transmission of an A-MPDU consisting of the initial transmission of a single MPDU not containing
49
an MSDU and that is not an individually addressed Management frame
50
51 — Transmission of a group addressed MPDU, not in an A-MPDU consisting of more than one MPDU
52
— Transmission of a null data PPDU (NDP)
53
54 — Transmission of a VHT NDP Announcement frame and NDP or transmission of a Beamforming
55
Report Poll frame, where these fit within the TXOP limit and it is only the response and the immedi-
56
ately preceding SIFS that cause the TXOP limit to be exceeded.
57
58 — Transmission of one of the following sequences, provided that the sequence fits within the TXOP
59
limit and it is only the response and the immediately preceding SIFS that causes the TXOP limit to
60
be exceeded:
61
62 • An HE NDP Announcement frame and HE sounding NDP
63
• An HE NDP Announcement frame and HE sounding NDP and BFRP Trigger frame
64
65 • A BFRP Trigger frame

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 Except as described above, a STA shall fragment an individually addressed MSDU or MMPDU so that the
2 initial transmission of the first fragment does not cause the TXOP limit to be exceeded.
3
4 NOTE—The TXOP limit is not exceeded for the following situations:
5
6 — Initial transmission of an MPDU containing an unfragmented though fragmentable (see 10.2.7 and 26.3 (Frag-
7 mentation and defragmentation)) MSDU/MMPDU
8
9 — Initial transmission of the first fragment of a fragmented MSDU/MMPDU, except for an if the MSDU/MMPDU
10 is fragmented into 16 fragments
11
— Initial transmission of an A-MSDU
12

14 that MSDU/ MMPDU was retransmitted, except for an if the MSDU/MMPDU is fragmented into 16 fragments

16 — Initial transmission of a dynamic fragment of a fragmented MSDU/MMPDU, except for either the first dynamic
17 fragment of a fragmented MSDU/MMPDU using the minimum fragment size specified by the receiver STA, or
18 the 16th dynamic fragment of a fragmented MSDU/MMPDU

20
21 Management frame, unless this is a retransmission of that MPDU

23

24

26 response does not fit within the TXOP limit
27
28
If the TXOP holder exceeds the TXOP limit, it should use as high a PHY rate as possible to minimize the
29
30 duration of the TXOP.
31
32
The duration of a TXOP for a mesh STA that has dot11MCCAActivated true shall not exceed the time
33
34 between the start of the TXOP and the end of the current MCCAOP reservation.
35
36 NOTE—The rules in this subclause also apply to priority-downgraded MSDUs and A-MSDUs (see 10.23.4.2 (Conten-
37 tion based admission control procedures)).

39
If the Duration field in a frame carried in an HE TB PPDU is set to 0, the HE TB PPDU shall not include any
40
frames that solicit a control response frame from the AP.

43 10.23.2.10 Truncation of TXOP
44
45
46 Change the 7th paragraph as follows:
47
48
In a non-DMG BSS, a non-S1G non-HE STA shall interpret the reception of a CF-End frame as a NAV
49
50 reset, i.e., it resets its NAV to 0 at the end of the PPDU containing this frame. After receiving a CF-End
51 frame with a matching BSSID(TA) without comparing Individual/Group bit, an AP may respond by trans-
52 mitting a CF-End frame after SIFS. An HE STA interprets the reception of a CF-End frame as defined in
53
26.2.5 (Truncation of TXOP).
54
55
56 Change the 10th paragraph as follows:
57
58
59 Non-DMG non-S1G non-HE STAs that are not S1G STAs that receive a CF-End frame reset their NAV and
60 can start contending for the medium without further delay. An HE STA that receive a CF-End frame may
61 start contending for the medium without further delay as defined in 26.2.5 (Truncation of TXOP). A DMG
62
STA that receives a CF-End frame can start contending for the medium at the end of the time interval equal
63
to the value in Duration/ID field of the frame if none of its NAVs has a nonzero value (10.40.10 (Updating
64
65 multiple NAVs)).

36 The Multi-TID BlockAck variant shall be used for all BlockAck frames related to an HT-immediate agree-
37 ment transmitted inside a PSMP sequence and shall not be used otherwise. For non-HE STAs, the Multi-TID
38 BlockAckReq variant shall be used for all BlockAckReq frames related to an HT-immediate agreement
39
transmitted inside a PSMP sequence and shall not be used otherwise. The Multi-TID BlockAckReq variant
40
can be used between HE STAs to solicit a Multi-STA BlockAck frame for Multi-TID A-MPDUs.
41
42
43 In a DMG BSS, if the Compressed BlockAckReq variant is used related to an HT-immediate agreement,
44
then all of the following BlockAck and BlockAckReq frames transmitted as part of the HT-immediate
45
agreement shall use the Compressed BlockAck and Compressed BlockAckReq variants.

49
agreement, then all of the following BlockAck and BlockAckReq frames transmitted as part of the HT-
50
51 immediate agreement shall use the Extended Compressed BlockAck and Extended Compressed BlockAck-
52 Req variants.
53
54
55 Where the terms BlockAck and BlockAckReq are used within 10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack extensions)
56 and 10.25.7 (HT-delayed block ack extensions), the appropriate variant according to this subclause (e.g.,
57 Compressed, Multi-TID) is referenced by the generic term. The term BlockAck as used within 10.25.6 (HT-
58
immediate block ack extensions) includes the additional NDP_1M BlockAck, NDP_2M BlockAck, BAT

and BlockAck frame variants.

37 the DA field of the A-MSDU subframe set to the GCR group address before sending a BlockAckReq frame

39 AckReq frame shall be set to the MAC address of the destination STA. Upon reception of the BlockAck
40

41

42
43 ment with one or more of the HE STAs for this group address, the originator may send a GCR MU-BAR

50

35
36 10.27.6 Protection rules for HE STAs
37
38
An HE STA operating in the 2.4 GHz band is subject to all of the rules for HT STAs that apply to that band,
39
40 except that a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to HE_SU, HE_ER_SU, HE_MU or
41 HE_TB may be substituted for a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to HT_MF.
42
43
An HE STA operating in the 5 GHz band is subject to all of the rules for VHT STAs that apply to that band,
44
except that a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to HE_SU, HE_ER_SU, HE_MU or
45
46 HE_TB may be substituted for a PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT set to VHT.
47
48 Additionally, an HE STA can use the MU-RTS/CTS frame exchange procedure.
