## 第 337 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 Insert new Clauses 26 and 27 following Clause 25 as follows:
2
3
4
26. High Efficiency (HE) MAC specification
5
6
7
8 26.1 Introduction
9
10
An HE STA supports the MAC and MLME functions defined in Clause26 (High Efficiency (HE) MAC
11
12 specification) in addition to the MAC functions defined in Clause10 (MAC sublayer functional descrip-
13 tion), the MLME functions defined in Clause11 (MLME), and the security functions defined in Clause 12
14 except when the functions in Clause26 (High Efficiency (HE) MAC specification) supersede the functions
15
in Clause10 (MAC sublayer functional description) or Clause11 (MLME). A frame successfully transmit-
16
ted by a non-AP STA in response to a Basic Trigger frame is a successful frame exchange initiated by the
17
18 STA as referred to in Clause11 (MLME) and Clause 12.
19
20
21 26.2 HE channel access
22
23
26.2.1 TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS
24
25
26 In an HE BSS, the use of RTS/CTS can be TXOP duration-based or PSDU length-based. An HE AP may
27 configure a non-AP HE STA to use the TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS exchanges to help mitigate interfer-
28
ence in dense environments.
29
30
31 An HE AP may set the TXOP Duration RTS Threshold subfield in the HE Operation Parameters field in the
32 HE Operation element it transmits to a value between 1 and 1022 to enable TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS
33
exchanges of its associated STAs. The AP may set the TXOP Duration RTS Threshold field to 1023 to dis-
34
able TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS exchanges of its associated STAs. The AP may in Beacon and Probe
35
36 Response frames set the TXOP Duration RTS Threshold field to 0 to make no changes to TXOP duration-
37 based RTS/CTS exchanges of its associated STAs.
38
39
If the TXOP Duration RTS Threshold subfield in the HE Operation Parameters field in the most recently
40
received HE Operation element sent by the AP with which a non-AP HE STA is associated is equal to a non-
41
42 zero value, then the non-AP HE STA shall set dot11TXOPDurationRTSThreshold to the value of the TXOP
43 Duration RTS Threshold subfield. Otherwise, the non-AP HE STA shall not update dot11TXOPDura-
44 tionRTSThreshold.
45
46
47 The TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS exchange is disabled at a non-AP HE STA if dot11TXOPDurationRT-
48 SThreshold is 1023. The TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS exchange is enabled at a non-AP HE STA when
49 dot11TXOPDurationRTSThreshold is less than 1023.
50
51
A non-AP HE STA shall use an RTS/CTS exchange to initiate a TXOP if TXOP duration-based RTS/CTS
52
53 exchange is enabled at a non-AP HE STA and the following conditions are met:
54
— The STA intends to transmit individually addressed frames to the HE AP or to a TDLS peer STA
55
56 — The TXOP duration is greater than or equal to 32µs×dot11TXOPDurationRTSThreshold
57
58
Otherwise, the non-AP HE STA follows the rules defined in 10.3.1 (General).
59
60
61 26.2.2 Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification
62
63
A STA shall classify a received PPDU as an inter-BSS PPDU if at least one of the following conditions is
64
65 true:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 337
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 338 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is not 0 and is not the BSS color of the BSS of which the
2 STA is a member.
3
4 — The PPDU is a VHT PPDU with RXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID not equal to the
5 BSSID[39:47] of the BSS in which the STA is associated or any of the other BSSs in the same mul-
6
tiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS belongs and the RXVECTOR parameter
7
GROUP_ID is 0.
8
9
— The PPDU is a VHT PPDU with RXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID[5:8] not equal to the 4
10
LSBs of the BSS color announced by the BSS of which the STA whose dot11PartialBSSColorImple-
11
12 mented is equal to true is a member and RXVECTOR parameter GROUP_ID equal to 63 when the
13 Partial BSS Color field in the most recent HE Operation element is 1.
14
15 — The PPDU is either a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU with the RXVECTOR parameter
16 UPLINK_FLAG equal to 0 and the STA is an AP.
17
18 — The PPDU carries a frame that has a BSSID field, the value of which is not the BSSID of the BSS in
19 which the STA is associated or any of the other BSSs in the same multiple BSSID set or co-hosted
20 BSSID set to which its BSS belongs or the wildcard BSSID.
21
22 — The PPDU carries a frame that does not have a BSSID field but has both an RA field and TA field,
23 neither value of which is equal to the BSSID of the BSS in which the STA is associated or any of the
24
other BSSs in the same multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS belongs. The
25
Individual/Group bit in the TA field value is forced to 0 prior to comparison.
26
27
28 A STA shall classify the received PPDU as an intra-BSS PPDU if at least one of the following conditions is
29
true:
30
31 — The RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR of the PPDU carrying the frame is the BSS color of the
32
BSS of which the STA is a member or the BSS color of any TDLS links to which the STA belongs if
33
the STA is an HE STA associated with a non-HE AP.
34
35
— The PPDU is a VHT PPDU with RXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID equal to the
36
BSSID[39:47] of the BSS in which the STA is associated or any of the other BSSs in the same mul-
37
38 tiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS belongs and the RXVECTOR parameter
39 GROUP_ID equal to 0.
40
41 — The PPDU is a VHT PPDU with RXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID[5:8] equal to the 4 LSBs
42 of the BSS color announced by of the BSS of which the STA whose dot11PartialBSSColorImple-
43 mented is equal to true is a member, the RXVECTOR parameter GROUP_ID is equal to 63 and the
44
Partial BSS Color field in the most recent HE Operation element is 1.
45
46 — The PPDU carries a frame that has an RA, TA or BSSID field value that is equal to the BSSID of the
47
BSS or the BSSID of any BSS in which the STA is associated or any of the other BSSs in the same
48
multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS belongs. The Individual/Group bit in
49
50 the TA field value is forced to the value 0 prior to the comparison.
51
— The PPDU carries a Control frame that does not have a TA field and that has an RA field value that
52
53 matches the saved TXOP holder address of the BSS or any BSS in which the STA is associated or
54 any of the other BSSs in the same multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS
55 belongs.
56
57
NOTE—See 10.19 (Group ID and partial AID in VHT and CMMG PPDUs) for the definition of PARTIAL_AID[5:8]
58
and BSSID[39:47].
59
60
61 Otherwise, the PPDU cannot be determined as an intra-BSS or inter-BSS PPDU.
62
63
If, based on the MAC address information of a frame carried in a received PPDU, the received PPDU satis-
64
65 fies both intra-BSS and inter-BSS conditions, then the received PPDU is classified as an intra-BSS PPDU.
338 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 339 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 If the received PPDU satisfies the intra-BSS conditions using the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR and
2 also satisfies the inter-BSS conditions using MAC address information of a frame carried in the PPDU, then
3
the classification made using the MAC address information takes precedence.
4
5
6 If a STA determines that the BSS color is disabled (see 26.17.3.3 (Disabling BSS color)), then the RXVEC-
7 TOR parameter BSS_COLOR of a PPDU shall not be used to classify the PPDU.
8
9
26.2.3 SRG PPDU identification
10
11
12 Identification of SRG and non-SRG PPDUs is used during SRG OBSS PD spatial reuse operation as
13 described in 26.10 (Spatial reuse operation).
14
15
A non-AP HE STA that has received a Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP with a
16
value of 1 in the SRG Information Present subfield shall use information provided in the Spatial Reuse
17
18 Parameter Set element to identify BSSs that are members of the STA’s SRG to determine if a received inter-
19 BSS PPDU is an SRG PPDU. An HE AP may use an SRG that is different from the SRG that it transmits to
20 other STAs in Spatial Reuse Parameter Set elements to determine whether or not a received inter-BSS
21
PPDU is an SRG PPDU.
22
23
24 A received HE PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU is an SRG PPDU if the bit in the SRG BSS Color Bitmap
25 field indexed by the value of the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is 1 (see 9.4.2.252 (Spatial Reuse
26 Parameter Set element)). A received VHT PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU is an SRG PPDU if the
27
GROUP_ID parameter of the RXVECTOR has a value of 0 and the bit in the SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap
28
field that corresponds to the numerical value of PARTIAL_AID[0:5] of the RXVECTOR is 1 (see 9.4.2.252
29
30 (Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element)).
31
32 A received PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU is an SRG PPDU if BSSID information from a frame carried in
33
the PPDU is correctly received and the bit in the SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap field that corresponds to the
34
numerical value of BSSID[39:44] is 1.
35
36
37 A VHT PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU and that is received with RXVECTOR parameter GROUP_ID
38
equal to 0 is an SRG PPDU if the bit in the SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap field that corresponds to the numeri-
39
cal value of bits [39:44] of the RA field of any correctly received frame from the PPDU is 1.
40
41
42 A VHT PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU and that is received with RXVECTOR parameter GROUP_ID
43 equal to 63 is an SRG PPDU if the bit in the SRG Partial BSSID Bitmap field that corresponds to the numer-
44
ical value of bits [39:44] of the TA field of any correctly received frame from the PPDU is 1.
45
46
47 An HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU that is an inter-BSS PPDU and that is received with
48 the RXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG equal to 1 is an SRG PPDU if the bit in the SRG Partial
49 BSSID Bitmap field that corresponds to the numerical value of bits [39:44] of the RA field of any correctly
50
received frame from the PPDU is 1.
51
52
53 Otherwise, the PPDU is not determined to be an SRG PPDU. A non-AP HE STA that has not received a
54 Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP with a value of 1 in the SRG Information Pres-
55 ent subfield shall not classify any received PPDUs as an SRG PPDU. An HE AP that has not transmitted a
56
Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element with a value of 1 in the SRG Information Present subfield may classify
57
received PPDUs as SRG PPDUs using information that it has not transmitted.
58
59
60 26.2.4 Updating two NAVs
61
62
A non-AP HE STA shall maintain two NAVs and an HE AP may maintain two NAVs: an intra-BSS NAV
63
and a basic NAV. The intra-BSS NAV is updated by an intra-BSS PPDU. The basic NAV is updated by an
64
65 inter-BSS PPDU or a PPDU that cannot be classified as intra-BSS or inter-BSS. The mechanism by which a
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 339
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 340 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 PPDU is classified intra-BSS or inter-BSS is defined in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classifica-
2 tion).
3
4
Maintaining two NAVs is beneficial in dense deployment scenarios in which a STA requires protection from
5
6 frames transmitted by STAs within its BSS, i.e., intra-BSS, and avoid interference from frames transmitted
7 by STAs in a neighboring BSS, i.e., inter-BSS. For example, in a TXOP initiated by the AP with which a
8 STA is associated for an HE TB PPDU transmission, the intra-BSS NAV of the STA is set by the AP to pre-
9
vent the STA from contending for the channel. The basic NAV of the STA is not updated by transmissions
10
from the AP during the TXOP so that if the basic NAV of the STA is nonzero and the STA receives, from
11
12 the AP, a Trigger frame with the CS Required subfield equal to 1, the STA will not respond (see 26.5.2.5
13 (UL MU CS mechanism)).
14
15
The requirements in 10.3.2.1 (CS mechanism) apply to an HE STA maintaining two NAVs with the excep-
16
17 tion of the virtual CS indication of medium. For an HE STA maintaining two NAVs, if both the NAV timers
18 are 0, the virtual CS indication is that the medium is idle; if at least one of the two NAV timers is nonzero,
19 the virtual CS indication is that the medium is busy.
20
21
The procedure in 10.3.2.9 (CTS and DMG CTS procedure) applies to an HE STA maintaining two NAVs,
22
23 and the NAV referred by the description in 10.3.2.4 (Setting and resetting the NAV) is the basic NAV.
24
25 The duration information is indicated by a frame as follows:
26
27 — If there is a Duration field in the frame, then the duration information is indicated by the Duration
28 field.
29
— If the frame is a PS-Poll, then the duration information is equal to the time, in microseconds, required
30
31 to transmit one Ack frame plus one SIFS under the data rate selection rules. If the calculated dura-
32 tion information includes a fractional microsecond, that duration information is rounded up to the
33 next higher integer.
34
35
A STA shall update the intra-BSS NAV with the duration information indicated by the received frame in a
36
37 PSDU if and only if all the following conditions are met:
38
— The frame is identified as intra-BSS according to the rule described in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-
39
BSS PPDU classification).
40
41 — The indicated duration is greater than the current intra-BSS NAV value.
42
43 — The RA of the received frame is not the STA’s MAC address, or the STA is not a TXOP holder and
44 the PPDU carrying the frame does not contain a frame that solicits an immediate response from the
45 STA, or the STA is not a TXOP holder and the received frame is a Trigger frame.
46
47
A STA shall update the basic NAV with the duration information indicated by the received frame in a PSDU
48
49 if and only if all the following conditions are met:
50
— The frame is identified as inter-BSS or cannot be identified as intra-BSS or inter-BSS according to
51
the rule described in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification).
52
53 — The indicated duration is greater than the current basic NAV value.
54
— The RA of the received frame is not the STA’s MAC address.
55
56
57 A STA that is a TXOP holder shall not update the intra-BSS NAV with the duration information indicated
58
by the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION.
59
60
61 A STA that is not a TXOP holder shall update the intra-BSS NAV with the duration information indicated
62 by the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION for an HE PPDU if and only if all the following condi-
63 tions are met:
64
65 — The RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is not UNSPECIFIED.
340 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 341 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The PPDU that carried information of the RXVECTOR parameter is identified as intra-BSS accord-
2 ing to the rule described in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification).
3
4 — The STA does not receive a frame with a Duration field in the PPDU.
5 — The duration information indicated by the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is greater
6
than the current intra-BSS NAV of the STA.
7
8
9 A STA shall update the basic NAV with the duration information indicated by the RXVECTOR parameter
10 TXOP_DURATION for an HE PPDU if and only if all the following conditions are met:
11
— The RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is not UNSPECIFIED.
12
13 — The PPDU that carried information for the RXVECTOR parameter is identified as inter-BSS or can-
14
not be identified as intra-BSS or inter-BSS according to the rule described in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and
15
inter-BSS PPDU classification).
16
17 — The STA does not receive a frame with a Duration field in the PPDU.
18
— The duration information indicated by the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is greater
19
20 than the current basic NAV of the STA.
21
NOTE 1—If a PS-Poll frame is received in an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, or HE MU PPDU, then the RXVEC-
22
TOR parameter TXOP_DURATION does not indicate duration information (see 26.11.5 (TXOP_DURATION)).
23
24 NOTE 2—If a STA receives an HE PPDU with the duration information indicated by both a frame with a Duration field
25 and the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION, then the duration information indicated by the RXVECTOR
26 parameter TXOP_DURATION is ignored.
27
28 NOTE 3—The additional rules of NAV consideration for a STA that is solicited for an immediate response are described
29 in 10.3.2.9 (CTS and DMG CTS procedure), 10.3.2.11 (Acknowledgment procedure), and 26.5.2.5 (UL MU CS mecha-
30 nism).
31
32 A PHY-CCARESET.request primitive shall be issued if one of the following condition is met:
33
— One NAV is reset, and the other NAV timer is 0.
34
35 — Both NAVs are reset simultaneously.
36
37
The NAVs (if any) are updated at the expected end of the PPDU as defined in 10.3.2.4 (Setting and resetting
38
39 the NAV).
40
41 An HE STA that used information from an RTS or MU-RTS Trigger frame as the most recent basis to
42
update its NAV may reset the NAV that is updated by the RTS or MU-RTS Trigger frame if no PHY-
43
RXSTART.indication primitive is received from the PHY during a period with a duration of
44
45 2×aSIFSTime+CTS_Time+aRxPHYStartDelay+2×aSlotTime starting when the MAC receives a
46 PHY-RXEND.indication primitive corresponding to the detection of the RTS or MU-RTS Trigger frame
47 (see 10.3.2.4 (Setting and resetting the NAV) for the definition of CTS_Time).
48
49
26.2.5 Truncation of TXOP
50
51
52 A non-AP HE STA that is not associated with an HE AP shall interpret the reception of a CF-End frame as a
53
NAV reset, i.e., it reset its maintained NAV to 0 at the end of the PPDU containing this frame.
54
55
56 An HE AP that maintains one NAV (see 10.3.2.1 (CS mechanism)) and receives a CF-End frame should
57 reset the NAV unless either of following conditions are met:
58
— The received CF-End frame is carried in an inter-BSS PPDU and the most recently updated NAV
59
was due to a frame carried in an intra-BSS PPDU (see 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classi-
60
61 fication)).
62
— The received CF-End frame is carried in an intra-BSS PPDU and the most recently updated NAV
63
was due to a frame carried in an inter-BSS PPDU (see 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classi-
64
65 fication)).
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 341
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 342 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE STA that maintains two NAVs (see 26.2.4 (Updating two NAVs)) and receives a CF-End frame
2 should reset the basic NAV if the received CF-End frame carried in an inter-BSS PPDU and reset the intra-
3
BSS NAV if the received CF-End frame is carried in an intra-BSS PPDU.
4
5
6 An HE STA that maintains two NAVs may reset both NAVs if the received CF-End frame is carried in an
7 intra-BSS PPDU and the basic NAV was updated due to a frame carried in a PPDU that cannot be identified
8 as either an inter-BSS PPDU or intra-BSS PPDU.
9
10
An HE STA that receives a CF-End frame and resets all its maintained NAV(s) can start contending for the
11
12 medium without further delay.
13
14 26.2.6 MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frame exchange procedure
15
16
26.2.6.1 General
17
18

30 26.2.6.2 MU-RTS Trigger frame transmission
31
32
33 In each 20 MHz channel occupied by the PPDU that contains an MU-RTS Trigger frame, the transmitter of
34
the MU-RTS Trigger frame shall request at least one non-AP STA to send a CTS frame response that occu-
35
36 pies the 20 MHz channel. The transmitter of an MU-RTS Trigger frame shall not request a non-AP STA to
37 send a CTS frame response in a 20 MHz channel that is not occupied by the PPDU that contains the MU-
38 RTS Trigger frame.
39
40
41 After transmitting an MU-RTS Trigger frame, the AP shall wait for a CTSTimeout interval of
42
aSIFSTime+aSlotTime+aRxPHYStartDelay that begins when the MAC receives the PHY-TXEND.con-
43
44 firm primitive for the transmitted MU-RTS Trigger frame. If the MAC does not receive a PHY-
45 RXSTART.indication primitive during the CTSTimeout interval, the AP shall conclude that the transmis-
46 sion of the MU-RTS Trigger frame has failed, and, if the MU-RTS Trigger frame initiated a TXOP, the AP
47
shall invoke its backoff procedure. If the MAC receives a PHY-RXSTART.indication primitive during the
48
49 CTSTimeout interval, then the MAC shall wait for the corresponding PHY-RXEND.indication primitive to
50 determine whether the MU-RTS Trigger frame transmission was successful. The receipt of a CTS frame
51 from any non-AP STA addressed by the MU-RTS Trigger frame before the PHY-RXEND.indication primi-
52
tive shall be interpreted as the successful transmission of the MU-RTS Trigger frame, permitting the frame
53
54 exchange sequence to continue. The receipt of any other type of frame shall be interpreted as a failure of the
55 MU-RTS Trigger frame transmission. In this instance, the AP may process the received frame and, if the
56 MU-RTS Trigger frame initiated a TXOP, shall invoke its backoff procedure at the PHY-RXEND.indication
57
primitive.
58
59
60 NOTE—If an AP transmits an MU-RTS Trigger frame in an already initiated TXOP and the MU-RTS Trigger frame
61 transmission fails, then the AP might perform a PIFS recovery as described in 10.23.2.8 (Multiple frame transmission in
62 an EDCA TXOP) or invoke the backoff procedure described in 10.23.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure).
63
64
65 An MU-RTS Trigger frame shall not be carried in a VHT MU PPDU or an HE MU PPDU.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 343
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 344 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.2.6.3 CTS frame response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame
2
3
4 If a non-AP STA receives an MU-RTS Trigger frame, the non-AP STA shall commence the transmission of
5
a CTS frame response at the SIFS time boundary after the end of a received PPDU when all the following
6
conditions are met:
7
8
9 — The MU-RTS Trigger frame has one of the User Info fields addressed to the non-AP STA. The User
10 Info field is addressed to a non-AP STA if the AID12 subfield is equal to the 12 LSBs of the AID of
11
the STA and the MU-RTS Trigger frame is sent by the AP with which the non-AP STA is associated
12
13 or by the AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID if the non-AP STA is associated with an AP
14 corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID and has indicated support for receiving Control frames
15
with TA field set to the transmitted BSSID by setting the Rx Control Frame To MultiBSS subfield to
16
17 1 in the HE Capabilities element that the non-AP STA transmits.
18
19 — The UL MU CS condition indicates that the medium is idle (see 26.5.2.5 (UL MU CS mechanism)).
20
21
22 Otherwise, the non-AP STA shall not send a CTS frame response.
23
24
NOTE 1—The RU Allocation subfield in the User Info field addressed to the non-AP STA indicates whether the CTS
25
frame response is to be sent on the primary 20 MHz channel, primary 40 MHz channel, primary 80 MHz channel,
26
27 160MHz channel, or 80+80 MHz channel as described in 9.3.1.22.5 (MU-RTS Trigger frame format).
28
29 NOTE 2—A combination of virtual CS and ED-based CCA during the SIFS after the PPDU containing the MU-RTS
30 Trigger frame is used to determine the state of the medium (see 26.5.2.5 (UL MU CS mechanism)).
31
32
33 The CTS frame sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame shall be carried in a non-HT or non-HT dupli-
34 cate PPDU (see Clause17) with a 6 Mb/s rate and with the TXVECTOR parameter SCRAMBLER_INI-
35
TIAL_VALUE set to the same value as the RXVECTOR parameter SCRAMBLER_INITIAL_VALUE of
36
37 the PPDU carrying the MU-RTS Trigger frame. The PPDU carrying the CTS frame shall be transmitted on
38 the 20MHz channels indicated in the RU Allocation subfield of the User Info field of the MU-RTS Trigger
39
frame.
40
41
42 NOTE—A bandwidth signaling TA is not used in an MU-RTS Trigger frame or a CTS frame response to an MU-RTS
43 Trigger frame (see 9.3.1.22 (Trigger frame format) and 9.3.1.3 (CTS frame format)). As a result, the TXVECTOR
44
parameter CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT is not present when transmitting an MU-RTS Trigger frame or CTS frame
45
response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame. In Figure 17-7 (Data scrambler) the first 7 bits of scrambling sequence of an
46
MU-RTS Trigger frame or CTS frame response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame are not defined by Table 17-7 (Contents of
47
48 the first 7 bits of the scrambling sequence).
49
50
The Power Management and More Data subfields in a CTS frame sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger
51
52 frame shall be set to 0.
53
54
NOTE—Other subfields of the Frame Control field of the CTS frames sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame are
55
set as described in Figure 9-26 ((Frame Control field subfield values within Control frames carried in a non-S1G
56
PPDU)).
57
58
59

26.2.7 EDCA operation using MU EDCA parameters
41
42
43 An HE AP shall set the QoS Info field of an MU EDCA Parameter Set element (if present) to the same value
44
as the QoS Info field of an EDCA Parameter Set element (if present). An HE AP may change the MU EDCA
45
parameters by including the MU EDCA Parameter Set element with updated MU EDCA parameters in the
46
47 Beacon frames and Probe Response frames it transmits. The EDCA Parameter Set Update Count subfield in
48 the QoS Info field of the EDCA Parameter Set element and MU EDCA Parameter Set element is incre-
49 mented every time any of the EDCA parameters or the MU EDCA parameters change.
50
51
A non-AP HE STA shall update the dot11EDCATable and dot11MUEDCATable that correspond to fields
52
53 in an EDCA Parameter Set element or an MU EDCA Parameter Set element within an interval of time equal
54 to one beacon interval after receiving an updated EDCA or MU EDCA parameter set from its associated AP.
55 When updating its MIB attributes, an HE STA stores the value of the EDCA Parameter Set Update Count
56
subfield in the QoS Info field of the received EDCA Parameter Set element or MU EDCA Parameter Set ele-
57
ment.
58
59
60 A non-AP HE STA shall use the EDCA Parameter Set Update Count subfield in the QoS Capability element
61 of Beacon frames, where present, to determine whether the STA is using the current EDCA (and optionally
62
MU EDCA) parameter values. If the EDCA Parameter Set Update Count subfield value is different from the
63
value that has been stored, the STA shall query the updated EDCA (and any MU EDCA) parameter values
64
65 by sending a Probe Request frame to the AP.

1 NOTE—If the QoS Capability element is present in a Beacon frame, the EDCA Parameter Set element and the MU
2 EDCA Parameter Set element are not present. In this case, the only way for an HE STA to obtain the updated parameters
3 is to send a Probe Request frame to the AP.
4
5 A non-AP HE STA that receives a Basic Trigger frame that contains a User Info field addressed to the STA
6
shall update its CWmin[AC], CWmax[AC], AIFSN[AC] and MUEDCATimer[AC] state variables to the
7
values contained in the dot11MUEDCATable, for all the ACs from which at least one QoS Data frame was
8
9 transmitted successfully in an HE TB PPDU in response to the Trigger frame. A QoS Data frame is transmit-
10 ted successfully by the STA in an HE TB PPDU for an AC if it requires immediate acknowledgment and the
11 STA receives an immediate acknowledgment for that frame, or if the QoS Data frame does not require
12
immediate acknowledgment.
13
14
15 The MUEDCATimer[AC] state variable is updated with the value contained in the MU EDCA Timer sub-
16 field of the MU EDCA Parameter Set element. The backoff counter maintenance corresponding to the
17 updated state variables shall follow the rules in 10.23.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure) except that if
18
AIFSN[AC] is 0 then the EDCAF corresponding to that AC shall be suspended until the MUEDCA-
19
Timer[AC] reaches 0 or is reset to 0. The updated MUEDCATimer[AC] shall start at the end of the immedi-
20
21 ate response if the transmitted HE TB PPDU contains at least one QoS Data frame for that AC that requires
22 immediate acknowledgment, and shall start at the end of the HE TB PPDU if the transmitted HE TB PPDU
23 does not contain any QoS Data frames for that AC that require immediate acknowledgment.
24
25
26 In a non-AP HE STA, each MUEDCATimer[AC] shall uniformly count down without suspension to 0 when
27 its value is nonzero.
28
29 NOTE 1—A non-AP STA that sends a frame to the AP with an OM Control subfield containing a value of 1 in the UL
30 MU Disable subfield or a value of 0 in the UL MU Disable subfield and a value of 1 in the UL MU Data Disable subfield
31 does not participate in UL MU operation. As such it is exempt from updating its EDCA access parameters to the values
32 contained in the MU EDCA Parameter Set element as defined in this subclause.
33
NOTE 2—A non-AP STA does not update its state variables to the values contained in the MU EDCA Parameter Set
34
element if any of the following apply:
35
36 — The Trigger frame addressed to the STA is not a Basic Trigger frame
37 — The STA does not include QoS Data frames in the HE TB PPDU response sent in response to the Basic Trigger
38 frame
39
— The STA transmits the HE TB PPDU in response to a Basic Trigger frame following the rules defined in 26.5.4
40
(UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA))
41
42 NOTE 3—The TXOP limits are not updated by the procedure defined in this subclause, but by that in 10.23.2.9 (TXOP
43 limits).
44
45
A non-AP STA that sends frames that are not addressed to its associated AP may use the EDCA parameters
46
47 values that are contained in the most recently received EDCA Parameter Set element sent by the AP with
48 which the STA is associated, or to the default EDCA parameter values (see Table 9-137 (Default EDCA
49 Parameter Set element parameter values if dot11OCBActivated is false)), following the rules in 10.2.3.2
50
(HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)).
51
52
53 If the MUEDCATimer[AC] of a non-AP HE STA reaches 0, either by counting down or due to a reset fol-
54 lowing the reception of an MU EDCA Reset frame, the STA shall update CWmin[AC], CWmax[AC] and
55 AIFSN[AC] to the values that are contained in the most recently received EDCA Parameter Set element sent
56
by the AP with which the STA is associated.
57
58
59 A non-AP HE STA that sends a frame with an OM Control subfield with the UL MU Disable subfield set to
60 1 or with the UL MU Disable subfield set to 0 and the UL MU Data Disable subfield set to 1 as defined in
61 26.9.3 (Transmit operating mode (TOM) indication) may set the MUEDCATimer[AC] for all ACs to 0 on
62
receiving an immediate acknowledgment from the OMI responder. The STA continues the current EDCA
63
backoff procedure without modifying the QSRC[AC], QLRC[AC] or the backoff counter for the associated
64
65 EDCAF, regardless of whether the MUEDCATimer[AC] has reached zero, until the STA invokes a new
346 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 347 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 EDCA backoff procedure. The STA follows the rules defined in 10.23.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure) for
2 updating CWmin, CWmax and AIFSN for that AC.
3
4
5 A non-AP HE STA that receives an individually addressed MU EDCA Reset frame from its associated AP
6 may reset the MUEDCATimer[AC] to 0 for an AC if the bit corresponding to that AC in the Affected ACs
7 subfield is equal to 1 when the MUEDCATimer[AC] of the STA is not equal to 0. The STA may invoke a
8
new EDCA backoff procedure after the MUEDCATimer[AC] is reset for that AC and after CWmin[AC],
9
CWmax[AC] and AIFSN[AC] are updated for that AC, as per this subclause, in response to the MUEDCA-
10
11 Timer[AC] reset.

26.2.8 Multiple frame transmission in an EDCA TXOP in the 6 GHz band
14
15
16 A STA that operates in the 6 GHz band and transmits multiple frames shall follow the rules defined in
17 10.23.2.7 (Sharing an EDCA TXOP) with the exceptions listed below.
18
19 NOTE—In the 6 GHz band, the TXOP field in the HE-SIG-A field can be understood by all STAs. The TXOP field pro-
20 vides TXOP protection equivalent to non-HT duplicate RTS/CTS.
21
22
In a TXOP without non-HT duplicate PPDUs, if the TXOP is protected by the TXOP field in the HE-SIG-A
23
field of the first HE PPDU in the TXOP, i.e., the TXOP field is not set to UNSPECIFIED, the TXOP holder
24
25 shall set the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH of the subsequent PPDUs in the TXOP to indicate
26 a bandwidth that is the same or narrower than the bandwidth indicated by the CH_BANDWIDTH parameter
27 of the first HE PPDU in the TXOP.
28
29 NOTE—Frame exchanges in a TXOP that is protected by RTS/CTS follow the rules in 10.23.2.8 (Multiple frame trans-
30 mission in an EDCA TXOP).
31
32
Additionally, if the first HE PPDU whose TXOP field in HE-SIG-A is not set to UNSPECIFIED is a DL HE
33
34 MU PPDU with preamble puncture, then the TXOP holder shall use the 20 MHz channels for the non-initial
35 PPDU that are within the set of 20 MHz channels where pre-HE modulated fields of the first HE PPDU
36 whose TXOP field in HE-SIG-A is not set to UNSPECIFIED are located.
37
38
Within an obtained TXOP that does not include HE PPDUs whose TXOP field in HE-SIG-A is not
39
40 UNSPECIFIED nor non-HT duplicate PPDUs, the TXOP holder shall set the TXVECTOR parameter
41 CH_BANDWIDTH of a non-initial PPDU to be the equal to or less than the TXVECTOR parameter
42 CH_BANDWIDTH of the preceding PPDU that was transmitted in the same TXOP, subject to the following
43
constraints:
44
45 — If the preceding PPDU is a DL HE MU PPDU with preamble puncture, the TXOP holder shall set
46
the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH of the non-initial PPDU to a value whose corre-
47
sponding 20 MHz channels are within a set of 20 MHz channels where pre-HE modulated fields of
48
49 the preceding PPDU are located.
50
— If the non-initial PPDU is a DL HE MU PPDU with preamble puncture, the TXOP holder shall set
51
the TXVECTOR parameter RU_ALLOCATION of the non-initial PPDU to a value whose corre-
52
53 sponding RU is within a set of 20 MHz channels where pre-HE modulated fields of the preceding
54 PPDU are located.
55
56
57 26.3 Fragmentation and defragmentation
58
59
60 26.3.1 General
61
62
An HE STA shall follow the fragmentation procedures defined in 10.2.6 (Fragmentation/defragmentation
63
overview), 10.4 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and MMPDU fragmentation), and 10.5 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and
64
65 MMPDU defragmentation) except that some of the rules are relaxed as defined in the subclauses below.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 347
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 348 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 Dynamic fragmentation provides further flexibility in aggregating data to fit in a constrained PPDU duration
2 (see 26.5 (MU operation)).
3
4
5 Subclauses 10.2.6 (Fragmentation/defragmentation overview) and 10.4 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and MMPDU
6 fragmentation) define the procedure to generate uniformly fragmented MSDUs or MMPDUs, where the
7 length of each fragment is the same, except for the last fragment. These fragments are not included in an A-
8
MPDU under HT-immediate block ack agreements.
9
10
11 An HE STA can negotiate the use of different levels of dynamic fragmentation:
12
— Level 1: support for one dynamic fragment that is a non-A-MPDU, no support for dynamic frag-
13
ments in an A-MPDU that does not contain an S-MPDU.
14
15
— Level 2: support for dynamic fragments in an A-MPDU that does not contain an S-MPDU, subject to
16
the following conditions:
17
18 • No more than one dynamic fragment of any given MSDU or A-MSDU in the A-MPDU; this
19
dynamic fragment is sent under a block ack agreement.
20
21 • No more than one MPDU that is a dynamic fragment of an MMPDU in the A-MPDU (see 26.6.2
22 (A-MPDU padding in an HE PPDU) and 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-
23 TID A-MPDU)).
24
25 — Level 3: support for dynamic fragments in an A-MPDU that does not contain an S-MPDU, subject to
26 the following conditions:
27
• No more than four dynamic fragments of any given MSDU or A-MSDU in the A-MPDU; these
28
29 dynamic fragments are sent under a block ack agreement.
30 • No more than one MPDU that is a dynamic fragment of an MMPDU in the A-MPDU (see 26.6.2
31
(A-MPDU padding in an HE PPDU) and 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-
32
TID A-MPDU)).
33
34
NOTE 1—An HE STA that is operating in level 2 or level 3 also supports transmitting and receiving level 1 dynamic
35
fragments.
36
37 NOTE 2—Under level 2, there might be multiple Data frames that are dynamic fragments, but these have different
38 sequence numbers and/or UPs.
39
40
This subclause defines the procedure for generating nonuniformly fragmented MSDUs, A-MSDUs or
41
42 MMPDUs, where the length of each fragment is not necessarily the same. The length of the first fragment
43 shall be greater than or equal to the minimum fragment size indicated in the Minimum Fragment Size sub-
44 field in the HE Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element sent by the recipient STA. If
45
the length of the MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU is less than the minimum fragment size, then the MSDU, A-
46
MSDU or MMPDU shall not be fragmented. The fragments generated with dynamic fragmentation are
47
48 referred to as dynamic fragments.
49
50 These procedures allow the inclusion of one dynamic fragment that is a non-A-MPDU in level 1, level 2 and
51
level 3, and allow the inclusion of one or more dynamic fragments within an A-MPDU that contains one or
52
more MPDUs in level 2 and level 3.
53
54
55 An HE STA follows the rules defined in 26.3.2 (Dynamic fragmentation) for generating these fragments and
56
the rules defined in 26.3.3 (Dynamic defragmentation) for defragmenting of the received dynamic frag-
57
ments. In the subclauses 26.3.2 (Dynamic fragmentation) and 26.3.3 (Dynamic defragmentation), the HE
58
59 STA follows the fragmentation level that is indicated in the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the
60 HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits unless an ADDBA
61 Extension element is included in the ADDBA Request and ADDBA Response frames exchanged during the
62
block ack setup procedure, in which case the HE STA follows the fragmentation level that is indicated in an
63
ADDBA Extension element in the ADDBA Response frame it receives (see the subclause 26.3.3.1 (Gen-
64
65 eral)) during the block ack setup procedure.
348 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 349 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE STA shall set the HE Fragmentation Operation subfield in the ADDBA Extension element, if pres-
2 ent, in the ADDBA Request or ADDBA Response frame to a value that is less than or equal to the value of
3
the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element it transmits.
4
5
6 An HE STA shall set the HE Fragmentation Operation subfield in the ADDBA Extension element, if pres-
7 ent, in the ADDBA Response frame to a value that is less than or equal to the value of HE Fragmentation
8
Operation subfield in the ADDBA Extension element, if present, in the received ADDBA Request frame.
9
10
11 26.3.2 Dynamic fragmentation
12
13
26.3.2.1 General
14
15
16 With level 1 and level 2 dynamic fragmentation, the following apply:
17
— An originator STA transmitting one or more dynamic fragments shall solicit an immediate response
18
19 from the recipient STA for each of the fragments.
20
— The originator STA shall transmit the fragments in order as defined in 10.4 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and
21
22 MMPDU fragmentation).
23
24 With level 3 dynamic fragmentation, not all dynamic fragments require an immediate response and dynamic
25
fragments are not required to be sent in order.
26
27
28 An HE STA may transmit dynamic fragments of an A-MSDU provided the A-MSDU Fragmentation Sup-
29 port subfield in the HE Capabilities element transmitted by the recipient is 1.
30
31
32 The originator STA shall not transmit concurrently the dynamic fragments of a number of outstanding
33 MSDUs and A-MSDUs (if supported) to the same recipient STA that is greater than N , where N for
max max
34 MSDUs and A-MSDUs (if supported) is calculated based on the Maximum Number of Fragmented
35
MSDUs/A-MSDUs subfield in the HE Capabilities element transmitted by the recipient STA. The term out-
36
standing refers to an MPDU containing all or part of an MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU for which transmis-
37
38 sion has been started, and for which delivery of the MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU has not yet been
39 completed (i.e., an acknowledgment of the final fragment has not been received and the MSDU, A-MSDU
40 or MMPDU has not been discarded due to retries, lifetime, or for some other reason).
41
42 NOTE—Dynamic fragments of outstanding MMPDUs are not counted in the check against the N limit.
max
43
44
An originator STA may retransmit the full MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU if all the previously transmitted
45
46 dynamic fragments of that MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU have explicitly failed at the receiving STA. An
47 originator STA may retransmit a failed fragment if one or more of the previously transmitted fragments of
48 that MSDU, A-MSDU, or MMPDU have explicitly failed at the receiving STA (see below for a definition of
49
this term). The frame body length and contents of the retransmitted fragment shall be the same as the ini-
50
tially transmitted fragment and shall remain fixed for the lifetime of the MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU at
51
52 that STA except when the frame is not encrypted, all the fragments preceding the initial transmitted frag-
53 ment were received and all the fragments following the initial transmitted fragment have either explicitly
54 failed or have not been transmitted, in which case the frame body length and contents of the retransmitted
55
fragment may be different from the initially transmitted fragment.
56
57
58 A fragment has explicitly failed at the receiving STA if the originator STA receives an immediate response
59 that contains:
60
61 — A valid first MPDU that is not an Ack frame, Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck
62 frame, of which all the preceding pre-EOF MPDU delimiters are received.
63
— A Multi-STA BlockAck frame that does not contain a BA Information field with TID equal to that of
64
65 the fragment
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 349
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 350 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — A Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame that contains a BA Information field
2 with TID equal to that of the fragment, but does not acknowledge receipt of the fragment
3
4
5 An originator STA shall not transmit to a recipient STA an MPDU that is not carried in an A-MPDU or an
6 A-MPDU that carries dynamic fragments that do not satisfy the conditions in 26.3.2.2 (Level 1 dynamic
7 fragmentation), 26.3.2.3 (Level 2 dynamic fragmentation) and 26.3.2.4 (Level 3 dynamic fragmentation).
8
9
10 26.3.2.2 Level 1 dynamic fragmentation
11
12
An originator STA may transmit one dynamic fragment of an MSDU, A-MSDU (if supported by the recipi-
13
ent) or MMPDU that is a non-A-MPDU and that is not sent under a block ack agreement to a recipient STA
14
15 using level 1 dynamic fragmentation if the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE MAC Capa-
16 bilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element received from the recipient STA is 1, 2 or 3.
17
18
An originator STA may transmit a fragmented MSDU or A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient) under a
19
20 block ack agreement to a recipient STA using level 1 dynamic fragmentation provided the following condi-
21 tion is met: the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element received from the
22 recipient STA is 1, 2 or 3 and for the block ack agreement associated with the TID of the MSDU or A-
23
MSDU, the HE Fragmentation Operation subfield is 1 if the ADDBA Extension element is present in the
24
ADDBA Response frame received from the recipient STA.
25
26
27 26.3.2.3 Level 2 dynamic fragmentation
28
29
30 An originator STA may transmit fragmented MSDUs or A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient) under a
31 block ack agreement to a recipient STA using level 2 dynamic fragmentation provided one of the following
32 conditions is met:
33
34 — The Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element received from the
35 recipient STA is 2, and for the block ack agreement associated with the TID of the MSDU or A-
36 MSDU, the HE Fragmentation Operation subfield is 2 if the ADDBA Extension element is present
37
in the ADDBA Response frame received from the recipient STA.
38
39 — The Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element received from the
40 recipient STA is 3, and for the block ack agreement associated with the TID of the MSDU or A-
41
MSDU, the ADDBA Extension element is present and the HE Fragmentation Operation subfield is 2
42
in the ADDBA Response frame received from the recipient STA.
43
44
45 An originator STA may transmit to a recipient STA the following:
46
47 — One dynamic fragment of an MSDU, A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient), or MMPDU that is a
48 non-A-MPDU using level 1 dynamic fragmentation.
49
50 — Up to one dynamic fragment of an MSDU or A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient) for each
51 MSDU or A-MSDU and up to one dynamic fragment of an MMPDU in an A-MPDU where the A-
52 MPDU contains at least one dynamic fragment using level 2 dynamic fragmentation; the dynamic
53
fragment of an MSDU or A-MSDU shall be sent under a block ack agreement.
54
55
NOTE—The originator STA follows the rules in 10.12.8 (Transport of S-MPDUs) for generating the S-MPDU, the rules
56
in 10.25.6.7 (Originator’s behavior) for generating the A-MPDU and the rules in 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-
57
enabled single-TID A-MPDU) for generating a multi-TID A-MPDU that can contain the fragment of the MMPDU.
58
59
60 26.3.2.4 Level 3 dynamic fragmentation
61
62
An originator STA may transmit fragmented MSDUs or A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient) under a
63
block ack agreement or fragmented MMPDU to a recipient STA using level 3 dynamic fragmentation pro-
64
65 vided the following conditions are met:
350 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 351 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element received from the
2 recipient STA is 3.
3
4 — For the block ack agreement associated with the TID of the MSDU or A-MSDU, the HE Fragmenta-
5 tion Operation subfield is 3 if the ADDBA Extension element is present in the ADDBA Response
6 frame received from the recipient STA.
7
8
Level 3 fragmentation allows multiple fragments of an MSDU or A-MSDU included in the same A-MPDU,
9
10 reducing the fragments’ transmission delay.
11
12
An originator STA may transmit to a recipient STA the following:
13
14 — One dynamic fragment of an MSDU, A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient), or MMPDU that is a
15 non-A-MPDU using level 1 dynamic fragmentation.
16
• The originator STA shall follow the rules defined in 10.12.8 (Transport of S-MPDUs) for gener-
17
18 ating the S-MPDU.
19
— Up to four dynamic fragments of an MSDU or A-MSDU (if supported by the recipient) for each
20
MSDU or A-MSDU, and up to one dynamic fragment of an MMPDU in an A-MPDU, where the A-
21
22 MPDU contains at least one dynamic fragment using level 3 dynamic fragmentation; the dynamic
23 fragment of an MSDU or A-MSDU shall be sent under block ack agreement.
24
• The originator STA shall set the Fragment Number subfield of each MPDU to a value less than 4.
25
26 • The originator STA shall follow the rules defined in 10.25.6.7 (Originator’s behavior) for gener-
27 ating the A-MPDU with the exception that the A-MPDU shall contain MPDUs whose range of
28
the Sequence Number subfields does not exceed B /4, where B is the length of the Block Ack
29 L L
Bitmap field of the Compressed BlockAck or Multi-STA BlockAck frame that corresponds to a
30
31 TID of a transmitted fragment (see 10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack extensions) and 26.4 (HE
32 acknowledgment procedure)).
33 • The originator STA shall ensure that WinSize does not exceed B /4, where B is the length of
O L L
34
the Block Ack Bitmap field of the Compressed BlockAck or Multi-STA BlockAck frame that
35
corresponds to a TID of a transmitted fragment (see 10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack exten-
36
37 sions) and 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure)).
38
39 26.3.3 Dynamic defragmentation
40
41
42 26.3.3.1 General
43
44 An HE STA shall set the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information
45
field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 0 if it does not support dynamic fragments. Otherwise, the
46
HE STA shall set the Dynamic Fragmentation Support subfield as follows:
47
48 — Set to 1, 2 or 3 if the STA supports reception of dynamic fragments following the procedure defined
49
in 26.3.2.2 (Level 1 dynamic fragmentation)
50
51 — Set to 2 or 3 if the STA supports reception of dynamic fragments following the procedure defined in
52 26.3.2.3 (Level 2 dynamic fragmentation)
53
54 — Set to 3 if the STA supports reception of dynamic fragments following the procedure defined in
55 26.3.2.4 (Level 3 dynamic fragmentation)
56
57
An HE STA shall set dot11HEDynamicFragmentationLevel to the value of Dynamic Fragmentation Support
58
subfield in the HE Capabilities element it transmits if it supports reception of dynamic fragments.
59
60
61 If a fragment is sent under level 1, level 2 or level 3 fragmentation, it belongs to dynamic fragment.
62
63
Defragmentation of dynamic fragments shall follow the rules defined in 10.5 (MSDU, A-MSDU, and
64
65 MMPDU defragmentation) with the following exceptions:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 351
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 352 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The recipient STA shall support the concurrent reception of dynamic fragments of a number of out-
2 standing MSDUs and A-MSDUs when supported from the same transmitting STA that is equal to
3
N , where N for MSDUs and A-MSDUs (if supported) is obtained from the Maximum Number
4 max max
Of Fragmented MSDUs/A-MSDUs Exponent subfield in the HE Capabilities element transmitted
5
6 by the STA. The term outstanding refers to an MPDU containing all or part of an MSDU or A-
7 MSDU for which transmission has been started, and for which delivery of the MSDU or A-MSDU
8 has not yet been completed (i.e., an acknowledgment of the final fragment has not been received and
9
the MSDU, A-MSDU or MMPDU has not been discarded due to retries, lifetime, or for some other
10
reason). The recipient STA shall support the concurrent reception of dynamic fragments of one out-
11
12 standing MMPDU from a transmitting STA.
13
— The recipient STA may be subject to the receive timer rules for each of the MSDUs, A-MSDUs and
14
15 MMPDUs defined in 10.6 (Defragmentation).
16
17
A STA that has dot11AMSDUFragmentationOptionImplemented true shall set the A-MSDU Fragmentation
18
Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element to 1. Otherwise, the STA shall set the A-MSDU Fragmenta-
19
20 tion Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element to 0.
21
22
26.3.3.2 Level 1 dynamic defragmentation
23
24
25 Upon reception of a non-A-MPDU or A-MPDU that carries a dynamic fragment, the recipient STA
26
responds with an Ack frame or a Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragment is contained in an
27
MPDU that solicits an immediate response. The receiver STA shall follow the rules defined in 10.3.2.9 (Ack
28
29 procedure) for generating the Ack frame for the soliciting dynamic fragment that is an MPDU, or carried in
30 an S-MPDU or ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU and the rules defined in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment proce-
31 dure) for generating the Multi-STA BlockAck frame that contains the acknowledgment for the soliciting S-
32
MPDU that carries one dynamic fragment and carried in an HE TB PPDU, ack-enabled single-TID A-
33
MPDU or ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU.
34
35
36 26.3.3.3 Level 2 dynamic defragmentation
37
38
39 Upon reception of a non-A-MPDU that is a dynamic fragment or A-MPDU that carries one or more dynamic
40 fragments, the recipient STA responds with one of the following frames:
41
42 — An Ack frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragment is a non-A-MPDU or con-
43 tained in an A-MPDU and solicits the immediate response. The recipient STA shall follow the rules
44 defined in 10.3.2.9 (Ack procedure) for generating the Ack frame for the soliciting dynamic frag-
45
ment that is an MPDU or carried in an S-MPDU or ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU and the rules
46
defined in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure) for generating the Multi-STA BlockAck frame that
47
48 contains the acknowledgment for the soliciting S-MPDU carried in an HE TB PPDU, ack-enabled
49 single-TID A-MPDU or ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU.
50
51 — A Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragments of an
52 MSDU or A-MSDU (if any), up to one fragment for each MSDU or A-MSDU, are contained in an
53 A-MPDU that contains an MPDU that solicits an immediate response. The recipient STA shall fol-
54
low the rules defined in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmission of BlockAck frames by an HT STA,
55
DMG STA, or S1G STA) for generating the BlockAck frame and the rules in 26.4 (HE acknowledg-
56
57 ment procedure) for generating the Multi-STA BlockAck frame, except that the STA shall:
58
• Set to 0 the LSB of the Fragment Number subfield in the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control
59
60 subfield of the Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame that corresponds to
61 a TID of a received fragment that is sent under a block ack agreement.
62
• Set to 1 each bit of the Block Ack Bitmap field that corresponds to a Sequence Number subfield
63
and TID subfield of a received fragment that is sent under a block ack agreement contained in the
64
65 soliciting A-MPDU that is not an S-MPDU.

1 — The STA shall update the corresponding block acknowledgment record for an MSDU or A-MSDU
2 for which fragments are received only if that MSDU or A-MSDU is successfully reconstructed (see
3
10.6 (Defragmentation)). Otherwise the STA shall not update the block ack record for that MSDU or
4
A-MSDU.

NOTE—The recipient STA sets the bits of the Block Ack Bitmap field that correspond to a Sequence Number and TID
7
subfield of an MPDU that is not a fragment as defined in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmission of BlockAck frames by

11 A recipient STA shall discard any fragments of incomplete MSDUs or A-MSDUs that have been received
12 during an HT-immediate block ack session for a TID if it receives a BlockAckReq frame from the originator

14
Starting Sequence Number field of the BlockAckReq frame or that have a sequence number that is less than

26.3.3.4 Level 3 dynamic defragmentation
20
21
22 Upon reception of a non-A-MPDU that is a dynamic fragment or A-MPDU that carries one or more dynamic
23 fragments, the recipient STA responds with one of the following frames:
24
25 — An Ack frame or a Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragment is a non-A-MPDU or con-
26 tained in an A-MPDU that solicits the immediate response. The recipient STA shall follow the rules
27 defined in 10.3.2.11 (Acknowledgment procedure) for generating the Ack frame for the soliciting
28
dynamic fragment that is a MPDU, or carried in an S-MPDU or ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU
29
and the rules defined in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure) for generating the Multi-STA Block-
30
31 Ack frame that contains the acknowledgment for the soliciting S-MPDU carried in an HE TB PPDU,
32 ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU or ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU.
33
34 — A Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragments of an
35 MSDU or A-MSDU (if any) corresponding to a TID, one or more fragments for each MSDU or A-
36 MSDU, are contained in an A-MPDU where at least one received MPDU’s Fragment Number field
37
corresponding to the same TID as the received fragments is of nonzero value that solicits the imme-
38
diate response. The recipient STA shall follow the rules in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmission of
39
40 BlockAck frames by an HT STA, DMG STA, or S1G STA) for generating the BlockAck frame and
41 the rules in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure) for generating the Multi-STA BlockAck frame,
42 except that the STA shall:
43
44 • Set to 1 the LSB of the Fragment Number subfield in the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control
45 subfield of the Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame that corresponds to
46 a TID of a received fragment that is sent under a block ack agreement.
47
• Set to 1 each bit in position B of the Block Ack Bitmap field that corresponds to a received frag-
48
49 ment that is sent under a block ack agreement and shall set it to 0 otherwise, with B calculated as:
50 B = 4 × (SN – SSN) + FN, where the operations on the sequence numbers are performed modulo
51 4096.
52
SN is the value of the Sequence Number subfield of an MPDU containing the fragment for which
53
the receive status is indicated.
54
55 SSN is the value of the Starting Sequence Number subfield of the Block Ack Starting Sequence
56 Control subfield of the BlockAck frame.
57 FN is the value in the Fragment Number subfield.
58
59 — A Compressed BlockAck frame or Multi-STA BlockAck frame if the received fragments of an
60 MSDU or A-MSDU (if any) corresponding to a TID, one or more fragments for each MSDU or A-
61 MSDU, are contained in an A-MPDU where all the received MPDUs' Fragment Number fields cor-
62
responding to the same TID as the received fragments are of zero values that solicit the immediate
63
response. The recipient STA shall follow the rules in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmission of
64
65 BlockAck frames by an HT STA, DMG STA, or S1G STA) for generating the BlockAck frame and

1 the rules in 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure) for generating the Multi-STA BlockAck frame,

29
30 26.4 HE acknowledgment procedure
31
32
26.4.1 Overview
33
34
35 The HE acknowledgment procedure builds on the features defined for HT-immediate block ack (see 10.25.6
36
(HT-immediate block ack extensions)), with the following extensions:
37
38 — Support for a Multi-STA BlockAck frame
39
— Support for a MU-BAR Trigger frame
40
41 — Support for a Multi-TID BlockAckReq frame
42
— Support for BlockAck Bitmap field lengths of 32, 64, 128 and 256
43
44 — Acknowledging QoS Data frames with two or more TIDs using a Multi-STA BlockAck frame
45
— Acknowledging QoS Data frames with one or more TIDs, and a Management frame using a Multi-
46
47 STA BlockAck frame
48
— Acknowledging all MPDUs in a PPDU using a variant of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame
49
50 — Acknowledging MPDUs from multiple associated STAs using a single Multi-STA BlockAck frame
51
— Acknowledging MPDUs from multiple unassociated STAs with a single Multi-STA BlockAck
52
frame
53
54
55 An HE STA shall be able to respond with Compressed BlockAck frames if HT-immediate block ack is sup-
56
ported in the role of recipient (see 10.25.6.1 (Introduction)). An HE STA shall be able to respond with a
57
Multi-STA BlockAck frame if multi-TID A-MPDU operation (26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-
58
59 enabled single-TID A-MPDU)) is supported in the role of recipient.
60
61 A non-AP HE STA that is associated with an AP and that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck frame shall set the
62
AID11 subfield in the Per AID TID Info field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame to 0 and the RA field to the
63
MAC address of the intended recipient. A non-AP HE STA that is not associated with an AP shall not send
64
65 a Multi-STA BlockAck frame.
354 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 355 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE AP that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck frame where the Per AID TID Info fields are addressed to
2 more than one STA shall set the RA field to the broadcast address. An HE AP that sends a Multi-STA
3
BlockAck frame where the Per AID TID Info fields are all addressed to a single recipient STA and that is
4
sent in response to an HE TB PPDU may set the RA field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame to either the
5
6 address of the recipient STA or to the broadcast address. An HE AP that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck
7 frame where the Per AID TID Info fields are all addressed to a single recipient STA and that is not sent in
8 response to an HE TB PPDU shall set the RA field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame to the address of the
9
recipient STA.
10
11
12 An HE AP that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck frame to an associated STA shall set the AID11 subfield in the
13 Per AID TID Info field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame to the 11 LSBs of the AID of the intended STA.
14 An HE AP that sends a Multi-STA BlockAck frame to an unassociated STA shall set the AID11 subfield in
15
the Per AID TID Info field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame to 2045.
16
17
18 An HE STA that transmits a Multi-STA BlockAck frame shall use a rate, HT-MCS, <VHT-MCS, NSS>
19 tuple or <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple that is supported by all recipient STAs.
20
21
An HE STA that receives a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that is a response to frames requiring acknowledg-
22
23 ment shall examine Per AID TID Info field received in the Multi-STA BlockAck frame, and shall process
24 each Per AID TID Info field using the procedure defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-
25 STA BlockAck frame).
26
27
A non-AP HE STA that receives a Multi-STA BlockAck frame that is a response to frames requiring
28
29 acknowledgment but that do not belong to an established a block ack agreement shall examine each Per AID
30 TID Info field received in the Multi-STA BlockAck frame as follows:
31
— If the Ack Type field is 1 and the TID field is less than 8, then the Per AID TID Info field indicates
32
33 the acknowledgment of a tagged MPDU that is a QoS Data frame with the indicated TID. The BA
34 Information field is addressed to the STA if the AID of the BA Information field contains the STA’s
35 AID, and is processed according to the procedure defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context in a
36
Multi-STA BlockAck frame).
37
38 — If the Ack Type field is 1 and the TID field is 15, then the Per AID TID Info field indicates the
39 acknowledgment of a tagged MPDU that is a Management frame that solicits acknowledgment or a
40
PS-Poll frame. The BA Information field is addressed to the STA if the AID of the BA Information
41
field contains the STA’s AID, and is processed according to the procedure defined in 26.4.2
42
43 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame).
44 — If the Ack Type field is 0, and the AID field is 2045, and the TID field is 15, then Per AID TID Info
45
field indicates the acknowledgment of a tagged MPDU that is a Management frame soliciting imme-
46
diate acknowledgment. The RA field in the Per AID TID Info field is the MAC address of an unasso-
47
48 ciated STA for which the Per AID TID Info subfield is intended. The BA Information field is
49 addressed to the STA if the RA field of the BA Information field contains the STA’s MAC address,
50 and is processed according to the procedure defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-
51
STA BlockAck frame).
52
53
54 An HE AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true shall not send to a non-AP STA that is associ-
55 ated with an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in the multiple BSSID set a Multi-STA Block-
56
Ack frame with the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID unless the HE AP has received from the non-AP
57
STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx Control Frame To MultiBSS subfield in HE MAC Capabilities
58
59 Information field equal to 1.
60
61 An AP that transmits a Multi-STA BlockAck frame addressed to HE STAs shall set the TA field of the
62
frame to the MAC address of the AP unless dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented is true and the Multi-STA
63
BlockAck frame is directed to STAs from at least two different BSSs of the multiple BSSID set, in which
64
65 case, the AP shall set the TA field of the frame to the transmitted BSSID.

1 NOTE—An AP sets the TA field of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame that is not carried in HE MU PPDU to the transmit-

15 AC.

26.4.2 Acknowledgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame
18
19
20 A recipient of an A-MPDU shall set the Ack Type subfield and TID subfield in the Per AID TID Info field
21 of the Multi-STA BlockAck frame sent as a response depending on the acknowledgment context as follows:
22
23 — An HE AP that receives an A-MPDU that includes one MPDU, and the MPDU is a tagged MPDU
24 that is a Management frame that solicits an acknowledgment prior to association may generate a
25 Multi-STA BlockAck frame using the procedure defined in the pre-association ack context.
26
27 — An HE STA that receives an A-MPDU that does not include a tagged MPDU but does include one or
28 more untagged MPDUs that are QoS Data frames with Normal Ack or Implicit BAR ack policy
29 belonging to the same block ack agreement may generate a Multi-STA BlockAck frame as follows:
30
• If all MPDUs in the A-MPDU are received successfully, then the recipient may follow the proce-
31
32 dure described in the all ack context as defined below.
33 • Otherwise, the recipient shall follow the procedure defined in the block ack context.
34
— If an HE STA supports ack-enabled aggregation by setting the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support
35
36 subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field to 1 and the A-MPDU includes a tagged
37 MPDU that is a Management frame that solicits acknowledgment, and one or more MPDUs (either
38 tagged MPDUs or untagged MPDUs) that are QoS Data frames with Normal Ack or Implicit BAR
39
ack policy, then the recipient shall generate Multi-STA BlockAck frame as follows:
40
41 • If all the MPDUs in the A-MPDU are received successfully, then the recipient may follow the
42 procedure defined in the all ack context.
43
• Otherwise:
44
45 • For the MPDU that is a Management frame, the recipient shall create a Per AID TID info field
46 using the procedure defined in the ack context with the TID value set to 15.
47 • For the tagged MPDUs that are QoS Data frames, the recipient shall create a Per AID TID info
48
field using the procedure defined in the ack context with the TID set to the TID of the QoS
49
Data frame
50
51 • For the untagged MPDUs that are QoS Data frames, the recipient shall create a Per AID TID
52 info field using the procedure defined in block ack context with the TID set to the TID of the
53 QoS Data frame
54
55 — If an HE STA supports multi-TID aggregation and the A-MPDU does not include a tagged MPDU
56 but does include untagged MPDUs that are QoS Data frames with Implicit BAR ack policy and are
57 belonging to more than one block ack agreement, then the recipient shall generate a Multi-STA
58
BlockAck frame as follows:
59
60 • If all MPDUs in the A-MPDU are received successfully, then the recipient may follow the proce-
61 dure described in the all ack context
62
• Otherwise, for each TID included the received A-MPDU, the recipient shall create a per AID
63
TID info field using the procedure defined in the block ack context with the TID set to the TID of
64
65 the QoS Data frame
356 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 357 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 NOTE—A STA indicates the maximum number of Per AID TID Info fields with the same AID excluding the one for a
2 Management frame that it can include in the Multi-STA BlockAck frame in the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support field
3 in the HE Capabilities element it transmits.
4
5
The procedures for the different acknowledgment contexts for generating a Multi-STA BlockAck frame are
6
defined as follows:
7
8
a) All ack context: if the originator had set the All Ack Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element
9
to 1, then the recipient may set the Ack Type field to 1 and the TID subfield to 14 to indicate the
10
11 reception of all the MPDUs carried in the eliciting A-MPDU or multi-TID A-MPDU. Otherwise the
12 recipient shall not set the Ack Type field to 1 and the TID subfield to 14. The Multi-STA BlockAck
13 frame shall contain only one Per AID TID Info field addressed to an originator in the Multi-STA
14
BlockAck frame. The recipient determines that all the MPDUs carried in the eliciting A-MPDU
15
were received if there were no MPDU delimiter CRC errors and no MPDU FCS errors in that A-
16
17 MPDU.
18
b) Pre-association ack context: A recipient receiving a Management frame from an unassociated STA,
19
20 that requires an acknowledgment, shall set the Ack Type field to 0, AID subfield to 2045, and the
21 TID field to 15 in the Per AID TID Info field, and the RA field of the Per AID TID Info field to the
22 intended recipient’s MAC address to indicate the reception of that Management frame.
23
24 c) Ack context: A recipient that sets the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support subfield in the HE Capabil-
25 ities element to 1 and that receives a tagged MPDU soliciting acknowledgment shall set the Ack
26
Type field to 1 and, if the tagged MPDU is a QoS Data frame, set the TID field to the TID of the
27
QoS Data frame, or, if the tagged MPDU is a Management frame or PS-Poll frame, set the TID field
28
29 to 15.
30
31 If a received A-MPDU contains more than one tagged MPDU that solicits an immediate acknowl-
32
edgment, then the Multi-STA BlockAck frame shall contain multiple Per AID TID Info fields, with
33
Ack Type field equal to 1, one for each such received tagged MPDU requesting an acknowledg-
34
35 ment.
36
37 The TID field is set to the TID of the QoS Data or QoS Null frame that is being acknowledged and
38
set to 15 for a PS Poll frame or Management frame that is being acknowledged.
39
40 d) Block ack context: The recipient shall set the Ack Type field to 0 and the TID field of a Per AID
41
TID Info field to the TID value of MPDUs requesting block acknowledgment that are carried in the
42
eliciting A-MPDU or multi-TID A-MPDU.
43
44
45 The Multi-STA BlockAck frame may contain multiple occurrences of these Per AID TID Info fields
46 addressed to an originator, one for each MPDU that is requesting block acknowledgment, in which
47
case the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control and Block Ack Bitmap fields shall be set according to
48
10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack extensions) for each block ack session, and according to 26.3
49
50 (Fragmentation and defragmentation) for each block ack session with dynamic fragmentation.
51
52 The allowed values for the TID field in this context are 0 to 7 (for indicating block acknowledgment
53
of QoS Data frames).
54
55
56 Variable bitmap lengths may be included in the Per AID TID Info field when the originator and
57 recipient negotiate their use as defined in 26.4.3 (Negotiation of block ack bitmap lengths).
58
59
60 The Ack Type subfield(s) in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame shall be set to 0 if the Multi-STA BlockAck
61 frame is sent in response to an MU-BAR Trigger frame.
62
63
Upon receipt of a Multi-STA BlockAck frame the originator shall examine each Per AID TID Info field and
64
65 shall perform the following operations:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 357
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 358 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — If the AID subfield is 0 for an AP originator or the non-AP STA’s AID for a non-AP STA originator,
2 the Ack Type field is 0 and the TID field is less than 8 then the BlockAck Starting Sequence Con-
3
trol, TID and Block Ack Bitmap fields of the Per AID TID Info field are processed according to
4
10.25.6 (HT-immediate block ack extensions), 26.3 (Fragmentation and defragmentation), and as
5
6 defined below.
7
8 — If the AID subfield is 2045, the Ack Type field is 0 and the TID field is 15, then the Per AID TID
9 Info field indicates the acknowledgment of a single Management frame sent by the unassociated
10 STA as defined by the acknowledgment context.
11
12 — If the AID subfield is 0 for an AP originator or the non-AP STA’s AID for a non-AP STA originator,
13 the Ack Type field is 1 and the TID is less than or equal to 7 or is equal to 15, then the Per AID TID
14
Info field indicates the acknowledgment of an tagged MPDU that is a QoS Data frame identified by
15
the value of the TID, a Management frame or a PS-Poll frame.
16
17
— If the AID subfield is 0 for an AP originator or the non-AP STA’s AID for a non-AP STA originator,
18
19 the Ack Type field is 1 and the TID subfield of AID TID Info field is 14, then the Per AID TID Info
20 field indicates the acknowledgment of all MPDUs carried in the eliciting A-MPDU as defined by the
21 acknowledgment context.
22
23
24 If an associated non-AP STA that does not support the UORA procedure receives a Multi-STA BlockAck
25 frame in response to an eliciting frame, and if the Multi-STA BlockAck frame contains a Per AID TID Info
26
subfield with the AID11 subfield set to 2045, then the STA shall ignore this Per AID TID Info subfield, and
27
shall continue to parse the following Per AID TID Info subfields (if any).
28
29
30
26.4.3 Negotiation of block ack bitmap lengths
31
32
33
Both the Compressed BlockAck frame and Multi-STA BlockAck frame allow different Block Ack Bitmap
34
subfield lengths. The length of the Block Ack Bitmap subfield is indicated in the Fragment Number subfield
35
36 of the Block Ack Starting Sequence Control field as defined in 9.3.1.8 (BlockAck frame format). The
37 allowed Block Ack Bitmap lengths for each of the negotiated buffer sizes are defined in Table26-1 (Negoti-
38 ated buffer size and Block Ack Bitmap subfield length).
39

56 nator has set the 32-bit BA Bitmap Support field in the HE MAC Capabilities Infor-

62

32 frame that solicits acknowledgment shall set the Duration field accounting for the largest BlockAck Bitmap

43 26.4.4 Per-PPDU acknowledgment selection rules
44
45
26.4.4.1 General
46
47
48 A STA that transmits a PPDU can solicit different immediate responses for frames contained in the PPDU
49 by using the Ack Policy Indicator subfield of QoS Data or QoS Null frames, the type of the frame, PPDU
50
format, number of TIDs in the A-MPDU and the EOF/Tag field setting of the A-MPDU delimiter.
51
52
53 26.4.4.2 Responding to an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU with an SU PPDU
54
55
An HE STA that receives an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU carrying an A-MPDU that includes MPDUs
56
57 that solicits acknowledgment and that does not include a triggering frame shall respond using an SU PPDU
58 as follows:
59
1) If the A-MPDU includes only one MPDU and the MPDU is a tagged MPDU that is either a
60
61 QoS Data frame or QoS Null frame with Normal Ack ack policy, or a Management frame that
62 solicits acknowledgment, then the STA shall respond with an Ack frame.
63
2) If the A-MPDU includes only one MPDU and the MPDU is an EOF MPD that is a PS-Poll
64
65 frame the STA shall respond with an Ack frame or a QoS Data frame.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 359
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 360 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 3) If the HE STA supports ack-enabled aggregation by setting the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Sup-
2 port subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field to 1, and if the A-MPDU includes
3
more than one MPDU, only one of which solicits acknowledgment and the MPDU that solicits
4
acknowledgment is a tagged MPDU that is a QoS Data frame or a QoS Null frame with Normal
5
6 Ack ack policy, or a Management frame that solicits acknowledgment, then the HE STA shall
7 respond with an Ack frame.
8
4) If the A-MPDU does not include a tagged MPDU but does include one or more untagged
9
10 MPDUs that are QoS Data frames belonging to the same block ack agreement and with the Ack
11 Policy Indicator subfield equal to Implicit BAR for at least one MPDU, then the STA shall
12 respond with a Compressed BlockAck frame as defined in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmis-
13
sion of BlockAck frames by an HT STA, DMG STA, or S1G STA) or, if all the MPDUs carried
14
in the eliciting A-MPDU were received, the STA may respond with a Multi-STA BlockAck
15
16 frame with Ack Type field set to 1 and the TID field set to 14 as defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowl-
17 edgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame) if the originator of the A-MPDU has indi-
18 cated support for the all ack context by setting the All Ack Support subfield in the HE MAC
19
Capabilities Information field to 1.
20
21 5) If the HE STA supports ack-enabled aggregation by setting the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Sup-
22 port subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field to 1, and if the A-MPDU includes a
23 Management frame that solicits an acknowledgment, and one or more QoS Data frames with
24
ack policy Normal Ack or Implicit BAR, then the STA shall respond with a Multi-STA Block-
25
Ack frame as defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame).

ment, then the STA shall respond with a Multi-STA BlockAck frame as defined in 26.4.2

31 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck frame).

33 26.4.4.3 Responding to an HE MU PPDU with an SU PPDU
34
35
If an AP intends to solicit an immediate response in an SU PPDU the following apply:
36
37 — An AP shall set the Ack Policy Indicator subfield in the QoS Data and QoS Null frames to Normal
38 Ack or Implicit BAR in at most one A-MPDU in the HE MU PPDU (see 10.3.2.13.1 (Acknowledg-
39
ment procedure for DL MU PPDU in SU PPDU) for an example of this sequence).
40
41 — The A-MPDUs in the HE MU PPDU shall not contain a Management frame that solicits acknowl-
42 edgment.
43
44
An HE STA that receives an HE MU PPDU with an A-MPDU that contains MPDUs that solicit acknowl-
45
46 edgment and that does not include a triggering frame shall respond using an SU PPDU as follows:
47
1) If the A-MPDU carries only one MPDU and the MPDU is a tagged MPDU that is either a QoS Data
48
frame or QoS Null frame with Normal Ack ack policy, then the STA shall respond with an Ack
49
50 frame.
51 2) If the HE STA supports ack-enabled aggregation by setting the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support
52
subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field to 1, and if the A-MPDU includes more than
53
one MPDU, only one of which solicits acknowledgment and the MPDU that solicits acknowledg-
54
55 ment is a tagged MPDU that is a QoS Data frame or a QoS Null frame with Normal Ack ack policy,
56 then the HE STA shall respond with an Ack frame.
57
3) If the A-MPDU does not include a tagged MPDU but does include one or more untagged MPDUs
58
59 that are QoS Data frame belonging to the same block ack agreement and with the Ack Policy Indica-
60 tor subfield equal to Implicit BAR for at least one MPDU, then the STA shall respond with a Com-
61 pressed BlockAck frame as defined in 10.25.6.5 (Generation and transmission of BlockAck frames
62
by an HT STA, DMG STA, or S1G STA) or, if all the MPDUs carried in the eliciting A-MPDU
63
were received, the STA may respond with a Multi-STA BlockAck frame with the Ack Type set to 1
64
65 and the TID field set to 14 as defined in 26.4.2 (Acknowledgment context in a Multi-STA BlockAck
