16 26.5.5 Buffer status report operation
17
18
A non-AP STA delivers buffer status reports (BSRs) to assist its AP in allocating UL MU resources. The
19
20 non-AP STA can either implicitly deliver BSRs in the QoS Control field or BSR Control subfield of any
21 frame transmitted to the AP (unsolicited BSR) or explicitly deliver BSRs in any frame sent to the AP in
22 response to a BSRP Trigger frame (solicited BSR). The buffer status reported in the QoS Control field con-
23
sists of a queue size value for a given TID (see 9.2.4.5.6 (Queue Size subfield)). The buffer status reported in
24
the BSR Control field consists of an ACI bitmap, delta TID, a high priority AC, and two queue sizes (see
25
26 9.2.4.6a.4 (BSR Control)).
27
28
An HE STA shall set the BSR Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1 if
29
dot11HEBSRControlImplemented is true; otherwise the HE STA shall set the BSR Support subfield to 0.
30
31
32 A non-AP STA reports its buffer status (unsolicited BSR) to the AP with which it is associated in the QoS
33
Control field in QoS Null and QoS Data frames and in the BSR Control subfield (if present) in QoS Null,
34
QoS Data and Management frames as defined below:
35
36 — The HE STA shall report the queue size for a given TID in the Queue Size subfield of the QoS Con-
37
trol field in QoS Data or QoS Null frames it transmits; the STA may set the Queue Size subfield to
38
255 to indicate an unknown/unspecified queue size for that TID.
39
40
• The HE STA may aggregate multiple QoS Data frames or QoS Null frames in an A-MPDU to
41
report the queue size for different TIDs. The HE STA shall follow the A-MPDU aggregation
42
43 rules defined in 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU) for aggre-
44 gating QoS Data frames with multiple TIDs. The HE STA does not follow the rules defined in
45 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU) for QoS Null frames with
46
No Ack ack policy.
47
48 — The HE STA may report the buffer status in the BSR Control subfield of frames it transmits if the AP
49 has indicated its support in the BSR Support subfield of its HE Capabilities element; otherwise the
50
STA shall not report the buffer status in the BSR Control subfield.
51
52 • The HE STA shall report the queue size for its preferred AC, indicated by the ACI High subfield,
53
in the Queue Size High subfield of the BSR Control subfield; the STA may set the Queue Size
54
High subfield to 255 to indicate an unknown/unspecified queue size for that AC.
55
56 • The HE STA shall report the queue size for the ACs, indicated by the ACI Bitmap subfield, in
57 the Queue Size All subfield of the BSR Control subfield; the STA may set the Queue Size All
58
subfield to 255 to indicate an unknown/unspecified BSR for those ACs.
59
• The HE STA shall set the Delta TID subfield according to Table9-24e (Delta TID subfield
60
61 encoding), and the Scaling Factor subfield as defined in 9.2.4.6a.4 (BSR Control).
62
63 NOTE 1—The STA can send an unsolicited BSR in response to certain Trigger frames except MU-RTS and BSRP (with
64 or without RA-RUs, as defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation) and in 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-
65 based random access (UORA))) or it can send the unsolicited BSR after accessing the WM using EDCA.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 391
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 392 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 NOTE 2—The STA might include a BSR Control subfield in a QoS Data or QoS Null frame. In this case the Queue Size
2 subfield in the QoS Control field and the Queue Size High and Queue Size All subfields in the BSR Control subfield
3 might differ, and any of the three subfields might be set to 255 to indicate unspecified or unknown queue size. The STA
4 might include only the BSR Control subfield in a Management frame.
5
6
An AP can also solicit one or more associated non-AP STAs for their BSR(s) by sending a BSRP Trigger
7
frame (see 9.3.1.22.6 (BSRP Trigger frame format)). The non-AP STA responds (solicited BSR) as defined
8
9 below:
10
— The non-AP STA that receives a BSRP Trigger frame shall follow the rules defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-
11
AP STA behavior for UL MU operation) to generate the HE TB PPDU if the Trigger frame contains
12
13 the 12 LSBs of the non-AP STA’s AID in any of the User Info fields; otherwise if the non-AP STA’s
14 buffers are not empty and the non-AP STA supports the UORA procedure, it may follow the rules
15 defined in 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA)) to gain access to an RA-RU and
16
generate the HE TB PPDU when the Trigger frame contains one or more RA-RUs.
17
18 — The non-AP STA shall include in the HE TB PPDU one or more QoS Null frames containing one or
19 more of the following:
20
21 • The QoS Control field(s) with Queue Size subfields for each of the TIDs for which the non-AP
22 STA has queue size to report to the AP.
23
• The BSR Control subfield with the Queue Size All subfield indicating the queue size for the
24
25 ACs, indicated by the ACI Bitmap subfield, for which the non-AP STA has queue size to report
26 to the AP if the AP has indicated its support in the BSR Support subfield of its HE Capabilities
27 element. The non-AP STA shall set Delta TID, SF, ACI High and Queue Size High subfields of
28
the BSR Control subfield as defined in 9.2.4.6a.4 (BSR Control).
29
30 — The non-AP STA shall not solicit an immediate response for the frames carried in the HE TB PPDU
31 (e.g., the Ack Policy Indicator subfield of a QoS Null frame shall not be set to Normal Ack or
32
Implicit BAR).
33
34
NOTE 1—As with unsolicited BSR, the STA might include a BSR Control subfield in a QoS Null frame that is sent in
35
response to the BSRP Trigger frame. In this case, the Queue Size subfield in the QoS Control field and the Queue Size
36
High and Queue Size All subfields in the BSR Control subfield might differ, and any of the three subfields might be set
37
to 255 to indicate an unspecified or unknown queue size.
38
39 NOTE 2—An AP does not send a BSRP Trigger frame containing the 12 LSBs of the AID of the non-AP STA that sets
40 the UL MU Disable field to 1.
41
42
An AP may include a BSRP Trigger frame together with other Control, Data and Management frames in one
43
44 A-MPDU to a non-AP STA if the HE Capabilities element received from the non-AP STA has the BSRP
45 BQRP A-MPDU Aggregation field equal to 1. A non-AP STA constructs the A-MPDU contained in the HE
46 TB PPDU sent in response to a BSRP Trigger frame as described in 26.5.2.4 (A-MPDU contents in an HE
47
TB PPDU).
48
49
50 The NDP feedback report procedure described in 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure) can be used for
51 buffer status feedback operation. An AP that sent an NFRP Trigger frame to one or more non-AP STAs may
52 send a BSRP Trigger frame to those non-AP STAs to get more precise buffer status information.
53
54
55 26.5.6 Bandwidth query report operation
56
57
A non-AP STA may send bandwidth query reports (BQRs) to assist its AP in allocating DL MU and UL MU
58
resources. The non-AP STA may either implicitly deliver BQRs in the BQR Control subfield of a frame
59
60 transmitted to the AP (unsolicited BQR) or explicitly deliver BQRs in a frame sent to the AP in response to
61 a BQRP Trigger frame (solicited BQR).
62
63
An HE STA shall set the BQR Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1 if
64
65 dot11HEBQRControlImplemented is true; otherwise the HE STA shall set the BQR Support subfield to 0.
392 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 393 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 The HE STA may report the channel availability information as specified in 27.3.20.6.5 (Per 20 MHz CCA
2 sensitivity) in the BQR Control subfield of frames it transmits if the AP has indicated its support in the BQR
3
Support subfield of its HE Capabilities element; otherwise the STA shall not report the channel availability
4
information in the BQR Control subfield.
5
6
7 The availability of each 20 MHz subchannel reported in the BQR Control field shall be reported for the 20
8 MHz subchannels located in the operating channel of the reporting STA (see 9.2.4.6a.6 (BQR Control)).
9
10 NOTE—The STA can send an unsolicited BQR in response to certain Trigger frames except NFRP, MU-RTS and
11 BQRP (with or without RA-RUs, as defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation) and in 26.5.4
12 (UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA))) or it can send the unsolicited BQR after accessing the WM using EDCA.
13
14
An AP may solicit BQRs from a non-AP STA only if the non-AP STA has indicated support by setting the
15
BQR Support field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1; otherwise the AP shall not solicit BQRs
16
17 from the non-AP STA. An AP may solicit BQRs from one or more non-AP HE STAs that support generat-
18 ing BQRs, by sending a BQRP Trigger frame (see 9.3.1.22.8 (BQRP Trigger frame format)).
19
20
An AP shall not transmit a BQR Trigger frame with the User Info field addressed to a non-AP STA unless it
21
has received from the non-AP STA an HE Capabilities element with the BQR Support subfield equal to 1.
22
23
24 If a non-AP STA supports BQR operation and receives a BQRP Trigger frame with a User Info field
25 addressed to the non-AP STA, then the non-AP STA shall respond by following the procedure in 26.5.2.3.1
26
(General).
27
28
29 If a non-AP STA supports both BQR operation and the UORA procedure and receives a BQRP Trigger
30 frame from the AP with which it is associated and that allocates RA-RUs for associated STAs but that does
31
contain a User Info field addressed to the non-AP STA, then the non-AP STA shall respond by following the
32
procedure in 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA)).
33
34
35 If the non-AP STA responds with an HE TB PPDU using one of the above procedures, then the A-MPDU
36 carried in the HE TB PPDU shall include one or more QoS Null frames containing a BQR Control subfield
37
with the channel availability information of the STA. The non-AP STA shall not solicit an immediate
38
response for the frames carried in the HE TB PPDU. The Ack Policy Indication subfield of the frame shall
39
40 be set to No Ack.
41
NOTE 1—An AP does not send a BQRP Trigger frame addressed to a STA that has set the UL MU Disable field to 1.
42
43
NOTE 2—The channel availability information of the STA is limited to the STA's operating channel width. See
44
9.2.4.6a.6 (BQR Control).
45
46
An AP may include a BQRP Trigger frame together with other Control, Data and Management frames in
47
48 one A-MPDU to a STA if the HE Capabilities element received from the STA has the BSRP BQRP A-
49 MPDU Aggregation field equal to 1. A non-AP STA constructs the A-MPDU contained in the HE TB
50 PPDU sent in response to a BQRP Trigger frame as described in 26.5.2.4 (A-MPDU contents in an HE TB
51
PPDU).
52
53
54 26.5.7 NDP feedback report procedure
55
56 26.5.7.1 General
57
58
59 The NDP feedback report procedure allows an HE AP to collect feedback that is not channel sounding from
60 multiple non-AP HE STAs.
61
62
An HE AP sends an NFRP Trigger frame to solicit NDP feedback report response from many non-AP STAs
63
that are identified by a range of scheduled AIDs in the Trigger frame. The NDP feedback report response
64
65 from a non-AP STA is an HE TB feedback NDP (see 27.3.18 (HE TB feedback NDP)). A non-AP STA uses
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 393
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 394 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 the information carried in the NFRP Trigger frame to know if it is scheduled, and in this case, to derive the
2 parameters for the transmission of the response.
3
4
In this subclause, the NDP feedback report procedure is described.
5
6
7 26.5.7.2 STA behavior
8
9
A non-AP STA shall set the NDP Feedback Report Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element to 1 if it
10
supports NDP feedback report and set it 0, otherwise.
11
12
13 A non-AP STA shall not transmit an NDP feedback report response unless it is explicitly enabled by an AP
14 in one of the operation modes described in this subclause. The inter frame space between a PPDU that con-
15
tains an NFRP Trigger frame and the NDP feedback report poll response is SIFS. A non-AP STA shall com-
16
mence the transmission of an NDP feedback report response at the SIFS time boundary after the end of a
17
18 received PPDU, if all the following conditions are met:
19
— The received PPDU contains an NFRP Trigger frame
20
21 — The non-AP STA is scheduled by the NFRP Trigger frame
22
— The NDP feedback report support subfield in HE MAC Capabilities Information field is set to 1
23
24 — The non-AP STA intends to provide a response to the type of the NDP feedback contained in the
25 NFRP Trigger frame, as described in 26.5.7.4 (NDP feedback report for a resource request).
26
27
A non-AP STA that does not satisfy all of the above conditions shall not respond to the NFRP Trigger
28
frame.
29
30
31 A non-AP STA is scheduled to respond to the NFRP Trigger frame if all the following conditions are met:
32
— The non-AP STA is associated with an AP corresponding to the BSSID indicated in the TA field of
33
34 the NFRP Trigger frame or the non-AP STA is associated with an AP corresponding to a nontrans-
35 mitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID set and the TA field of the NFRP Trigger frame is set to the trans-
36 mitted BSSID of that multiple BSSID set.
37
38 — The non-AP STA’s AID is greater than or equal to the starting AID and less than starting AID +
39 N , using the Starting AID subfield in the eliciting Trigger frame, and with N the total number
STA STA
40 of non-AP STAs that are scheduled to respond to the NFRP Trigger frame. N is calculated by the
STA
41
following equation, with UL BW subfield and Number Of Spatially Multiplexed Users subfield from
42
the eliciting Trigger frame:
43
44 N STA = 18 × 2BW × (value of the Number Of Spatially Multiplexed Users subfield + 1)
45
46 A non-AP STA shall obtain NDP feedback report parameter values from the most recently received NDP
47
Feedback Report Parameter Set element carried in a Beacon, Probe Response, or (Re)Association Response
48
frame from its associated AP unless the non-AP STA is associated with an AP corresponding to a nontrans-
49
50 mitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID set, in which case it shall follow the rules in 11.1.3.8.4 (Inheritance of
51 element values) to determine the NDP feedback parameter values. If the NDP Feedback Report Parameter
52 Set element is not received in a Management frame with a TA equal to the BSSID of the associated AP or to
53
the transmitted BSSID of the multiple BSSID set, the non-AP STA shall use default values for the NDP
54
Feedback Report parameters.
55
56
57 An NDP feedback report response is an HE TB feedback NDP as defined in 27.3.4 (HE PPDU formats).
58
59
A non-AP STA transmitting an NDP feedback report shall set the TXVECTOR parameter as for transmit-
60
ting an HE TB PPDU in response to a Trigger frame that is not an MU-RTS Trigger frame as described in
61
62 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation), except for the following parameters:
63 — The FORMAT parameter shall be set to HE_TB
64
65 — The APEP_LENGTH parameter shall be set to 0
394 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 395 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The RU_ALLOCATION parameter shall be set to be maximum RU size for the BW
2
— The RU_TONE_SET_INDEX parameter shall be set with the following equation, with the value of
3
4 the Starting AID subfield in the User Info field of the eliciting Trigger frame:
5 • RU_TONE_SET_INDEX = 1 + ((AID – Starting AID) mod (18 × 2BW))
6
7 — The NUM_STS parameter shall be set to 1
8
— The SPATIAL_REUSE parameter shall be set to PSR_DISALLOW
9
10 — The STARTING_STS_NUM parameter shall be set with the following equation, with the values of
11 the Starting AID subfield in the User Info field of the eliciting Trigger frame:
12
• STARTING_STS_NUM = (AID – Starting AID) / 18 / 2BW
13
14 — The MCS parameter shall be set to 0
15
— The DCM parameter shall be set to 0
16
17 — The FEC_CODING parameter shall be set to BCC_CODING
18
— The TXPWR_LEVEL_INDEX parameter shall be set to the value based on the Transmit Power
19
20 Control for an HE TB PPDU and based on the value of the AP Tx Power subfield and the UL Target
21 Receive Power subfield in the User Info field of the eliciting Trigger frame (see 27.3.15.2 (Power
22 pre-correction))
23
24 NOTE—The subcarriers for each RU_TONE_SET index are contained in a 20 MHz channel and can be transmitted by a
25 20 MHz operating STA.
26
27
26.5.7.3 AP behavior
28
29
30 An AP shall set the NDP Feedback Report Support subfield in the HE Capabilities element to 1 if it supports
31 NDP feedback report and set it 0 otherwise.
32
33
An AP may include the NDP Feedback Report Parameter Set element in Beacon frames, Probe Response
34
frames and (Re)Association Response frames in order to modify parameters for NDP Feedback Report oper-
35
36 ation. The procedure of NDP Feedback report described in this subclause allows operation even if the NDP
37 Feedback Report Parameter Set element is not sent by the AP.
38
39
The NFRP Trigger frame shall be transmitted in a non-HT PPDU or HT PPDU, or as a tagged MPDU in a
40
VHT, HE ER SU PPDU or HE SU PPDU.
41
42
43 An AP that transmits an NFRP Trigger frame shall set the TA field of the frame to the MAC address of the
44 AP, unless dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented is true and the Trigger frame is directed to STAs from at least
45
two different BSSs of a multiple BSSID set, in which case, the AP shall set the TA field of the frame to the
46
transmitted BSSID.
47
48
49 Following the transmission from an AP of an NFRP Trigger frame, multiple STAs may simultaneously send
50
NDP feedback report responses to the AP. Based on the RXVECTOR parameter NDP_REPORT, which
51
provides the detected status array for the resources of each spatial stream and tone set assigned by the Trig-
52
53 ger frame, the AP can derive the list of AIDs from the resources of which an NDP feedback report response
54 was sent, and their response.
55
56
The AP shall not send any acknowledgment in response to the reception of NDP feedback report responses.
57
58
59 26.5.7.4 NDP feedback report for a resource request
60
61 If the Feedback Type subfield in the User Info field of the NFRP Trigger frame is 0, a STA that is scheduled
62
may send an NDP feedback report response in order to signal to the AP that it is in the awake state and that
63
it might have frames in its queues for UL MU. If the STA does not satisfy either of the conditions in Table
64
65 26-3 (FEEDBACK_STATUS description), then the STA shall not respond to the NFRP Trigger frame.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 395
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 396 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 Each STA that is scheduled is assigned a STARTING_STS_NUM and an RU_TONE_SET_INDEX to
2 transmit a FEEDBACK_STATUS bit.
3
4
5 The meaning of the FEEDBACK_STATUS bit is defined in Table26-3 (FEEDBACK_STATUS descrip-
6 tion):
7
8
9
Table 26-3—FEEDBACK_STATUS description
10
11
12
FEEDBACK_STATUS Condition
13
14
0 The STA is in the awake state and reports buffered octets
15
for transmission not exceeding the resource request buf-
16
fer threshold.
17
18
NOTE—The STA can use this value to indicate that it is
19
in the awake state even if it does not have any buffered
20
octets for transmission, for example to solicit delivery of
21
BUs known to be buffered at the AP.
22
23 1 The STA is in the awake state and reports buffered octets
24 for transmission exceeding the resource request buffer
25 threshold.
26
27
28
29 The resource request buffer threshold is equal to 2(Resource request buffer threshold exponent) octets, using the
30 Resource Request Buffer Threshold Exponent subfield in the most recently received NDP Feedback Report
31
Parameter Set element sent by the AP with which the STA is associated. The resource request buffer thresh-
32
old is equal to 256 octets if the STA did not receive an NDP Feedback Report Parameter Set element from
33
34 the AP with which the STA is associated.
35
36
26.5.7.5 Power save operation with NDP feedback report procedure
37
38
39 An HE AP that sends an NFRP Trigger frame to a non-AP STA in PS mode and receives an NDP feedback
40 report response from the STA shall assume the STA to be or to have transitioned to the awake state and:
41
42 — follow the rules defined in 11.2.3.5 (Power management with APSD) to deliver BUs to the STA as if
43 the STA had sent a U-APSD trigger frame, if the STA is using U-APSD
44
— follow the rules defined in 11.2.3.1 (General) to deliver BUs to the STA as if the STA had sent a PS-
45
46 Poll frame, if the STA is not using U-APSD
47
— in addition, the AP shall follow the rules defined in 26.8 (TWT operation) for delivery of BUs during
48
49 the TWT SP if the STA operates with TWT.
50
51 NOTE—As the AP does not send an acknowledgment in response to the NDP Feedback Report response, a STA that
52 sends an NDP Feedback Report response and indicates that it is in the awake state might still schedule for transmission a
53 PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame in order to indicate that it is in the awake state.
54
55 26.5.8 Use of TSPEC by HE STAs
56
57
In addition to the TS Setup operations as described in 11.4.4 (TS setup), a non-AP HE STA may use a
58
59 TSPEC contained in a Basic ADDTS Request frame to provide its traffic characteristics and QoS require-
60 ments to an HE AP that supports the reception of Basic ADDTS Request frame in order to facilitate efficient
61 scheduling for HE AP’s UL and DL MU operations. A TSPEC provided by a non-AP HE STA is used by a
62
receiving HE AP to facilitate the creation of a schedule for contention based channel access (EDCA) or MU
63
operation. How the HE AP uses the information provided by the non-AP HE STA is beyond the scope of the
64
65 specification.
396 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE AP transmits an ADDTS Response frame as a response to ADDTS Request frame as described in
2 11.4.4 (TS setup).
3
4
5 A non-AP HE STA should send a DELTS frame with the corresponding TSID if the traffic associated with
6 the TSID has been terminated. When receiving a DELTS from a non-AP HE STA, the HE AP shall consider
7 the information provided in the TSPEC as no longer valid.
8

10 26.5.9 UL MU transmit power capabilities
11
12
A non-AP HE STA may use the UL MU Power Capabilities element in an (Re)Association Request frame in
13
order to inform an HE AP of the relative maximum transmit power at which the non-AP HE STA is capable
14
15 of transmitting an HE TB PPDU for each HE-MCS when using an RU size greater than or equal to 242 sub-
16 carriers.
17
18
An HE AP might use this information as an input into the algorithm used to schedule the non-AP HE STA
19
20 for UL MU transmission. The specification of the algorithm is beyond the scope of this standard.
21
22
23 26.6 A-MPDU operation in an HE PPDU
24
25
26.6.1 General
26
27
28 A-MPDU operation for an HE PPDU follows the procedures defined in 10.12 (A-MPDU operation) and,
29
additionally, the procedures defined in this subclause.
30
31
32 An HE STA that sends a Class 1 frame or a Class 2 frame in an HE PPDU shall send the frame as an S-
33 MPDU (see Table 9-533 (A-MPDU contents in the S-MPDU context)).
34
35
36 An HE STA that sends a VHT Capabilities element, an HT Capabilities element, or an HE 6 GHz Band
37 Capabilities element and an HE Capabilities element with Maximum A-MPDU Length Exponent Extension
38 field of 0 shall support in reception an A-MPDU pre-EOF padding with maximum length defined in 10.12.2
39
(A-MPDU length limit rules).
40
41
42 An HE STA that sends a VHT Capabilities element and an HE Capabilities element with Maximum A-
43 MPDU Length Exponent Extension subfield greater than 0 shall support reception of an HE PPDU with an
44
A-MPDU pre-EOF padding as defined in 10.12.2 (A-MPDU length limit rules) except that the maximum
45
length for the A-MPDU pre-EOF padding shall be equal to
46
20+Maximum A-MPDU Length Exponent Extension
47 min(2 –16 500 631). An HE STA that sets the Maximum A-
48 MPDU Length Exponent Extension subfield in the HE Capabilities element to a value greater than 0 shall
49 set the Maximum A-MPDU Length Exponent subfield of the VHT Capabilities element to 7.
50
51
NOTE—6500631 is defined in Table9-25 (Maximum data unit sizes (in octets) and durations (in microseconds)) as the
52
upper bound of A-MPDU size.
53
54
An HE STA that does not send a VHT Capabilities element but sends an HT Capabilities element and an HE
55
56 Capabilities element with Maximum A-MPDU Length Exponent Extension subfield greater than 0 shall
57 support in reception an A-MPDU pre-EOF padding in an HE PPDU as defined in 10.13.2 (A-MPDU length
58 limit rules) except that the maximum length for the A-MPDU pre-EOF padding shall be equal to 2(16 + Maxi-
59 mum A-MPDU Length Exponent Extension)–1. An HE STA that sets the Maximum A-MPDU Length Exponent
60
Extension subfield in the HE Capabilities element to a value greater than 0 shall set the Maximum A-MPDU
61
62 Length Exponent subfield of the HT Capabilities element to 3.
63
64 NOTE—An HE STA that is a VHT STA sends a VHT Capabilities element. An HE STA that is not a VHT STA does
65 not send a VHT Capabilities element.

47 26.6.2 A-MPDU padding in an HE PPDU
48
49
50 26.6.2.1 General
51
52
In an HE PPDU, a STA shall not add an A-MPDU subframe with the EOF/Tag field set to 1 and with the
53
MPDU Length field set to 0 before an A-MPDU subframe with a nonzero MPDU Length field. In an HE
54
55 PPDU, a STA should add an A-MPDU subframe with the EOF/Tag field set to 1 and with the MPDU
56 Length field set to 0 (i.e., EOF padding) immediately after the last A-MPDU subframe with a nonzero
57 MPDU Length field.
58
59
60 26.6.2.2 A-MPDU padding in an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU and HE MU PPDU
61
62
An HE STA that transmits a DL HE MU PPDU that contains one or more PSDUs, each of which carries an
63
A-MPDU, shall construct the A-MPDUs as described in 10.12.6 (A-MPDU padding for VHT PPDU) except
64
65 that one or more A-MPDU subframes with a nonzero MPDU Length field and with an EOF/Tag field equal
398 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 399 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 to 1 may be added if the A-MPDU is an ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU (see 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU
2 and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU)).
3
4
5 An HE STA that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or UL HE MU PPDU that contains one A-
6
MPDU shall construct the A-MPDU as described in 10.12.6 (A-MPDU padding for VHT PPDU) except that
7
one or more A-MPDU subframes with a nonzero MPDU Length field and with an EOF/Tag field equal to 1
8
9 may be added if the A-MPDU is an ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU (see 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-MPDU and
10 ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU)).
11
12
13 26.6.2.3 A-MPDU padding in an HE TB PPDU
14
15
16 A non-AP STA that transmits an HE TB PPDU shall construct the PSDU carried in the HE TB PPDU as
17 described in this subclause.
18
19
20 The STA calculates the PSDU_LENGTH as defined in 6.5 (PLME SAP interface) and initializes A-
21 MPDU_Length to 0.
22
23
24 The STA may add A-MPDU subframes to the A-MPDU contained in the PSDU provided that the following
25 constraints are fulfilled:
26
27 — The A-MPDU content constraints (see 10.12.1 (A-MPDU contents) and 26.6.3 (Multi-TID A-
28
MPDU and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU)) for the intended recipient
29
30
— The Length limit constraints (see 9.7.1 (A-MPDU format) and 10.12.2 (A-MPDU length limit rules))
31
for the intended recipient
32
33
— The MPDU start spacing constraints (see 10.12.3 (Minimum MPDU Start Spacing field)) for the
34
35 intended recipient
36
37
provided that the following conditions are met:
38
39
40 — The A-MPDU subframes have a value greater than 0 in the MPDU Length field, or have a value 0 in
41 the MPDU Length field and a value 0 in the EOF/Tag field.
42
43 — After incrementing the A-MPDU_Length with the length of each such added A-MPDU subframe,
44 the relationship A-MPDU_Length ≤ PSDU_LENGTH is true.
45
46
47 Padding is then added such that the resulting A-MPDU contains exactly PSDU_LENGTH octets as follows:
48
49 — First, while A-MPDU_Length < PSDU_LENGTH and A-MPDU_Length mod 4 ≠ 0, add an octet to
50 the final A-MPDU subframe’s Padding subfield and increment A-MPDU_Length by 1.
51
52 — Then, while A-MPDU_Length + 4 ≤ PSDU_LENGTH, add an EOF padding subframe to the EOF
53
Padding Subframes field and increment A-MPDU_Length by 4.
54
55
— Finally, while A-MPDU_Length < PSDU_LENGTH, add an octet to the EOF Padding Octets sub-
56
field and increment A-MPDU_Length by 1.
57
58
59
An A-MPDU pre-EOF padding is constructed from each user from any of the following:
60
61
— A-MPDU subframes constructed from the MPDUs available for transmission from any AC that is
62
63 selected by the STA
64
65 — A-MPDU subframes with 0 in the MPDU Length field and 0 in the EOF/Tag field
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 399
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

1 26.6.3 Multi-TID A-MPDU and ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU
2
3
26.6.3.1 General
4
5
6 A non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU that does not contain a tagged MPDU but does con-
7
tain untagged MPDUs from at least two TIDs (see Table9-534c (A-MPDU contents in the HE non-ack-
8
enabled multi-TID immediate response context)).
9
10
11 An ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU that contains at least one tagged MPDU that solicits
12
acknowledgment and one or more MPDUs from at least one TID (see Table9-534d (A-MPDU contents in
13
the HE ack-enabled multi-TID immediate response context)).
14
15
16
An ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU that contains one tagged MPDU that solicits acknowl-
17
edgment and one or more untagged MPDUs that do not solicit an immediate response (see Table9-534b (A-
18
19 MPDU contents in the HE ack-enabled single-TID immediate response context)).
20
21
An HE STA with dot11HEAMPDUwithMultipleTIDOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the Multi-
22
TID Aggregation Rx Support subfield to a nonzero value in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in
23
24 the HE Capabilities element it transmits. An HE STA with dot11HEAMPDUwithMultipleTIDOptionImple-
25 mented equal to false shall set the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support subfield to 0.
26
27
An HE STA with dot11AckEnabledAMPDUOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the Ack-Enabled
28
29 Aggregation Support subfield to 1 in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities ele-
30 ment it transmits. An HE STA with dot11AckEnabledAMPDUOptionImplemented equal to false shall set
31 the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support subfield to 0.
32
33
34 A multi-TID A-MPDU is either a non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU or an ack-enabled multi-TID A-
35 MPDU. A first HE STA may transmit a non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU to a second HE STA if the
36 first HE STA has received from the second STA an HE Capabilities element where the Multi-TID Aggrega-
37
tion Rx Support subfield is nonzero. A first HE STA may transmit an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU or a
38
non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU to a second HE STA if the first HE STA has received from the second
39
40 HE STA an HE Capabilities element where the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support subfield is nonzero and
41 where the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support subfield is 1. Otherwise the first HE STA shall not transmit a
42 multi-TID A-MPDU to the second HE STA.
43
44
45 An HE STA shall not transmit a multi-TID A-MPDU or ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU in a VHT PPDU
46 or a HT PPDU.
47
48
49 A non-AP STA shall not send a non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU in an HE TB PPDU unless it is in
50 response to a Basic Trigger frame where the TID Aggregation Limit field of the User Info field addressed to
51 the STA is greater than 1. A non-AP STA shall not send an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU in an HE TB
52
PPDU unless it is in response to a Basic Trigger frame where the TID Aggregation Limit field of the User
53
Info field addressed to the STA is greater than 0.
54
55
NOTE—An ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU solicited by a Basic Trigger frame with TID Aggregation Limit field
56
equal to 1 can contain one Management frame that solicits acknowledgment and one or more QoS Data frames from the
57
same TID if the AP supports reception of ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDUs.
58
59
60 A multi-TID A-MPDU shall not be transmitted in an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU,
61 unless the TXOP limit is greater than 0 for the AC that is used to gain access to the medium. The AC used to
62
gain access to the medium is the primary AC (see 10.23.2.9 (TXOP limits)). If the TXOP limit of the pri-
63
mary AC is greater than 0, then the STA may aggregate QoS Data frames from one or more TIDs in the A-
64
65 MPDU under the following conditions:

1 — The A-MPDU shall be carried in either an HE SU PPDU or an HE ER SU PPDU transmitted by the
2 STA within the obtained TXOP or an HE MU PPDU transmitted by a non-AP STA within the
3
obtained TXOP
4
5 — The A-MPDU shall contain one or more MPDUs with any of the TIDs that correspond to the primary
6 AC
7
— If no more MPDUs can be aggregated in the A-MPDU from any of the TIDs that correspond to the
8
9 primary AC then the A-MPDU may additionally contain one or more MPDUs with TIDs that do not
10 correspond to the primary AC if the TIDs correspond to any AC that has a higher priority with
11 respect to the primary AC and the addition of these MPDUs does not cause the STA to exceed the
12

13
14
15 An HE AP may aggregate MPDUs from any TIDs in a multi-TID A-MPDU for DL HE MU PPDU transmis-
16 sion and the number of TIDs in the multi-TID A-MPDU shall not be more than the Multi-TID Aggregation
17 Rx Support announced by the recipient.
18
19
The Multi-STA BlockAck frame is used to acknowledge the MPDUs in a multi-TID A-MPDU as defined in
20
21 26.4 (HE acknowledgment procedure).
22
23 In a multi-TID A-MPDU, MPDUs with the same TID are not necessarily contiguous.
24
25
A non-AP HE STA that transmits a multi-TID A-MPDU in an HE TB PPDU should aggregate QoS Data
26
27 frames in the following order:
28
— First, any and all MPDUs that correspond to the Preferred AC subfield of the Trigger Dependent
29
User Info field addressed to the STA in the Trigger frame.
30
31 — Then, any and all MPDUs that correspond to any AC that has a higher priority.
32
— Then, any MPDUs that correspond to any AC that has a lower priority.
33
34
35 The aggregation of the QoS Data frames is subject to:
36
— The limit indicated in the TID Aggregation Limit subfield in the Trigger Dependent User Info field
37
addressed to the STA in the Trigger frame.
38
39 — The restrictions in Table26-2 (Relation between TID Aggregation Limit field, Multi-TID Aggrega-
40
tion Tx Support field and solicited immediate response).
41
42 — The duration indicated in the UL Length subfield in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame.
43
44 NOTE 1—These rules permit a STA to not aggregate any MPDUs from the preferred AC or a higher priority AC, and
45 permit a STA to aggregate MPDUs in any order.
46
NOTE 2—A multi-TID A-MPDU allows the aggregation of a Management frame regardless of the value indicated in
47
the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities
48
element as long as the indicated in the value of the TID Aggregation Limit subfield in the Trigger Dependent User Info
49
field of a the Basic Trigger frame is nonzero.
50
51
An non-AP HE STA that transmits a single-TID A-MPDU in an HE TB PPDU should select the TID in the
52
53 following order:
54 — A TID that corresponds to the Preferred AC subfield of the Trigger Dependent User Info field
55
addressed to the STA in the Trigger frame, if there is a corresponding MPDU for that TID.
56
57 — Otherwise, a TID that corresponds to any AC that has a higher priority, if there is a corresponding
58 MPDU for that TID.
59
— Otherwise, a TID that corresponds to any AC that has a lower priority.
60
61
62 The TID selection is subject to:
63
— The limit indicated in the TID Aggregation Limit subfield in the Trigger Dependent User Info field
64
65 addressed to the STA in the Trigger frame.

1 — The duration indicated in the UL Length subfield in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame.
2
3 NOTE—These rules permit a STA to not select MPDUs from the preferred AC or a higher priority AC.
4
5 26.6.3.2 Ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU operation
6
7
An ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU with contents defined in Table9-534b (A-MPDU con-
8
9 tents in the HE ack-enabled single-TID immediate response context).
10
NOTE—An ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU does not contain more than one of the following frames: QoS Data
11
frames, Management frame that solicits acknowledgment. In this case the Management frame that solicits acknowledg-
12
ment is treated as if it were a QoS Data frame with TID equal to 15.
13
14
15 An HE STA shall not transmit an ack-enabled single-TID A-MPDU to a recipient STA unless it has received
16 from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element with the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support subfield
17 equal to 1.
18
19
26.6.3.3 Non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU operation
20
21
22 A non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU with contents defined in Table9-534c (A-MPDU
23
contents in the HE non-ack-enabled multi-TID immediate response context).
24
25 NOTE—A non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU might include other frames such as a Trigger frame, BlockAck frame,
26 or QoS Null frame (see Table9-531 (A-MPDU contents in the non-HE data enabled immediate response context))
27
28
An HE STA shall not transmit a non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU to a recipient STA unless it has
29
received from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element where the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support
30
31 subfield is nonzero.
32
33 A STA that receives a non-ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU responds as defined in 26.4.4 (Per-PPDU
34
acknowledgment selection rules).
35
36
37 26.6.3.4 Ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU operation
38
39
An ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU is an A-MPDU with contents defined in Table9-534d (A-MPDU con-
40
tents in the HE ack-enabled multi-TID immediate response context).
41
42
43 QoS Data frames with the same TID shall have the same ack policy.
44
45
QoS Data frames with the same TID shall be carried in A-MPDU subframes with the same value in the EOF/
46
Tag field setting.
47
48
49 In an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU, the EOF/Tag field in the MPDU delimiter of each A-MPDU sub-
50 frame carrying a frame that solicits an Ack frame as defined in Table9-534d (A-MPDU contents in the HE
51
ack-enabled multi-TID immediate response context) shall be set to 1. The EOF/Tag field in all other A-
52
MPDU subframes in the A-MPDU that carry frames shall be set to 0.
53
54
55 An HE STA shall not transmit an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU to a recipient STA unless it has received
56
from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element where the Multi-TID Aggregation Rx Support subfield
57
is nonzero and the Ack-Enabled Aggregation Support subfield is 1.
58
59
60 A STA that receives an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU responds as defined in 26.4.4 (Per-PPDU
61 acknowledgment selection rules).
62
63
A STA that transmits an ack-enabled multi-TID A-MPDU that contains at least two tagged MPDUs shall
64
65 ignore the immediate response if it is an Ack frame.

## 第 403 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.7 HE sounding protocol
2
3
26.7.1 General
4
5
6 Transmit beamforming and DL MU-MIMO require knowledge of the channel state to compute a steering
7 matrix that is applied to the transmit signal to optimize reception at one or more receivers. HE STAs use the
8
HE sounding protocol to determine the channel state information. The HE sounding protocol provides
9
explicit feedback mechanisms, defined as HE non-trigger-based (non-TB) sounding and HE trigger-based
10
11 (TB) sounding, where the HE beamformee measures the channel using a training signal (i.e., an HE sound-
12 ing NDP) transmitted by the HE beamformer and sends back a transformed estimate of the channel state.
13 The HE beamformer uses this estimate to derive the steering matrix.
14
15
16 The HE beamformee returns an estimate of the channel state in an HE compressed beamforming/CQI report
17 carried in one or more HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frames. There are three types of HE compressed
18 beamforming/CQI report:
19
20 — SU feedback: The HE compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an HE Compressed Beam-
21 forming Report field
22
— MU feedback: The HE compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an HE Compressed Beam-
23
forming Report field and HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report field
24
25 — CQI feedback: The HE compressed beamforming/CQI report consists of an HE CQI Report field
26
27 NOTE—Use of HE TB sounding does not necessarily imply MU feedback. HE TB sounding is also used to obtain SU
28 feedback and CQI feedback.
29
30
The HE compressed beamforming/CQI report is carried in a single HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI
31
frame if the resulting frame is less than or equal to 11454 octets in length (see 26.7.3 (Rules for HE sound-
32
33 ing protocol sequences)). Otherwise, the HE beamforming feedback is segmented and each segment is car-
34 ried in an HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame.
35
36
An HE beamformer shall support a maximum MPDU length for the HE compressed beamforming/CQI
37
38 report that is the minimum of 11454 octets and the maximum length of the HE compressed beamforming/
39 CQI report that the HE beamformer intends to solicit from its HE beamformees.
40
41
26.7.2 HE sounding protocol
42
43
44 An SU beamformer is an HE STA that sets the SU Beamformer subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Infor-
45 mation field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1.
46
47
An SU beamformee is an HE STA that sets the SU Beamformee subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Infor-
48
49 mation field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1. A non-AP HE STA shall set the SU Beamfor-
50 mee subfield to 1. An HE AP may set the SU Beamformee subfield to 1.
51
52
An MU beamformer is an HE AP that sets the MU Beamformer subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Infor-
53
mation field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1. An HE AP that indicates support for 4 or more
54
55 space-time streams in the Tx HE-MCS Map ≤80MHz subfield in the Supported HE-MCS And NSS field in
56 the HE Capabilities element shall set the MU Beamformer subfield to 1. A non-AP HE STA shall set the
57 MU Beamformer subfield to 0. An MU beamformer is also an SU beamformer and shall set the SU Beam-
58
former subfield to 1.
59
60
NOTE—A non-AP STA might use the setting of the MU Beamformer subfield to determine the AP with which it will
61
associate.
62
63
A non-AP HE STA shall support operation as an MU beamformee. An HE AP does not support operation as
64
65 an MU beamformee.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 403
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 404 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 The term HE beamformer refers to both the SU beamformer and MU beamformer. The term HE beamfor-
2 mee refers to both the SU beamformee and MU beamformee.
3
4
The type of feedback (SU, MU or CQI) solicited by an HE beamformer from an HE beamformee is indi-
5
6 cated in the Feedback Type And Ng and Codebook subfields in the STA Info field identifying the HE beam-
7 formee in the HE NDP Announcement frame as defined in Table9-29a (Feedback Type And Ng subfield
8 and Codebook Size subfield encoding for HE TB sounding) and Table9-29b (Feedback Type And Ng sub-
9
field and Codebook Size subfield encoding for HE non-TB sounding).
10
11
12 The bandwidth (partial or full) of the feedback solicited by an HE beamformer from an HE beamformee
13 depends on the Partial BW Info subfield in the STA Info field identifying the HE beamformee in the HE
14 NDP Announcement frame, the bandwidth of the HE NDP Announcement frame and the value of the Disal-
15
lowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield, if present. Full bandwidth feedback is solicited if the RU Start Index
16
subfield in the Partial BW Info subfield is 0, the Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield is absent or con-
17
18 tains all zeroes and the RU End Index subfield in the Partial BW subfield is the value shown in Table26-4
19 (Settings for BW, RU Start Index, and RU End Index fields in HE NDP Announcement frame) where partial
20 bandwidth is not supported by the HE beamformer, for the bandwidth of the HE NDP Announcement frame.
21
22
Other settings of the Partial BW Info subfield solicit partial bandwidth feedback. Punctured sounding is
23
24 indicated by the inclusion of a nonzero Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield in the HE NDP Announce-
25 ment frame and in such a case, the disallowed subchannels are applied to the tone information to be included
26 in the feedback after selecting tones for feedback based on the RU Start Index and RU End Index subfield
27
values and HE NDP Announcement frame bandwidth as described above. See Table26-4 (Settings for BW,
28
RU Start Index, and RU End Index fields in HE NDP Announcement frame).
29
30
31 The RU Start Index and RU End Index subfields shall cover at least one allowed subchannel.
32
33
An SU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth SU feedback from an SU beamformee in an HE non-TB
34
35 sounding sequence. An SU beamformer shall not solicit partial bandwidth SU feedback in an HE non-TB
36 sounding sequence. An SU beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth or full bandwidth SU feedback from
37 an SU beamformee in an HE TB sounding sequence if the SU beamformee indicates support by setting the
38
Triggered SU Beamforming Feedback subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE
39
Capabilities element it transmits to 1.
40
41
42 An MU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth MU feedback from an MU beamformee in an HE TB sound-
43 ing sequence. An MU beamformer may solicit partial bandwidth MU feedback from an MU beamformee in
44
an HE TB sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the Triggered MU Beam-
45
forming Partial BW Feedback subfield to 1. An MU beamformer shall not solicit MU feedback in an HE
46
47 non-TB sounding sequence.
48
49 An MU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth or partial bandwidth CQI feedback from an MU beamfor-
50
mee in an HE TB sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the Triggered CQI
51
Beamforming Feedback subfield to 1.
52
53
54 An MU beamformer may solicit full bandwidth CQI feedback from an MU beamformee in an HE non-TB
55 sounding sequence if the MU beamformee indicates support by setting the Non-Triggered CQI Beamform-
56
ing Feedback subfield to 1.
57
58
59 An HE beamformer may solicit punctured SU feedback from an HE beamformee in an HE TB sounding
60 sequence if the HE beamformee indicates support for punctured sounding by setting the Punctured Sounding
61 Support subfield in the HE Capabilities elements it transmits to 1. An HE beamformer shall indicate punc-
62
tured subchannels in the HE sounding NDP of an HE TB sounding sequence by setting the appropriate bits
63
of the Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield of the STA Info field with the AID11 subfield set to 2047 in
64
65 the preceding HE NDP Announcement frame. An SU beamformer that indicates punctured subchannels in

26.7.3 Rules for HE sounding protocol sequences
21
22
23 An HE non-TB sounding sequence is initiated by an HE beamformer with an individually addressed HE
24 NDP Announcement frame comprising exactly one STA Info field, followed after SIFS by an HE sounding
25
NDP. The HE beamformee responds after SIFS with an HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame.
26
27
28 The AID11 subfield of the STA Info field shall be set to the AID of the STA identified by the RA field of the
29 HE NDP Announcement frame, or to 0 if the STA identified by the RA field is a mesh STA, AP or IBSS
30 STA.
31
32
An example of an HE non-TB sounding sequence with a single HE beamformee is shown in Figure26-7
33
34 (An example of HE non-TB sounding).
35
36
37
38 HE beamformer HE NDP HE sounding
39 Announcement SIFS NDP SIFS
40
HE Compressed
41 HE beamformee Beamforming/CQI
42
43
44 Figure 26-7—An example of HE non-TB sounding
45
46
An HE beamformer that initiates the HE non-TB sounding sequence shall transmit the HE NDP Announce-
47
48 ment frame with a single STA Info field, the STA Info field having a value in the AID11 field other than
49 2047 and with the AID11 field in that STA Info field set to the AID of the STA identified by the RA field or
50 to 0 if the STA identified by the RA field is a mesh STA, AP or IBSS member STA.
51
52
An HE beamformer may initiate an HE non-TB sounding sequence with an HE beamformee to solicit SU
53
54 feedback over full bandwidth.
55
56
An HE beamformer shall not initiate an HE non-TB sounding sequence with an HE NDP Announcement
57
frame that has a Partial BW Info subfield that indicates less than full bandwidth (see Table26-4 (Settings for
58
59 BW, RU Start Index, and RU End Index fields in HE NDP Announcement frame)).
60
61 An HE TB sounding sequence is initiated by an HE beamformer with a broadcast HE NDP Announcement
62
frame with two or more STA Info fields, followed after a SIFS by an HE sounding NDP followed after a
63
SIFS by a BFRP Trigger frame. Each HE beamformee responds after a SIFS with an HE Compressed Beam-
64
65 forming/CQI frame.

27

30 An HE beamformer may initiate an HE TB sounding sequence to solicit MU feedback over full bandwidth.
31
32
33 An HE beamformer may initiate an HE TB sounding sequence to solicit a feedback variant only if the feed-
34 back variant is computed based on parameters supported by the HE beamformee; otherwise the HE beam-
35 former shall not solicit a feedback variant computed based on parameters not supported by the HE
36
beamformee (see 26.7.2 (HE sounding protocol)).

39 An HE AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true shall not send an HE NDP Announcement
40 frame with the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID to a non-AP STA that is associated with an AP corre-
41
sponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in the multiple BSSID set unless the AP has received from the non-AP
42
STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx Control Frame To MultiBSS subfield in the HE MAC Capabil-
43
44 ities Information field equal to 1. An AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true shall not send an
45 individually addressed HE NDP Announcement frame with the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID to a
46 non-AP STA associated with an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID.
47
48
49 An AP that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame identifying HE STAs shall set the TA field of the
50 frame to the MAC address of the AP, unless dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented is true and the HE NDP
51 Announcement frame identifies STAs from at least two different BSSs of the multiple BSSID set, in which
52
case, the AP shall set the TA field of the frame to the transmitted BSSID. If the HE NDP Announcement
53
frame is transmitted in a non-HT duplicate PPDU then the TA field of the HE NDP Announcement frame is
54
55 a bandwidth signaling TA (see 10.6.6.6 (Channel Width selection for Control frames)).
56
57
An HE beamformer that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame to an HE beamformee that is an AP,
58
TDLS peer STA, mesh STA or IBSS STA, shall include one STA Info field in the HE NDP Announcement
59
60 frame and shall set the AID11 field in the STA Info field of the frame to 0.
61
62
An HE beamformer that is an AP and that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame to one or more HE
63
beamformees shall set the AID11 field in the STA Info field identifying a non-AP STA to the 11 LSBs of the
64
65 AID of the non-AP STA.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE NDP Announcement frame shall not include multiple STA Info fields that have the same value in the
2 AID11 subfield.
3
4
5 An HE beamformer that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame initiating an HE TB sounding sequence
6 may include a STA Info field with an AID11 subfield value of 2047 to indicate disallowed subchannels
7 during punctured channel operation. When present, the STA Info field with AID11 value of 2047 shall be
8
the first STA Info field in the frame. An HE beamformer that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame
9
shall not include more than one STA Info field with an AID11 subfield value of 2047. An HE beamformer
10
11 that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame initiating a HE non-TB sounding sequence shall not include
12 a STA Info field with an AID11 subfield value of 2047.
13
14
In an HE TB sounding sequence, a STA Info field in the HE NDP Announcement frame that solicits SU or
15
16 MU feedback indicates the subcarrier grouping, Ng, codebook size and the number of columns, Nc, to be
17 used by the HE beamformee identified by the STA Info field for the generation of the SU or MU feedback.
18
19
In an HE non-TB sounding sequence where the STA Info field in the HE NDP Announcement frame solicits
20
SU feedback, the subcarrier grouping, Ng, codebook size and the number of columns, Nc, used for the gen-
21
22 eration of the SU feedback are determined by the HE beamformee.
23
24
In an HE TB sounding sequence, a STA Info field in the HE NDP Announcement frame that solicits CQI
25
feedback indicates the Nc to be used by the HE beamformee identified by the STA Info field for the genera-
26
27 tion of the CQI feedback.
28
29
In an HE non-TB sounding sequence where the STA Info field in the HE NDP Announcement frame solicits
30
CQI feedback, the Nc used for the generation of the CQI feedback is determined by the HE beamformee.
31
32
33 An HE beamformer that has initiated an HE TB sounding sequence may send another BFRP Trigger frame
34
in the same TXOP as shown in Figure26-8 (An example of HE TB sounding). The HE beamformer uses the
35
additional BFRP Trigger frames to solicit HE compressed beamforming/CQI reports from HE beamformees
36
37 not addressed in a previous BFRP Trigger frame or to solicit retransmission of an HE compressed beam-
38 forming/CQI report. An HE beamformer shall not transmit a BFRP Trigger frame that identifies a STA iden-
39 tified in the HE NDP Announcement frame of an HE TB sounding sequence unless it is in the same TXOP
40
as the HE TB sounding sequence.
41
42
43 An HE beamformer that transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame as part of an HE TB sounding
44 sequence shall set the Nc subfield of the STA Info field to indicate a value less than or equal to the minimum
45 of:
46
47 — The maximum number of supported spatial streams according to the corresponding HE beamfor-
48 mee’s Rx HE-MCS Map ≤80MHz, Rx HE-MCS Map 160MHz, and Rx HE-MCS Map
49
80+80MHz subfields in the Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field in the HE Capabilities element
50
sent by the HE beamformee.
51
52 — The maximum number of supported spatial streams according to the Rx NSS subfield value in the
53
most recently received Operating Mode Notification frame, Operating Mode Notification element
54
with the Rx NSS Type subfield equal to 0, or OM Control subfield sent by the corresponding HE
55
56 beamformee (see 26.9 (Operating mode indication)).
57
— The maximum Nc indicated by the Max Nc subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field in
58
the HE Capabilities element sent by the HE beamformee.
59
60

62
End Index subfields in a STA Info field to indicate the starting 26-tone RU and the ending 26-tone RU,
63
respectively, of the solicited HE compressed beamforming/CQI report (see 9.3.1.19 (VHT/HE NDP
64
65 Announcement frame format)). For preamble punctured sounding, the RU Start Index and RU End Index

21 In an HE non-TB sounding sequence, an HE beamformer shall solicit full bandwidth feedback. In an HE TB
22

sounding protocol)).

An HE beamformee that receives an HE NDP Announcement frame soliciting SU feedback as part of an HE
52

53
54 with Nc in the range 1 to 8, Ng=4 or Ng=16, and codebook size (ϕ, ψ) = {4, 2} or (ϕ, ψ) = {6, 4}. The HE

59 HE non-TB sounding sequence shall generate an HE compressed beamforming/CQI report for CQI feed-

61
62
An HE beamformee that receives an HE NDP Announcement frame soliciting CQI feedback as part of an
63
HE TB sounding sequence shall generate an HE compressed beamforming/CQI report for CQI feedback
64
65 with Nc determined by the HE beamformer.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE beamformee that receives an HE NDP Announcement frame from an HE beamformer with which it
2 is associated and that contains the HE beamformee’s MAC address in the RA field (indicating a non-TB
3
sounding sequence) and also receives an HE sounding NDP a SIFS after the HE NDP Announcement frame
4
shall transmit its HE compressed beamforming/CQI report a SIFS after the HE sounding NDP. The
5
6 TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH for the PPDU containing the HE compressed beamforming/
7 CQI report shall be set to indicate a bandwidth not wider than that indicated by the RXVECTOR parameter
8 CH_BANDWIDTH of the HE sounding NDP.
9
10
An HE beamformee that receives an HE NDP Announcement frame as part of an HE TB sounding sequence
11
12 with a STA Info field identifying it soliciting SU or MU feedback shall generate an HE compressed beam-
13 forming/CQI report using the feedback type, Ng, codebook size, and Nc indicated in the STA Info field. If
14 the HE beamformee then receives a BFRP Trigger frame with a matching STA Info field, the HE beamfor-
15
mee transmits an HE TB PPDU containing the HE compressed beamforming/CQI report following the rules
16
defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation). If the HE NDP Announcement frame has
17
18 the TA field set to the transmitted BSSID, and the HE beamformee is a non-AP STA associated with an AP
19 corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID that supports receiving Control frames with TA field set to the
20 transmitted BSSID, then the HE compressed beamforming/CQI report sent in response shall have the RA
21
field set to as defined in 26.5.2.3.5 (RA field for frames carried in an HE TB PPDU).
22
23
NOTE—A non-AP HE beamformee that transmits an OM Control subfield with the UL MU Disable field set to 1 does
24
not respond to BFRP Trigger frames (see 26.9 (Operating mode indication)).
25
26
An HE beamformee that is a non-AP STA that transmits an HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI Report shall
27
28 set the RU Start Index and RU End Index subfields of the HE MIMO Control field to indicate the range of
29 tones for which compressed beamforming/CQI information is provided. If the HE NDP Announcement
30 frame that solicited the feedback includes a Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap field with a nonzero value, then
31
a beamformee that indicates support for punctured sounding by setting the Punctured Sounding Support sub-
32
field in the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits to 1 shall include a Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap
33
34 subfield in the solicited feedback with the same value as the Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield of the
35 HE NDP Announcement frame that solicited the feedback to indicate subcarriers for which feedback infor-
36 mation is not provided from within the range of subcarriers indicated by the RU Start Index and RU End
37
Index subfields.
38
39
40 An HE beamformee that transmits HE compressed beamforming feedback shall include neither the HE
41 Compressed Beamforming Report information nor the HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information
42
if the transmission duration of the PPDU carrying the HE Compressed Beamforming Report information
43
and any HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information would exceed the maximum PPDU duration.
44
45
46 The Sounding Dialog Token Number field in the HE MIMO Control field shall be set to the same value as
47 the Sounding Dialog Token Number field in the corresponding HE NDP Announcement frame.
48
49
An HE beamformer that sends a BFRP Trigger frame shall set the Feedback Segment Retransmission Bit-
50
51 map fields of the BFRP Trigger frame to all 1s except when the HE beamformer intends to solicit the
52 retransmission of segmented feedback as defined in 26.7.4 (Rules for generating segmented feedback).
53
54 NOTE—The BFRP Trigger frame contains one or more User Info fields, each of which identifies an HE beamformee.
55
56 The SNR per subcarrier computation is recommended to be done on at least 4 subcarriers in a 26-tone RU.

59 26.7.4 Rules for generating segmented feedback
60
61 If the HE compressed beamforming/CQI report solicited by the HE beamformer would result in an HE Com-
62
pressed Beamforming/CQI frame that exceeds 11454 octets in length, then the HE compressed beamform-
63
ing/CQI report shall be split into up to 8 feedback segments. Each feedback segment shall be included in a
64
65 separate HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame and shall contain successive portions of the HE com-
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 411
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 412 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 pressed beamforming/CQI report. Each feedback segment shall be of equal length except the last feedback
2 segment that may be smaller. Each HE Compressed Beamforming/CQI frame that includes a feedback seg-
3
ment that is not the last feedback segment shall have a length of 11454 octets. Each feedback segment is
4
identified by the value of the Remaining Feedback Segments subfield and the First Feedback Segment sub-
5
6 field in the HE MIMO Control field as defined in 9.4.1.64 (HE MIMO Control field); the other nonreserved
7 subfields of the HE MIMO Control field shall be the same for all feedback segments. All feedback segments
8 shall be sent in a single A-MPDU contained in a PPDU and shall be included in the A-MPDU in the
9
descending order of the Remaining Feedback Segments subfield values.
10
11
12 An HE beamformer that sends a BFRP Trigger frame, in its first attempt to retrieve an HE compressed
13 beamforming/CQI report from an HE beamformee, shall solicit all possible feedback segments by setting all
14
of the bits in the Feedback Segment Retransmission Bitmap subfield to 1 in the User Info field identifying
15
the HE beamformee.
16
17
18 An HE beamformer that fails to receive some or all of the feedback segments of the HE compressed beam-
19 forming/CQI report from the HE beamformee, may solicit the selective retransmission of missing feedback
20
segments by sending a BFRP Trigger frame that indicates in the Feedback Segment Retransmission Bitmap
21
subfield of the User Info field identifying the HE beamformee the list of feedback segments solicited for
22
23 retransmission (see 9.3.1.22.3 (BFRP Trigger frame format)).
24
25 NOTE—In an HE non-TB sounding sequence, if the HE beamformer does not receive all feedback segments from the
26 HE beamformee, the HE beamformer cannot use a BFRP Trigger frame to request retransmission of the feedback seg-
27 ments. In this case the HE beamformee can only repeat the entire non-TB sounding sequence.
28
29 An HE beamformer that fails to receive the first feedback segment (identified by the First Feedback Seg-
30
ment field set to 1), may solicit the selective retransmission of the missing feedback segments assuming the
31
HE compressed beamforming/CQI report is split into 8 feedback segments. The HE beamformer may also
32
33 solicit the retransmission of all feedback segments by setting all of the bits in the Feedback Segment
34 Retransmission Bitmap subfield to 1 in the User Info field identifying the HE beamformee.
35
36
An HE beamformee that transmits an HE compressed beamforming/CQI report including the HE Com-
37
38 pressed Beamforming Report information and any HE MU Exclusive Beamforming Report information in
39 response to a BFRP Trigger frame shall either transmit only the feedback segments indicated in the Feed-
40 back Segment Retransmission Bitmap field in the User Info field of the BFRP Trigger frame identifying the
41
HE beamformee or transmit all the feedback segments available at the HE beamformee, excluding the feed-
42
back segments that do not exist at the HE beamformee.
43
44
NOTE—If an HE beamformer solicits the missing feedback segments from a beamformee and does not receive a
45
response from the beamformee, the HE beamformer might either initiate an HE TB sounding sequence or transmit an
46
additional BFRP Trigger frame to the HE beamformee.
47
48

50
51
The TXVECTOR parameters for an HE sounding NDP shall be set as follows:
52
53 — FORMAT is set to HE_SU
54

58 — If HE_LTF_TYPE is 2xHE-LTF, then GI_TYPE is set to either 0u8s_GI or 1u6s_GI
59
— If HE_LTF_TYPE is 4xHE-LTF, then GI_TYPE is set to 3u2s_GI
60
61 — NUM_STS indicates two or more space-time streams if the Feedback Type field in the HE MIMO
62
Control field of the preceding HE NDP Announcement frame indicates either SU or MU, or one or
63
more space-time streams if the Feedback Type field in the HE MIMO Control field of the preceding
64
65 HE NDP Announcement frame indicates CQI. See below for additional constraints on NUM_STS.

26.8 TWT operation
41
42
43 26.8.1 General
44
45
Target wake time (TWT) allows an AP to manage activity in the BSS in order to minimize contention
46
47 between STAs and to reduce the required amount of time that a STA utilizing a power management mode
48 needs to be awake. This is achieved by allocating STAs to operate at nonoverlapping times and/or frequen-
49 cies, and concentrate the frame exchanges in predefined service periods.
50
51
An HE STA negotiates individual TWT agreements, as defined in 10.47 (Target wake time (TWT)), subject
52
53 to the additional rules and restrictions that are defined in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements) and 26.8.7
54 (HE subchannel selective transmission). A non-AP HE STA establishes membership in broadcast TWT
55 schedules, as defined in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation), which are used as defined in 26.8.3.3 (Rules for
56
TWT scheduled STA), 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT), and 26.14.3 (Opportunistic power
57
save). An HE AP delivers broadcast TWT parameter sets to non-AP HE STAs as described in 26.8.3.2
58
59 (Rules for TWT scheduling AP), 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT) and 26.14.3 (Opportunistic
60 power save).
61
62
A STA does not need to be aware of the values of TWT parameters of the TWT agreements of other STAs in
63
the BSS of the STA or of TWT agreements of STAs in other BSSs. A STA does not need to be aware that a
64
65 TWT service period (SP) is used to exchange frames with other STAs. Frames transmitted during a TWT SP
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 413
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 414 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 are carried in any PPDU format supported by the pair of STAs that have established the TWT agreement
2 corresponding to that TWT SP, including HE MU PPDU, HE TB PPDU, etc.
3
4
5 An HE STA with dot11TWTOptionImplemented equal to true shall set:
6
— The TWT Requester Support subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities element that it transmits if it sup-
7
8 ports operating in the role of a TWT requesting STA; otherwise set to 0.
9
— The TWT Responder Support subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits if it sup-
10
ports operating in the role of a TWT responding STA; otherwise set to 0.
11
12
— The Broadcast TWT Support subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities element that it transmits if it sup-
13
ports operating in the role of a TWT scheduled STA or in the role of a TWT scheduling AP; other-
14
15 wise set to 0.
16
17
An HE AP shall set the TWT Responder Support subfield of the Extended Capabilities element and HE
18
Capabilities element to 1.
19
20
21 An HE AP may request TWT participation by all associated STAs that have declared support for TWT. A
22
non-AP STA declares support for the role of TWT requesting STA by setting the TWT Requester Support
23
subfield in the Extended Capabilities element or in the HE Capabilities element to 1 and declares support for
24
25 the role of TWT scheduled STA by setting the Broadcast TWT Support subfield in the HE Capabilities ele-
26 ment to 1. The HE AP makes the request for TWT participation by setting the TWT Required subfield to 1
27 in HE Operation elements it transmits. A STA that supports TWT and that has received an HE Operation
28
element with the TWT Required subfield equal to 1 from the HE AP with which it is associated shall either
29
negotiate individual TWT agreements, as defined in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements), or participate in
30
31 broadcast TWT operation, as defined in 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation).
32
33 NOTE—The AP sets the TWT Required subfield to 1 if it is not available outside TWT SPs (see 26.8.2 (Individual TWT
34 agreements) and 26.8.3 (Broadcast TWT operation)). The AP might not be available outside TWT SPs if it sets the
35 Responder PM Mode subfield to 1 (see 10.47.7 (TWT Sleep Setup)).
36
37 26.8.2 Individual TWT agreements
38
39
40 An HE STA may negotiate individual TWT agreements with another HE STA as defined in 10.47.1 (TWT
41 overview), except that the STA:
42
43 — May set the Responder PM Mode subfield to 1 if it is a TWT responding STA that intends to go to
44 doze state outside of TWT SPs.
45
• If the TWT responding STA is an AP then it may set the Responder PM Mode subfield to 1 only
46
47 if all non-AP STAs that are associated to it indicate support of TWT and the AP has set the TWT
48 Required subfield to 1 in the HE Operation element it transmits; otherwise it shall set the
49 Responder PM Mode subfield to 0.
50
51 • An AP that sets the Responder PM Mode subfield to 1 follows the rules defined in 10.47.7 (TWT
52 Sleep Setup).
53
— Shall set the Implicit subfield to 1 and the NDP Paging Indicator subfield to 0 in all TWT elements
54
55 that it transmits during the TWT setup.
56
— May set the Trigger subfield to 1 in the TWT element it transmits during the TWT setup to negotiate
57
58 a trigger-enabled TWT.
59
• A successful TWT agreement whose Trigger subfield in the TWT response sent by the AP is 1 is
60
a trigger-enabled TWT; otherwise it is not a trigger-enabled TWT.
61
62
— Shall set the TWT Channel subfield in the TWT element it transmits to 0 unless the HE STA sets up
63
a subchannel selective transmission operation as defined in 26.8.7 (HE subchannel selective trans-
64
65 mission).
414 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 415 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — May set the TWT Protection field to 1 to indicate that TXOPs within the TWT SPs shall be initiated
2 with a NAV protection mechanism, such as (MU) RTS/CTS, or CTS-to-self frame; otherwise it shall
3
set it to 0.
4
5 — An HE STA shall not use the RAW mechanism for protection of TWT SPs.
6
7
8 An HE STA that successfully sets up a TWT agreement with another HE STA shall follow the rules defined
9 in 10.47.1 (TWT overview) and 10.47.4 (Implicit TWT operation), except that all the additional rules
10 defined in 26.8 (TWT operation) supersede all the respective rules defined in 10.47.1 (TWT overview) and
11 10.47.4 (Implicit TWT operation). A TWT or TWT SP that is set up under an implicit TWT agreement is an
12
implicit TWT or implicit TWT SP, respectively (see 10.47.1 (TWT overview)). A TWT or TWT SP that is
13
set up under a trigger-enabled TWT agreement is a trigger-enabled TWT or trigger-enabled TWT SP,
14
15 respectively. The TWT responding STA of a trigger-enabled TWT agreement is an HE AP.
16
17
An example of individual TWT operation is shown in Figure26-9 (Example of individual TWT operation),
18
19 where the AP is the TWT responding STA and STA 1 and STA 2 are the TWT requesting STAs.

Figure 26-9—Example of individual TWT operation
42
43
44 In this example, STA 1 sends a TWT request to the TWT responding STA to setup a trigger-enabled TWT
45
agreement. The TWT responding STA accepts the TWT agreement with STA 1 and confirms the acceptance
46
in the TWT response sent to STA 1. Subsequently, the TWT responding STA sends an unsolicited TWT
47
48 response to STA 2 to setup a trigger-enabled TWT agreement with STA 2. Both these TWT agreements are
49 setup as announced TWTs. During the trigger-enabled TWT SP, the TWT responding STA sends a Basic
50 Trigger frame to which the TWT requesting STAs indicate that they are awake during the TWT SP. STA 1
51
indicates that it is awake by sending a PS-Poll frame and STA 2 indicates that it is awake by sending a QoS
52
Null frame in response to the Basic Trigger frame. STA 1 and STA 2 receive their DL BUs in a subsequent
53
54 exchange with the TWT responding STA and go to doze state outside of this TWT SP.
55
56
An HE STA may execute the individual TWT setup exchanges defined in Table26-5 (TWT setup exchange
57
for unsolicited TWT and recommended broadcast TWT switch) in addition to the exchanges defined in
58
59 10.47 (Target wake time (TWT)). An HE STA that intends to set up an individual TWT shall set the Negoti-
60 ation Type subfield to 0 as defined in 10.47 (Target wake time (TWT)) or as defined in Table26-5 (TWT
61 setup exchange for unsolicited TWT and recommended broadcast TWT switch). The HE STA may respond
62
to the TWT request with a TWT response that has the Negotiation Type subfield equal to 3 as indicated in
63
Table26-5 (TWT setup exchange for unsolicited TWT and recommended broadcast TWT switch) to pro-
64
65 vide recommended broadcast TWT schedules for the requesting STA.
