## 第 417 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An HE AP may send an unsolicited TWT response with the Trigger subfield equal to 1 to a non-AP HE STA
2 that has set the TWT Requester Support subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits to the
3
AP. The TWT response shall have one of these values in the TWT Setup Command field: Accept TWT,
4
Alternate TWT or Dictate TWT. An unsolicited TWT response with TWT Setup Command field of Alter-
5
6 nate TWT or Dictate TWT contains an advisory notification to the recipient of TWT parameters that are
7 likely to be accepted by the AP if the recipient transmits a subsequent TWT request to the AP that includes
8 those TWT parameters. An unsolicited TWT response with the TWT Setup Command field of Accept TWT
9
creates a TWT agreement between the two STAs. A STA that receives an unsolicited TWT response with
10
the TWT Setup Command field of Accept TWT may transmit a TWT Teardown frame to delete the unsolic-
11
12 ited individual TWT agreement.
13
14 NOTE—The HE AP might send an unsolicited TWT response to a non-AP HE STA with a TWT Flow Identifier that
15 corresponds to an existing TWT agreement. The unsolicited TWT response with TWT Setup Command field of Accept
16 TWT will indicate new TWT parameters that are different from the previously negotiated TWT parameters for that TWT
17 agreement.
18
19 An HE STA shall not transmit BAT, TACK, or STACK frames, which are allowed in 10.47.2 (TWT
20 acknowledgment procedure)).
21
22
23 A TWT requesting STA should not transmit frames to the TWT responding STA outside of negotiated TWT
24 SPs and should not transmit frames that are not contained within HE TB PPDUs to the TWT responding
25 STA within trigger-enabled TWT SPs.
26
27 NOTE—The TWT requesting STA decides which frames to transmit within or outside a TWT SP and while it is recom-
28 mended that the TWT requesting STA not transmit using EDCA within or outside TWT SPs the TWT requesting STA
29 might still do so. If the STA decides to transmit then the STA might contend for access to the medium as defined in
30 10.23.2 (HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)) and in 26.2.7 (EDCA operation using MU EDCA parameters).
31
32
The TWT responding STA of a trigger-enabled TWT agreement shall schedule for transmission a Trigger
33
frame for the TWT requesting STA, as described in 26.5.2 (UL MU operation), within each TWT SP for that
34
35 TWT agreement except that the Trigger frame may be replaced by a frame carrying a TRS Control subfield
36 provided that the frame is carried in a DL MU PPDU and the AP allocates enough resources in the HE TB
37 PPDU for the STA to at least deliver its BSRs in response to the soliciting DL MU PPDU. The TWT
38
responding STA should solicit buffer status reports from the TWT requesting STA at the start of the TWT
39
SP following the procedure described in 26.5.5 (Buffer status report operation) or as described in 26.5.7
40
41 (NDP feedback report procedure). The TWT responding STA that intends to schedule for transmission addi-
42 tional Trigger frames during a trigger-enabled TWT SP shall set the More TF subfield in the Common Info
43 field of the Trigger frame to 1 to indicate that it will schedule for transmission another Trigger frame within
44
the same TWT SP. The TWT responding STA shall set the More TF subfield to 0 when the Trigger frame is
45
the last scheduled Trigger frame of the TWT SP or when the Trigger frame is scheduled for transmission
46
47 outside of a TWT SP.
48
49 NOTE 1—The TWT responding STA can cancel the transmission of a scheduled Trigger frame if the STA gains access
50 to the wireless medium outside of the TWT SP. The TWT responding STA does not schedule for transmission a Trigger
51 frame for the TWT requesting STA when the TWT agreement is not a trigger-enabled TWT agreement or when the
52 TWT requesting STA has sent an OM Control subfield that has the UL MU Disable subfield equal to 1 (see 26.9 (Oper-
53 ating mode indication).
54
NOTE 2—If the AP replaces the Trigger frame with a frame carrying a TRS Control field then it is recommended that
55
the AP allocate enough resources in subsequent Trigger frames sent during the TWT SP so that the STA can send as
56
much as possible of the data reported in the BSR.
57
58
59 A TWT requesting STA transmits an HE TB PPDU as a response to a Trigger frame that identifies it and is
60 sent during a trigger-enabled TWT SP (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)). A TWT requesting STA that is in PS
61 mode and is awake shall include a PS-Poll frame or a U-APSD trigger frame in the HE TB PPDU if the
62
TWT is an announced TWT unless the STA has already transmitted a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame or
63
transmitted any other indication that the STA is in the awake state within that TWT SP or has, previous to
64
65 the start of the TWT SP but after the end of the most recent TWT SP, indicated to the AP that it is currently
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 417
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 418 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 in the awake state. The STA may include other frames in the HE TB PPDU when other rules do not prohibit
2 their inclusion, see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation) and 9.7.3 (A-MPDU contents).
3
4 NOTE 1–A Trigger frame identifies a TWT requesting STA if it is sent by the AP with which the STA is associated and
5 the frame contains the 12 LSBs of the STA’s AID in any of its User Info fields. The Trigger frame can have multiple
6 recipients, each of which is identified by the presence of the 12 LSBs of the recipient’s AID in any of its User Info fields
7 (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)), and can have in the TA field the MAC address of the AP or the transmitted BSSID
8 under the conditions defined in 26.5.2.2.4 (Allowed settings of the Trigger frame fields and TRS Control subfield).
9
NOTE 2—Other indications that the STA is in the awake state are the transmission of an HE TB feedback NDP in
10
response to an NFRP Trigger frame (see 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure)) or the transmission of a frame that
11
indicates that the STA is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (STA power management modes)).
12
13
14 A TWT responding STA that receives a PS-Poll frame or a U-APSD trigger frame or any other indication
15 from a TWT requesting STA that is in PS mode during or before an announced TWT SP but after the end of
16
the most recent TWT SP, that the TWT requesting STA is in the awake state during the TWT SP shall fol-
17
low the rules defined in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation during the CP) except that the TWT responding STA should
18
19 deliver to the TWT requesting STA as many buffered BUs as are available at the TWT responding STA,
20 provided that the BU delivery does not exceed the duration of the TWT SP, the TWT requesting STA has
21 indicated that it is in the awake state for that TWT SP and as long as the TWT requesting STA has not
22
entered the doze state (see 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame exchange for individual TWT) and 26.8.5
23
(Power save operation during TWT SPs)).
24
25
NOTE—The indication that the TWT requesting STA is in the awake state for that TWT SP might be a PS-Poll, U-
26
APSD trigger frame, or any frame for which an immediate response is solicited and that follows the rules in 11.2.3.2
27
(Non-AP STA power management modes) but the corresponding immediate response frame is not received by the TWT
28
requesting STA. Other indications that the STA is in the awake state are the transmission of an HE TB feedback NDP in
29
response to an NFRP Trigger frame (see 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure)) or the transmission of a frame that
30
indicates that the STA is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power management modes)).
31
32
A TWT responding STA that sends frames to a TWT requesting STA that is in PS mode during an unan-
33
34 nounced TWT SP shall follow the rules defined in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation during the CP) except that the
35 TWT responding STA should deliver to the TWT requesting STA as many buffered BUs as are available at
36 the TWT responding STA, provided that the BU delivery does not exceed the duration of the TWT SP and
37
as long as the TWT requesting STA has not entered the doze state (see 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame
38
exchange for individual TWT) and 26.8.5 (Power save operation during TWT SPs)).
39
40
NOTE—The TWT responding STA can deliver the buffered BUs in A-MPDUs sent under a block ack agreement if the
41
TWT is an announced TWT and the TWT requesting STA is awake for that TWT SP, or if the TWT is an unannounced
42
TWT (at the start of which the TWT requesting STA is assumed to already be awake). The buffered BUs can be deliv-
43
ered in multiple PPDUs transmitted within the TWT SP. The TWT responding STA can transmit frames to TWT
44
requesting STA after the end of the TWT SP if the STA is in active mode.
45
46
A TWT responding STA may transmit to a TWT requesting STA that is in active mode at any time (see
47
48 11.2.3.2 (STA power management modes)). A TWT responding STA may transmit to a TWT requesting
49 STA that is in PS mode and awake outside of a TWT SP following the rules in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation).
50
51
26.8.3 Broadcast TWT operation
52
53
54 26.8.3.1 General
55
56
A TWT scheduling AP is an HE AP with dot11TWTOptionActivated equal to true that sets the Broadcast
57
TWT Support field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1 and that follows the rules in 26.8.3.2
58
59 (Rules for TWT scheduling AP), 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT), and those for periodic oppor-
60 tunistic power save defined in 26.14.3 (Opportunistic power save).
61
62
A TWT scheduling AP includes a broadcast TWT element in the Beacon frame as described in 26.8.3.2
63
(Rules for TWT scheduling AP). An AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID in a multiple BSSID set
64
65 shall follow the rules in 11.1.3.8.4 (Inheritance of element values).

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 A TWT scheduling AP may include a TWT element with the Negotiation Type subfield equal to 3 in an
2 (Re)Association Response frame or in a TWT setup frame to assign the recipient STA to a broadcast TWT
3
schedule without having received a request from the STA to become a member of the broadcast TWT sched-
4
ule if that STA has set the Broadcast TWT Support field of HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1.
5
6
7
A non-AP HE STA shall obtain TWT parameter values from the most recently received TWT element car-
8
9 ried in a Beacon, Probe Response, or (Re)Association Response frame from its associated AP unless the
10 non-AP HE STA is associated with an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID of a multiple BSSID
11 set, in which case it shall follow the rules in 11.1.3.8.4 (Inheritance of element values) to determine the
12
TWT parameter values.
13
14
15
A TWT scheduled STA is a non-AP HE STA that sets the Broadcast TWT Support field in the HE Capabil-
16
ities element it transmits to 1 and receives a broadcast TWT element transmitted by an HE AP that is a TWT
17
18 scheduling AP.
19
20
21 A TWT scheduled STA follows the schedule provided by the TWT scheduling AP as described in 26.8.3.3
22 (Rules for TWT scheduled STA), and, in addition, the rules in 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT)
23 if the STA supports the UORA procedure, and the rules in 26.14.3 (Opportunistic power save) if the STA
24
supports OPS operation. A TWT scheduled STA can negotiate the wake TBTT and wake interval for Bea-
25
con frames it intends to receive as described in 26.8.6 (Negotiation of wake TBTT and wake interval) or can
26
27 join a particular broadcast TWT as described below.
28
29
30 An example of broadcast TWT operation is shown in Figure26-10 (Example of broadcast TWT operation
31 with optional TBTT negotiation), where the AP is the TWT scheduling AP and STA 1 and STA 2 are the
32 TWT scheduled STAs.

57
The TWT scheduling AP includes a broadcast TWT element in the Beacon frame that indicates a broadcast
58
59 TWT SP during which the AP intends to send Trigger frames, or DL BUs to the TWT scheduled STAs. STA
60 1 and STA 2 wake to receive the Beacon frame to determine the broadcast TWT. During the trigger-enabled
61 TWT SP the AP sends a Basic Trigger frame to which STA 1 and STA 2 indicate that they are awake during
62
the TWT SP. STA 1 indicates that it is awake by sending a PS-Poll and STA 2 indicates that it is awake by
63
sending a QoS Null frame in response to the Basic Trigger frame. STA 1 and STA 2 receive their DL BUs in
64
65 a subsequent exchange with the AP and go to doze state outside of this TWT SP.

1 Each broadcast TWT is uniquely identified by the <broadcast TWT ID, MAC address> tuple, where the
2 broadcast TWT ID is the value of the Broadcast TWT ID subfield and is greater than 0 and the MAC address
3
is the address of the TWT scheduling AP.
4
5
6 Broadcast TWT schedules are advertised by the TWT scheduling AP in frames that carry TWT elements
7 with the Negotiation Type subfield set to 2 as described in 26.8.3.2 (Rules for TWT scheduling AP). Broad-
8 cast TWT schedules that are intended for member TWT scheduled STAs are identified by a Broadcast TWT
9
ID subfield that is greater than 0 and broadcast TWT schedules that are intended for all TWT scheduled
10
STAs are identified by a Broadcast TWT ID subfield equal to 0.
11
12
13 Negotiations to become a member of or terminate membership in a broadcast TWT, identified by a Broad-
14 cast TWT ID subfield greater than 0, are performed with an exchange of frames that carry TWT elements
15
with the Negotiation Type subfield set to 3 as described in 26.8.3.3 (Rules for TWT scheduled STA).
16
17
18 The TWT scheduling AP may send an unsolicited TWT response with the Trigger subfield set to 1 to a non-
19 AP HE STA that has set the Broadcast TWT Support subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities elements that it
20 transmits to the AP. The TWT response shall indicate one of the following values in the TWT Setup Com-
21
mand field: Accept TWT, Alternate TWT, or Dictate TWT. An unsolicited TWT response with TWT Setup
22
Command field indicating Alternate TWT or Dictate TWT contains an advisory notification to the recipient
23
24 of TWT parameters that are likely to be accepted by the AP if the recipient transmits a subsequent TWT
25 request to the AP that includes those TWT parameters. An unsolicited TWT response with a TWT Setup
26 Command field that indicates Accept TWT allocates a broadcast TWT schedule to the receiving STA. A
27
STA that receives an unsolicited TWT response with a TWT Setup Command field that indicates Accept
28
TWT may transmit a TWT Teardown frame or a TWT response with TWT Setup Command field indicating
29
30 Reject TWT to withdraw from the unsolicited broadcast TWT schedule.
31
32 26.8.3.2 Rules for TWT scheduling AP
33
34
35 A TWT scheduling AP may include a broadcast TWT element in a Beacon frame that is scheduled at a
36 TBTT (see 11.1.3.2 (Beacon generation in non-DMG infrastructure networks)). The TWT scheduling AP
37 shall include one or more TWT parameter sets in the TWT element, and each TWT parameter set may indi-
38
cate a periodic occurrence of TWTs. The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Last Broadcast Parameter Set
39
subfield to 0 in each TWT parameter set except for that the last (or only) TWT parameter set of the TWT
40
41 element that shall have the Last Broadcast Parameter Set subfield set to 1. The TWT scheduling AP shall set
42 the NDP Paging Indicator subfield to 0 and the Negotiation Type subfield to 2, and may set the Responder
43 PM Mode subfield to 0 in the TWT element (see 10.47.7 (TWT Sleep Setup)). Each TWT parameter set
44
specifies the TWT parameters of a specific broadcast TWT that are valid within a broadcast TWT SP. Each
45
specific broadcast TWT is identified as indicated in 26.8.3.1 (General). Individual STAs may have member-
46
47 ship in broadcast TWTs as the result of negotiation with a TWT scheduling AP as described in 26.8.3.1
48 (General).
49
50
The TWT scheduling AP sets the TWT parameters of each TWT parameter set as described below.
51
52
53 The TWT scheduling AP shall set the TWT Request subfield to 0 and the TWT Setup Command subfield as
54 defined in Table26-6 (Broadcast TWT announcements) and shall include the broadcast TWT element in the
55 Beacon frames for as long as there is at least one active broadcast TWT schedule. Broadcast TWT
56
announcements are broadcast TWT schedules advertised in broadcast TWT elements contained in broadcast
57
Management frames (see Table26-6 (Broadcast TWT announcements)).
58
59
60 The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Broadcast TWT Persistence subfield for each broadcast TWT to the
61 number of TBTTs for which the Broadcast TWT schedule will be in existence, counting forward from the
62
current TBTT. The AP may change the value of the Broadcast TWT Persistence subfield for any Broadcast
63
TWT within any transmitted TWT element. If the AP reduces the value of the subfield, it shall not reduce
64
65 the value by more than one as compared to the value transmitted during the immediately preceding beacon
420 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 421 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 interval. If the AP increases the value of the Broadcast TWT Persistence subfield, it may increase the value
2 by any amount as compared to the value transmitted during the immediately preceding TBTT.
3
4
A TWT scheduling AP that sets the TWT Setup Command subfield to Reject TWT shall indicate the TBTT
5
6 at which the periodic broadcast TWT will be terminated by setting the value of the Broadcast TWT Per-
7 sistence subfield to indicate the number of TBTTs that remain until the broadcast TWT schedule is termi-
8 nated. The broadcast TWT schedule terminates at the next TBTT that follows the TBTT at which the TWT
9
scheduling AP transmits the broadcast TWT element with Broadcast TWT Persistence subfield for that
10
broadcast TWT schedule equal to 0.A TWT scheduling AP may terminate the membership of a TWT sched-
11
12 uled STA in all broadcast TWTs by transmitting a TWT Teardown frame with the Teardown All TWT field
13 set to 1.
14
15
A TWT scheduling AP that sets the TWT Setup Command subfield to Alternate TWT shall indicate the
16
17 TBTT at which the periodic broadcast TWT parameter set will be modified by setting the Broadcast TWT
18 Persistence subfield to indicate the number of TBTTs that remain until the broadcast TWT schedule is mod-
19 ified. The broadcast TWT schedule will be modified at the next TBTT, which follows the TBTT at which
20
the TWT scheduling AP transmits the broadcast TWT element with Broadcast TWT Persistence subfield for
21
that broadcast TWT schedule equal to 0. The AP shall include in the broadcast TWT element the future
22
23 broadcast TWT parameter set that will take effect at that TBTT. The future broadcast TWT parameter set
24 shall have the same values in the TWT Setup Command and Broadcast TWT ID subfields as the current
25 broadcast TWT parameter set that is being modified and switch the TWT Setup Command subfield from
26
Alternate TWT to Accept TWT at that TBTT. The future broadcast TWT parameter set shall be in a Broad-
27
cast TWT Parameter Set field that is located after the Broadcast TWT Parameter Set field that contains the
28
29 current broadcast TWT parameter set.
30
NOTE—TWT scheduled STAs follow the broadcast TWT parameters that are included in the current broadcast TWT
31
parameter set and only switch to following the broadcast TWT parameters in the future broadcast TWT parameter set if
32
the TWT Setup Command field is equal to Accept TWT in the Broadcast TWT Parameter Set field that contains the
33
future broadcast TWT parameter set.
34
35
36 A TWT scheduling AP should indicate Alternate TWT or Reject TWT in the TWT Setup Command field of
37 the broadcast TWT element for as many TBTTs as needed to exceed the longest interval any STA is
38 expected to not receive Beacon frames either when the TWT parameters of a periodic TWT will change, or
39
when the periodic TWT specified by that TWT parameter set will be terminated.
40
41
42 The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Trigger field to 1 to indicate a trigger-enabled TWT. Otherwise, it
43 shall set the Trigger field to 0 (i.e., the TWT is not a trigger-enabled TWT). The AP is not expected to sched-
44 ule for transmission Trigger frames during a non-trigger-enabled TWT SP and is expected to schedule Trig-
45
ger frames during a trigger-enabled TWT SP as described below.
46
47
48 The TWT scheduling AP shall schedule for transmission of a Trigger frame addressed to one or more TWT
49 scheduled STAs during a trigger-enabled TWT SP except that the Trigger frame may be replaced by a frame
50
carrying a TRS Control subfield provided that the frame is carried in a DL MU PPDU and the AP allocates
51
enough resources in the HE TB PPDU for the STA to at least deliver its BSRs in response to the soliciting
52
53 DL MU PPDU. A TWT scheduling AP should not include the 12 LSBs of the STA’s AID in a User Info
54 field of a Trigger frame transmitted within a broadcast TWT SP unless the STA is in the awake state, has
55 established membership in the broadcast TWT with that Broadcast TWT ID, or has indicated to receive the
56
Beacon frame preceding the beacon interval that contains this TWT SP (see 26.8.6 (Negotiation of wake
57
TBTT and wake interval)).
58
59
60 The TWT scheduling AP that schedules for transmission additional Trigger frames during a trigger-enabled
61 TWT SP shall set the More TF subfield in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame to 1 to indicate that it
62
will schedule for transmission another Trigger frame within the same TWT SP. The TWT scheduling AP
63
shall set the More TF subfield to 0 if the Trigger frame is the last scheduled Trigger frame of the TWT SP or
64
65 if the Trigger frame is scheduled for transmission outside of a trigger-enabled TWT SP. The TWT schedul-

1 ing AP should poll as many STAs as possible among TWT scheduled STAs that are members of that non-
2 zero Broadcast TWT ID so that the STAs can perform a frame exchange with the TWT scheduling AP
3
during that TWT SP.
4
5
NOTE 1—The TWT scheduling AP does not intend to schedule for transmission of a Trigger frame for the TWT sched-
6
uled STA when the broadcast TWT is not a trigger-enabled TWT or when the TWT scheduled STA has sent an OM
7
Control subfield that has the UL MU disable bit equal to 1 (see 26.9 (Operating mode indication)).
8
9
NOTE 2—The TWT scheduling AP can cancel the transmission of a scheduled Trigger frame if the AP gains access to
10
the wireless medium outside of the TWT SP.
11
12 NOTE 3—If the AP replaces the Trigger frame with a frame carrying a TRS Control field then it is recommended that
13 the AP allocate enough resources in subsequent Trigger frames sent during the TWT SP so that the STA can send as
14 much as possible of the data reported in the BSR.
15
16
The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Flow Type field to 1 to indicate an unannounced TWT. Otherwise, it
17
18 shall set the Flow Type field to 0 to indicate an announced TWT.
19
20
The TWT scheduling AP should schedule delivery of individually addressed DL BUs during unannounced
21
22 TWT SPs with nonzero Broadcast TWT ID subfield.
23
24
The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield according to Table9-297a
25
(Broadcast TWT Recommendation field for a broadcast TWT element). The TWT scheduling AP shall set
26
27 the Trigger field to 1 if the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield is 1 or 2, and may set the Trigger field
28 to any value if the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield is 0 or 3.
29
30
31 A TWT scheduling AP that has advertised a broadcast TWT with a Broadcast TWT ID equal to 0 shall
32 schedule the following:
33
34 — The delivery of group addressed DL BUs during the broadcast TWT SPs located within the beacon
35 interval that follows the DTIM Beacon frame if the TWT parameter set indicated non-trigger enabled
36 unannounced TWT SP and had the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield equal to 0.
37
38 — The transmission of a Trigger frame that does not contain an RA-RU during the broadcast TWT SPs
39 if the TWT parameter set indicated trigger-enabled announced TWT SP and had the Broadcast TWT
40 Recommendation subfield equal to 1. The Trigger frame shall contain at least one User Info field
41
addressed to a TWT scheduled STA whose TIM bit in the Beacon frame is 1 and that is not a mem-
42
ber of any nonzero broadcast TWT during this beacon interval.
43
44
— The transmission of a Trigger frame that contains at least one RA-RU (see 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-
45
based random access (UORA))) during the broadcast TWT SPs if the TWT parameter set indicated a
46
47 trigger enabled announced TWT SP and had the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield set to 2
48 (see 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT).
49
50 — The transmission of a TIM frame or FILS Discovery frame at the start of a broadcast TWT SP if the
51 TWT parameter set indicated a non-trigger enabled unannounced TWT SP and had the Broadcast
52 TWT Recommendation subfield set to 3 (see 26.14.3.2 (AP operation for opportunistic power save)).
53
54
55 A Trigger frame transmitted during a broadcast TWT SP whose TWT parameter set has the Broadcast TWT
56 Recommendation subfield equal to 0 or 3 may contain zero or more RA-RUs (see 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-
57
based random access (UORA))). A Trigger frame transmitted during a broadcast TWT SP whose TWT
58
parameter set has the Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield equal to 1 shall contain no RA-RU.
59
60
61 The TWT scheduling AP shall set the Target Wake Time field to the TSF timer [10: 25] that corresponds to
62
the next TWT that is scheduled for this TWT parameter set when it queues for transmission the frame that
63
contains the TWT element. The TSF timer at which the next TWT is scheduled has bits 0 to 9 equal to 0 and
64
65 bits 26 to 63 equal to the same value as the respective bits in the current TSF timer.

1 The TWT scheduling AP shall include a nonzero value for the TWT wake interval in the TWT Wake Inter-
2 val Exponent and TWT Wake Interval Mantissa fields for a periodic TWT and a zero value for an aperiodic
3
TWT.
4
5
6 The TWT parameters are valid for each successive TWT of a periodic TWT and for the only TWT of an ape-
7 riodic TWT.
8
9
The TWT scheduling AP shall include a unique value in the Broadcast TWT ID subfield for each Broadcast
10
11 TWT to allow identification of each Broadcast TWT unless the TWT Setup Command field is Alternate
12 TWT or the Broadcast TWT ID subfield is zero.
13
14 NOTE—The broadcast TWT element contains two Broadcast TWT Parameter Set fields with the same Broadcast TWT
15 ID subfield value if the TWT Setup Command field indicates Alternate TWT in one of the Broadcast TWT Parameter
16 Set fields. The broadcast TWT element might contain multiple Broadcast TWT Parameter Set fields with the Broadcast
17 TWT ID subfield equal to 0.
18
19
A TWT scheduling AP that receives a PS-Poll or a U-APSD trigger frame or any other indication from a
20
TWT scheduled STA that is in PS mode during or before a specific announced TWT SP but after the end of
21
22 the most recent TWT SP preceding the specific TWT SP (if any), that the TWT scheduled STA is in the
23 awake state during the TWT SP shall follow the rules defined in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation) except that the AP
24 should deliver to the TWT scheduled STA as many buffered BUs as are available at the AP, provided that
25
the BU delivery does not exceed the duration of the TWT SP, the TWT scheduled STA has indicated that it
26
is in the awake state for that TWT SP and as long as the TWT scheduled STA has not entered the doze state
27
28 (see 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT) and 26.8.5 (Power save operation
29 during TWT SPs)).
30
31 NOTE—The indication that the TWT scheduled STA is in the awake state for that TWT SP might be a PS-Poll, U-
32 APSD trigger frame, or any frame for which an immediate response is solicited and that follows the rules in 11.2.3.2
33 (Non-AP STA power management modes) but the corresponding immediate response frame is not received by the TWT
34 scheduled STA. Other indications that the STA is in the awake state are the transmission of an HE TB feedback NDP in
35 response to an NFRP Trigger frame (see 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure)) or the transmission of a frame that
36 indicates that the STA is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power management modes)).
37
38 A TWT scheduling AP that sends frames to a TWT scheduled STA that is in PS mode during an unan-
39
nounced TWT SP shall follow the rules defined in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation) except that the AP should deliver
40
to the TWT scheduled STA as many buffered BUs as available at the AP, provided that the BU delivery
41
42 does not exceed the duration of the TWT SP and as long as the TWT scheduled STA has not entered the
43 doze state (see 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT) and 26.8.5 (Power save
44 operation during TWT SPs).
45
46 NOTE—The TWT scheduling AP can deliver the buffered BUs in A-MPDUs sent under a BlockAck agreement if the
47 TWT is an announced TWT and the TWT scheduled STA is awake for that TWT SP, or if the TWT is an unannounced
48 TWT (at the start of which the TWT scheduled STA is assumed to already be awake). The buffered BUs can be deliv-
49 ered in multiple PPDUs transmitted within the TWT SP. The TWT scheduling AP can exceed the duration of the TWT
50 SP if the TWT scheduled STA is in active mode.
51
52
A TWT scheduling AP may transmit to a TWT scheduled STA that is in active mode at any time (see
53
54 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power management modes)). A TWT scheduling AP may transmit to a TWT sched-
55 uled STA that is in PS mode and awake outside of a TWT SP following the rules in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation).
56
57
A TWT scheduling AP that receives a TWT element with the TWT Request field equal to 1, the Negotiation
58
Type subfield equal to 3 and the TWT Setup Command field set to Suggest or Demand may respond with a
59
60 frame containing a TWT element as shown in Table26-7 (Broadcast TWT membership exchanges).
61
62
A TWT scheduling AP that receives a TWT element with the TWT Request field equal to 1, the Negotiation
63
Type subfield equal to 3 and the TWT Setup Command field set to Reject shall delete the membership of the
64
65 STA corresponding to the TA of the MMPDU that contained the TWT element from the broadcast TWT

1 schedule that has the Broadcast TWT ID value that is equal to the value of the Broadcast TWT ID field of
2 the TWT element.
3
4
A TWT scheduling AP may transmit a broadcast TWT announcement at any time. Valid broadcast TWT
5
6 announcements are described in Table26-6 (Broadcast TWT announcements).

53 (TWT)) or 26.8 (TWT operation) are not allowed. The initiating frame is a TWT response.
54

56 Probe Response, FILS Discovery, and Beacon frames. The TWT element has the TWT Request field equal to 0 and
57 the Negotiation Type subfield equal to 2. The TWT scheduling AP can include a TWT parameter set with Broadcast
58 TWT ID value 0 to indicate a TWT allocated for all STAs, and Broadcast TWT ID greater than 0 to indicate a TWT
59 intended to TWT scheduled STAs that are members of that broadcast TWT.

## 第 425 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.8.3.3 Rules for TWT scheduled STA
2
3
4 A TWT scheduled STA that receives a broadcast TWT element in a Beacon frame shall follow the rules
5 defined in this subclause to interact with the TWT scheduling AP.
6
7
8 A TWT scheduled STA should not transmit frames to the TWT scheduling AP outside of broadcast TWT
9 SPs and should not transmit frames that are not contained within HE TB PPDUs to the TWT scheduling AP
10
within trigger-enabled broadcast TWT SPs, except that the STA can transmit frames within negotiated indi-
11
vidual TWT SPs as defined in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements).
12
13
14 NOTE—The TWT scheduled STA decides which frames to transmit within or outside a TWT SP and while it is recom-
15 mended that the TWT scheduled STA not transmit using EDCA within or outside TWT SPs, the TWT scheduled STA
16 might still do so. If the STA decides to transmit then the STA might contend for accessing the medium as defined in
17 10.23.2 (HCF contention based channel access (EDCA)) and in 26.2.7 (EDCA operation using MU EDCA parameters).
18
19
A TWT scheduled STA may request to become a member of a broadcast TWT by transmitting a frame to its
20
associated AP that contains a TWT element with the Negotiation Type subfield set to 3 and the TWT Setup
21
22 Command field set to Request TWT or Suggest TWT or Demand TWT. The TWT Parameter set indicates
23 the Broadcast TWT ID of the broadcast TWT that the STA is requesting to join. See Table26-7 (Broadcast
24 TWT membership exchanges).
25
26
27 A TWT scheduled STA may terminate membership in a broadcast TWT by transmitting a frame to its asso-
28 ciated AP that contains a TWT element with the Negotiation Type subfield set to 3 and the TWT Setup
29
Command field set to Reject TWT or by transmitting a TWT Teardown frame that has the Negotiation Type
30
subfield set to 3. A TWT scheduled STA may terminate membership in all broadcast TWTs by transmitting
31
32 a TWT Teardown frame with the Teardown All TWT field set to 1.
33
34
35 A TWT scheduled STA that receives a TWT element with the TWT Request field equal to 0, the Negotia-
36 tion Type subfield equal to 3 and the TWT Setup Command field indicating Accept TWT is a member of the
37 broadcast TWT identified by the <broadcast TWT ID, MAC address> tuple, where the broadcast TWT ID is
38
the value of the Broadcast TWT ID subfield in the TWT element and the MAC address that is the TA of the
39
MMPDU that contained the TWT element is equal to the MAC address of the AP with which the STA is
40
41 associated, regardless of whether the TWT scheduled STA had previously transmitted a corresponding TWT
42 element to the AP with the TWT Setup Command field indicating Request TWT, Suggest TWT or Demand
43 TWT.
44
45
46 Valid broadcast TWT membership exchanges are described in Table26-7 (Broadcast TWT membership
47 exchanges).
48
49
50

28 NOTE 2—The initiating frame and response frame settings not listed in the tables in 10.47 (Target wake time (TWT))
29 or 26.8 (TWT operation) are not allowed. The initiating frame is a TWT request if the TWT element contained in the
30 frame has the TWT Request field equal to 1 (see Table9-297 (TWT Setup Command field values)); otherwise it is a

33

34

37 ation Request, and TWT Setup frames with TWT Request field equal to 1. The TWT element has the Negotiation
38 Type subfield equal to 3 and the Broadcast TWT ID(s) that the STA intends to join or withdraw. MMPDUs that con-
39 tain a broadcast TWT element generated by a TWT scheduled AP can be (Re)Association Response, and TWT Setup
40 frames with TWT Request field equal to 0. The TWT element has the Negotiation Type subfield equal to 3 and the
41 Broadcast TWT ID(s) to which the STA is assigned or from which the STA is withdrawn.
42
43
44
45 A TWT scheduled STA that is in PS mode may enter the doze state after receiving a Beacon frame with a
46 TWT element indicating the existence of a broadcast TWT and shall be in the awake state at the broadcast
47 TWT start times for which the STA has indicated it will be awake by any of the following means:
48
49 — Establishing a membership for the unannounced broadcast TWT with those broadcast TWT IDs
50
51 — Negotiating a wake TBTT and wake interval between Beacon frames that the STA receives, as
52
defined in 26.8.6 (Negotiation of wake TBTT and wake interval)
53
54
— Having sent a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame during the beacon interval
55
56
— Having sent another indication that it is in the awake state during that beacon interval
57
58
59 NOTE 1—Other indications that the STA is in the awake state are the transmission of an HE TB feedback NDP in
60 response to an NFRP Trigger frame (see 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure)) or the transmission of a frame that
61 indicates that the STA is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power management modes)).
62
63 NOTE 2—The STA might indicate that it will not be awake at certain broadcast TWT start times by sending a TWT
64 Information frame. The AP might indicate to a STA that it need not be awake at certain broadcast TWT start times by
65 sending a TWT information frame (see 26.8.4 (Use of TWT Information frames)).

## 第 428 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 A TWT scheduled STA is not required to be in the awake state at broadcast TWT SP start times correspond-
2 ing to the broadcast TWT that has the broadcast TWT ID value of 0.
3
4
5 A TWT scheduled STA that did not receive a Beacon frame at a TBTT shall act as if it had received the
6 expected Beacon frame containing a TWT element for a broadcast TWT, if the missed beacon corresponds
7 to a TBTT that is within the next n TBTTs beyond the most recently received Beacon frame that included a
8
TWT element for that broadcast TWT, where n is equal to 1 plus the value obtained from the Broadcast
9
TWT Persistence subfield of the corresponding Broadcast TWT, except that n is infinite if the Broadcast
10
11 TWT Persistence subfield is 255.
12
13 A TWT scheduled STA transmits an HE TB PPDU as a response to a Trigger frame that is addressed to it
14
and is sent during a trigger-enabled TWT SP (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)). A TWT scheduled STA that
15
is in PS mode and is awake during an announced TWT SP shall include a PS-Poll frame or a U-APSD trig-
16
17 ger frame in the HE TB PPDU if it intends to solicit buffered BUs from the TWT scheduling AP (see
18 11.2.3.7 (Receive operation for STAs in PS mode)) unless the STA has already transmitted within that TWT
19 SP a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame or has transmitted any other indication that the STA is in the awake
20
state within that TWT SP, or has, previous to the start of the TWT SP but after the end of the most recent
21
TWT SP, indicated to the AP that it is currently in the awake state. A TWT scheduled STA that is in PS
22
23 mode shall transition to the awake state at the start of an unannounced TWT SP of which it is a member. The
24 STA may include other frames in the HE TB PPDU when other rules do not prohibit their inclusion (see
25 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)) and 9.7.3 (A-MPDU contents).
26
27 NOTE 1—A TWT scheduling AP sets the bit in the TIM element of the Beacon frame that corresponds to the AID of the
28 TWT scheduled STA to 1 to indicate that it expects the TWT scheduled STA to solicit available buffered BUs (see
29 11.2.3.7 (Receive operation for STAs in PS mode)).
30
31 NOTE 2—Other indications that the STA is in the awake state are the transmission of an HE TB feedback NDP in
32 response to an NFRP Trigger frame (see 26.5.7 (NDP feedback report procedure)) or the transmission of a frame that
33 indicates that the STA is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power management modes)).
34
35
A TWT scheduled STA should not send frames that do not satisfy the Broadcast TWT Recommendation
36
subfield recommendations in Table9-297a (Broadcast TWT Recommendation field for a broadcast TWT
37
38 element) during the corresponding TWT SP(s). Frames sent as a response to a Trigger frame are subject to
39 further restrictions as defined in 26.5.2 (UL MU operation) and 9.7.3 (A-MPDU contents).
40
41
26.8.4 Use of TWT Information frames
42
43
44 26.8.4.1 General
45
46
An HE STA may transmit a TWT Information frame to its peer STA during an individual TWT agreement,
47
48 broadcast TWT schedule, or at any time as defined in 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame exchange for indi-
49 vidual TWT), 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT) and 26.8.4.4 (TWT Informa-
50 tion frame exchange for flexible wake time), respectively.
51
52 NOTE—An HE AP might include multiple TWT Information frames, each addressed to a different peer STA, in an HE
53 MU PPDU (see 26.5.1 (HE DL MU operation)).
54
55
The TWT Information frame shall have the Response Requested subfield equal to 0, the Next TWT Request
56
subfield equal to 0, and one of the following:
57
58
— A nonzero value in the Next TWT subfield if the frame is transmitted by a TWT responding STA, a
59
TWT scheduling AP, or by any HE STA to a peer STA that has set the Flexible TWT Schedule Sup-
60
61 port field to 1 in the HE Capabilities element it transmits.
62
• The value of the Next TWT subfield shall be selected from existing TWT values for an individ-
63
ual TWT agreement if the Flexible TWT Schedule Support field in the HE Capabilities element
64
65 received from the peer STA is 0 and shall be selected from existing TWT values for a broadcast
428 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 429 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 TWT schedule regardless of the value of the Flexible TWT Schedule Support field received from
2 the peer STA.
3
• The Next TWT subfield may contain any nonzero value if Flexible TWT Schedule Support field
4
5 in the HE Capabilities element received from the peer STA is 1.
6 • The All TWT field is 1 if the resumption applies to all broadcast TWT schedules followed by the
7 TWT scheduled STA and/or to all individual TWT agreements followed by the TWT responding
8
STA.
9
10 — A Next TWT subfield that is present if the frame is transmitted by a TWT requesting STA, a TWT
11
scheduled STA, or if the frame is transmitted by any HE STA to a peer STA that has set the Flexible
12
TWT Schedule Support field to 1 in the HE Capabilities element it transmits.
13
14 • The Next TWT subfield indicates the earliest TWT at which the individual TWT agreement or
15
broadcast TWT schedule is resumed and shall be selected from existing TWT values for that
16
TWT agreement or broadcast TWT schedule if the Flexible TWT Schedule Support field in the
17
18 HE Capabilities element received from the peer STA is 0.
19 • The All TWT field is 1 if the resumption applies to all broadcast TWT schedules followed by the
20
TWT scheduled STA and/or to all individual TWT agreements followed by the TWT requesting
21
STA.
22
23 • The Next TWT subfield may contain any nonzero value if Flexible TWT Schedule Support field
24 in the HE Capabilities element received from the peer STA is 1.
25
26 — A Next TWT subfield that is not present if the frame is transmitted by a TWT requesting STA or a
27 TWT scheduled STA to indicate suspension of the individual TWT agreement or broadcast TWT
28 schedule.
29
30 • The All TWT subfield is 1 if the suspension applies to all broadcast TWT schedules followed by
31 the TWT scheduled STA and/or to all individual TWT agreements followed by the TWT request-
32 ing STA.
33
34 NOTE—Information exchanged with TWT Information frames does not modify the TWT parameters of any existing
35 TWT session except if the TWT Information frame is sent under flexible TWT (see 26.8.4.4 (TWT Information frame
36 exchange for flexible wake time)).
37
38
The use of TWT Information frames for suspending and/or resuming existing individual TWT agreements is
39
40 described in 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame exchange for individual TWT). The use of TWT Information
41 frames for suspending and/or resuming existing broadcast TWT schedules is described in 26.8.4.3 (TWT
42 Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT). The use of TWT Information frames for providing a flex-
43
ible TWT that is independent of any existing TWT agreements or TWT schedules is described in 26.8.4.4
44
(TWT Information frame exchange for flexible wake time).
45
46
47 26.8.4.2 TWT Information frame exchange for individual TWT
48
49
An HE STA that has an individual TWT agreement may transmit a TWT Information frame to a peer STA
50
51 with which it has the agreement if the peer STA has set the TWT Information Frame Disabled field to 0 in
52 the TWT element sent during TWT setup; otherwise the HE STA shall not transmit a TWT Information
53 frame to the peer STA. The HE STA sets the fields of the TWT Information frame as defined in 26.8.4.1
54
(General).
55
56
57 A TWT requesting STA that receives a TWT Information frame containing a Next TWT subfield follows
58 the rules in 10.47.4 (Implicit TWT operation) and the rules below.
59
60
61 A TWT requesting STA that receives an acknowledgment in response to a TWT Information frame trans-
62 mitted by the STA:
63
— That does not contain a Next TWT subfield shall consider the corresponding TWT agreement sus-
64
65 pended until the TWT session is resumed.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 429
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 430 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — That contains a Next TWT subfield shall consider the corresponding TWT agreement suspended and
2 shall resume the TWT agreement starting from the value indicated in the Next TWT subfield of the
3
transmitted TWT Information frame.
4
5 — Assumes that any other individual TWT agreements, broadcast TWT schedules (see 26.8.3 (Broad-
6
cast TWT operation)), are not affected by the transmission of this frame except when the All TWT
7
subfield of the TWT Information frame is equal to 1. Other default PS procedures are not affected by
8
9 the transmission of this frame (see 11.2 (Power management)).
10
11 NOTE—The TWT Flow Identifier, together with the MAC addresses of the TWT requesting STA and TWT responding
12 STA identifies the TWT agreement for which the TWT Information frame is sent (see 10.47.1 (TWT overview)).
13
14 If the TWT Information frame contains an All TWT subfield equal to 1 then the above rules apply to all indi-
15
vidual TWT agreements, except that the resumptions of the respective TWTs shall occur at the first TWT of
16
the respective TWT agreement that occurs not earlier than the Next TWT value contained in the TWT Infor-
17
18 mation frame, regardless of the value of the Flexible TWT Schedule Support field in the HE Capabilities
19 element exchanged between the two STAs.
20
21
A TWT requesting STA that is in PS mode and that transmits a TWT Information frame to a TWT respond-
22
23 ing STA shall suspend the corresponding TWT agreement and may transition to doze state after receiving
24 the acknowledgment even if it has previously transmitted a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame and has not yet
25 received the expected frames from the AP in response and shall resume TWT operation for the correspond-
26
ing TWT agreement at the specified TWT indicated (if any) in the TWT Information frame. A TWT request-
27
ing STA that is in PS mode and that receives a TWT Information frame from a TWT responding STA shall
28
29 suspend the TWT agreement and may go to doze state after transmitting the acknowledgment even if it has
30 previously transmitted a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame and has not yet received the expected frames
31 from the TWT responding STA in response and shall resume TWT operation for the corresponding TWT
32
agreement at the specified TWT indicated (if any) in the TWT Information frame.
33
34
35 26.8.4.3 TWT Information frame exchange for broadcast TWT
36
37
An HE STA that is a TWT scheduling AP may transmit a TWT Information frame to any of the members of
38
39 a broadcast TWT schedule if the member has set the TWT Information Frame Disabled field to 0 in the
40 TWT element sent when joining the broadcast TWT schedule. An HE STA that is a TWT scheduled STA
41 may transmit a TWT Information frame to the TWT scheduling AP corresponding to a broadcast TWT
42
schedule established by that STA if the AP has set the TWT Information Frame Disabled field to 0 in the
43
broadcast TWT element it transmits. The HE STA sets the fields of the TWT Information frame as defined
44
45 in 26.8.4.1 (General).
46
47
A TWT scheduled STA that receives a TWT Information frame that contains an All TWT subfield equal to
48
49 1 follows the rules defined in 26.8.3.3 (Rules for TWT scheduled STA), except that the TWT scheduled
50 STA shall consider all the broadcast TWT schedules as suspended and shall resume each broadcast TWT
51 schedule at the first TWT that occurs not earlier than the Next TWT subfield value contained in the received
52
TWT Information frame.
53
54
55 A TWT scheduled STA that receives an acknowledgment in response to a TWT Information frame transmit-
56 ted by the STA that contains an All TWT subfield equal to 1 and that does not contain a Next TWT subfield
57
shall consider all broadcast TWT schedules suspended, and can follow the default PS procedure defined in
58
11.2 (Power management) until the broadcast TWT schedules are resumed.
59
60
61 A TWT scheduled STA that receives an acknowledgment in response to a TWT Information frame transmit-
62
ted by the STA that contains an All TWT subfield equal to 1 and that contains a Next TWT subfield shall
63
suspend all broadcast TWT schedules and shall resume the broadcast TWT schedules at the first scheduled
64
65 TWT for each respective broadcast TWT schedule that occurs not earlier than the value indicated in the Next
430 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 431 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 TWT subfield contained in the transmitted TWT Information frame, regardless of the values of the Flexible
2 TWT Schedule Support field in the HE Capabilities element exchanged between the two STAs.
3
4 NOTE—TWT suspension and resumption as indicated by a TWT Information frame with the All TWT subfield equal to
5 1 applies to all broadcast TWT schedules of the TWT scheduling AP.
6
7
A TWT scheduled STA that is in PS mode and that transmits a TWT Information frame to a TWT schedul-
8
ing AP shall suspend the corresponding broadcast TWT schedule and may transition to doze state after
9
10 receiving the acknowledgment, even if it has previously transmitted a PS-Poll or U-APSD trigger frame and
11 has not yet received the expected frames from the TWT scheduling AP in response, and shall resume TWT
12 operation for the corresponding broadcast TWT schedule at the specified TWT indicated (if any) in the
13
TWT Information frame. A TWT scheduled STA that is in PS mode and that receives a TWT Information
14
frame from a TWT scheduling AP shall suspend the corresponding broadcast TWT schedule and may transi-
15
16 tion to doze state after transmitting the acknowledgment, even if it has previously transmitted a PS-Poll or
17 U-APSD trigger frame and has not yet received the expected frames from the TWT scheduling AP in
18 response, and shall resume TWT operation for the corresponding broadcast TWT schedule at the specified
19
TWT indicated (if any) in the TWT Information frame.
20
21
22 26.8.4.4 TWT Information frame exchange for flexible wake time
23
24
An HE STA may transmit a TWT Information frame that contains a flexible TWT to a peer STA if the peer
25
STA has set the Flexible TWT Schedule Support field in the HE Capabilities it transmits to 1; otherwise the
26
27 HE STA shall not transmit a TWT Information frame that contains a flexible TWT to the peer STA.
28
29 A flexible TWT is a nonzero value indicated in the Next TWT subfield of a TWT Information frame with
30
All TWT subfield equal to 0, which is independent from any existing TWT values of TWT agreements that
31
the HE STA might be following (if any). The HE STA sets the fields of the transmitted TWT Information
32
33 frame as defined in 26.8.4.1 (General).
34
35 NOTE—Flexible TWT support does not depend on the STA’s TWT capabilities, i.e., the STA can use flexible TWT
36 without being required to set up an individual TWT agreement or broadcast TWT schedule.
37
38 An HE STA that receives acknowledgment for a TWT Information frame with flexible TWT and that con-
39 tains a TWT Flow Identifier that identifies an existing individual TWT agreement shall replace the next
40
TWT SP start time for that TWT agreement with the value contained in the Next TWT subfield of the TWT
41
Information frame.
42
43
44 A non-AP HE STA that receives acknowledgment for a TWT Information frame with flexible TWT and that
45
contains a TWT Flow Identifier that does not identify any existing individual TWT agreement preserves the
46
PM mode from the time the TWT Information frame was sent to the time indicated in the Next TWT sub-
47
48 field of the TWT Information frame as described below.
49
50 NOTE—If the TWT Information frame has the All TWT field equal to 1 then the TWTs are suspended and resumed as
51 described in 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame exchange for individual TWT) and 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame
52 exchange for broadcast TWT).
53
54 A non-AP HE STA that transmits a TWT Information frame that contains a flexible TWT to a peer STA
55 may go to doze state after receiving the acknowledgment sent in response to the TWT Information frame if
56
it is in PS mode (i.e., the PM subfield of the Frame Control field of the TWT Information frame is 1) and
57
may be unavailable if it is in active mode (i.e., the PM subfield of the Frame Control field of the TWT Infor-
58
59 mation frame is 0) and shall be in the awake state at the time it indicated in the Next TWT subfield of the
60 TWT Information frame and shall be in the PS mode if the PM subfield of the TWT Information frame was
61 1 and in active mode if the PM subfield of the TWT Information frame was 0. The STA, once in the awake
62
state, shall follow the rules that correspond to the power management mode of the STA, which are defined in
63
11.2.3 (Power management in a non-DMG infrastructure network) for the Active and PS mode, and in 26.8
64
65 (TWT operation) when the STA operates within TWT SPs.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 431
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 432 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 NOTE—An HE AP delivers DL BUs to the STA at or after the flexible TWT indicated in the flexible TWT by following
2 the rules in 11.2.3.6 (AP operation) if the STA does not follow TWT, and by following the rules in 26.8 (TWT opera-
3 tion) if the STA follows TWT and the delivery falls within a TWT SP. The STA is not required to send a frame at or
4 after the flexible TWT to indicate its awake state to the AP. If the STA is following U-APSD then the operation is
5 resumed at a time that occurs at the flexible TWT and if the STA is following an APSD schedule then the operation is
6 resumed at a time that occurs at or after the flexible TWT.
7
8
A non-AP HE STA that receives a TWT Information frame that contains a flexible TWT from a peer STA
9
may go to doze state after transmitting the acknowledgment if it is in PS mode and may be unavailable if it
10
11 is in active mode and shall be in the awake state at the time it indicated in the Next TWT subfield of the
12 TWT Information frame and shall be in the PS mode if the STA was in PS mode when it received the TWT
13 Information frame and in active mode if the STA was in active mode when it received the TWT Information
14
frame. The STA, once in the awake state, shall follow the rules that correspond to the power management
15
mode of the STA, which are defined in 11.2.3 (Power management in a non-DMG infrastructure network)
16
17 for the Active and PS mode, and in 26.8 (TWT operation) when the STA operates within TWT SPs.
18
19
26.8.5 Power save operation during TWT SPs
20
21
22 The following rules apply to TWT SPs for both broadcast TWT schedules and individual TWT agreements
23 where the TWT SP of a broadcast TWT is uniquely identified by the <broadcast TWT ID, MAC address of
24
TWT scheduling AP> tuple and the TWT SP of an individual TWT is uniquely identified by the <TWT flow
25
identifier, MAC address of TWT requesting STA, MAC address of TWT responding STA> triple.
26
27
28 A TWT requesting STA or a TWT scheduled STA that is not in PS mode and that transmits a frame with the
29 Power Management subfield set to 1 during a TWT SP shall remain in the awake state until the Adjusted-
30
MinimumTWTWakeDuration time has elapsed from the TWT SP start time or until a TWT SP termination
31
event is detected, whichever occurs first for that particular TWT SP.
32
33
34 A TWT requesting STA or a TWT scheduled STA in PS mode that is in the awake state for a TWT SP may
35
transition to the doze state after AdjustedMinimumTWTWakeDuration time has elapsed from the TWT SP
36
start time even if it has previously transmitted a PS-Poll frame or U-APSD trigger frame and has not yet
37
38 received the expected frames from the AP in response. For a trigger-enabled TWT SP, if the AdjustedMini-
39 mumTWTWakeDuration time has elapsed from the scheduled TWT SP start time and no Trigger frames are
40 received by the STA, the HE STA may enter doze state if no other condition requires the STA to remain
41
awake.
42
43
44 When a TWT SP termination event is detected within a TWT SP by a STA in PS mode that is participating
45 in the TWT SP, the STA may transition to the doze state without waiting for the expiration of the Adjusted-
46
MinimumTWTWakeDuration time as described in 10.47.1 (TWT overview), even if it has previously trans-
47
mitted a PS-Poll frame or U-APSD trigger frame and has not yet received the expected frames from the AP
48
49 in response.
50
51
A TWT requesting STA or a TWT scheduled STA shall classify any of the following events as a TWT SP
52
termination event:
53
54 1) The transmission by the TWT requesting STA of an acknowledgment in response to an individ-
55
ually addressed QoS Data or QoS Null frame sent by the TWT responding STA that had the
56
EOSP subfield equal to 1.
57
58
2) The transmission by the TWT scheduled STA of an acknowledgment in response to an individ-
59
ually addressed QoS Data or QoS Null frame sent by the TWT scheduling AP that had the
60
61 EOSP subfield equal to 1.
62
3) The transmission by the TWT requesting STA of an acknowledgment in response to an individ-
63
ually addressed frame that is neither a QoS Data frame nor a QoS Null frame, sent by the TWT
64
65 responding STA with the More Data field equal to 0.
432 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 433 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 4) The transmission by the TWT scheduled STA of an acknowledgment in response to an individ-
2 ually addressed frame that is neither a QoS Data frame nor a QoS Null frame, sent by the TWT
3
scheduling AP with the More Data field equal to 0.
4
5 5) The reception of an individually addressed or broadcast QoS Data or QoS Null frame sent by
6 the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP, that does not solicit an immediate response
7
and with the EOSP subfield equal to 1.
8
9 6) The reception of an individually addressed frame that is neither a QoS Data frame nor a QoS
10 Null frame, sent by the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP, that does not solicit an
11 immediate response and with the More Data field equal to 0.
12
13 7) The reception of a Trigger frame sent by the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP that
14 has the More TF field equal to 0 and is not addressed to the TWT requesting STA or TWT
15 scheduled STA provided that the TWT requesting STA or TWT scheduled STA is either awake
16
for an announced trigger-enabled TWT SP but did not transmit an indication that it is in the
17
awake state to the TWT responding STA or TWT scheduling AP or is awake for an unan-
18
19 nounced trigger-enabled TWT SP.
20 8) The transmission or reception by the TWT requesting STA of the acknowledgment for a TWT
21
Information frame that satisfies specific conditions in 26.8.4.2 (TWT Information frame
22
exchange for individual TWT) and 26.8.4.4 (TWT Information frame exchange for flexible
23
24 wake time).
25
9) The transmission or reception by the TWT scheduled STA of the acknowledgment for a TWT
26
Information frame that satisfies specific conditions in 26.8.4.3 (TWT Information frame
27
28 exchange for broadcast TWT) and 26.8.4.4 (TWT Information frame exchange for flexible
29 wake time).
30
31
The classification of a More Data field equal to 0 in an Ack, BlockAck and individually addressed Multi-
32
STA BlockAck frame as an event that terminates a TWT SP is only possible when both STAs have indicated
33
34 support of transmitting or receiving the frame with a nonzero More Data subfield, which is indicated in the
35 More Data Ack subfield of the QoS Info field of frames they transmit (see 11.2.3 (Power management in a
36 non-DMG infrastructure network)).
37
38 NOTE 1—A STA participating in multiple TWT SPs that overlap in time stays in the awake state until the latest Adjust-
39 edMinimumTWTWakeDuration time of all of the TWT SPs expires, except that a TWT SP termination event causes all
40 of the overlapping TWT SPs to terminate.
41
NOTE 2—A Trigger frame is addressed to the STA if the Trigger frame contains the AID of the STA in one of its User
42
Info fields (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)), and has in its TA field either the MAC address of its associated AP or the
43
transmitted BSSID (see 26.5.2.2.4 (Allowed settings of the Trigger frame fields and TRS Control subfield)). Otherwise,
44
the Trigger frame is not addressed to the STA. If the Trigger frame contains one or more RA-RUs for which the STA can
45
gain access according to 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA)) then the STA can follow the rules defined
46
in 26.14.2 (Power save with UORA and TWT) to determine an early TWT SP termination event.
47
48
49 26.8.6 Negotiation of wake TBTT and wake interval
50
51 A TBTT scheduled STA that intends to operate in power save mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA power
52
management modes)) may transmit a TWT request to the TBTT scheduling AP that identifies the wake
53
TBTT of the first Beacon frame and the wake interval between subsequent Beacon frames it intends to
54
55 receive. The TWT request shall contain:
56
— The Negotiation Type subfield equal to 1 and the TWT Setup Command field to Suggest TWT or
57
Demand TWT
58
59 — The requested first wake TBTT in the Target Wake Time field
60
— The requested wake interval between consecutive TBTTs in the TWT Wake Interval Mantissa and
61
62 TWT Wake Interval Exponent fields
63 — The requested TBTT wake duration in the Nominal Minimum TWT Wake Duration field
64
65 — All other fields in the TWT element are reserved.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 433
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 434 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 A TBTT scheduling AP that receives a TWT request from a STA whose value of the Negotiation Type sub-
2 field is 1 shall respond with a TWT response that contains either Accept TWT, Alternate TWT, or Reject
3
TWT in the TWT Setup Command field and, in the case of an Accept TWT, it shall also contain:
4
5 — The Negotiation Type subfield equal to 1
6
— The allocated first wake TBTT in the Target Wake Time field
7
8 — The allocated wake interval between consecutive TBTTs in the TWT Wake Interval Mantissa and
9 TWT Wake Interval Exponent fields
10
— The allocated TBTT wake duration in the Nominal Minimum TWT Wake Duration field
11
12 — All other fields in the TWT element are reserved
13
14
After successfully completing the negotiation, the TBTT scheduled STA may go to doze state until its TSF
15
16 matches the next negotiated wake TBTT provided that the STA is in power save mode, and no other condi-
17 tion requires the STA to remain awake. The TBTT scheduled STA shall be in the awake state to listen to
18 Beacon frames transmitted at negotiated wake TBTTs and shall operate as described in 26.8.3.3 (Rules for
19
TWT scheduled STA).
20
21
22 If the TBTT scheduled STA receives a Beacon frame from the TBTT scheduling AP at or after TBTT, the
23 TBTT scheduled STA may go to doze state until the next wake TBTT if no other condition requires the STA
24 to remain awake. The TBTT scheduled STA may go to doze state after a nominal minimum TBTT wake
25
duration time has elapsed from the TBTT start time if no other condition requires the STA to remain awake.
26
27
28 Either STA that is a party to an established wake TBTT agreement can tear down the wake TBTT agreement
29 by following the tear down procedure described in 10.47.8 (TWT Teardown) and by setting the Negotiation
30 Type subfield to 1 in the TWT Teardown frame.
31
32

33
34 a Wake TBTT agreement.

14 26.8.7 HE subchannel selective transmission
15
16
26.8.7.1 General
17
18
19 An HE STA that supports HE subchannel selective transmission (SST) operation shall set dot11HESubchan-
20
nelSelectiveTransmissionImplemented to true and shall set the HE Subchannel Selective Transmission Sup-
21
port field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1. An HE STA that does not support HE SST
22
23 operation shall set the HE Subchannel Selective Transmission Support field in the HE Capabilities element
24 it transmits to 0.
25
26
A non-AP HE STA with dot11HESubchannelSelectiveTransmissionImplemented equal to true is a HE SST
27
28 non-AP STA.
29
30 An HE AP with dot11HESubchannelSelectiveTransmissionImplemented equal to true is an HE SST AP.
31
32
33 An HE SST non-AP STA and an HE SST AP may set up SST operation by negotiating a trigger-enabled
34 TWT as defined in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements) except that:
35
— The TWT request may have a TWT Channel field with up to one bit set to 1 to indicate the secondary
36
37 channel requested to contain the RU allocations addressed to the HE SST non-AP STA that is a 20
38 MHz operating STA
39
— The TWT request may have a TWT Channel field with all the four LSBs or all the four MSBs set to
40
41 1 to indicate whether the primary 80 MHz channel or the secondary 80 MHz channel is requested to
42 contain the RU allocations addressed to the HE SST non-AP STA that is an 80 MHz operating STA
43
— The TWT response shall have a TWT Channel field with up to one bit set to 1 to indicate the second-
44
45 ary channel that will contain the RU allocations addressed to the HE SST non-AP STA that is a 20
46 MHz operating STA
47
— The TWT response shall have a TWT Channel field with all the 4 LSBs or all the 4 MSBs to indicate
48
49 whether the primary 80 MHz channel or the secondary 80 MHz channel will contain the RU alloca-
50 tions addressed to the HE SST non-AP STA that is a 80 MHz operating STA.
51
52
26.8.7.2 SST operation
53
54
55 An HE SST non-AP STA and HE SST AP that successfully sets up SST operation shall follow the rules
56 defined in this subclause.
57
58
If an HE SST AP causes its operating channel or channel width to change and if any secondary channel of a
59
60 trigger-enabled TWT is not within the new operating channel or channel width, then the HE SST AP and the
61 HE SST non-AP STA implicitly terminate the trigger-enabled TWT.
62
63
The HE SST AP follows the rules in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements) to exchange frames with the HE
64
65 SST non-AP STA during trigger-enabled TWT SPs, except that the AP shall ensure the following:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 435
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 436 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — The individually addressed RUs allocated in DL MU PPDUs and in Trigger frames addressed to the
2 HE SST non-AP STA are within the subchannel indicated in the TWT Channel field of the TWT
3
response and follows the RU restriction rules defined in 27.3.2.8 (RU restrictions for 20 MHz opera-
4
tion) if the HE SST non-AP STA is a 20 MHz operating STA and in 27.3.2.9 (80 MHz operating
5
6 non-AP HE STAs) if the HE SST non-AP STA is an 80 MHz operating STA.
7
— The TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH of a DL MU PPDU is not set to HE-CBW-
8
9 PUNC160-PRI20, HE-CBW-PUNC80+80-PRI20, HE-CBW-PUNC160-SEC40 or HE-CBW-
10 PUNC80+80-SEC40 if the DL MU PPDU is addressed to at least one HE SST non-AP STA that is
11 an 80 MHz operating STA operating in a secondary channel.
12
13 — The trigger-enabled TWT SPs do not overlap with TBTTs at which DTIM Beacon frames are sent.
14
— The same subchannel is used for all trigger-enabled TWT SPs with the same HE SST non-AP STA
15
16 that overlap in time.
17
18
An HE SST non-AP STA operating on the secondary channel shall not conduct OMI operation as defined in
19
26.9 (Operating mode indication) or OMN operation as defined in 11.41 (Notification of operating mode
20
21 changes) to change the operating bandwidth.
22
23
The HE SST non-AP STA follows the rules in 26.8.2 (Individual TWT agreements) to exchange frames with the
24
HE SST AP during trigger-enabled TWT SPs, except that the STA:
25
26 — Shall be available in the subchannel indicated in the TWT Channel field of the TWT response at
27
TWT start times
28
29 — Shall not access the medium in the subchannel using DCF or EDCAF
30
31 — Shall not respond to Trigger frames addressed to it (see 26.5 (MU operation) and 26.8.2 (Individual
32 TWT agreements)) unless it has performed CCA until a frame is detected by which it can set its NAV,
33
or until a period equal to NAVSyncDelay has transpired, whichever is earlier.
34
35 — Shall update its NAV according to 26.2.4 (Updating two NAVs) if it receives a PPDU in the sub-
36 channel
37
38
39 An HE SST non-AP STA may include a Channel Switch Timing element in (Re-)Association Request
40 frames it transmits to an HE SST AP to indicate the time required by the STA to switch between different
41 subchannels. The received channel switch time informs the HE SST AP of the duration of time that the HE
42
SST non-AP STA might not be available to receive frames before the TWT start time and after the end of the
43
trigger-enabled TWT SP.
44
45
NOTE—An HE SST STA in PS mode is not required to move to the primary channel after the end of the trigger-enabled
46
TWT SP.

26.9 Operating mode indication
50
51
52
26.9.1 General
53
54
55 OMI is a procedure used between an OMI initiator and an OMI responder. An HE STA that transmits a
56 frame including an OM Control subfield is defined as an OMI initiator. An HE STA with dot11OMIOption-
57
Implemented equal to true that receives a frame including an OM Control subfield is defined as an OMI
58
responder.
59
60
61 An HE STA with dot11OMIOptionImplemented equal to true shall set the OM Control Support subfield in
62
the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1; otherwise the
63
HE STA shall set the OM Control Support subfield to 0. An HE AP shall set dot11OMIOptionImplemented
64
65 to true and the HE AP shall implement the reception of the OM Control subfield.
436 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 437 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 An OMI initiator may send to an OMI responder an individually addressed QoS Data, QoS Null or Class 3
2 Management frame after association that contains the OM Control subfield and that solicits an immediate
3
acknowledgment to indicate a change in its receive operating mode (ROM) as defined in 26.9.2 (Receive
4
operating mode (ROM) indication) and/or transmit operating parameters (TOM) as defined in 26.9.3 (Trans-
5
6 mit operating mode (TOM) indication). An OMI responder implements the reception of an individually
7 addressed QoS Data, QoS Null or Class 3 Management frame that contains the OM Control subfield that
8
indicates a change in ROM and/or TOM parameters.
9
10
11
The OMI initiator shall indicate a change in its ROM parameters by including the OM Control subfield in a
12
QoS Data, QoS Null or Class 3 Management frame that solicits acknowledgment and is addressed to the
13
14 OMI responder as defined in 26.9.2 (Receive operating mode (ROM) indication).
15
16 NOTE—Frames that solicit an immediate acknowledgment are, for example, QoS Null frames and QoS Data frames
17 with Normal Ack or Implicit BAR ack policy and Action frames.
18
19
20 An HE STA can change its operating mode setting using either operating mode notification as described in
21 11.42 (Notification of operating mode changes), or the operating mode indication (OMI) procedure
22
described in this subclause. An HE STA should not transmit an OM Control subfield and an Operating
23
Mode field in the same PPDU. If a STA transmits both an OM Control subfield and Operating Mode field in
24
25 the same PPDU, then the OMI responder shall use the channel width and the maximum number of spatial
26 streams indicated by the most recently received OM Control subfield or Operating Mode field from the OMI
27
initiator.
28
29
30 NOTE—An OM Control field is transmitted before an Operating Mode field in the same frame.
31
32
The OMI initiator supports receiving PPDUs with a bandwidth up to the value indicated by the Channel
33
34 Width subfield and with a number of spatial streams, Nss, as indicated in the Rx NSS subfield of the OM
35 Control subfield and calculated in the Equation(26-4).
36
37
38 If the operating channel width of the STA is greater than 80 MHz, then the maximum number of spatial
39 streams that the STA supports in reception for a given HE-MCS as a function of the received HE PPDU
40
bandwidth BW at an HE STA transmitting an OM Control subfield is defined in Equation(26-4).
41
42
43
floor (Rx-NSS-from-OMI × (Max-HE-NSS-at-BW / Max-HE-NSS-at-80)) (26-4)
44
45
46 where
47
48 Rx-NSS-from-OMI is Rx NSS from the OM Control subfield transmitted by the STA
49
50 Max-HE-NSS-at-BW is the maximum NSS among all HE-MCS at BW MHz from the Supported HE-MCS
51 And NSS Set field transmitted by the STA as described in 26.15.4 (Rate selection constraints
52 for HE STAs)
53
54 Max-HE-NSS-at-80 is the maximum NSS among all HE-MCS at 80 MHz from the Supported HE-MCS
55
And NSS Set field transmitted by the STA
56
57
58 NOTE—If the operating channel width of the STA is greater than 80 MHz, then the Rx NSS subfield indicates the max-
59 imum number of spatial streams for PPDU bandwidths that are equal to or less than 80 MHz. If the operating channel
60 width of the STA is less than or equal to 80 MHz, then the Rx NSS subfield indicates the maximum number of spatial
61 streams, N SS , that the STA supports in reception.
62
63
The VHT channel width and the VHT NSS allowed at an HE STA transmitting an OM Control subfield are
64
65 defined in Table26-9 (Setting of the VHT Channel Width and VHT NSS at an HE STA transmitting the OM

19 values, the Max VHT NSS values are same, but the supported NSS can be different.
20
NOTE 2—1/2 × or 3/4 × Max VHT NSS support might end up being 0, indicating no support.
21
22 NOTE 3—Any other combination than the ones listed in this table is reserved.
23
24 NOTE 4—CCFS1 refers to the value of the Channel Center Frequency Segment 1 field of the most recently transmitted
25 VHT Operation element (if any) or HE Operation element.
26
NOTE 5—CCFS2 refers to the value of the Channel Center Frequency Segment 2 field of the most recently transmitted HT
27
Operation element.
28
29 NOTE 6—CCFS1 is nonzero when the current BSS bandwidth is 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz and the NSS support is at least
30 Max VHT NSS. CCFS2 is zero in this case.
31
NOTE 7—CCFS2 is nonzero when the current BSS bandwidth is 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz and the NSS support is less than
32
Max VHT NSS. CCFS1 is zero in this case.
33
34 NOTE 8—At most one of CCFS1 and CCFS2 is nonzero.
35
36 NOTE 9—A supported multiple of Max VHT NSS applies to both transmit and receive. A supported multiple of Max HE
37 NSS applies to receive
38
NOTE 10—Some combinations of Supported Channel Width Set and Extended NSS BW support might not occur in prac-
39
tice.
40
41
42
43 The OMI initiator shall indicate a change in its TOM parameters by including the OM Control subfield in a
44 QoS Data, QoS Null or Class 3 Management frame that solicits an immediate acknowledgment frame and is
45
addressed to the OMI responder as defined in 26.9.3 (Transmit operating mode (TOM) indication).
46
47
48 A non-AP STA OMI initiator that sends an OM Control subfield with UL MU Disable subfield equal to 0,
49 supports transmitting an HE TB PPDU with an RU allocation that is within the operating channel width indi-
50 cated in the Channel Width subfield and with a number of space-time streams, N , that is up to the value
STS
51
indicated by the Tx NSTS subfield of the OM Control subfield as defined in 27.8.3 (Transmit operating
52
mode (TOM) indication).
53
54
NOTE 1—To avoid possible frame loss, an OMI initiator can continue with its current operating channel width and
55
active receive chains and active transmit chains in HE TB PPDUs and not suspend HE TB PPDUs or Data frames in HE
56
TB PPDUs until it infers that the OMI responder has processed an OM Control subfield from the OMI initiator indicat-
57
ing any of the following changes:
58
59 — Reduced operating channel width
60
— Reduction in the number of active receive chains
61
62 — Reduction in the number of active transmit chains in HE TB PPDUs
63 — Suspension of UL MU operation
64
65 The OMI initiator might make this inference from any combination of the following:

## 第 440 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — By receiving a frame addressed to itself from the second HE STA in a PPDU with a bandwidth and NSS that are
2 less than or equal to the channel width and NSS, respectively, indicated in the OM Control subfield
3
— Based on the passage of time in some implementation dependent way, which is outside the scope of this Stan-
4
dard
5
6
NOTE 2—It might take a long time for a STA to change its operating mode following the transmission of the OM Con-
7
trol subfield and during that time the STA might not be able to receive frames resulting in frame loss. If a non-AP STA
8
cannot tolerate frame loss during that period it can set the Power Management subfield of the Frame Control field of the
9
frame that carries the OM Control subfield to 1 to indicate that the STA has entered power save. When the non-AP STA
10
has completed its operating mode change, it can send another frame (such as a QoS Null) with the Frame Control field
11
Power Management subfield set to 0 to indicate that the STA has exited power save.
