26.9.2 Receive operating mode (ROM) indication
14
15
16 ROM indication allows the OMI initiator to adapt the maximum operating channel width and/or the maxi-
17
mum number of spatial streams, Nss, it can receive from the OMI responder.
18
19
20 An OMI initiator that sends a frame that includes an OM Control subfield should change its OMI parame-
21 ters, Rx NSS and Channel Width, as follows:
22
— When the OMI initiator changes a ROM parameter from higher to lower, it should make the change
23
24 for that parameter only after the TXOP in which it received the immediate acknowledgment from
25 the OMI responder.
26
— When the OMI initiator changes a ROM parameter from lower to higher, it should make the change
27
28 for that parameter only after the TXOP in which it expects to receive acknowledgment from the
29 OMI responder.
30
31
An OMI initiator that is an HE AP should be capable of receiving within an operating channel width and
32
with N that are up to the values of the most recently transmitted Channel Width subfield and Rx NSS sub-
33 SS
34 field that the OMI initiator has successfully indicated in the OM Control subfield or in the Operating Mode
35 field sent to any associated STA.
36
37 NOTE—In the event of transmission failure of the frame containing the OM Control subfield, the OMI initiator attempts
38 the recovery procedure defined in 10.23.2.8 (Multiple frame transmission in an EDCA TXOP).
39
40 The OMI responder shall update the operating channel width and the maximum N values as obtained from
SS
41
the Channel Width and Rx NSS subfields, respectively, of the most recently received OM Control subfield
42
sent by the OMI initiator to send SU PPDUs and to assign an RU allocation in sent MU PPDUs, subject to
43
44 restrictions defined in 27.3.1.2 (OFDMA), addressed to the OMI initiator in subsequent TXOPs. The OMI
45 responder shall update the maximum N value to a value determined from the Rx NSS subfield and
SS
46 Table26-9 (Setting of the VHT Channel Width and VHT NSS at an HE STA transmitting the OM Control
47
subfield) if the Channel Width subfield of the OM Control field indicates 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz.
48
49
50 After transmitting the acknowledgment for the frame containing the OM Control subfield, the OMI
51 responder may transmit subsequent SU PPDUs or MU PPDUs that are addressed to the OMI initiator.
52
53 NOTE—The acknowledgment is transmitted a SIFS after the frame. A subsequent PPDU is a PPDU that is intended for
54 the OMI initiator and need not be the PPDU immediately following the acknowledgment.
55
56
An OMI initiator that is a non-AP STA may set the DL MU-MIMO Resound Recommendation subfield to 1
57
in the OM Control field in frames addressed to an OMI responder that is an AP to indicate that the non-AP
58
59 STA suggests that the AP resound the channel with the non-AP STA. An OMI initiator that is a non-AP
60 STA and that has no recommendation on the AP’s DL MU-MIMO operation shall set DL MU-MIMO
61 Resound Recommendation subfield to 0.
62
63
An OMI responder that receives a frame that carries an OM Control field with the DL MU-MIMO Resound
64
65 Recommendation field equal to 1 from an OMI initiator may resound the channel or increase the frequency
440 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 of the channel sounding with the OMI initiator if the OMI responder sends a DL MU-MIMO PPDU
2 addressed to the OMI initiator.

26.9.3 Transmit operating mode (TOM) indication
5
6
7 TOM indication allows the OMI initiator to suspend and resume responding to variants of the Trigger frame
8
and TRS Control subfields per the UL MU Disable and UL MU Data Disable subfields settings as indicated
9
in Table9-24b (UL MU Disable and UL MU Data Disable subfields encoding), or to adapt the maximum
10
11 operating channel width and/or the maximum number of space-time streams, N STS , that it can transmit in
12 response to a triggering frame sent by the OMI responder
13
14 NOTE—TOM indication does not relate to transmissions in PPDUs other than HE TB PPDUs. An AP does not perform
15 TOM indication as an OMI initiator.
16
17 An OMI initiator that is a non-AP STA may indicate changes in its transmit parameters by sending a frame
18
that contains the OM Control subfield to the OMI responder. The OMI initiator shall set:
19
20 — The UL MU Disable subfield to 1, to indicate that responding to a triggering frame is suspended (see
21 26.5.2 (UL MU operation).
22
23 • An AP that is an OMI initiator shall set the UL MU Disable subfield to 0.
24
— The Tx NSTS subfield to the maximum N that the STA will use for an HE TB PPDU sent in
25 STS
response to a triggering frame.
26
27 — The Channel Width subfield to the maximum operating channel width that the STA will use for an
28
HE TB PPDU sent in response to a triggering frame.
29
30
NOTE—Responding to all Trigger frame variants, including MU-RTS Trigger frames and NFRP Trigger frames, is sus-
31
pended.
32
33
If a non-AP HE STA has received the OM Control UL MU Data Disable RX Support field in the HE Capa-
34
35 bilities element set to 1, then the non-AP HE STA, acting as an OMI initiator, may set the UL MU Disable
36 subfield to 0 and the UL MU Data Disable subfield to 1 to indicate that only UL MU Data frame transmis-
37 sion is suspended but UL MU control response frame transmissions in response to a Basic Trigger frame is
38
not suspended (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)) Responses to other Trigger frame variants are not suspended.
39
management frame transmissions are not suspended.
40
41
42 An OMI initiator shall set the UL MU Disable subfield to 0 and the UL MU Data Disable subfield to 0 to
43 indicate resumption or continuation of participation in all triggered UL MU operations.
44
45
46 If an HE AP has set the OM Control UL MU Data Disable RX Support field in the HE Capabilities element
47 it transmits to 0, an associated STA shall not set the UL MU Data Disable subfield in the OM Control field
48 to 1.
49
50
An OMI initiator that sent a frame including the OM Control subfield should change its TOM parameters,
51
52 Tx NSTS, UL MU Disable, UL MU Data Disable and Channel Width, as follows:
53
— If the OMI initiator changes a TOM parameter from higher to lower, it should make the change for
54
that parameter only after the TXOP in which it received the immediate acknowledgment from the
55
56 OMI responder.
57
— If the OMI initiator changes a TOM parameter from lower to higher, it should make the change for
58
that parameter only after the TXOP in which it expects to receive acknowledgment from the OMI
59
60 responder.
61
62
The TOM parameters UL MU Disable and UL MU Data Disable changes from higher to lower if its value
63
changes from 0 to 1. The change of UL MU Disable from 1 to 0 and UL MU Data Disable from 0 to 1 is a
64
65 change from lower to higher.

40
41 26.10 Spatial reuse operation
42
43
26.10.1 General
44
45
46 The objective of HE spatial reuse operation is to allow the medium to be reused more often between OBSSs
47 in dense deployment scenarios by the early identification of signals from overlapping basic service sets
48
(OBSSs) and interference management.
49
50
51 There are two independent spatial reuse modes: OBSS PD-based spatial reuse and PSR-based spatial reuse.
52
53 An HE AP participating in spatial reuse may request an associated non-AP HE STA to gather information
54
regarding the neighborhood by sending a Beacon request (see 9.4.2.21.7 (Beacon request)) by following the
55
procedure described in 11.11 (Radio measurement procedures). An HE AP shall not set a measurement
56
57 mode in a Beacon request to an associated STA to a mode for which the STA has not explicitly indicated
58 support via the RM Enabled Capabilities element (see 9.4.2.45 (RM Enabled Capabilities element)). An HE
59 AP that sends a Beacon request for this purpose:
60
61 — May request that the non-AP HE STA gather information of BSSs matching a particular BSSID and/
62 or SSID
63
— May request that the non-AP HE STA generate a report that is only for the channel on which the
64
65 requesting AP is operating or to which the requesting AP is considering switching
442 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 443 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — Shall request that the non-AP HE STA include the HE Operation element of neighboring HE APs in
2 order to help determine the BSS color information of the neighboring APs
3
4
A non-AP HE STA that performs spatial reuse operation shall have dot11RadioMeasurementActivated set
5
6 to true and shall respond to a Beacon request from its associated AP with a Beacon report as described in
7 11.11 (Radio measurement procedures).
8
9
A Class B device as defined in 27.3.15.3 (Pre-correction accuracy requirements) shall not operate with the
10
11 procedures defined in this subclause.
12
13 26.10.2 OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation
14
15
26.10.2.1 General
16
17
18 OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation comprises two types of operation. The first type is defined in
19 26.10.2.2 (General operation with non-SRG OBSS PD level), and allows a STA, under specific conditions,
20
to ignore an inter-BSS PPDU using a non-SRG OBSS PD level. The second type is defined in 26.10.2.3
21
(General operation with SRG OBSS PD level) and allows a STA, under specific conditions, to ignore inter-
22
23 BSS PPDUs that are identified as being SRG PPDUs, using an SRG OBSS PD level. In addition to these dif-
24 ferences between the two types, Non-SRG OBSS PD Min offset is fixed and defined in the specification
25 while the SRG OBSS PD Min offset can be defined by the AP. A STA may operate using one of the two
26
types, neither type, or both types simultaneously.
27
28
29 26.10.2.2 General operation with non-SRG OBSS PD level
30
31
If the PHY of a STA issues a PHY-CCA.indication(BUSY) followed by a PHY-RXSTART.indication due
32
to a PPDU reception then the STA’s MAC sublayer may a) issue a PHY-CCARESET.request primitive
33
34 before the end of the PPDU and not update its basic NAV timer based on the PPDU or may b) not update its
35 basic NAV timer based on the PPDU if all the following conditions are met:
36
— The STA has not set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to the value
37
38 PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED in any HE PPDU it has transmitted in the cur-
39 rent beacon period and in the previous beacon period.
40
— The most recently received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP had the Non-
41
42 SRG OBSS PD SR Disallowed subfield equal to 0 or the non-AP STA has not received a Spatial
43 Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP or the STA is an AP and its most recently trans-
44 mitted Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element had the Non-SRG OBSS PD SR Disallowed subfield
45
equal to 0 or the STA is an AP and has not transmitted a Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element.
46
47 — The received PPDU is an inter-BSS PPDU (see 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classifica-
48 tion)) and the received PPDU is not a non-HT PPDU carrying a response frame (Ack, BlockAck or
49
CTS frame), or the received PPDU contains a CTS and a PHY-CCA.indication transition from
50
BUSY to IDLE occurred within the PIFS time immediately preceding the received CTS and that
51
52 transition corresponded to the end of an inter-BSS PPDU that contained an RTS that was ignored fol-
53 lowing this procedure.
54
— The STA is operating with an SRG OBSS PD level as described in 26.10.2.3 (General operation with
55
SRG OBSS PD level) and the received PPDU is not an SRG PPDU, or the STA is not operating with
56
57 an SRG OBSS PD level.
58
— The SPATIAL_REUSE subfield in the HE-SIG-A (if present) of the received PPDU is not set to
59
PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED.
60
61 — The received signal strength level, which is measured from the L-STF or L-LTF fields of the PPDU
62
or the PHY SYNC field, shortSYNC field or Long PHY SYNC field, whichever exists and which is
63
used to determine PHY-CCA.indication, is below the non-SRG OBSS PD level. The non-SRG
64
65 OBSS PD level is defined in 26.10.2.4 (Adjustment of OBSS PD and transmit power). If the STA

28

29

32
33 NOTE 2—An AP can get protection equivalent to SR_DELAYED by transmitting the Trigger frame in a non-HT PPDU

37 tiated within the duration of the received PPDU, then the TXOP and the duration of the transmitted PPDU
38
within that TXOP shall be limited to the duration of the received PPDU if the received PPDU is an HE MU
39
PPDU and the RXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE indicates SR_RESTRICTED.
40
41
NOTE—A STA sets the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_RESTRICTED in a PPDU if it allows
42
OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation, but only before the end of the PPDU.
43
44
A STA that ignores a PPDU following the procedure described in this subclause is deemed to perform non-
45
46 SRG OBSS PD-based spatial reuse.

48 26.10.2.3 General operation with SRG OBSS PD level
49
50

51
52 to a PPDU reception then the STA’s MAC sublayer may a) issue a PHY-CCARESET.request primitive
53 before the end of the PPDU and not update its basic NAV timer based on the PPDU or may b) not update its
54 basic NAV timer based on the PPDU if all the following conditions are met:
55
56 — The received PPDU is an SRG PPDU (see 26.2.3 (SRG PPDU identification))
57 — The received signal strength level, which is measured from the L-STF or L-LTF fields of the PPDU
58
or the PHY SYNC field, shortSYNC field or Long PHY SYNC field, whichever exists and which is
59
used to determine PHY-CCA.indication, is below the SRG OBSS PD level. The SRG OBSS PD
60
61 level is defined in 26.10.2.4 (Adjustment of OBSS PD and transmit power). If the STA has
62 dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true, it also follows the rules defined in 26.10.4 (Interaction

64
65 — The PPDU is not one of the following:

44 TX_PWR is the combined transmit power at the transmit antenna connector of all antennas in dBm and is set
45 following the rules in 11.8.6 (Transmit power selection) and, for transmission of HE TB PPDU, also follow-
46 ing the rules in 27.3.15.2 (Power pre-correction).
47
48 NOTE 1—The TX_PWR is 4 dB higher for APs with more than 2 spatial streams as those APs typically have higher
ref
49 transmit power than other devices, and as the OBSS PD procedure is based on a relative reduction of power.
50
51 NOTE 2—A STA might adjust its OBSS PD level provided that Equation(26-5) is satisfied. As an example, a non-AP
52 HE STA might monitor the beacons transmitted by the AP to which it is associated, measure their RSSI, and subtract a
53 value of 25 dB (as an approximation to the required RSNI) to provide a candidate OBSS PD level, and provided that
54 Equation(26-5) is satisfied, adjust its OBSS PD level to the candidate level.

1 26.10.2.5 OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction period
2
3
If a STA ignores an inter-BSS PPDU following the procedure in 26.10.2.3 (General operation with SRG
4
OBSS PD level), using a chosen SRG OBSS PD level, or following the procedure in 26.10.2.2 (General
5
6 operation with non-SRG OBSS PD level) using a chosen non-SRG OBSS PD level, then the STA shall start
7 an OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction period. This OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction period
8 shall be terminated at the end of the TXOP that the STA gains once its backoff reaches zero.
9
10
If a STA starts an OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction period with a chosen non-SRG OBSS PD level,
11
12 the STA’s transmit power as measured at the transmit antenna connector shall be equal or lower than the
13 TX_PWR , calculated with this chosen non-SRG OBSS PD level with Equation(26-6), with the appropri-
max
14 ate non-SRG parameters according to Table26-11 (Non-SRG OBSS PD Min and Non-SRG OBSS PD Max
15
values for non-AP STAs), for the transmissions of any PPDU that is not carrying a frame that is allowed to
16
be sent without regard to the busy/idle state of the medium until the end of the OBSS PD SR transmit power
17
18 restriction period.
19
20 If a STA starts an OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction period with a chosen SRG OBSS PD level, the
21
STA’s transmit power as measured at the transmit antenna connector shall be equal or lower than the TX_P-
22
WR , calculated with this chosen SRG OBSS PD level with Equation(26-6), with the appropriate SRG
23 max
24 parameters according to Table26-12 (SRG OBSS PD Min and SRG OBSS PD Max valuesfor non-AP
25 STAs), for the transmissions of any PPDU that is not carrying a response frame that is allowed to be sent
26 without regard to the busy/idle state of the medium until the end of the OBSS PD SR transmit power restric-
27
tion period.
28
29 NOTE—Examples of frames that are transmitted without regard to the busy/idle state of the medium include but are not
30 limited to a frame contained in an HE TB PPDU that is a response to a Trigger frame with the CS Required subfield set
31 to 0 and an Ack or BlockAck frame sent as an immediate response.
32
33
A STA may have multiple ongoing OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction periods that overlap in time.
34
35 NOTE 1—The STA’s transmit power is always equal or lower than the minimum TX_PWR among all TX_PWR
max max
36 from ongoing OBSS PD SR transmit power restriction periods.

26.10.2.6 OBSS PD-based spatial reuse backoff procedure
14
15
16 If an HE STA ignores an inter-BSS PPDU following the procedure in 26.10.2.2 (General operation with
17
non-SRG OBSS PD level), the HE STA may resume EDCAF procedures after the PHY-CCARE-
18
SET.request primitive is sent, provided that the medium condition is not otherwise indicated as BUSY.
19
20
21
26.10.3 PSR-based spatial reuse operation
22
23
24 26.10.3.1 General
25
26
27 An HE STA supporting PSR-based PSRT PPDU transmission indicates this by setting the PSR-based SR
28 Support subfield to 1 in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element (see
29
Table9-322b (Subfields of the HE PHY Capabilities Information field)). An HE-STA supporting PSR-
30
based PSRT PPDU reception indicates this by setting the PSR Responder subfield to 1 in the HE MAC
31
32 Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element (see Table9-322a (Subfields of the HE MAC
33 Capabilities Information field)).
34
35
36 An HE STA shall set the PSR-based SR Support field to 1 in the HE Capabilities element it transmits if it
37 supports transmitting an PSRT PPDU under the conditions specified in this subclause; otherwise the STA
38 shall set the PSR-based SR Support field to 0.
39
40
41 STAs that receive a Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from their associated AP that has a value of 1 in
42 the PSR Disallowed subfield shall not perform PSR-based SR transmissions.
43
44
45 An PSR opportunity is identified from the value of the RXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of an HE
46 TB PPDU and/or the contents of a Trigger frame. An HE STA may initiate an SR transmission during an
47 PSR opportunity for the duration of an ongoing PPDU when certain conditions, designed to avoid interfer-
48
ing with the reception of the ongoing PPDU at the recipient, are met. If the RXVECTOR parameter SPA-
49
TIAL_REUSE of the ongoing PPDU has the value PSR_DISALLOW or
50
51 PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED, no PSR-based SR transmission is allowed for the dura-
52 tion of that PPDU.
53
54
55 An AP sending a Trigger frame may set the Spatial Reuse n field(s) in the UL Spatial Reuse subfield in the
56 Common Info field of the Trigger frame to PSR_DISALLOW or, if permitted by the rules in 26.11.6 (SPA-
57 TIAL_REUSE), to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED to disallow OBSS STAs from per-
58
forming PSR-based SR transmission during the ensuing uplink PPDU duration. An AP sending a Trigger
59
frame shall not set the SR field in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame to SR_DELAYED or SR_RE-
60
61 STRICTED.
62
63
An HE STA shall set the SR Responder subfield in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1 if dot11SR-
64
65 ResponderOptionImplemented is true; otherwise the HE STA shall set the SR Responder subfield to 0.

This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

1 26.10.3.2 PSR-based spatial reuse initiation
2
3
An HE STA identifies an PSR opportunity if the following two conditions are met:
4
5 1) The STA receives a PHY-RXSTART.indication corresponding to the reception of a PSRR PPDU
6 that is identified as an inter-BSS PPDU (see 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification))
7
2) An PSRT PPDU is queued for transmission and the intended transmit power of the PSRT PPDU in
8
9 dBm minus log10(PPDU_BW / 20 MHz) dB is below the value of PSR - RPL, where PPDU_BW is
10 determined from Table26-10 (PPDU_BW determination) using the TXVECTOR parameter
11 CH_BANDWIDTH or CH_BANDWIDTH_IN_NON_HT of the PSRT PPDU, whichever is pres-
12
ent, and PSR is the value obtained from Table27-23 (Spatial Reuse field encoding for an HE TB
13
PPDU) based on at least one of:
14
15 a) The value of the UL Spatial Reuse field in the Common Info field of the Trigger frame of the
16
PSRR PPDU
17
18 b) The value of the RXVECTOR parameter Spatial Reuse of the HE TB PPDU that follows the
19 PSRR PPDU
20
21
RPL is the combined transmit power at the receive antenna connector, over the PSRR PPDU bandwidth,
22
23 during the non-HE portion of the HE PPDU preamble of the triggering PPDU, averaged over all antennas
24 used to receive the PPDU.
25
26
An HE STA that identifies an PSR opportunity may choose not to perform NAV update operations normally
27
executed based on the receipt of the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION and the Trigger frame
28
29 Duration field. See Figure26-13 (PSRR PPDU spatial reuse). A STA that identifies an PSR opportunity
30 may issue a PHY-CCARESET.request to ignore the associated HE TB PPDU(s) that are triggered by the
31 Trigger frame of the PSRR PPDU and that occurs within aSIFSTime+aRxPHYStartDelay+2×aSlotTime
32
of the end of the last symbol on the air of the PPDU that contained the Trigger frame, provided that the value
33
of the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR of the HE TB PPDU matches the BSS color of the PSRR
34
35 PPDU. A STA that identifies an PSR opportunity shall not transmit an PSRT PPDU that terminates beyond
36 the PPDU duration of the HE TB PPDU that is triggered by the Trigger frame of the PSRR PPDU.

26.10.3.3 PSR-based spatial reuse backoff procedure
58
59
60 If an HE STA identifies an PSR opportunity as allowed in 26.10.3.2 (PSR-based spatial reuse initiation), the
61 HE STA may continue the countdown of an existing backoff procedure provided that the medium condition
62
is not otherwise indicated as BUSY. If the HE STA receives another PPDU during the backoff procedure, it
63
shall suspend its back-off and subsequently, if an PSR opportunity is identified based on the identification of
64
65 the new PPDU as an PSRR PPDU, then the STA may resume its backoff procedure. The TXOP that the HE

1 STA gains once its backoff reaches zero shall not extend beyond the PSR opportunity endpoint that is the
2 earliest ending of all of the durations of all of the PSRR PPDUs that were used to confirm the PSR opportu-
3
nity and all of the durations indicated in the Common Info fields of Trigger frames within all PSRR PPDUs
4
that were used to confirm the PSR opportunity.

26.10.3.4 UL Spatial Reuse subfield of Trigger frame
20
21
22 An AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits a Trigger frame may determine the
23
value of the UL Spatial Reuse subfield of the Common Info field of the Trigger frame for each 20MHz sub-
24
channel for a 20 MHz, 40 MHz, or 80 MHz PPDU or for each 40 MHz subchannel for an 80+80 MHz or
25
26 160MHz PPDU by selecting the row in Table27-23 (Spatial Reuse field encoding for an HE TB PPDU)
27 that has a numerical value in the Meaning column that is the highest value that is less than or equal to the
28 value of the computed MAC parameter PSR_INPUT as follows:
29
30
31 PSR_INPUT = TX_PWR AP + Acceptable Receiver Interference Level AP (26-7)
32
33
where
34
TX_PWR is the total power at the antenna connector, in dBm, for that 20 MHz subchannel for a 20
35 AP
36 MHz, 40 MHz, or 80 MHz PPDU or for that 40 MHz subchannel for an 80+80 MHz or 160
37 MHz PPDU, over all antennas used to transmit the PSRR PPDU containing the Trigger frame.
38
Acceptable Receiver Interference Level is a value in dBm for that 20 MHz subchannel for a 20 MHz,
39 AP
40 40 MHz, or 80 MHz PPDU or for that 40MHz subchannels for an 80+80 MHz or 160MHz
41 PPDU and should be set to the expected receive signal power indicated by the UL Target
42
Receive Power subfield in the Trigger frame for the highest HE-MCS of the ensuing HE TB
43
PPDUs minus the minimum SNR value that yields ≤ 10% PER for that MCS, minus a safety
44
45 margin value not to exceed 5 dB as determined by the AP.
46
47
An AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits a Trigger frame may set the value of
48
the UL Spatial Reuse subfield value of the Common Info field of the Trigger frame in each 20MHz band-
49
50 width for 20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz PPDU or in each 40MHz bandwidth for 80+80 or 160MHz PPDU to
51 PSR_DISALLOW.
52
53
An AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to false that transmits a Trigger frame shall set the value
54
55 of the UL Spatial Reuse subfield value of the Common Info field of the Trigger frame in each 20MHz band-
56 width for 20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz PPDU or in each 40MHz bandwidth for 80+80 or 160MHz PPDU to
57 PSR_DISALLOW.

60 26.10.3.5 PSRT PPDU transmission requirements
61
62
An HE STA that identifies an PSR opportunity shall not transmit a frame during the PSR opportunity that
63
elicits a response transmission from a STA from which it has not received an HE Capabilities element with
64
65 the PSR Responder subfield equal to 1. An HE STA that identifies an PSR opportunity shall not transmit a
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 453
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 454 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 frame that does not include a CAS Control subfield with the PSRT PPDU subfield set to 1 and that solicits a
2 response transmission during that PSR opportunity.
3
4
5 26.10.3.6 PSRT PPDU reception and response transmission requirements
6
7
An HE STA that receives a PPDU that contains at least one frame with a CAS Control subfield with an
8
9 PSRT PPDU subfield equal to 1 shall not transmit a response PPDU elicited by the received PPDU if all out-
10 standing PSR and OBSS PD transmit power requirements are not met by the response transmission.
11
12
26.10.4 Interaction of OBSS PD and PSR-based spatial reuse
13
14
15 An HE STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that receives a PPDU that is identified as an
16
inter-BSS PPDU with a value other than PSR_DISALLOW or PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PRO-
17
HIBITED for the RXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE and fails to identify an PSR opportunity based
18
19 on the receipt of the PPDU shall disable OBSS PD SR operation on this PPDU.
20
21
An HE STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that receives a PPDU that is identified as an
22
inter-BSS PPDU with a value other than PSR_DISALLOW, SR_DELAYED or PSR_AND_NON_S-
23
24 RG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED for the RXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE and identifies an PSR
25 opportunity based on the receipt of the PPDU may disable OBSS PD SR operation on this PPDU.
26
27
28 An HE STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that receives a PPDU that is identified as an
29 inter-BSS PPDU with a value other than PSR_DISALLOW or PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PRO-
30 HIBITED in the Common Info Field SPATIAL_REUSE of a Trigger frame and fails to identify an PSR
31
opportunity based on the receipt of the PPDU shall disable OBSS PD SR operation on the HE TB PPDU that
32
is elicited by the Trigger frame.
33
34
35 An HE STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that receives a PPDU that is identified as an
36
inter-BSS PPDU with a value other than PSR_DISALLOW in the Common Info Field SPATIAL_REUSE
37
of a Trigger frame and identifies an PSR opportunity based on the receipt of the PPDU may disable OBSS
38
39 PD SR operation on the Data field of the HE TB PPDU that is elicited by the Trigger frame.
40
41
42 26.11 Rules for setting some TXVECTOR parameters for PPDUs transmitted by an
43
HE STA
44
45
46 26.11.1 STA_ID
47
48
49 Each parameter STA_ID in the TXVECTOR identifies the STA or group of STAs that is the recipient of an
50 RU in the HE MU PPDU transmitted with the TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG set to 0. For an indi-
51 vidually addressed RU the parameter STA_ID is set to the 11 LSBs of the AID of the STA receiving the
52
PSDU contained in that RU. If an RU is intended for one or more unassociated non-AP STAs, then the
53
parameter STA_ID for that RU is set to 2045. If an RU is intended for no user, then the parameter STA_ID
54
55 for that RU is set to 2046.
56
57
If an RU is intended for an AP (i.e., the TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG is 1), then the parameter
58
STA_ID contains only one element that is set to the 11 LSBs of the AID of the non-AP STA transmitting the
59
60 PPDU.
61
62 NOTE—A non-AP STA can transmit an UL HE MU PPDU to help the AP identify the transmitter of a failed PPDU so
63 that the AP can allocate resources for that non-AP STA in a later TXOP. All unassociated STAs share the same parame-
64 ter STA_ID value (i.e., 2045), which doesn’t uniquely identify the transmitter. Therefore an unassociated STA is not
65 allowed to transmit an UL HE MU PPDU.
454 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 455 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 If an RU is intended for multiple STAs for MU-MIMO then multiple STAs identified by STA-IDs in the
2 parameter STA_ID will use the same resource unit (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)). If an RU is intended for
3
multiple associated STAs and carries a single A-MPDU then the parameter STA_ID is set as follows:
4
5 — For an AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to false, if the RU is intended for more than
6 one associated STA in the BSS that is not a recipient of an individually addressed RU, the parameter
7
STA_ID is set to 0.
8
9 — For an AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true, if the RU is intended for more than one
10 associated STA in any of its BSSs that is not a recipient of an individually addressed RU, the param-
11
eter STA_ID is set to 0 for transmitted BSSID or to the value of the BSSID Index field correspond-
12
ing to that BSS (see 9.4.2.73 (Multiple BSSID-Index element)) for a nontransmitted BSSID. The
13
14 number of such elements shall not exceed the maximum number of BSSs of the multiple BSSID set.
15
— For an AP with dot11MultiBSSIDImplemented equal to true, if the RU is intended for more than one
16
17 associated STA on any of its BSSs that is not the recipient of an individually addressed RU or
18 another broadcast RU corresponding to parameter STA_ID equal 0 or equal to the BSSID Index of a
19 BSSID in a multiple BSSID set, the parameter STA_ID is set to 2047.
20
21
The parameter STA_ID values between 2008 and 2044 are reserved.
22
23
24 A non-AP STA shall not transmit an HE MU PPDU where the TXVECTOR parameter STA_ID includes
25
more than one entry in the range 1 to 2007.
26
27
28 26.11.2 UPLINK_FLAG
29
30
An HE STA transmitting an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU sets the TXVECTOR
31
32 parameter UPLINK_FLAG as follows:
33
— A STA transmitting an HE PPDU containing frames that are addressed to an AP shall set the
34
TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG to 1 unless the HE PPDU is an HE ER SU PPDU with the
35
36 TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION set to UNSPECIFIED and contains an RTS or CTS
37 frame in which case the STA may set the TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG to 0.
38
— Otherwise, the HE STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG to 0.
39
40
41 26.11.3 BEAM_CHANGE
42
43
An HE STA uses the TXVECTOR parameter BEAM_CHANGE to indicate a change in the spatial mapping
44
45 of the pre-HE-STF portion of the PPDU and the first symbol of HE-LTF (see Table27-1 (TXVECTOR and
46 RXVECTOR parameters)).
47
48
An HE STA that transmits an HE SU PPDU or an HE ER SU PPDU shall set the TXVECTOR parameter
49
50 BEAM_CHANGE to 1 if one or more of the following conditions are met:
51
— The number of spatial streams is greater than 2
52
53 — The PPDU is the first PPDU in a TXOP
54
55 — The PPDU carries a Trigger frame
56
57 26.11.4 BSS_COLOR
58
59
60 An HE STA that transmits HE Operation element shall select and advertise a BSS color as described in
61 26.17.3 (BSS color).
62
63
An HE STA that transmitted an HE Operation element shall set the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR
64
65 as follows:
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 455
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 456 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — For an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or DL HE MU PPDU, the parameter BSS_COLOR is set to
2 the value indicated in the BSS Color subfield of the HE Operation element if all the recipient STAs
3
are members of the HE STA’s BSS or the PPDU carries at least one triggering frame.
4
5 — For an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or DL HE MU PPDU, the parameter BSS_COLOR is set to
6
0 if the HE STA expects that at least one intended recipient STA is not a member of the HE STA’s
7
BSS and the PPDU does not carry a triggering frame.
8
9
10 A non-AP HE STA that transmits an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU to a STA that is not a member of
11
the transmitting STA’s HE BSS shall set the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR to 0.
12
13
14 The active BSS color is one of the following:
15
16 — The value of the BSS Color field in the most recently received HE Operation element if an HE STA
17 receives an HE Operation element from a peer HE STA.
18
19 — The value of the New BSS Color field in the most recently received BSS Color Change Announce-
20 ment element if an HE STA receives a BSS Color Change Announcement element from a peer HE
21
STA and the BSS color change TBTT has passed (see 26.17.3.4 (Selecting and advertising a new
22
BSS color)).
23
24
25 An HE STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR for an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or
26
UL HE MU PPDU that is addressed to a peer STA to the active BSS color value, if the HE STA has estab-
27
lished any of the following:
28
29
— An association with the peer STA
30
31 — A TDLS link with the peer STA
32
33 — An IBSS membership with the peer STA
34
35 NOTE 1—A non-AP HE STA sets the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR for an HE TB PPDU to the active color
36 (see 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation)).
37
38 NOTE 2—An HE mesh STA sets the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR for an HE PPDU that it transmits to a peer
39 HE mesh STA to the value in the BSS Color subfield of its transmitted HE Operation element.
40
41
An HE STA that receives an HE PPDU with RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR with a value between 1
42
and 63 follows the spatial reuse rule described in 26.10 (Spatial reuse operation).
43
44
NOTE—An HE STA that receives an HE PPDU with the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR equal to 0 might not
45
follow the spatial reuse rules described in 26.10 (Spatial reuse operation).
46
47
48 An HE STA that receives an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU with the RXVECTOR
49
parameter BSS_COLOR equal to 0 shall not discard the HE PPDU on that basis.
50
51
52 While the BSS Color Disabled subfield is 1, an HE STA shall continue to advertise a nonzero value (same as
53 before the color was disabled) in the BSS Color subfield of HE Operation element and in the TXVECTOR
54
parameter BSS_COLOR of an HE PPDU that it transmits.
55
56
NOTE—A non-AP HE STA sets the TXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR of an HE PPDU that it transmits to the value
57
advertised by the AP it intends to communicate with even if the AP has disabled BSS color.
58
59
60 If the value of TXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID[5:8] for VHT PPDUs with the TXVECTOR param-
61 eter GROUP_ID equal to 63 transmitted by an HE AP to any associated STA would not be consistent with
62
the partial BSS color (i.e., BCB(0:3) described in 26.17.4 (AID assignment)) announced by the HE AP, then
63
the HE AP shall set the Partial BSS Color field in the HE Operation element to 0. Otherwise, the HE AP may
64
65 set the Partial BSS Color subfield in the HE Operation element to 1 (see 26.17.4 (AID assignment)).

## 第 457 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.11.5 TXOP_DURATION
2
3
The TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION of an HE PPDU indicates duration information for NAV
4
setting and protection of the TXOP except that the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is set to
5
6 UNSPECIFIED to indicate no duration information.
7
8 NOTE—The value of TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is converted to an indication in the TXOP field of
9 HE-SIG-A as described in Table27-18 (HE-SIG-A field of an HE SU PPDU and HE ER SU PPDU), Table27-20 (HE-
10 SIG-A field of an HE MU PPDU), and Table27-21 (HE-SIG-A field of an HE TB PPDU). The indication in the TXOP
11 field of HE-SIG-A is converted to the RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION as described in Table27-1
12 (TXVECTOR and RXVECTOR parameters).
13
14 A STA that is not a TXOP responder and that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, or HE MU
15 PPDU may set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to UNSPECIFIED.
16
17
18 A STA that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, or HE MU PPDU that carries a PS-Poll frame
19 shall set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to UNSPECIFIED.
20
21
A STA that is a TXOP responder that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, or HE TB PPDU shall
22
set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to UNSPECIFIED if the RXVECTOR parameter
23
24 TXOP_DURATION of the HE PPDU that solicits a response from the STA is UNSPECIFIED.
25
26
An HE AP that has set the BSS Color Disabled field in the HE Operation element to 1 shall set the TXVEC-
27
TOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to UNSPECIFIED for an HE PPDU that it transmits to non-AP STAs
28
29 associated to it.
30
31 If the BSS Color Disabled field is 1 in the HE Operation element most recently received from an AP by a
32
non-AP STA, then the non-AP STA should set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to
33
UNSPECIFIED for an HE PPDU that is not an HE TB PPDU sent to that AP.
34
35
36 A STA that transmits an HE TB PPDU shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to
37
UNSPECIFIED if any one of the following condition is met:
38
39 — The RXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION is present for the PPDU that solicits a response
40 from the STA and is not UNSPECIFIED
41
42 — The PPDU that solicits a response from the STA is not an HE PPDU
43
44 A STA that transmits a frame with a Duration field in an HE PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter TXO-
45
P_DURATION not set to UNSPECIFIED shall set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION to the
46
smaller of the duration information indicated by the Duration field and 8448.
47
48
NOTE—For a TXOP responder, the Duration field in a frame carried in a response PPDU is set based on the Duration
49
field in a frame carried in the PPDU that solicits a response from the TXOP responder as described in 9.2.5.7 (Setting for
50
control response frames) or 9.2.5.8 (Setting for other response frames).
51
52
53 If a STA transmits either an HE TB feedback NDP or an HE TB PPDU carrying a PS-Poll frame with the
54 TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION not set to UNSPECIFIED, it shall calculate the duration infor-
55 mation and set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION for the HE TB feedback NDP or HE TB
56
PPDU to the value of the computed duration information. The TXOP responder shall calculate duration
57
information equal to the duration information indicated by the Duration field of the frame that solicits the
58
59 response minus the time, in microseconds, between the end of the PPDU carrying the frame that soliciting
60 the HE TB PPDU and the end of the HE TB PPDU. If the calculated duration information includes a frac-
61 tional microsecond, the duration information is rounded up to the next higher integer. If the calculated dura-
62
tion information is smaller than 8448µs, the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION shall be set to the
63
calculated duration information. Otherwise, the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION shall be set to
64
65 8448.

1 NOTE—The time is equal to SIFS plus the duration of the HE TB PPDU, where the duration of the HE TB PPDU is
2 defined in Equation(27-134).
3
4 In the 6 GHz band, a TXOP holder shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter TXOP_DURATION for a trans-
5

12 26.11.6 SPATIAL_REUSE
13
14
The contents of the Spatial Reuse fields are carried in the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE for an
15
HE PPDU indicating spatial reuse information. The behavior of STAs upon reception of an HE PPDU with
16
17 different SPATIAL_REUSE values is described in 26.10.2 (OBSS PD-based spatial reuse operation) and
18 26.10.3 (PSR-based spatial reuse operation). The different values that may be indicated in the SPA-
19 TIAL_REUSE parameter of the TXVECTOR are listed in Table27-22 (Spatial Reuse field encoding for an
20
HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU, and HE MU PPDU) and Table27-23 (Spatial Reuse field encoding for an
21
HE TB PPDU). The value PSR_DISALLOW is used to prohibit PSR-based spatial reuse during the trans-
22
23 mission of the corresponding PPDU. The value PSR_AND_NON_SRG_ OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED is used
24 to prohibit both PSR-based spatial reuse and non-SRG OBSS PD-based spatial reuse during the transmission
25 of the corresponding PPDU. The interpretation of other values are described in this subclause and in 26.10
26
(Spatial reuse operation). The conditions for a STA to set the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter to its different
27
values are described in this subclause.
28
29
30 For a PPDU with a value of HE_TB for the TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT, the SPATIAL_REUSE
31 parameter contains an array of one to four values, depending on the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BAND-
32
WIDTH. If the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW20, CBW40 or CBW80, the first value
33
in the array is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to the lowest frequency 20 MHz subband, the
34
35 second value, if present, applies to the second lowest frequency 20 MHz subband, the third value, if present,
36 applies to the third lowest frequency 20 MHz subband and the fourth value, if present, applies to the highest
37 frequency 20 MHz subband. If the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH is CBW160 or CBW80+80,
38
the first value in the array is the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter that applies to the lowest frequency 40 MHz
39
subband, the second value applies to the second lowest frequency 40 MHz subband, the third value applies
40
41 to the third lowest frequency 40 MHz subband and the fourth value applies to the highest frequency 40 MHz
42 subband. Each value in the SPATIAL_REUSE parameter array shall individually conform to the rules in
43 this subclause.
44
45
An HE STA that transmits an HE TB PPDU sets the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE as defined
46
47 in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
48
49 An AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits an HE ER SU PPDU that does not
50
contain a Trigger frame should set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_DISALLOW.
51
52
53 A non-AP STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER
54 SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU may set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE, when permitted by
55 other conditions, to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED if the HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_val-
56
ue15_allowed subfield of the SR Control field of the most recently received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set
57
element from its associated AP is equal to 1. Otherwise, the non-AP STA shall set it to PSR_DISALLOW.
58
59
60 An HE STA that transmits an HE TB PPDU determines the value of the TXVECTOR parameter SPA-
61 TIAL_REUSE according to 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
62
63
An HE AP with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to true that transmits an HE SU PPDU, HE ER SU
64
65 PPDU, or HE MU PPDU may set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_DISALLOW to
458 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 459 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 disallow OBSS STAs from performing PSR-based SR transmission during the duration of the corresponding
2 PPDU.
3
4
5 An HE STA with dot11HEPSROptionImplemented set to false may set the TXVECTOR parameter SPA-
6 TIAL_REUSE to PSR_DISALLOW for any PPDU that is not an HE TB PPDU, an HE NDP PPDU, a
7
PPDU containing an HE NDP Announcement frame, or a PPDU containing a response to an HE NDP
8
Announcement frame.
9
10
11
A STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of an HE PPDU to PSR_DISALLOW or, if
12
13 permitted by the other rules in this subclause, to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED, if the
14 STA is a non-AP HE STA and the PSR Disallowed subfield of the SR Control field of the most recently
15 received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP is equal to 1.
16
17
18 An HE STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_P-
19 D_PROHIBITED for an HE NDP PPDU.
20
21
22 An HE STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_P-
23
D_PROHIBITED for a PPDU containing an NDP Announcement frame and in any frame that is transmitted
24
as a response to an NDP Announcement frame.
25
26
27
A non-AP HE STA may set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of an HE PPDU to
28
PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED if the HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed sub-
29
30 field of the SR Control field of the most recently received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its
31 associated AP is equal to 1. If the HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed subfield of the SR Control field
32 of the most recently received Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP is equal to 0, or if
33
STA has not received a Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element from its associated AP, the STA shall not set
34
the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of any HE PPDU to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_P-
35
36 D_PROHIBITED, unless the HE PPDU contains an NDP, an NDP Announcement frame or is a frame that is
37 transmitted as a response to an NDP Announcement frame.
38
39
40 An AP HE STA may set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of an HE PPDU to
41 PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED if the HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed sub-
42
field of the SR Control field of the most recently transmitted Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element is equal to
43
1. If the HESIGA_Spatial_reuse_value15_allowed subfield of the SR Control field of the most recently
44
45 transmitted Spatial Reuse Parameter Set element is equal to 0, or if the AP has not transmitted a Spatial
46 Reuse Parameter Set element, the AP shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE of any
47 HE PPDU to PSR_AND_NON_SRG_OBSS_PD_PROHIBITED.
48
49
50 An HE AP that transmits an HE SU PPDU or an HE ER SU PPDU that contains a Trigger frame should set
51 the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED. An HE STA that transmits an HE MU
52
PPDU shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED.
53
54
55
An HE STA that transmits an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU shall not set the TXVECTOR parameter
56
SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_RESTRICTED.
57
58
59
An HE AP that transmits an HE MU PPDU that contains a Trigger frame should set the TXVECTOR
60
parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_RESTRICTED.
61
62
63
An HE STA that transmits a PPDU that does not contain a Trigger frame shall not set the TXVECTOR
64
65 parameter SPATIAL_REUSE to SR_DELAYED or SR_RESTRICTED.

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.11.7 INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS and RU_ALLOCATION
2
3
The indication of which subchannels are punctured in an HE sounding NDP or in an HE NDP Announce-
4
5 ment frame that is carried in a non-HT duplicate PPDU is conveyed from the MAC to the PHY through the
6 TXVECTOR parameters INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS and RU_ALLOCATION. The parameter INAC-
7 TIVE_SUBCHANNELS may be present in the TXVECTOR of a non-HT duplicate PPDU that carries an
8
HE NDP Announcement frame or of an HE sounding PPDU. The parameter INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS
9
shall not be present otherwise. The setting of the RU_ALLOCATION parameter for other PPDUs is speci-
10
11 fied in 26.5.2.3.3 (TXVECTOR parameters for HE TB PPDU response to Trigger frame), 26.5.2.3.4
12 (TXVECTOR parameters for HE TB PPDU response to TRS Control subfield) and 26.5.7.2 (STA behav-
13 ior).
14
15
16 INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS is an 8-bit bitmap with an encoding that is the same as the encoding for the
17 Disallowed Subchannel Bitmap subfield defined in 9.3.1.19 (VHT/HE NDP Announcement frame format).
18
A bit in the INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS bitmap is set to 1 to indicate that no energy is transmitted on the
19
corresponding subchannel for the corresponding PPDU. The RU_ALLOCATION parameter is set to a value
20
21 that corresponds to the puncturing signaled by the INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS bitmap. If a bit in the
22 INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS bitmap corresponds to a 20 MHz subchannel outside the PPDU bandwidth,
23 then the bit is set to 1.
24
25
26 A STA transmitting an HE sounding NDP may set the TXVECTOR parameter INACTIVE_SUBCHAN-
27 NELS to any value provided that the bit representing the primary 20 MHz channel is set to 0.
28
29
30 If an HE AP transmits an HE NDP Announcement frame in a PPDU with punctured channels, then the
31 TXVECTOR parameters FORMAT, NON_HT_MODULATION, CH_BANDWIDTH and INACTIVE_-
32 SUBCHANNELS shall be set as follows:
33
34 — The TXVECTOR parameter FORMAT shall be set to NON_HT
35
36 — The TXVECTOR parameter NON_HT_MODULATION shall be set to NON_HT_DUP_OFDM.
37
— The INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS parameter may have any value, except that the bit in the bitmap
38
39 representing the primary 20 MHz subchannel shall be set to 0.
40
— The CH_BANDWIDTH parameter value shall be set to CBW80 if there are no bits set to 0 in the
41
42 INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS bitmap that correspond to any 20 MHz subchannel of the secondary
43 80 MHz and at least one bit set to 0 that corresponds to any 20 MHz subchannel of the secondary 40
44 MHz
45
46 — The CH_BANDWIDTH parameter value shall be set to CBW160 if there is at least one bit set to 0 in
47 the INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS bitmap that corresponds to any 20 MHz subchannel of the sec-
48
ondary 80 MHz
49
50
51 Each 8 bits of the RU_ALLOCATION are set to 113 (01110001 in binary representation) for the 242-tone
52 RU that is most closely aligned in frequency with the 20 MHz subchannel that is indicated as disallowed by
53
the value 1 in the INACTIVE_SUBCHANNELS parameter. Each 8 bits of the RU_ALLOCATION are set
54
to 192 (11000000 in binary representation) for the 242-tone RU that is most closely aligned in frequency
55
56 with the 20 MHz subchannel that is indicated as not disallowed by the value 0 in the INACTIVE_SUB-
57 CHANNELS parameter.
58
59
26.11.8 TRIGGER_RESPONDING
60
61
62
An non-AP HE STA that transmits a non-HT or non-HT duplicate PPDU shall set the TXVECTOR parame-
63
ter TRIGGER_RESPONDING to true if the PPDU is sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame. Other-
64
65 wise, the non-AP HE STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter TRIGGER_RESPONDING to false.

1 26.11.9 STBC and DCM
2
3
An HE STA shall not transmit an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter
4
5 STBC set to 1 if any of the following conditions are met:
6
— TXVECTOR parameter DCM is set to 1
7
8 — TXVECTOR parameter NUM_STS is not equal to 2
9
— TXVECTOR parameters HE_LTF_TYPE and GI_TYPE are set to 4x HE-LTF and 0u8s_GI, respec-
10
11 tively
12
13
An HE STA shall not transmit an HE TB PPDU with the TXVECTOR parameter STBC set to 1 if any of the
14

15
16 — TXVECTOR parameter DCM is set to 1
17
18 — TXVECTOR parameter NUM_STS is not equal to 2

21

22

24

26

27

36 26.12 HE PPDU post-FEC padding and packet extension
37
38
39 An HE STA with dot11PPEThresholdsRequired set to false may set the PPE Thresholds Present subfield in
40 HE Capabilities elements that it transmits to 0.
41
42
An HE STA with dot11PPEThresholdsRequired set to true shall set the PPE Thresholds Present subfield in
43
44 HE Capabilities elements that it transmits to 1.
45
46
A STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 and the Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 0 in
47
HE Capabilities elements that it transmits has as a nominal packet padding of 0µs for all constellations, NSS
48
49 and RU allocations that it supports.
50
51
A STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 and the Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 1 in
52
the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits has a nominal packet padding of 8µs for all constellations,
53
54 NSS and RU allocations that it supports.
55
56 A STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 0 and the Nominal Packet Padding subfield to 2 in
57
the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits has nominal packet padding of 16µs for all constellations,
58
NSS and RU allocations that it supports.
59
60
61 A STA that sets the PPE Thresholds Present subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities elements that it transmits
62
shall indicate its nominal packet padding per constellation, NSS and RU allocation by setting the subfields
63
of the PPE Thresholds field according to 9.4.2.248 (HE Capabilities element) and using the corresponding
64
65 values from dot11PPEThresholdsMappingTable.

8 26.13 Link adaptation using the HLA Control subfield
9
10
This subclause applies to frame exchange sequences that include PPDUs containing an HE variant HT Con-
11
trol field.
12
13
14 An HE STA shall set the HE Link Adaptation Support subfield in the HE Capabilities Information field in
15
the HE Capabilities element it transmits to the value of dot11HEMCSFeedbackOptionImplemented.
16
17
18 A STA that supports HE link adaptation using the HLA Control subfield shall set the HE Link Adaptation
19 Support subfield in the HE Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element to 2 or 3, depend-
20
ing on its own link adaptation feedback capability. A STA shall not send an MRQ to a STA that has not set
21
the HE Link Adaptation Support subfield to 3 in the HE Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities
22
23 element. A STA shall not send an unsolicited MFB in any frame that contains an HLA Control subfield to a
24 STA that has not set the HE Link Adaptation Support subfield to either 2 or 3 in the HE Capabilities Infor-
25 mation field in the HE Capabilities element.
26
27
28 The MFB requester may set the MRQ subfield to 1 and Unsolicited MFB subfield to 0 in the HLA Control
29 subfield of a frame to request a STA to provide link adaptation feedback. In each request, the MFB requester
30 shall set the MSI field to a value ranging from 0 to 6. For the MFB requester, how to choose the MSI value
31
is implementation dependent.
32
33
34 The appearance of more than one instance of an HLA Control subfield with the MRQ field equal to 1 within
35 a single PPDU shall be interpreted by the receiver as a single request for link adaptation feedback.
36
37
38 The MFB requester shall specify the RU index and BW requesting the link adaptation feedback.
39
40
On receipt of an HLA Control subfield with the MRQ subfield equal to 1, an MFB responder computes the
41
HE-MCS, NSS, and DCM of the RU and BW specified in the MRQ and these estimates are based on the
42
43 same RU of the PPDU carrying the MRQ. The PPDU carrying MRQ shall include the RU requested for
44 MFB. The MFB responder labels the result of this computation with the MSI value from the HLA Control
45 subfield in the received frame carrying the MRQ. The MFB responder may include the received MSI value
46
in the MSI field of the corresponding response frame. In the case of a delayed response, this allows the MFB
47
requester to associate the MFB with the soliciting MRQ.
48
49
50 An MFB responder that sends a solicited MFB shall set the Unsolicited MFB subfield to 0 and MRQ sub-
51
field to 0 in the HLA Control subfield.
52
53
54 The STA receiving MFB may use the received MFB to compute the appropriate HE-MCS, DCM and NSS.
55
56
The MFB responder may send a solicited response frame with any of the following combinations of HE-
57
58 MCS, NSS, and MSI:
59
— HE-MCS = 15, NSS = 7, MSI = 0–6: the responder will not provide feedback for the request that had
60
61 the MSI value.
62
— HE-MCS = valid value, NSS = valid value, MSI = 0–6: the responder is providing feedback for the
63
request that had the MSI value. The MSI value in the response frame matches the MSI value of the
64
65 MRQ request.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 463
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 464 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 A STA sending unsolicited MFB using the HLA Control subfield shall set the Unsolicited MFB subfield to
2 1.
3
4
5 Unsolicited HE-MCS, NSS, DCM, BW, and RU estimates reported in an HLA Control subfield sent by a
6
STA are computed based on the most recent PPDU received by the STA that matches the description indi-
7
cated by the PPDU format, Tx Beamforming, and Coding Type subfields in the same HLA Control subfield.
8
9
10
In an unsolicited MFB response the PPDU Formats, Coding Type, and Tx Beamforming subfields are set
11
according to the RXVECTOR parameters of the received PPDU from which the HE-MCS, RU, BW, and
12
13 NSS are estimated, as follows:
14
15 — The PPDU format subfield is set and encoded as follows:
16
17 • 0 if the parameter FORMAT is equal to HE_SU
18
• 1 if the parameter FORMAT is equal to HE_MU
19
20 • 2 if the parameter FORMAT is equal to HE_ER_SU
21
22 • 3 if the parameter FORMAT is equal to HE_TB
23
24 — The Coding Type subfield is set to 0 if the parameter FEC_CODING is equal to BCC_CODING and
25 set to 1 if equal to LDPC_CODING.
26
27 — The Tx Beamforming subfield is set to 1 if the parameter BEAMFORMED is equal to 1 and set to 0
28
if equal to 0.
29
30
— The BW subfield shall indicate a bandwidth less than or equal to the bandwidth indicated by the
31
parameter CH_BANDWIDTH.
32
33
— The RU subfield indicates the RU at which the recommended HE-MCS is applied. The recom-
34
35 mended RU shall be within an RU or a bandwidth in which the received HE PPDU is located.
36
37
38 For either a solicited or an unsolicited response, the recommended HE-MCS and NSS subfields of HLA
39 Control subfield shall be selected from the HE-MCS and NSS set supported by the recipient STA.
40
41
42 The HE-MCS subfield of HLA Control subfield is the recommended data rate, for given transmission prop-
43 erties carried in the RXVECTOR of the PPDU used for MFB estimation, which results in an estimated
44
frame error rate of 10% or lower for an MPDU length of 3895 octets.
45
46
NOTE—Some HE PPDU might not be able to carry 3895 octets due to PPDU duration limitations.
47
48
49
If the MFB requester sets the MRQ subfield to 1 and sets the MSI subfield to a value that matches the MSI
50
subfield value of a previous request for which the responder has not yet provided feedback, the responder
51
52 shall discard or abandon the computation for the MRQ that corresponds to the previous use of that MSI sub-
53 field value and start a new computation based on the new request.
54
55
56 A STA may respond immediately to a current request for MFB with a frame containing an MSI field value
57 and NSS, HE-MCS, and DCM subfields that correspond to a request that precedes the current request.
58
59
60 A non-AP HE STA may set the UL HE TB PPDU MFB to 1 in the HLA Control field it transmits to the AP
61 to indicate that the NSS, HE-MCS, DCM, BW and RU Allocation in the HLA Control field represent the
62
recommended MFB for the HE TB PPDU sent from the non-AP HE STA. The AP should not exceed the
63
recommended RU size indicated in the most recently received RU Allocation field of the HLA Control field
64
65 when it sends a triggering frame addressed to the STA.

## 第 465 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.14 Power management
2
3
26.14.1 Intra-PPDU power save for non-AP HE STAs
4
5
6 Intra-PPDU power save is the power save mechanism for an HE STA to enter the doze state or become
7
unavailable until the end of a received PPDU that is identified as an intra-BSS PPDU. The STA can enter the
8
doze state if it is in PS mode and can become unavailable if it is in active mode (see 11.2.3.2 (Non-AP STA
9
10 power management modes)).
11
12
A non-AP HE STA that has dot11IntraPPDUPowerSaveOptionActivated equal to true operates in intra-
13
PPDU power save mode.
14
15
16 A non-AP HE STA that is in intra-PPDU power save mode may enter the doze state or become unavailable
17
until the end of a PPDU currently being received if one of the following conditions is met:
18
19 — The PPDU is an HE MU PPDU where the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is the BSS color of
20
the BSS in which the STA is associated, the RXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG is 0 and the
21
RXVECTOR parameters STA_ID do not include the identifier of the STA or the broadcast identi-
22
23 fier(s) intended for the STA and the BSS Color Disabled subfield is 0 in the most recently received
24 HE Operation element from the AP with which it is associated.
25
— The PPDU is an HE MU PPDU, HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU and one of the following condi-
26
27 tions are true:
28
• The RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is the BSS color of the BSS in which the STA is
29
30 associated, the RXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG is 1 and the BSS Color Disabled sub-
31 field is 0 in the most recently received HE Operation element from the AP with which it is asso-
32 ciated.
33
• The RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is the BSS color of the BSS in which the STA is
34
35 associated, the RXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG is 0 and a PHY-RXEND.indica-
36 tion(UnsupportedRate) primitive was received and the BSS Color Disabled subfield is 0 in the
37 most recently received HE Operation element from the AP with which it is associated.
38
39 — The PPDU is an HE TB PPDU where the RXVECTOR parameter BSS_COLOR is the BSS color of
40 the BSS in which the STA is associated and the BSS Color Disabled subfield is 0 in the most
41 recently received HE Operation element from the AP with which it is associated.
42
43 — The PPDU is a VHT PPDU where the RXVECTOR parameter PARTIAL_AID is the BSSID[39:47]
44 of the BSS in which the STA is associated or any of the other BSSs in the same multiple BSSID set
45
or co-hosted BSSID set to which its BSS belongs and the RXVECTOR parameter GROUP_ID is 0.
46
47 — The PPDU is a PPDU with:
48
49 • An A-MPDU including TA or RA equal to either the BSSID of the BSS in which the STA is
50 associated or any of the other BSSs in the same multiple BSSID set or co-hosted BSSID set to
51 which its BSS belongs and,
52
• The RA is not the individual MAC address of the STA or the group address(es) of the STA.
53
54 — The PPDU is either an HE MU PPDU with the RXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG set to 0 or
55
a VHT MU PPDU containing an A-MPDU with
56
57 • The RA(s) in the A-MPDU is (are) equal to the STA’s individual address and,
58
• The STA has received in the A-MPDU at least one MPDU delimiter with EOF equal to 1 and
59
60 with MPDU length field equal to 0.
61
62
A non-AP HE STA that is in intra-PPDU power save mode and has entered doze state or has become
63
unavailable shall continue to operate its NAV timers and consider the medium busy and shall transition to
64
65 the awake state at the end of the PPDU.
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 465
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

1 A non-AP HE STA that is in intra-PPDU power save mode may discard a PPDU identified as an inter-BSS
2 PPDU as defined in 26.2.2 (Intra-BSS and inter-BSS PPDU classification) until the end of the PPDU.
3
4 NOTE—The STA can contend for access to the medium immediately on the expiry of the NAV timers.

26.14.2 Power save with UORA and TWT
7
8
9 This subclause defines the power save mechanisms for a non-AP HE STA that is operating in PS mode and
10 is UORA and TWT capable.
11
12
An HE AP may indicate start times for one or more broadcast TWT SPs containing Trigger frames with ran-
13
14 dom access allocations in the broadcast TWT element that is included in a Management frame as described
15 in 26.8.3.2 (Rules for TWT scheduling AP). An example of power save operation is shown in Figure26-14
16 (Example of power save operation with UORA and TWT).
17
18
19
20
21
22

24

38

40 A TWT SP with RA-RU is a TWT SP corresponding to a Broadcast TWT Parameter Set field in a TWT ele-
41 ment that has a Broadcast TWT ID subfield equal to 0, Flow Type subfield equal to 0, Trigger subfield equal
42 to 1, and a Broadcast TWT Recommendation subfield equal to 2. An associated HE STA that supports the
43
TWT and UORA procedure when operating in PS mode, upon receiving a Beacon frame from its associated
44
AP carrying a TWT element indicating a schedule for TWT SP(s) with RA-RU, may enter the doze state if
45
46 no other condition requires it to be awake. The STA may transition to awake state at the start of a TWT SP
47 with RA-RU and follow the procedure in 26.5.4 (UL OFDMA-based random access (UORA))) to send an
48 HE TB PPDU to its associated AP.
49
50
51 An HE STA shall follow the procedure described in 26.8.5 (Power save operation during TWT SPs) to
52 determine if TWT SP termination event has occurred and may enter doze state if no other condition requires
53 the STA to remain awake.
54
55
A non-AP HE STA shall decrement its OBO counter by following the procedure in 26.5.4.3 (Transmission
56
57 procedure for UORA). If the OBO counter decrements to a nonzero value, then the STA may enter the doze
58 state until the end of the current TWT SP if the STA has not declared to the AP that it is in awake state (as
59 described in 26.8.3.3 (Rules for TWT scheduled STA)), no other condition requires it to remain awake and
60
one of the following conditions is met:
61
