26.14.3 Opportunistic power save
5
6
7 26.14.3.1 General
8
9
An OPS non-AP STA is a non-AP HE STA that sets the OPS Support subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities
10
11 Information field in the HE Capabilities element to 1.
12
13 An OPS AP is an AP HE STA that sets the OPS Support subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information
14
field in HE Capabilities element to 1.
15
16
17 The objective of the opportunistic power save mechanism is to allow OPS non-AP STAs to be unavailable
18 or to be in doze state so that they can save power for a defined period.
19
20
The opportunistic power save mechanism has two modes: aperiodic and periodic.
21
22
23 In the aperiodic mode, an OPS AP sends an OPS frame or a FILS Discovery frame at any time to provide the
24 scheduling information for all OPS non-AP STAs for the OPS period that follows the transmission of the
25
OPS frame or FILS Discovery frame. Based on this information, the OPS non-AP STAs that are in active
26
mode may be unavailable during the OPS period and the OPS non-AP STAs that are in PS mode may be in
27
28 doze state during the OPS period.
29
30 In the scheduled mode, an OPS AP splits a beacon interval into several periodic broadcast TWT SPs and
31
provides, at the beginning of each SP, the scheduling information for all OPS non-AP STAs. Based on this
32
information, the OPS non-AP STAs that are in active mode may be unavailable until the next TWT SP and
33
34 the OPS non-AP STAs that are in PS mode may be in doze state until the next TWT SP.
35
36
26.14.3.2 AP operation for opportunistic power save
37
38
39 To enable aperiodic opportunistic power save, an OPS AP shall schedule for transmission an OPS frame or a
40 FILS Discovery frame with the RA field set to the broadcast address that includes a TIM element (see
41 9.4.2.5 (TIM element)) and an OPS element (see 9.4.2.257 (OPS element)). The AP should transmit a FILS
42
Discovery frame instead of an OPS frame if the target transmission time closely aligns with the transmission
43
time of a FILS Discovery frame. The OPS Duration field in the OPS element shall be set to the duration of
44
45 the OPS period that immediately follows the transmission of the OPS frame or FILS Discovery frame. The
46 TIM element is encoded specifically as defined in 9.4.2.5 (TIM element) in order to provide the information
47 of which STAs are not scheduled during the OPS period. If the OPS AP sets the bit corresponding to an OPS
48
non-AP STA in the traffic indication virtual bitmap field carried by the Partial Virtual Bitmap of the TIM
49
element of the OPS frame or FILS Discovery frame to 0, the AP should send neither individually addressed
50
51 frames to the STA nor Trigger frames with a User Info field that addresses the STA during the OPS period.
52
53 To enable periodic opportunistic power save, an OPS AP shall include a TWT element in beacons to set a
54
periodic Broadcast TWT SP with the following information:
55
56 — The Broadcast TWT Recommendation field set to 3
57
— The Broadcast TWT ID subfield is set to 0
58
59
60 At the beginning of these periodic TWT SPs with the Broadcast TWT Recommendation field set to 3, the
61 AP shall schedule for transmission a TIM frame or a FILS Discovery frame with the RA field set to the
62
broadcast address that includes a TIM element (see 9.4.2.5 (TIM element)). The FILS Discovery frame may
63
include an OPS element. The AP should transmit a FILS Discovery frame instead of a TIM frame if the
64
65 TWT SP start time closely aligns with the transmission time of a FILS Discovery frame. If the OPS AP also
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 467
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 468 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 operates with TIM Broadcast and uses TIM frames for opportunistic power save, the OPS AP should align
2 the transmission time of a TIM frame for TIM Broadcast, with the start time of the broadcast TWT SP with
3
the Broadcast TWT Recommendation field set to 3. If the OPS AP sets the bit corresponding to an OPS non-
4
AP STA in the traffic indication virtual bitmap carried in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the TIM element
5
6 of the TIM frame or FILS Discovery frame to 0, the AP should send neither individually addressed frames to
7 the STA nor Trigger frames with a User Info field that addresses the STA during the TWT SP and until the
8 next TWT SP with the Broadcast TWT Recommendation field set to 3.
9
10
26.14.3.3 STA operation for opportunistic power save
11
12
13 With aperiodic opportunistic power save, if an OPS non-AP STA with AID N that is in the awake state
14 receives a TIM element and an OPS element in an OPS frame or a FILS Discovery frame from the associ-
15
ated OPS AP, then the STA may be unavailable if the STA is in active mode or may be in doze state if the
16
STA is in PS mode until the end of the OPS period indicated in the OPS element, if the bit N in the traffic
17
18 indication virtual bitmap carried in the Partial Virtual Bitmap field of the current TIM element is 0, unless
19 other conditions not related to operation with the OPS AP require the STA to be in the awake state. At the
20 end of the OPS period, the STA shall be in the awake state, unless determined otherwise by other power save
21
protocols.
22
23
24 With periodic opportunistic power save, if an OPS non-AP STA with AID N that is in the awake state
25 receives from the OPS AP with which it associated a TIM element with bit N of the traffic indication virtual
26
bitmap field equal to 0 in a TIM frame or FILS Discovery frame within a broadcast TWT SP with the Broad-
27
cast TWT Recommendation field set to 3, then the STA may be unavailable if the STA is in active mode or
28
29 may be in doze state if the STA is in PS mode during the TWT SP and until the next TWT SP with the
30 Broadcast TWT Recommendation field set to 3, unless other conditions not related to operation with the
31 OPS AP require the STA to be in the awake state.
32
33
An OPS non-AP STA shall not operate with TIM broadcast procedure if its associated AP is an OPS AP.
34
35
36 26.14.4 HE dynamic SM power save
37
38
A STA that supports HE dynamic SM power save has dot11HEDynamicSMPowerSaveOptionImplemented
39
set to true and shall set the HE Dynamic SM Power Save subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information
40
41 field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1.
42
43
A non-AP HE STA in dynamic SM power save mode (see 11.2.6 (SM power save)) that sets the HE
44
Dynamic SM Power Save subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities ele-
45
46 ment it transmits to 1 shall follow the dynamic SM power save procedures defined in 11.2.6 (SM power
47 save) and shall also enable its multiple receive chains if it responds to a Trigger frame that starts a frame
48 exchange sequence that satisfies the following conditions:
49
— The Trigger frame is transmitted with a single spatial stream.
50
51 — The Trigger frame is from the associated AP or from the AP corresponding to the transmitted BSSID
52
if the non-AP HE STA is associated with an AP corresponding to a nontransmitted BSSID and has
53
indicated support for receiving Control frames with TA set to the transmitted BSSID by setting the
54
55 Rx Control Frame To MultiBSS subfield to 1 in the HE Capabilities element that the non-AP HE
56 STA transmits.
57
— The Trigger frame is an MU-RTS Trigger frame, BSRP Trigger frame or BQRP Trigger frame that
58
59 includes a User Info field with the AID12 subfield equal to the 12 LSBs of the AID of the non-AP
60 HE STA (see 26.5.2.2.1 (General)).
61
62
The non-AP HE STA shall, subject to its spatial stream capabilities (see 9.4.2.55.4 (Supported MCS Set
63
field), 9.4.2.157.3 (Supported VHT-MCS and NSS Set field) and 9.4.2.248 (HE Capabilities element)) and
64
65 operating mode (see 11.41 (Notification of operating mode changes) and 26.9 (Operating mode indication)),
468 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 469 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 be capable of receiving a PPDU that is sent using more than one spatial stream a SIFS after the end of the
2 PPDU that it sends in response. The STA may switch back to single receive chain mode immediately after
3
the end of the frame exchange sequence.
4
5
NOTE 1—A Trigger frame always solicits an immediate response.
6
7 NOTE 2—A non-AP HE STA that is in dynamic SM power save mode and that sets the HE Dynamic SM Power Save
8 subfield in the HE MAC Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element it transmits to 1 cannot distin-
9 guish between a Trigger frame that precedes a MIMO transmission and a Trigger frame that does not precede a MIMO
10 transmission and, therefore, always enables its multiple receive chains if it responds to an MU-RTS Trigger frame,
11 BSRP Trigger frame, or BQRP Trigger frame that has a User Info field addressed to it.
12
13 NOTE 3—The STA determines the end of the frame exchange sequence as described in 11.2.6 (SM power save).
14
15
26.15 PPDU format, BW, MCS, NSS, and DCM selection rules
16
17
18 26.15.1 General
19
20
21 An HE STA can transmit different PPDUs formats, with different transmit parameters, such as channel
22 width, MCS, NSS, DCM. This subclause defines the rules followed by an HE STA for selecting these
23 parameters depending on the capabilities of the intended receiver(s) and other considerations.
24
25
26.15.2 PPDU format selection
26
27
28 An HE STA that transmits non-HT, HT, or VHT PPDUs shall follow the rules in 10.6 (Multirate support).
29
An HE STA may transmit an HE SU PPDU to a peer HE STA subject to the restrictions defined below.
30
31
32 An HE AP may transmit an HE MU PPDU as defined in 26.5.1 (HE DL MU operation). A non-AP HE STA
33 transmits HE TB PPDUs as defined in 26.5.2 (UL MU operation).
34
35
A STA shall not transmit a 242-tone HE ER SU PPDU to a peer non-AP STA if the most recently received
36
37 OM Control field from that peer non-AP STA, if any, has the ER SU Disable subfield equal to 1.
38
39 A STA shall not transmit a 242-tone HE ER SU PPDU to an AP if the most recently received HE Operation
40
element from that AP has the ER SU Disable subfield equal to 1.
41
42
43 A STA shall not transmit a 106-tone HE ER SU PPDU to a peer STA if the HE Capabilities element
44 received from that peer STA has the Partial Bandwidth Extended Range field equal to 0.
45
46
A non-AP STA, TDLS STA or IBSS STA shall not transmit a 20 MHz HE MU PPDU with just a 106-tone
47
48 RU to a peer STA unless it has received from the peer STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx Partial
49 BW SU In 20 MHz HE MU PPDU subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
50
51 NOTE—A non-AP STA transmitting an HE MU PPDU sets the TXVECTOR parameter UPLINK_FLAG to 1 if the
52 PPDU is sent to the AP and to 0 if the PPDU is sent to a TDLS STA (see 26.11.2 (UPLINK_FLAG)). The HE MU
53 PPDU format enables the non-AP STA to include its AID (i.e., transmitter’s AID if the UPLINK_FLAG is 1 and the
54 receiver’s AID if the UPLINK_FLAG is 0) in the PHY header of the PPDU and its use is out of scope of the standard.
55
56 An HE STA shall not transmit an HE MU PPDU with a single user being allocated an RU occupying the
57
entire PPDU bandwidth and a compressed HE-SIG-B to a peer STA unless the HE STA has received from
58
the peer STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx Full BW SU Using HE MU PPDU With Compressed
59
60 HE-SIG-B subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
61
62
An HE STA shall not transmit an HE MU PPDU with a single user being allocated an RU occupying the
63
entire PPDU bandwidth and a noncompressed HE-SIG-B to a peer STA unless the PPDU bandwidth is less
64
65 than or equal to 80 MHz and the HE STA has received from the peer STA an HE Capabilities element with
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 469
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 470 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 the Rx Full BW SU Using HE MU PPDU With Non-Compressed HE-SIG-B subfield in the HE PHY Capa-
2 bilities Information field equal to 1.
3
4
An HE STA shall send Control frames following the rules defined in 10.6.6 (Rate selection for Control
5
6 frames) with the following exceptions:
7
— A Control frame shall be carried in an HT PPDU, VHT PPDU, HE ER SU PPDU or HE SU PPDU
8
when the Control frame is sent using an STBC frame.
9
10 — A Control frame sent by the AP as a response to an HE TB PPDU may be carried in any PPDU for-
11
mat that is supported by the intended receivers.
12
13 — A Trigger frame that is not an MU-RTS Trigger frame may be carried in any PPDU format that is
14 supported by the intended receivers subject to the restrictions in 26.5.2 (UL MU operation).
15
16 — A Control frame is carried in an HE TB PPDU if it is sent as a response to a PPDU that contains a
17 Trigger frame that is not an MU-RTS Trigger frame or if it is sent as a response to a PPDU that con-
18 tains a frame containing a TRS Control subfield (see 26.5.2 (UL MU operation)).
19
— A Control frame sent by an HE STA as a response to an HE ER SU PPDU that does not contain a
20
21 triggering frame should be carried in an HE ER SU PPDU unless the most recent PPDU sent by the
22 HE STA to the recipient of the Control frame, after association, was not an HE ER SU PPDU in
23 which case the Control frame should be carried in a non-HT PPDU.
24
25 — A Control frame sent by an HE STA as a response to an HE SU PPDU or a non-HT PPDU that does
26 not contain a triggering frame should be carried in a non-HT PPDU unless the most recent PPDU
27 sent by the HE STA to the recipient of the Control frame, after association, was an HE ER SU PPDU
28
in which case the Control frame should be carried in an HE ER SU PPDU.
29
30 — A control response frame shall not be sent in an HE ER SU PPDU if the channel bandwidth of the
31 PPDU containing the frame that elicited the response is greater than 20 MHz.
32
33 — A Control frame that is not solicited by another frame and is not a Trigger frame may be carried in an
34 HE ER SU PPDU.
35
— A Control frame sent in the 6 GHz band as a response to an HE SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU, and that
36
37 is not carried in HE TB PPDU, may be carried in an HE SU PPDU if the transmit time of HE SU
38 PPDU is less than or equal to the PPDU duration of a non-HT PPDU containing the Control frame
39 sent at the primary rate (see 10.6.6.5.2 (Selection of a rate or MCS)).
40
41 NOTE 1—A change in the format of the PPDU containing the control response frame (between non-HT and HE ER SU
42 PPDU) occurs in subsequent TXOPs. A STA that solicits a control response frame from a responding STA accounts for
43 the PPDU format of the control response frame to calculate the expected duration of the TXOP. The responding STA
44 determines that the most recent PPDU sent to the soliciting STA is received if it receives an immediate acknowledgment
45 by the soliciting STA in response to the PPDU.
46
NOTE 2—A STA does not transmit a Control frame in an HE ER SU PPDU to a receiving STA unless the receiving
47
STA indicates that HE ER SU PPDU reception is enabled.
48
49
50 An HE STA should send an Ack frame that is the response to a Fine Timing Measurement frame carried in a
51 VHT PPDU or HT PPDU in the same PPDU format as the PPDU carrying the Fine Timing Measurement
52
frame.
53
54
55 26.15.3 MCS, NSS, BW and DCM selection
56
57 An HE STA shall follow the rules defined in 10.6 (Multirate support) and 26.15.4 (Rate selection constraints
58
for HE STAs) for selecting the rate, MCS, NSS, and the rules defined in 10.3.2.8 (VHT and S1G RTS proce-
59
dure), 10.3.2.9 (CTS and DMG CTS procedure), 10.6.6.6 (Channel Width selection for Control frames) and
60
61 10.6.12 (Channel Width in non-HT and non-HT duplicate PPDUs) for selecting the channel width (BW) of
62 transmitted PPDUs with the following exceptions:
63
— HE-MCS, NSS, and BW selection for an HE TB PPDU are defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behav-
64
65 ior for UL MU operation).
470 Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved.
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 471 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 — Rate and BW selection for a CTS sent in response to an MU-RTS Trigger frame are defined in 26.2.6
2 (MU-RTS Trigger/CTS frame exchange procedure).
3
4 — A STA that transmits a Control frame carried in a non-HT PPDU that is a response to a frame
5 received in an HE ER SU PPDU shall set the rate of the non-HT PPDU to 6 Mb/s.
6
7 — A STA that transmits a Control frame that is an S-MPDU carried in an HE ER SU PPDU and that is
8 a response to a frame received in an HE ER SU PPDU shall use the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple <HE-
9 MCS 0, 1>.
10
11 — NSS and BW selection is further constrained as defined in 26.9 (Operating mode indication), 11.42
12 (Notification of operating mode changes), 26.15.2 (PPDU format selection), 26.17 (HE BSS opera-
13 tion) and in the remaining subclauses of 26.15 (PPDU format, BW, MCS, NSS, and DCM selection
14
rules).
15
16
17 An HE STA that transmits an HE PPDU to a receiving STA shall use an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple that is sup-
18 ported by the receiving STA as indicated by the Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field in the HE Capabili-
19
ties element that the receiving STA transmits. If the Supported HE-MCS and NSS set of the receiving STA
20
or STAs is not known, the transmitting STA shall transmit using a <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple in the basic HE-
21
22 MCS and NSS set if the basic HE-MCS and NSS set is not empty, otherwise the transmitting STA shall
23 transmit using a <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple in the mandatory HE-MCS and NSS Set. An HE STA is subject to
24 all of the rules for HT STAs and VHT STAs that apply to its operating band (see 10.27 (Protection mecha-
25
nisms)).
26
27
28 An HE STA may transmit an HE PPDU with 1024-QAM on a 26-, 52-, and 106-tone RU to a recipient STA
29 if it has received from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element with the Rx 1024-QAM < 242-tone RU
30
Support subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1; otherwise the HE STA shall not
31
transmit an HE PPDU with 1024-QAM on a 26-, 52-, and 106-tone RU.
32
33
34 An HE AP shall not set the UL HE-MCS subfield of a User Info field in a Trigger frame to 10 or 11 for a 26-
35
, 52-, or 106-tone RU allocation unless the User Info field is addressed to a non-AP HE STA from which the
36
HE AP has received an HE Capabilities element with the Tx 1024-QAM < 242-tone RU Support subfield in
37
38 the HE PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 1.
39
40 An HE STA that sends a Control frame in response to a frame carried in an HE SU PPDU or an HE ER SU
41
PPDU or an HE MU PPDU that carries a frame with the Normal Ack or Implicit BAR ack policy shall set
42
the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH to indicate a channel width that is the same as the channel
43
44 width indicated by the RXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH of the frame eliciting the response. If
45 the most recently received PPDU sent by the responding STA to the soliciting STA after association was an
46 HE ER SU PPDU, the soliciting STA shall set the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH to CBW20
47
for an HE SU PPDU and to ER-RU-242 or ER-RU-H-106 for an HE ER SU PPDU.
48
49
NOTE—A preamble punctured HE MU PPDU cannot carry a frame with Normal Ack or Implicit BAR ack policy
50
whose acknowledgment would be transmitted in one or more punctured 20 MHz channels of the preamble punctured HE
51
MU PPDU (see 26.4.4.3 (Responding to an HE MU PPDU with an SU PPDU)).
52
53
54 If a control response frame is transmitted in an HE SU PPDU or HE MU PPDU, the channel width
55 (CH_BANDWIDTH parameter of the TXVECTOR) shall be selected first according to 10.6.6.6 (Channel
56 Width selection for Control frames), and then the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple shall be selected from a set of
57
<HE-MCS, NSS> tuples called the CandidateMCSSet. The CandidateMCSSet is defined in 10.6.6.5.3 (Con-
58
trol response frame MCS computation) except that the set additionally contains the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples
59
60 for an HE STA.
61
62
An HE STA may transmit an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU with DCM applied to the Data field to a
63
recipient STA if it has received from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element with the DCM Max Con-
64
65 stellation Rx subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field greater than 0; otherwise the HE STA
Copyright © 2020 IEEE. All rights reserved. 471
This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

## 第 472 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 shall not transmit an HE SU PPDU or HE ER SU PPDU with DCM applied to the Data field to the recipient
2 STA.
3
4
An HE STA may transmit to a recipient STA an HE MU PPDU with DCM applied to the HE-SIG-B field
5
6 and/or an RU in the Data field addressed to the STA if it has received from the recipient STA an HE Capa-
7 bilities element with the DCM Max Constellation Rx subfield in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field
8 greater than 0; otherwise the HE STA shall not transmit to the recipient STA an HE MU PPDU with DCM
9
applied to the HE-SIG-B field and/or an RU in the Data field addressed to the STA.
10
11
12 An HE STA transmits an HE TB PPDU with DCM as defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-AP STA behavior for UL
13 MU operation). An HE AP shall not set the DCM subfield of a User Info field in a Trigger frame to 1 if it has
14
not received from the recipient STA an HE Capabilities element with the DCM Max Constellation Tx sub-
15
field in the HE PHY Capabilities Information field greater than 0.
16
17
18 An HE STA that transmits an HE PPDU with DCM applied to the Data field to a recipient STA shall use an
19 RU size that is less than or equal to the maximum RU size indicated in the DCM Max RU subfield in the HE
20
PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element received from the recipient STA.
21
22
23 An HE AP that transmits a Trigger frame addressed to a recipient STA that solicits an HE TB PPDU with
24 DCM shall set the RU Allocation subfield in the Trigger frame to indicate an RU size that is less than or
25 equal to the maximum RU size indicated in the DCM Max RU subfield in HE PHY Capabilities Information
26
field in the HE Capabilities element received from the recipient STA.
27
28
29 An HE STA that transmits an HE PPDU with DCM applied to the Data field to a recipient STA shall use an
30 NSS that is less than or equal to the value indicated in the DCM Max NSS Rx subfield in the HE PHY Capa-
31
bilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element received from the recipient STA.
32
33
34 An HE AP that transmits a Trigger frame with a User Info field addressed to a recipient STA and with the
35 UL DCM subfield in the User Info field set to 1 shall set the Number Of Spatial Streams subfield in the SS
36 Allocation subfield in the User Info field to less than or equal to the DCM Max NSS Tx subfield in HE PHY
37
Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element received from the recipient STA.
38
39
40 An HE AP shall not transmit a Trigger frame with the UL STBC subfield set to 1 and the UL BW subfield
41 set to indicate a bandwidth less than or equal to 80 MHz if at least one User Info field is addressed to a non-
42
AP HE STA from which the HE AP has received an HE Capabilities element with the STBC Tx ≤ 80 MHz
43
subfield in HE PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 0.
44
45
46 An HE AP shall not transmit a Trigger frame with the UL STBC subfield set to 1 and the UL BW subfield
47 set to indicate 80+80 MHz or 160 MHz if at least one User Info field is addressed to a non-AP HE STA from
48
which the HE AP has received an HE Capabilities element with the STBC Tx > 80 MHz subfield in HE
49
PHY Capabilities Information field equal to 0.
50
51
52 An HE STA that sends a Control frame in an HE ER SU PPDU format should use:
53
— DCM encoding if the most recently received PPDU sent by the HE STA, after association, to the
54
55 STA soliciting the Control frame used DCM; otherwise the STA should not use DCM for the Control
56 frame.
57
— 106-tone HE ER SU PPDU if the most recently received PPDU sent by the HE STA, after associa-
58
59 tion, to the STA soliciting the Control frame was a 106-tone HE ER SU PPDU; otherwise the STA
60 should not use a 106-tone HE ER SU PPDU for the Control frame.
61
62 NOTE—Transmit parameter switching occurs in subsequent TXOPs. A STA that solicits a Control frame from a peer
63 STA accounts for the transmit parameter of the Control frame to calculate the expected duration of the TXOP. The
64 responding STA determines that the most recent PPDU sent to the soliciting STA is received if it receives an immediate
65 acknowledgment by the soliciting STA in response to the PPDU.

1 26.15.4 Rate selection constraints for HE STAs
2
3
26.15.4.1 Receive HE-MCS and NSS Set
4
5
6 The receive HE-MCS and NSS set is the set of <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples for PPDU bandwidths less than or
7
equal to 80 MHz, 160 MHz PPDUs or 80+80 MHz PPDUs that a STA is capable of receiving. The receive
8
HE-MCS and NSS set for a first STA is determined by a second HE STA for each <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple
9
10 NSS = 1, …, 8 and PPDU bandwidth (less than or equal to 80 MHz, and 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz) from the
11 Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field in the HE Capabilities element received from the first STA as fol-
12 lows:
13
14 — If support for the HE-MCS for NSS spatial streams at that PPDU bandwidth is mandatory (see 27.1.1
15 (Introduction to the HE PHY)), then the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that bandwidth is supported by
16 the first STA on receive.
17
18 — Otherwise, if the Max HE-MCS For n SS subfield (n = NSS) in each Rx HE-MCS Map b subfield for
19 b{≤80 MHz, 160 MHz, 80+80 MHz} indicates support and neither the Operating Mode field nor
20 the OM Control subfield is received from the first HE STA, then the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at
21
PPDU bandwidth b for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on receive as
22
defined in 9.4.2.248.4 (Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field).
23
24
— Otherwise,
25
26 • If the Operating Mode field is received from the first HE STA, the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that
27 PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on receive as
28
defined in 9.4.2.248.4 (Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field) and by Equation(9-2a).
29
30 • If the OM Control subfield is received from the first HE STA, the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that
31 PPDU bandwidth for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on receive as
32 defined in 9.4.2.248.4 (Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field) and by Equation(26-4).
33
34 — Otherwise, the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth is not supported by the first STA on
35 receive.
36
37
The <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples excluded by 26.15.4.3 (Additional rate selection constraints for HE PPDUs)
38
39 can also be eliminated from the receive HE-MCS and NSS set.
40
41
An HE STA shall not, unless explicitly stated otherwise, transmit an HE PPDU unless the <HE-MCS, NSS>
42
tuple and bandwidth used are in the receive HE-MCS and NSS set of the receiving STA(s).
43
44
45 26.15.4.2 Transmit HE-MCS and NSS Set
46
47
48 The transmit HE-MCS and NSS set is the set of <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples for PPDU bandwidth less than or
49 equal to 80 MHz, 106 MHz PPDUs or 80+80 MHz PPDUs that a STA is capable of transmitting. The trans-
50 mit HE-MCS and NSS set of a first STA is determined by a second STA for each <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple
51
NSS = 1, …, 8 and PPDU bandwidth (less than or equal to 80 MHz, and 160 MHz or 80+80MHz) from the
52
Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field received from the first STA as follows:
53
54 — If support for the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that bandwidth is mandatory (see 27.1.1 (Introduction to
55
the HE PHY)), then the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth is supported by the first
56
STA on transmit.
57
58
— Otherwise, if the Max HE-MCS For n SS subfield (n = NSS) in each Tx HE-MCS Map b subfield for
59
b{≤80 MHz, 160 MHz, 80+80 MHz} indicates support, then the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at
60
61 PPDU bandwidth b for a given operating channel width is supported by the first STA on transmit as
62 defined in 9.4.2.248.4 (Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field).
63
— Otherwise, the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple at that PPDU bandwidth is not supported by the first STA on
64
65 transmit.

STS
2 operating mode (TOM) indication) from its transmit HE-MCS and NSS set.

5 26.15.4.3 Additional rate selection constraints for HE PPDUs
6
7
A STA shall not transmit a 20MHz or 40MHz HE PPDU with an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple that has HE-MCS
8
9 0, 1, 2 or 3 and NSS less than or equal to 4 to a receiver STA that has marked as unsupported the HT-MCS
10 with value HE-MCS+8×(NSS–1) in the Rx MCS Bitmask subfield in the Supported MCS Set field in the
11 HT Capabilities element it transmits. The transmission of a 20 MHz or 40 MHz HE PPDU with HE-MCS
12
greater than 3 is not subject to this constraint.
13
14
15 A STA shall not transmit an 80 MHz, 160 MHz or 80+80 MHz HE PPDU with an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple
16 that has HE-MCS 0 or 1 and NSS less than or equal to 4 to a receiver STA that has marked as unsupported
17
the HT-MCS values of both 2×HE-MCS+8×(NSS–1) and 2×HE-MCS+1+8×(NSS–1) in the Rx
18
MCS Bitmask subfield in the Supported MCS Set field in the HT Capabilities element it transmits. The
19
20 transmission of an 80 MHz, 160 MHz or 80+80MHz HE PPDU with HE-MCS greater than 1 is not subject
21 to this constraint.
22
23
24 An example tabulation of this behavior is given in Table26-14 (Example of rate selection for HE PPDUs).
25
26
27 Table 26-14—Example of rate selection for HE PPDUs
28
29
30 <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples
31 HT-MCSs that are marked <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples that are not used for
32 as unsupported that are not used for CBW80, CBW160, and
33 CBW20 and CBW40 CBW80+80
34
35 0, 8, 16 <0, 1>, <0, 2>, <0, 3> -
36
37 1, 9 <1, 1>, <1, 2> -
38
39 10 <2, 2> -
40
3 <3, 1> -
41

0, 1 <0, 1>, <1, 1> <0, 1>
43
44 2, 3 <2, 1>, <3, 1> <1, 1>
45
46 0, 1, 8, 9 <0, 1>, <1, 1>, <0, 2>, <1, 2> <0, 1>, <0, 2>
47
48
49
50 26.15.4.4 Rx Supported VHT-MCS and NSS Set
51
52
53 For each <VHT-MCS, NSS> tuple, NSS = 1, …, 8, and bandwidth (20 MHz, 40 MHz, 80 MHz, and 160
54 MHz or 80+80 MHz) from the Supported VHT-MCS and NSS Set field received from a first STA, a second
55 HE STA shall follow the rules in subclause 10.6.13.1 (Rx Supported VHT-MCS and NSS Set) to determine
56
the Rx Supported VHT-MCS and NSS Set of the first HE STA with the following exception:
57
58 — If the second HE STA receives OM Control subfield from the first HE STA, the receive HE-MCS
59
and NSS Set of a first HE STA is determined by a second HE STA according to 9.4.2.157.3 (Sup-
60
ported VHT-MCS and NSS Set field) and Table26-9 (Setting of the VHT Channel Width and VHT
61
62 NSS at an HE STA transmitting the OM Control subfield).
63
64 NOTE—If the second STA receives both an Operating Mode field and an OM Control subfield from the first STA, the
65 rules in 26.9.1 (General) apply.

1 26.15.5 Additional rules for ER beacons and group addressed frames
2
3
An AP that transmits group addressed frames in an HE ER SU PPDU shall transmit the HE ER SU PPDU
4
with an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple where the HE-MCS is a mandatory HE-MCS and NSS=1.
5
6
7 A group addressed frame (including a Beacon frame) transmitted in an HE ER SU PPDU shall be sent as an
8 S-MPDU (see Table 9-532 (A-MPDU contents in the S-MPDU context)), except for group addressed Data
9
frames, which are not required to be sent as an S-MPDU, but are required to follow the rules in 10.12.4 (A-
10
MPDU aggregation of group addressed Data frames).
11
12
13 The HE AP transmitting the HE ER SU PPDU shall set the TXVECTOR parameters as follows:
14
— CH_BANDWIDTH to ER-RU-242
15

17

18

20

31 — BEAM_CHANGE as defined in 26.11.3 (BEAM_CHANGE)

35

37
<HE-MCS, NSS> tuple where the HE-MCS is a mandatory HE-MCS and NSS=1.
38
39 NOTE—An AP does not send a Beacon frame in an HE SU PPDU (an HE beacon) unless it is operating in the 6 GHz
40 band (see 26.17.2.2 (Beacons in the 6 GHz band)).
41
42
A group addressed frame (including a Beacon frame) transmitted in an HE SU PPDU shall be sent as an S-
43
MPDU (see Table 9-532 (A-MPDU contents in the S-MPDU context)), except for group addressed Data
44
45 frames, which are not required to be sent as an S-MPDU, but are required to follow 10.12.4 (A-MPDU
46 aggregation of group addressed Data frames).
47
48
If the HE SU PPDU contains a group addressed frame intended for at least one STA that is not associated to
49
50 the AP, then the HE AP shall set the TXVECTOR parameters for the HE PPDU as follows:
51 — CH_BANDWIDTH to CBW20
52
53 — HE_LTF_TYPE to 2xHE-LTF and GI_TYPE to 0u8s_GI or 1u6s_GI, or HE_LTF_TYPE to 4xHE-
54 LTF and GI_TYPE to 3u2s_GI
55
— FEC_CODING to BCC_CODING
56
57 — STBC to 0
58
— DCM to 0
59
60 — DOPPLER to 0
61
62 — BEAMFORMED to 0
63 — NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING to 16µs
64
65 — NO_SIG_EXTN to false in the 2.4 GHz band and true otherwise

1 — BEAM_CHANGE as defined in 26.11.3 (BEAM_CHANGE)

26.15.7 Additional rules for group addressed frames in an HE MU PPDU
10
11
12 An HE AP may include group addressed frames in an HE MU PPDU subject to the rules defined in this sub-
13 clause.
14
15
16 An HE AP shall not include a Beacon frame in an HE MU PPDU.
17
18 An HE AP that includes a group addressed frame in an HE MU PPDU shall ensure that the frame is included
19
in a broadcast RU in the HE MU PPDU. The HE AP shall additionally ensure that the following conditions
20
are satisfied for the broadcast RU:
21
22 — The RU allocation shall comply with the rules in 26.5.1.3 (RU allocation in an HE MU PPDU) and
23
27.3.2.8 (RU restrictions for 20 MHz operation)
24
25 — The <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple shall have a mandatory HE-MCS and NSS = 1
26
— The broadcast RU shall be located within:
27
28 • The primary 20 MHz channel if the group addressed frame is a FILS Discovery or a Probe
29
Response frame, except when the primary 20 MHz channel does not coincide with a PSC and the
30
AP is a 6 GHz-only AP, in which case the broadcast RU may be in a PSC that is within the BSS
31
32 operating channel width (see 26.17.2.3 (Scanning in the 6 GHz band)). The broadcast RU size
33 shall not exceed 106 subcarriers if the MU PPDU has a bandwidth that is greater than 20 MHz.
34 • The primary 20 MHz channel if the group addressed frame is addressed to at least one associated
35
non-AP STA that has not declared to be in the awake state. The broadcast RU size shall not
36
exceed 106 subcarriers if the MU PPDU has a bandwidth that is greater than 20 MHz.
37
38 • A bandwidth that is indicated as supported in reception by one or more associated non-AP STAs,
39 if the group addressed frame is addressed only to those non-AP STAs and the STAs have
40
declared to be in the awake state. The broadcast RU size shall not exceed the minimum common
41
bandwidth that is supported in reception by all STAs in the HE Capabilities element they trans-
42
43 mit or in the most recently sent OM Control or OMN frames.
44 • The SST subchannel if the group addressed frame is addressed to one or more HE SST STAs, the
45 primary 20 MHz channel does not coincide with the subchannel assigned to the HE SST STAs
46
and the frame is not addressed to any STAs other than the HE SST STAs in that subchannel (see
47
26.8.7.2 (SST operation)). The broadcast RU size shall not exceed 106 subcarriers if the SST
48
49 subchannel is 20 MHz.
50
— The TXVECTOR parameters listed below shall be set as follows:
51
52 • HE_LTF_TYPE to 2xHE-LTF and GI_TYPE to 0u8s_GI or 1u6s_GI, or HE_LTF_TYPE to
53 4xHE-LTF and GI_TYPE to 3u2s_GI
54
• FEC_CODING to BCC_CODING
55
56 • STBC to 0
57 • DCM to 0
58
• DOPPLER to 0
59
• BEAMFORMED to 0
60
61 • NOMINAL_PACKET_PADDING to 16 µs
62 • NO_SIG_EXTN to false in the 2.4 GHz band and true otherwise
63
• BEAM_CHANGE as defined in 26.11.3 (BEAM_CHANGE)
64
65 • STA_ID as defined in 26.11.1 (STA_ID)

1 Group addressed frames transmitted in an HE MU PPDU shall be sent as an S-MPDU (see Table 9-535 (A-
2 MPDU contents in the S-MPDU context)) except that group addressed Data frames are not required to be

7 26.15.8 Additional rules for PPDUs sent in the 6 GHz band
8
9
10 An HE STA that transmits a PPDU that is not sent in response to a Trigger frame in the 6GHz band and that
11 contains a frame that is not a control response frame with the Address 1 field set to the MAC address of an
12 HE AP with which it is not associated and from which it has received a FILS Discovery frame or an HE
13
Operation element shall ensure that the PPDU meets the following conditions:
14
15 — The bandwidth of the PPDU is less than or equal to the operating bandwidth of the HE BSS as indi-
16
cated in the BSS Operating Channel Width subfield of the FILS Discovery frame or in the Channel
17
Width subfield of the HE Operation element sent by the AP
18
19 — The PPDU is transmitted with a number of spatial streams that is less than or equal to the maximum
20
number of spatial streams of the HE BSS as indicated in the Maximum Number of Spatial Stream
21
subfield of the FILS Discovery frame or in the Basic HE-MCS and NSS Set field of the HE Opera-
22
23 tion element sent by the AP
24
— If the PPDU is an HE PPDU, then the PPDU is transmitted with an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple provid-
25
26 ing a data rate that is greater than or equal to the minimum rate indicated in the FILS Minimum Rate
27 field (if present) of the FILS Discovery frame or in the Minimum Rate field of the HE Operation ele-
28 ment sent by the AP.
29
30 — If the PPDU is a non-HT PPDU then the PPDU is transmitted with a data rate that is greater than or
31 equal to the minimum of <R, 54 Mb/s>, where R is the minimum rate indicated in the FILS Mini-
32 mum Rate field (if present) of the FILS Discovery frame or in the Minimum Rate field of the HE
33
Operation element sent by the AP
34
35
36 An HE STA that transmits a PPDU that is not sent in response to a Trigger frame in the 6 GHz band and that
37 contains a frame that is not a control response frame with Address 1 field set to the MAC address of the AP
38
with which it is associated shall ensure that the PPDU meets the following conditions:
39
40 — If the PPDU is a non-HT (duplicate) PPDU then the PPDU is transmitted with a data rate that is
41
greater than or equal to the minimum of <R, 54 Mb/s>, where R is the minimum rate indicated in the
42
Minimum Rate field of the HE Operation element sent by the AP.
43
44 — If the PPDU is an HE PPDU then the PPDU is transmitted with an <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple providing
45
a data rate that is not less than the data rate indicated in the Minimum Rate field of the HE Operation
46
element sent by the AP.
47
48
49 An HE STA that transmits a PPDU that is not an HE TB PPDU in the 6 GHz band and that contains a frame
50
that is not a control response frame with Address 1 field set to the MAC address of an AP with which it is
51
not associated shall determine a local maximum transmit power for that transmission following the rules in
52
53 11.7.5 (Specification of regulatory and local maximum transmit power levels), if the local maximum trans-
54 mit power is received in Transmit Power Envelope elements and combinations of Country elements and
55 Power Constraint elements in the most recent Beacon or Probe Response frame, on the channel from that
56
AP.

## 第 479 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 26.17 HE BSS operation
2
3
4 26.17.1 Basic HE BSS operation
5
6
The Beacon frames generated within an HE BSS contain an HE Operation element.
7
8
9 An HE STA has dot11HEOptionImplemented equal to true.
10
11
A STA that is operating in an HE BSS shall be able to receive and transmit at each of the <HE-MCS, NSS>
12
13 tuple values indicated by the Basic HE-MCS And NSS Set field of the HE Operation parameter of the
14 MLME-START.request primitive and shall be able to receive at each of the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuple values
15 indicated by the Supported HE-MCS and NSS Set field in the HE Capabilities parameter of the MLME-
16
START.request primitive.
17
18
19 The basic HE-MCS and NSS set is the set of <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples that are supported by all HE STAs
20 that are members of an HE BSS. It is established by the STA that starts the HE BSS, indicated by the Basic
21
HE-MCS And NSS Set field of the HE Operation parameter in the MLME-START.request primitive. Other
22
HE STAs determine the basic HE-MCS and NSS set from the Basic HE-MCS And NSS Set field of the HE
23
24 Operation element in the BSSDescription derived through the scan mechanism (see 11.1.4.1 (General)).
25
26
An HE STA shall not attempt to join (MLME-JOIN.request primitive) a BSS unless it supports (i.e., is able
27
to both transmit and receive using) all of the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples in the basic HE-MCS and NSS set.
28
29
NOTE—An HE STA does not attempt to (re)associate with an HE AP unless the STA supports (i.e., is able to both
30
transmit and receive using) all of the <HE-MCS, NSS> tuples in the Basic HE-MCS And NSS Set field in the HE Oper-
31
ation element transmitted by the AP because the MLME-JOIN.request primitive is a necessary precursor to (re)associa-
32
tion.
33
34
35 A STA operating in the 2.4 GHz band that sets dot11HEOptionImplemented to true shall set
36 dot11HighThroughputOptionImplemented to true. A STA operating in the 5 GHz or 6 GHz band that sets
37
dot11HEOptionImplemented to true shall set both dot11VHTOptionImplemented and dot11HighThrough-
38
putOptionImplemented to true. A non-AP STA that sets dot11HEOptionImplemented to true shall set dot11-
39
40 MultiBSSIDImplemented to true.
41
42
An HE STA operating in the 6 GHz band is a VHT STA except that it is exempt from following VHT and
43
44 HT functionalities and/or requirements that are not applicable or that are superseded by equivalent HE func-
45 tionalities and/or requirements (see Clause26 (High Efficiency (HE) MAC specification) and Clause27
46 (High Efficiency (HE) PHY specification)), and that it shall use the HE format instead of the VHT, HT_GF,
47
or HT_MF format for PPDUs transmitted in the 6 GHz band. Additional HE functionalities and/or require-
48
ments for the 6 GHz band are defined in 26.17.2 (HE BSS operation in the 6 GHz band).
49
50
51 A STA that is an HE AP or an HE mesh STA declares the channel widths at which it is capable of operating
52
in the PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element that it transmits (see Table 9-322b
53
(Subfields of the HE PHY Capabilities Information field)).
54
55
56 An HE AP operating in the 5 GHz or 6 GHz bands shall set B1 in the Supported Channel Width Set field in
57
the PHY Capabilities Information field in the HE Capabilities element to indicate support for 40 MHz and
58
80 MHz channel width.
59
60
61 A STA transmitting an HT Capabilities element and HE Capabilities element shall set the Supported Chan-
62
nel Width Set subfield of the HT Capabilities element to 1 if either B0 or B1 of the Supported Channel
63
Width Set subfield in the HE Capabilities element is 1 unless the STA is a 20 MHz-only non-AP HE STA, in
64
65 which case the Supported Channel Width Set subfield of the HT Capabilities element shall be set to 0.

This is an unapproved IEEE Standards Draft, subject to change.

1 A STA transmitting a VHT Capabilities element and HE Capabilities element shall set the Supported Chan-
2 nel Width Set subfield of the VHT Capabilities element to indicate the same channel width as indicated in
3
the HE Capabilities element unless the STA is a 20 MHz-only non-AP HE STA, in which case the Sup-
4
ported Channel Width Set subfield of the VHT Capabilities element is reserved.
5
6
7 At a minimum, an HE STA sets the Rx MCS Bitmask subfield of the Supported MCS Set field of its HT
8
Capabilities element according to the setting of each Rx HE-MCS Map b subfield, b{≤80 MHz, 160
9
MHz, 80+80 MHz}, of the Supported HE-MCS And NSS Set field of its HE Capabilities element as fol-
10
11 lows: for each Max HE-MCS For n SS subfield, 1≤n≤4, of each Rx HE-MCS Map b subfield, b{≤80
12 MHz, 160 MHz, 80+80 MHz}, with a value other than 3 (no support for that number of spatial streams), the
13 STA shall indicate support for HT MCSs 8×(n–1) to 8×(n–1)+7 in the Rx MCS Bitmask subfield,
14
where n is the number of spatial streams, except for those HT-MCSs marked as unsupported as described in
15

18 An HE AP or an HE mesh STA shall set the VHT Operation Information Present field in the HE Operation
19 element to 0 if a VHT Operation element is present in the frame that carries the HE Operation element or if
20

21
shall set the VHT Operation Information Present field in the HE Operation element to 1 if a VHT Operation
22
23 element is not present in the frame that carries the HE Operation element and the frame is sent in the 5 GHz
24 band.

26

27
28 mation Present field set to 1 shall do one of the following to set the BSS operating channel:
29

30

31

34 HE Operation element to indicate the BSS bandwidth as defined in Table 11-24 (VHT BSS band-
35
width) and Table 11-26 (Extended NSS channel width) respectively based on the Extended NSS BW
36
Support and Supported Channel Width Set fields.
37
38 — Set the STA Channel Width subfield and Channel Center Frequency Segment 2 subfield in the HT
39
Operation Information field in the HT Operation element, the Channel Width subfield in the VHT
40
Operation Information field in the HE Operation element, the Channel Center Frequency Segment 0
41
42 and Channel Center Frequency Segment 1 subfields in the VHT Operation Information field in the
43 HE Operation element to indicate the BSS bandwidth as defined in Table 11-24 (VHT BSS band-
44 width) and Table 11-26 (Extended NSS channel width) respectively based on the Rx HE-MCS Map
45
≤ 80 MHz, Rx HE-MCS Map 160 MHz, and Rx HE-MCS Map 80+80 MHz fields.
46
47
NOTE 1—The Channel Center Frequency Segment 2 is 0 if Table 11-24 (VHT BSS bandwidth) is applied.
48
49 NOTE 2—These two methods give the same result.
50
51
The setting of the Channel Center Frequency Segment 0, Channel Center Frequency Segment 1 and Channel
52
Center Frequency Segment 2 subfields is defined in Table 11-25 (Setting of Channel Center Frequency Seg-
53
54 ment 0, Channel Center Frequency Segment 1 and Channel Center Frequency Segment 2 subfields), except
55 that the Max NSS support is provided by the HE STA in frames that contain an HE Capabilities element (see
56 9.4.2.248 (HE Capabilities element)) and an Operating Mode field (see 9.2.4.6.4.3 (Operating Mode) and
57
9.4.1.53 (Operating Mode field)), where in the table the maximum NSS support refers to the HE maximum
58
NSS support instead of the VHT maximum NSS support for an HE STA.
59
60
61 An HE STA shall determine the channelization using the information in the Primary Channel field of the HT
62
Operation element when operating in 2.4 GHz and the combination of the information in the Primary Chan-
63
nel field in the HT Operation element and the Channel Center Frequency Segment 0 and Channel Center
64
65 Frequency Segment 1 subfields in the VHT Operation Information field in the VHT Operation element if

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 operating in the 5 GHz band (see 21.3.14 (Channelization)). An HE STA determines the channelization as
2 defined in 26.17.2 (HE BSS operation in the 6 GHz band) if operating in the 6 GHz band.
3
4
5 An HE AP or an HE mesh STA shall set the Secondary Channel Offset subfield in the HT Operation Infor-
6 mation field in the HT Operation element to indicate the secondary 20 MHz channel as defined in Table 9-
7 168 (HT Operation element fields and subfields), if the BSS bandwidth is more than 20 MHz.
8
9
10 An HE STA that is a member of an HE BSS shall follow the rules in 11.40.1 (Basic VHT BSS functionality)
11 when transmitting a 20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz or 80+80 MHz HE PPDUs with the following
12 exceptions:
13
14 — An HE TB PPDU sent in response to a triggering frame follows the rules defined in 26.5.2.3 (Non-
15 AP STA behavior for UL MU operation).
16
17 — An 80MHz, 160MHz or 80+80 MHz DL HE MU PPDU with preamble puncturing may be trans-
18 mitted if the primary 20 MHz or the primary 40 MHz are occupied by the transmission and certain
19
20MHz subchannels of the secondary channel are idle (see Table27-20 (HE-SIG-A field of an HE
20
MU PPDU) and 10.23.2.5 (EDCA channel access in VHT, HE, or TVHT BSS)).
21
22
23 An HE STA shall not transmit to a recipient HE STA using a channel width that is not indicated as supported
24
in the Supported Channel Width Set subfield in the HE Capabilities element received from that HE STA.
25
26
27 An HE STA shall not transmit to a recipient HE STA using a channel width that exceeds the BSS channel
28 width in the Channel Width field that is contained in:
29
30 — The HE Operation element most recently exchanged with the recipient STA, if any, and if the Chan-
31 nel Width field is present
32
33 — Otherwise, the VHT Operation element most recently exchanged with the recipient STA, if any
34
35 — Otherwise, the HT Operation element most recently exchanged with the recipient STA, if any.
36
37
A STA shall not transmit an HE PPDU to a recipient STA that carries a frame that is not an HE Compressed
38
Beamforming/CQI frame (see 26.7.3 (Rules for HE sounding protocol sequences)) and that exceeds the
39
40 maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the VHT Capabilities element last received from the recipi-
41 ent STA in the 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz band or, if a VHT Capabilities element has not been received from the
42 recipient STA, that exceeds the maximum A-MSDU length indicated in the HT Capabilities element last
43
received from the recipient STA in the 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz band.
44
45
46 A STA shall not transmit an HE PPDU to a recipient STA that carries a frame that is not an HE Compressed
47 Beamforming/CQI frame (see 26.7.3 (Rules for HE sounding protocol sequences)) and that exceeds the
48
maximum MPDU length capability indicated in the HE 6GHz Band Capabilities element last received from
49
the recipient STA in the 6 GHz band.
50
51
52 An HE AP shall set the RIFS Mode field in the HT Operation element to 0.
53
54
55 An HE STA follows the rules in 11.40 (VHT BSS operation) for channel selection, determining scanning
56 requirements, channel switching, NAV assertion and antenna indication when operating in the 5 GHz or 6
57
GHz band unless explicitly stated otherwise in Clause26 (High Efficiency (HE) MAC specification). An
58
HE STA shall additionally follow the rules in 26.17.2 (HE BSS operation in the 6 GHz band) for scanning
59
60 and operation in the 6 GHz band.
61
62
An HE STA shall follow the rules in 11.16 (20/40 MHz BSS operation) for channel selection, determining
63
scanning requirements, channel switching, NAV assertion when operating in 2.4 GHz unless explicitly
64
65 stated otherwise in Clause26 (High Efficiency (HE) MAC specification).

49
50 mesh STA.

53
6GHz non-AP STA.

1 A STA 6G shall set dot11SpectrumManagementRequired to true and operate as defined in 11.7 (TPC proce-

7 A 6 GHz AP may respond with a (Re)Association Response frame with the Status Code field indicating
8 DENIED_POOR_CHANNEL_CONDITIONS if it receives a (Re)Association Request frame from a non-
9
AP STA below a minimum RSSI threshold value. A 6 GHz AP may send a Disassociation frame with the
10
Reason Code field indicating POOR_RSSI_CONDITIONS to an associated non-AP STA if it receives
11
12 frames from the STA below a minimum RSSI threshold value for a sufficiently long period of time. How an
13 AP selects a minimum RSSI threshold value or sufficient interval of time is out of scope of this specifica-
14 tion.
15
16
17 A 6 GHz non-AP STA that receives a (Re)Association Response frame with the Status Code field indicating
18 DENIED_POOR_CHANNEL_CONDITIONS or a Disassociation frame with the Reason Code field indi-
19 cating POOR_RSSI_CONDITIONS from a 6 GHz AP should not transmit a (Re)Association Request frame
20
or a Probe Request frame to the AP until one of the following condition is met:
21
22 — Sufficient time has passed since it received the (Re)Association Response frame or Disassociation
23 frame from the AP.
24
— The STA has determined that a (Re)Association Request frame or Probe Request frame that it trans-
25
26 mits will be received by the AP at a sufficiently high RSSI level and in sufficiently good conditions
27 compared with its previous transmission to the AP.
28
29
How a 6 GHz non-AP STA determines sufficient time has passed or a suitable RSSI threshold is out of
30
scope of the standard.

33 26.17.2.2 Beacons in the 6 GHz band
34
35
A 6 GHz AP transmits Beacon frames as defined in 11.1 (Synchronization), which may be contained in a
36
non-HT PPDU, non-HT duplicate PPDU, or HE SU PPDU.
37
38
39 A 6 GHz AP that transmits a Beacon frame in a non-HT PPDU follows the rules in 10.6.5.1 (Rate selection
40
for non-STBC Beacon and non-STBC PSMP frames).
41
42
43 A 6 GHz AP that transmits a Beacon frame in a non-HT duplicate PPDU shall follow the rules in 10.6.5.1
44 (Rate selection for non-STBC beacon and non-STBC PSMP frames) and shall set the TXVECTOR parame-
45 ter CH_BANDWIDTH of the PPDU to a value that is up to the operating channel width of the BSS.
46
47
If a 6 GHz AP schedules a Beacon frame for transmission in a non-HT duplicate PPDU then it shall set the
48
49 Duplicate Beacon subfield to 1 in the 6 GHz Operation Information field of the HE Operation element it
50 transmits; otherwise the AP shall set the Duplicate Beacon subfield to 0.
51
52
A 6 GHz AP that transmits a Beacon frame in an HE SU PPDU shall follow the rules defined in 26.15.6
53
(Additional rules for HE beacons and group addressed frames).
54
55
NOTE—An AP does not transmit a Beacon frame in an HE SU PPDU or non-HT duplicate PPDU in the 2.4GHz or
56
5GHz bands.

1 A 6 GHz AP may set dot11ColocatedRNRImplemented to true and shall set dot11ShortSSIDListImple-

6 A STA 6G shall not transmit a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address that includes a
7 Short SSID List element with more than one Short SSID field.
8
9 NOTE—In bands other than the 6 GHz band, there might be more than one Short SSID field in a Short SSID List ele-
10 ment in a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address. A Probe Request frame does not contain more than
11 one Short SSID List element (see Table 9-38 (Probe Request frame body)). A Probe Request frame transmitted to the
12 broadcast destination address that contains a Short SSID List element has the SSID field of the SSID element set to the
13 SSID of a known AP or set to the one-octet value 128 if the STA does not know any SSID (see 11.1.4.3.2 (Active scan-

16 26.17.2.3.2 AP behavior for fast passive scanning
17
18
A 6 GHz AP that does not share the same co-located AP set as an AP operating in the 2.4 GHz band or
19
20 5GHz band is referred to as a 6 GHz-only AP.
21
22
A 6 GHz-only AP that intends to be efficiently discovered by STAs using scanning in the 6 GHz band shall
23
have dot11FILSFDFrameBeaconMaximumInterval set to a nonzero value that is less than or equal to 20
24
25 TUs.
26
NOTE—A 6 GHz-only AP intends to be efficiently discovered by STAs using scanning in the 6 GHz band if it sched-
27
ules for transmission FILS Discovery, Beacon, or unsolicited Probe Response frames every 20 TUs or less.
28
29
30 If a 6 GHz AP has dot11UnsolicitedProbeResponseOptionActivated equal to true, then the AP shall have
31 dot11FILSFDFrameBeaconMaximumInterval set to a nonzero value that is less than or equal to 20 TUs;
32 otherwise the AP may have dot11FILSFDFrameBeaconMaximumInterval set to any value.
33
34
35 A 6 GHz AP that has dot11FILSFDFrameBeaconMaximumInterval equal to a nonzero value shall schedule
36 for transmission FILS Discovery frames as described in 11.46.2.1 (FILS Discovery frame transmission),
37 except that the following apply:
38
— If the FILS Discovery frame is contained in a DL HE MU PPDU then it shall be included in a broad-
39
40 cast RU of the DL HE MU PPDU as defined in 26.15.7 (Additional rules for group addressed frames
41 in an HE MU PPDU).
42
— If dot11UnsolicitedProbeResponseOptionActivated is true, all FILS Discovery frames shall be omit-
43
44 ted and an unsolicited broadcast Probe Response frame shall be scheduled for transmission at the tar-
45 get transmit time instead of each omitted FILS Discovery frame.
46
— If dot11UnsolicitedProbeResponseOptionActivated is false, then a FILS Discovery frame may be
47
48 omitted and an unsolicited broadcast Probe Response frame shall be scheduled for transmission at
49 the target transmit time instead of each omitted FILS Discovery frame.
50
51
A 6 GHz AP may send an unsolicited broadcast Probe Response frame. The Probe Response frame may be
52
included in a broadcast RU of a DL HE MU PPDU as defined in 26.15.7 (Additional rules for group
53
54 addressed frames in an HE MU PPDU). The Probe Response may be carried in a non-HT duplicate PPDU in
55 which case the PPDU shall have the TXVECTOR parameter CH_BANDWIDTH set to a value that is up to
56 the operating channel width of the BSS.
57
58
59 A 6 GHz AP that transmits a FILS Discovery frame carrying an FD Capability field shall set the PHY Index
60 subfield to 4.
61
62
An AP that corresponds to a nontransmitted BSSID does not schedule for transmission FILS Discovery
63
frames (see 11.46.2.1 (FILS Discovery frame transmission)) or unsolicited broadcast Probe Response
64
65 frames (see 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response)).

1 See 11.1.3.8 (Multiple BSSID procedure) for procedures on advertisement of nontransmitted BSSIDs in a
2 multiple BSSID set.
3
4
5 A 6 GHz-only AP should set up the BSS with a primary 20 MHz channel that coincides with a preferred
6 scanning channel (PSC) (see 26.17.2.3.3 (Non-AP STA scanning behavior)).
7
8 NOTE—An AP might initiate a BSS with a primary channel that coincides with a PSC in order to assist STAs that are
9 scanning the 6 GHz band to discover the BSS. The AP might subsequently switch its operating channel to a non-PSC
10 (e.g., using a CSA mechanism) if it does not expect additional (not yet associated) STAs will need to discover the BSS.

13

the element (see 11.1.4.3.4 (Criteria for sending a response)). If a 6 GHz AP receives a Probe Request frame

17 field of the Probe Response frame shall be set to the broadcast address unless the AP is not indicating its
18 actual SSID in the SSID element of its Beacon frames.

26.17.2.3.3 Non-AP STA scanning behavior
21
22
23 The set of 20 MHz channels in the 6 GHz band, with channel center frequency, ch_a = Channel starting fre-
24
quency – 55 + 80 × n (MHz) are referred to as preferred scanning channels (PSCs). Channel starting fre-
25
quency is defined in 27.3.23.2 (Channel allocation in the 6 GHz band), and n=1,…,15.
26
27
NOTE—PSCs might not all be available in a specific location due to regulatory restrictions. A STA scanning the 6 GHz
28
band knows where these PSCs are located since their position is fixed.
29
30
31 A non-AP STA that is actively scanning a channel in the 6 GHz band shall operate as defined in 11.1.4.3.2
32 (Active scanning procedure for a non-DMG STA), unless a given rule is superseded by the rules defined in
33 26.17.2.3 (Scanning in the 6 GHz band).
34
35
The non-AP STA shall not transmit a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address with the
36
37 Address 3 field set to the wildcard BSSID, and the SSID set to the wildcard SSID.
38
39
The non-AP STA shall not send a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address with the Address
40
3 field (BSSID) set to the BSSID of an AP from which it has already received a Probe Response or a Beacon
41
42 frame since the start of its scanning on that channel.
43
44 The non-AP STA shall not send a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address with the Address
45
3 field (BSSID) set to the BSSID of a nontransmitted BSSID if it has already received the nontransmitted
46
BSSID profile for that BSSID via a Beacon frame or Probe Response frame sent by the transmitted BSSID
47
48 since the start of its scanning on that channel.
49
50
Until the FILSProbeTimer reaches dot11FILSProbeDelay, the non-AP STA shall not send a Probe Request
51
frame to the broadcast destination address with the SSID field set to the SSID of an AP for which it has
52
53 received a Reduced Neighbor Report or Neighbor Report element with the Unsolicited Probe Responses
54 Active subfield corresponding to that AP set to 1 and that indicates that the AP is operating in that channel.
55 Until the FILSProbeTimer reaches dot11FILSProbeDelay, the non-AP STA shall not send a Probe Request
56
frame to the broadcast destination address with the Address 3 field set to the BSSID of an AP for which it
57
has received a Reduced Neighbor Report or Neighbor Report element with the Unsolicited Probe Responses
58
59 Active subfield corresponding to that AP set to 1 and that indicates that the AP is operating in that channel.
60 Until the FILSProbeTimer reaches dot11FILSProbeDelay, the non-AP STA shall not send a Probe Request
61 frame to the broadcast destination address with the Short SSID field set to the short SSID that corresponds to
62
the SSID of an AP for which it has received a Reduced Neighbor Report or Neighbor Report element with
63
the Unsolicited Probe Responses Active subfield corresponding to that AP set to 1 and that indicates that the
64
65 AP is operating in that channel.

## 第 487 页

IEEE P802.11ax/D8.0, October 2020
1 The non-AP STA shall not transmit more than one Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address
2 with the Address 3 field set to the wildcard BSSID and the SSID field not set to the wildcard SSID during
3
each 20 TU period scanning the channel. The non-AP STA shall not transmit more than three Probe Request
4
frames to the broadcast destination address with Address 3 field set to a non-wildcard BSSID during each 20
5
6 TU period scanning the channel.
7
8
The non-AP STA shall set dot11FILSProbeDelay to a value equal to or greater than 20 TU.
9
10
11 NOTE—A non-AP STA waits for at least 20 TU so that it maximizes the probability of receiving FILS Discovery or
12 broadcast Probe Response frames, if any, sent by an AP in that channel (see 26.17.2.3.2 (AP behavior for fast passive
13 scanning)).
14
15
If the non-AP STA is scanning a channel, then the following apply:
16
17
— If the STA has received a FILS Discovery frame indicating that an AP is operating in that channel, or
18
if the STA has received a Reduced Neighbor Report or Neighbor Report element indicating that an
19
20 AP is operating in that channel then the STA may, subject to the other rules in this clause, send a
21 Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address in that channel, with the SSID field set to
22 the SSID that corresponds to that AP or with the Short SSID field of the Short SSID List element set
23
to the short SSID that corresponds to that AP and/or with the Address 3 field set to the BSSID of that
24
AP, starting from step c) of 11.1.4.3.2 (Active scanning procedure for a non-DMG STA)
25
26
— Otherwise, if the channel is a PSC and the STA has determined the medium to be idle for a continu-
27
28 ous period of at least dot11MinPSCProbeDelay from the start of the scan on the channel then the
29 STA may, subject to other rules in this subclause, send a Probe Request frame to the broadcast desti-
30 nation address in that channel, with the SSID field set to the SSID that corresponds to an AP or with
31
the Short SSID field of the Short SSID List element set to the short SSID that corresponds to an AP,
32
and/or with the Address 3 field set to the BSSID of an AP, after invoking the backoff procedure,
33
34 described in 10.23.2.2 (EDCA backoff procedure) and starting from step c) of 11.1.4.3.2 (Active
35 scanning procedure for a non-DMG STA)
36
37 — Otherwise, if the STA has discovered the presence of an AP in that channel through means that are
38 out of scope of the standard and the AP might be detected by the STA (see the detected access point
39 (AP) definition in 3.2 (Definitions specific to IEEE 802.11)), then the STA may send a Probe
40
Request frame to the broadcast destination address in that channel, with the Address 3 field set to the
41
BSSID of that AP starting from step c) of 11.1.4.3.2 (Active scanning procedure for a non-DMG
42
43 STA)
44
45 — Otherwise, if the FILSProbeTimer reaches dot11FILSProbeDelay and the channel is a PSC, then the
46 STA may, subject to the other rules in this subclause, send a Probe Request to the broadcast destina-
47 tion address in that channel, starting from step c) of 11.1.4.3.2 (Active scanning procedure for a non-
48
DMG STA),
49
50
— Otherwise, the STA shall not send a Probe Request frame to the broadcast destination address in that
51
channel.
52
53
54 NOTE—The STA might send an individually addressed Probe Request frame to an AP for reasons other than active scan
55 (e.g. to obtain an updated EDCA parameter set) even if it has already received a FILS Discovery, Probe Response or
56 Beacon frame from that AP.
57
58
If a non-AP STA sends a Probe Request frame in the 6 GHz band that includes a FILS Request Parameters
59
element, then the non-AP STA shall set the value of PHY Support Criterion subfield in the element to either
60
61 0 or 3.
62
63
See 11.1.3.8 (Multiple BSSID procedure) for procedures on discovery of nontransmitted BSSIDs in a multi-
64
65 ple BSSID set.
